EDCS-05000579.summary: This inscription details a commercial transaction where Herac received and tasted the Veturianum belonging to Pontius Astigi, with financial figures provided by Martialis.

EDCS-05000580.translation: Of the Antonii, Melissus and Peregrinus.
EDCS-05000580.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or ownership by Melissus and Peregrinus, possibly brothers or partners of the Antonii family.

EDCS-05000581.translation: Oni received and tasted the Aelianum of Aelia Marciana. The cost was 56, the profit 125. Anicetus.
EDCS-05000581.summary: This inscription documents a business transaction where Oni purchased and sampled a product associated with Aelia Marciana, with figures recorded by Anicetus.

EDCS-05000582.summary: This inscription probably records the ownership or a dedication by Quintus Vinisius Serenus.

EDCS-05000584.translation: Flos, aged 65, from the estate of Gaius Visius Crispinus.
EDCS-05000584.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flos, who was 65 years old and came from the property of Gaius Visius Crispinus.

EDCS-05000591.translation: To the divine spirit of Genava.
EDCS-05000591.summary: This is a dedication to the divine spirit of Genava.

EDCS-05200471.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. His mother set this up.
EDCS-05200471.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a mother for her son, Kalinicus.

EDCS-05200472.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Aetronius set this up for his most dutiful freedwoman, Aetronia Euchis.
EDCS-05200472.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Aetronius for his freedwoman Aetronia Euchis.

EDCS-05200473.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Metellius Sabinus.

EDCS-05200474.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. His sister Titia set this up for Titus Annius Successus.
EDCS-05200474.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titia for her brother Titus Annius Successus.

EDCS-05200476.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pupus, son of Lucius, set this up for his most beloved wife, Marcella, daughter of Marcellus.
EDCS-05200476.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Pupus for his wife Marcella.

EDCS-05200478.translation: Titus Coelius Germanus, a butcher, dedicated this from his own funds.
EDCS-05200478.summary: This is a dedication made by Titus Coelius Germanus, a butcher, using his own money.

EDCS-05200480.translation: Numerius willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-05200480.summary: This inscription records Numerius fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-05200481.translation: Her mother made this for Prima, daughter of Trovnis.
EDCS-05200481.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a mother for her daughter, Prima.

EDCS-05200482.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cornelia Calliope, who lived twenty-four years. Volcacius Thallus to his dearest wife.
EDCS-05200482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Volcacius Thallus for his wife Cornelia Calliope.

EDCS-05200483.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aptina, daughter of Aptius. Quarta, her mother.
EDCS-05200483.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Quarta for her daughter Aptina.

EDCS-05200484.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Scymnia Chresis. Lucius Latinius Helenus to his excellent wife.
EDCS-05200484.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Latinius Helenus for his wife Scymnia Chresis.

EDCS-05200485.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Maesius Maximus to Secundinus, a deserving slave.
EDCS-05200485.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Maesius Maximus for his slave Secundinus.

EDCS-05200486.translation: Lucius Cornelius Celer, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-05200486.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Lucius Cornelius Celer, identifying his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05200487.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Iulia Cornelia. Cornelius Ianuaris, his freedman.
EDCS-05200487.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Cornelius Ianuaris for his freedwoman Iulia Cornelia.

EDCS-05200488.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The heirs of Caius Iulius Pius.
EDCS-05200488.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating that the heirs of Caius Iulius Pius are responsible for the burial.

EDCS-05200489.translation: To Baebius Solimarus and Iulia, his wife. Cintugnatus Messinus Quintulus, their son.
EDCS-05200489.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Cintugnatus Messinus Quintulus for his parents, Baebius Solimarus and Iulia.

EDCS-05200490.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Celsia Aventina set up this for Titus Celsinus Honoratus, her most devoted patron.
EDCS-05200490.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Celsia Aventina for her patron Titus Celsinus Honoratus.

EDCS-05200491.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vegeta, identifying her father and her status as a freedwoman.

EDCS-05200492.translation: To the Divine Shades. Grata, daughter of Iarravia, freedman of Priscus.
EDCS-05200492.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Grata, likely set up by her freedman, Priscus.

EDCS-05200493.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely naming two individuals, possibly on a shared tomb or monument.

EDCS-05200494.translation: Peregrina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to her own Mars.
EDCS-05200494.summary: This is a votive inscription where Peregrina dedicates an altar or offering to Mars.

EDCS-05200497.translation: Quintus willingly and deservedly made an altar to Silvanus.
EDCS-05200497.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the dedication of an altar to the god Silvanus by Quintus.

EDCS-05200498.translation: Titia Peregrina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-05200498.summary: This is a votive inscription where Titia Peregrina dedicates an altar or offering.

EDCS-05401073.translation: To Manius Avelius Paterno, son of Manius, decurion of Falerna, who lived 19 years, 10 months, and 19 days. Manius Avelius Marcellus and Comisia Tranquillina, his parents, [set this up] for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-05401073.summary: This is a funerary monument for Manius Avelius Paterno, a decurion, erected by his parents.

EDCS-05401076.translation: The most unfortunate parents had [this] made for Publius Emilio Paternus, son of Lucius, their most dutiful son.
EDCS-05401076.summary: This is a funerary monument for Publius Emilio Paternus, erected by his grieving parents.

EDCS-05401348.translation: Imperator Caesar Augustus, in his 10th consulship, with 11 tribunician powers.
EDCS-05401348.summary: This inscription records the honors and powers granted to Emperor Augustus, likely on a public building or monument, dated to around 43 BCE.

EDCS-05401350.translation: Emperor Antoninus Pius, in his 11th consulship, [had this] put up.
EDCS-05401350.summary: This inscription records an act or building project initiated by Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely dating to his 11th consulship (148 CE).

EDCS-06100394.summary: This is a short inscription possibly dedicating something to the Emperor Constantine, implying loyalty or a religious context.

EDCS-06100398.translation: Between the Valerian gardens and the vineyards of Priterius.
EDCS-06100398.summary: This is a boundary inscription defining the limits of land owned by Priterius.

EDCS-06100399.translation: On the front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet, for Publius Licinius Propinquus.
EDCS-06100399.summary: This is a boundary inscription detailing the dimensions of a plot of land dedicated to Publius Licinius Propinquus.

EDCS-06100401.translation: Euclides Nigella had this made as instructed by the will of Gaius Julius Tigranus, son of Voltinia, for two bronze [coins].
EDCS-06100401.summary: This is a funerary or dedication inscription commissioned by Euclides Nigella for Gaius Julius Tigranus, likely a patron, as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-06100402.translation: Lucius Praecianus, son of Sextus, of the Aniensis tribe, [serving in] the legion.
EDCS-06100402.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Lucius Praecianus, son of Sextus, and noting his tribal affiliation and military service.

EDCS-07000727.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of an object or monument as Lucius Afranius Alexander.

EDCS-07000729.translation: To the spirits of the underworld of Raucia Fortunata.
EDCS-07000729.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the deceased Raucia Fortunata.

EDCS-07000730.translation: Titus Iccius Agathon erected this for his brother, to the spirits of the underworld of Marcus Minicius Agathonichus.
EDCS-07000730.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Titus Iccius Agathon dedicated a monument to the spirits of his brother, Marcus Minicius Agathonichus.

EDCS-07000732.summary: This is a label or inscription on a container, likely indicating the quality and owner of the wine.

EDCS-07000734.translation: 42 Roman feet, 113 Roman feet in the field, of Oenodorus.
EDCS-07000734.summary: This is a boundary inscription detailing land measurements associated with Oenodorus.

EDCS-07000735.translation: To Massilia, thousand measures of dry, pure wheat from Cavarum, with Rubrius, 1 measure.
EDCS-07000735.summary: This is a record of a delivery or sale, possibly related to grain, involving Massilia and quantities specified by Rubrius.

EDCS-07000736.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, likely indicating its origin and quality.

EDCS-07000737.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or sarcophagus.

EDCS-07000739.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, specifying its type and origin.

EDCS-07000742.translation: Old Massic wine from Quintus Helvius Martialis Domitius Uminus.
EDCS-07000742.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, identifying the wine type and likely the producer or owner.

EDCS-07000743.translation: Amineum wine from Titus Aelius Herrianus.
EDCS-07000743.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, indicating the wine type and its owner or producer.

EDCS-07000744.translation: Mulsum wine at 60 units from Quintus I. T. Pomponius Secundus.
EDCS-07000744.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, likely indicating a specific type of spiced wine and its volume or price.

EDCS-07000745.translation: Excellent Picatum wine from Gaius Aelius Censorinus.
EDCS-07000745.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, describing the quality and origin of the wine.

EDCS-07000746.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or sarcophagus.

EDCS-07000747.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a dedication on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-07000751.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or sarcophagus.

EDCS-07000756.translation: Falernian wine, aged nine years, of Caius Lassius Africanus.
EDCS-07000756.summary: This is a label for a wine amphora, indicating the type and age of the wine, and its owner.

EDCS-07000758.translation: The best mackerel garum, for Quintus Caecilius.
EDCS-07000758.summary: This is a label for a garum (fish sauce) amphora, indicating its quality and intended recipient.

EDCS-07400565.translation: The hands of Murius Nervius. Marcus, son of Titus, in front, [in width] feet.
EDCS-07400565.summary: This inscription likely relates to a boundary marker or property, indicating measurements and an owner.

EDCS-07600296.translation: Ocracius Fronto, son of Ocracius, set this up for the Mogontiones.
EDCS-07600296.summary: This is a dedication to a group of deities or spirits called Mogontiones, made by Ocracius Fronto.

EDCS-07600298.translation: Quintus Nertius Apthorus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the August Mothers.
EDCS-07600298.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a payment or offering to the Imperial Mothers, made by Quintus Nertius Apthorus.

EDCS-08400613.translation: For the memory of Viria Melpomene, my dearest mother, Severina made this at her own expense for herself.
EDCS-08400613.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a mother, Viria Melpomene, funded by Severina.

EDCS-08400743.translation: Maiurrus, Caius Flavius Secundinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08400743.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a payment or offering, likely to a deity associated with Maiurrus, made by Caius Flavius Secundinus.

EDCS-08400744.translation: Vigilia, daughter of Metius Massa, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Mars Olloudius.
EDCS-08400744.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a payment or offering to the god Mars Olloudius, made by Vigilia, daughter of Metius Massa.

EDCS-08400746.summary: This is a dedication to the god Neptune, made by Veratia Montana.

EDCS-08400751.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Emperor Constantine. The approximate date is the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-08400752.translation: For the safety of Marcus Julius Ligur, procurator of Caesar, Agathocles, slave, fulfilled his vow to Pan.
EDCS-08400752.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Agathocles, a slave, for the safety of Marcus Julius Ligur, a procurator, possibly in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400753.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: for Marcus Moltelius Secundinus, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, flamen and duovir of Antipolis, his heirs from his will.
EDCS-08400753.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Moltelius Secundinus, who held important local magistracies in Antipolis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400754.translation: Rutilia Secunda to her father Lucius Rutilius, duovir four times.
EDCS-08400754.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Rutilia Secunda to her father, Lucius Rutilius, who served multiple terms as duovir, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400755.translation: To Secundinus, on the public horse.
EDCS-08400755.summary: This brief inscription likely honors Secundinus, possibly an equestrian official, with the date inferable to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400756.translation: Caius Tullius Flavianus, son of a decurion, from Catina in the province of Sicily, a resident of Antipolis, made this living for his most deserving wife, Iulia Caeliana, freedwoman Niallusae, and for himself.
EDCS-08400756.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Caius Tullius Flavianus for his wife Iulia Caeliana and himself, indicating their origin and residence in Antipolis during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400757.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Titus Valerius Nepos to his father Titus Valerius Paternus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, duovir, flamen, and decurion, who was also on the public horse, pontifex, and decurion.
EDCS-08400757.summary: This funerary dedication by Titus Valerius Nepos to his father Titus Valerius Paternus lists numerous civic and religious offices held by the deceased in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400759.translation: Caius Venusius Andron, a corporately recognized Augustalis and sexvir, to his most sweet daughter Venusia Anthimilla.
EDCS-08400759.summary: This funerary dedication by Caius Venusius Andron to his daughter Venusia Anthimilla identifies the dedicator with prominent civic and religious roles, dating it to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400760.translation: [...lia]m alive made for himself. Recentius Zeses made this for Verginia Paternus [and] Veninia Marcella, his most pious daughter.
EDCS-08400760.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Recentius Zeses for his daughter Veninia Marcella, with a partial mention of another individual, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400763.translation: Marcella, flaminica and priestess, alive, made this for herself and completed the memorial.
EDCS-08400763.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument prepared by Marcella, who held religious offices, for herself, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400764.summary: This inscription fragment likely records the name and profession of Sextus Julius Cae.

EDCS-08400765.translation: Gaius Julius Catullinus dedicated this gift to the College of the Waterskin Makers.
EDCS-08400765.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Julius Catullinus to the College of the Waterskin Makers.

EDCS-08400766.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For the boy Septentrionis, aged XII, who danced for two days in the theatre at Antipolis and pleased [the audience].
EDCS-08400766.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates a young boy named Septentrionis who died after performing in the theatre at Antipolis.

EDCS-08400769.translation: Aeliae Iuliae Celsae. Aelius Felicianus and Varia Emerita made [this] for their daughter.
EDCS-08400769.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aelius Felicianus and Varia Emerita dedicated this monument to their daughter, Aelia Iulia Celsa.

EDCS-08400770.translation: Gaius Albucius Euvaristus made [this] while alive and ordered it to be made by his will.
EDCS-08400770.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Albucius Euvaristus made this monument for himself during his lifetime and also arranged for it in his will.

EDCS-08400771.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gaius Albucius Optatus, who lived 21 years. Gaius Albucius Optatus and Maximia Marcella dedicated [this] to their dearest son.
EDCS-08400771.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Albucius Optatus and Maximia Marcella dedicated this monument to their son, Gaius Albucius Optatus, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-08400772.translation: For Valeria. Gaius Albucius Ortrus made [this] for his most deserving wife, with whom he lived for 30 years without any complaint.
EDCS-08400772.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Albucius Ortrus dedicated this monument to his wife Valeria, with whom he lived for 30 years.

EDCS-08400773.translation: For Lucius Albucius Scaevianus. Albucia Chrysis, mother, made [this] for her son and for herself and her descendants during her lifetime.
EDCS-08400773.summary: This inscription records that Albucia Chrysis made a monument for her son Lucius Albucius Scaevianus, and also for herself and her descendants, during her lifetime.

EDCS-08400774.translation: Aristia Eutychia made [this] for herself while alive.
EDCS-08400774.summary: This inscription records that Aristia Eutychia made this monument for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-08400775.translation: For Numisia Marcella. Lucius Tauganius Fronto dedicated [this] to his deserving wife. Lucius Tauganius Fronto made [this] for himself while alive.
EDCS-08400775.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Tauganius Fronto dedicated a monument to his wife Numisia Marcella and also made one for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-08400776.translation: Lucius Attius Verus ordered by his will that this be made for himself, for his father Quintus Attius, and for his uncle Lucius Attius Rut.
EDCS-08400776.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Attius Verus, dedicating a monument to himself and his relatives, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400777.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius C. P. made this for his brother Titus, son of C., and Attia G.
EDCS-08400777.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius C. P., dedicating a monument to his deceased brother Titus and Attia G., likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400778.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Maticius Albucianus [made this] for his wife Aurelia Lucilia, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-08400778.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Quintus Maticius Albucianus, dedicating a monument to his wife Aurelia Lucilia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400779.translation: Marcus Multilius Paternus and Lucilia, his foster-child, made this while alive for Balbia Pata, and for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-08400779.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Multilius Paternus and Lucilia, made during their lifetime for Balbia Pata and their descendants, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400780.translation: Trophime made this for Aulus Calpurnius P., aged 10 years and 6 months, his most dutiful son, and for himself.
EDCS-08400780.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Trophime, dedicating a monument to his young son Aulus Calpurnius P. and himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400782.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maccius Regillus and Domitia Felicissima [made this] for their most dutiful foster-son, Domitius Macarius.
EDCS-08400782.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Maccius Regillus and Domitia Felicissima, dedicating a monument to their deceased foster-son Domitius Macarius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400783.translation: Domitius Primigenius Soter Felix [made this] for his most dutiful father, Marcus Domitius Zosimus.
EDCS-08400783.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Domitius Primigenius Soter Felix, dedicating a monument to his deceased father Marcus Domitius Zosimus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400785.translation: Erius Ferox made this while alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-08400785.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Erius Ferox, made during his lifetime for himself and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400786.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Adrettius and Titus Flavius Moderatus made this for their most dutiful father, Titus Flavius Valerius.
EDCS-08400786.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Titus Flavius Adrettius and Titus Flavius Moderatus, dedicating a monument to their deceased father Titus Flavius Valerius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400787.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Valerianus and Voconia Primitiva [made this] for their son Titus Flavius Valerius, who lived 28 years and 7 months.
EDCS-08400787.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavius Valerianus and Voconia Primitiva, dedicating a monument to their deceased son Titus Flavius Valerius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400788.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maelia Secunda, mother, made this for herself and her own while alive for Caius Gallius Paternus, son of Lucius Maelius Avitus, most dutiful.
EDCS-08400788.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Caius Gallius Paternus by his mother.

EDCS-08400790.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Granius Vitalio made this for his daughter Grania Valeria, who lived for 4 years.
EDCS-08400790.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grania Valeria, dedicated by her father Marcus Granius Vitalio.

EDCS-08400791.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iovius Julius Ligur, most pious son of Iovia Santippe, who lived for 2 years and 3 months. You too, traveler, pause a moment, hoping that such things might befall you, having been snatched away by too cruel fate. Iovius Julius Ligur, most pious son of Iovia Santippe, who lived for 2 years and 3 months.
EDCS-08400791.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, Iovius Julius Ligur, who died at the age of 2 years and 3 months, dedicated by his mother Iovia Santippe.

EDCS-08400792.translation: For Julia Marcia. Nevibius Licininus and Muniniella Marciana, her heirs.
EDCS-08400792.summary: This inscription records the heirs of Julia Marcia.

EDCS-08400793.translation: For Julia Victorina. Valerius Cornelianus, her son, and Abicelia Secundina, his wife, made this.
EDCS-08400793.summary: This funerary epitaph for Julia Victorina was made by her son Valerius Cornelianus and his wife Abicelia Secundina.

EDCS-08400794.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iuventia Sabina and her son.
EDCS-08400794.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iuventia Sabina and her son.

EDCS-08400795.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Livius Nicostratus and Livia Nice made this while alive for their father Lucius Livius Onesimus and their patron Lucius Livius Hermes, and for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-08400795.summary: This funerary epitaph was made by Marcus Livius Nicostratus and Livia Nice for their father and patron.

EDCS-08400796.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Look, traveler, as you pass by, and lament my title, how prematurely I met death. I was snatched away at the age of thirty years, the most cherished light of life, and I alone from my family lived without a little one. My mother wept miserably for me, because I was left without the honor of piety. Raielia Secundina, mother, made this for her most pious son Quintus Luccunius Verus.
EDCS-08400796.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Luccunius Verus, who died at 30, dedicated by his grieving mother Raielia Secundina.

EDCS-08400801.translation: For Gaius Pompeius Blanus, husband of Velia Capitolina, and for their son Marcus Pompeius, and daughter Pompeia. The heirs, by will.
EDCS-08400801.summary: This inscription records the heirs designated by will for Gaius Pompeius Blanus, his wife Velia Capitolina, their son Marcus Pompeius, and daughter Pompeia.

EDCS-08400802.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publia Mansueta, for herself and for Gaius Lucius, son of Lucius, L.
EDCS-08400802.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publia Mansueta and Gaius Lucius.

EDCS-08400803.translation: Valeria Marcella, mother of the most dutiful Quadratiae Sextinae, who lived 31 years, made this monument for her daughter and herself while alive.
EDCS-08400803.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Valeria Marcella for her daughter Quadratiae Sextinae and herself.

EDCS-08400804.translation: To the departed spirits. Quiamelius Celer [set this up] for his excellent wife Calvesiae Tyche and for her mother Valeria Elpis.
EDCS-08400804.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Quiamelius Celer for his wife Calvesiae Tyche and her mother Valeria Elpis.

EDCS-08400805.translation: To the departed spirits. Reius Aristius Primitivus [set this up] for his most deserving patroness Aristiae Fileteni and for Reius Hermes for his wife.
EDCS-08400805.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Reius Aristius Primitivus for his patroness Aristiae Fileteni and for his wife.

EDCS-08400806.translation: To the departed spirits. Tullius Primus set this up for his deserving son Tullius Valerius.
EDCS-08400806.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Tullius Primus for his son Tullius Valerius.

EDCS-08400807.translation: Lucius Valerius Byblus, the heir, ordered it to be made and set it up.
EDCS-08400807.summary: This is an funerary inscription by Lucius Valerius Byblus, the heir.

EDCS-08400808.translation: Lucius Valerius Quintilianus [set this up] for his dearest and most dutiful son Lucius Valerius Lucilianus, son of Lucius, who lived 2 years, 6 months, and 18 days.
EDCS-08400808.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Valerius Quintilianus for his young son Lucius Valerius Lucilianus.

EDCS-08400809.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Blatus [set this up] for his most dutiful daughter Valeriae Paternae, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-08400809.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Valerius Blatus for his daughter Valeria Paternae.

EDCS-08400810.translation: To the departed spirits. Verginia [set this up] for her daughter Valeriae Saturnini, Saturnina.
EDCS-08400810.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Verginia for her daughter Valeria Saturnina.

EDCS-08400811.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription to Titus Veniclutius.

EDCS-08400812.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Vibius Acilianus and Lucius Verginius Glaphyrinus, the heirs, [set this up] for the well-deserving Venusio Victorinus.
EDCS-08400812.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Vibius Acilianus and Lucius Verginius Glaphyrinus for Venusio Victorinus.

EDCS-08400813.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vecilia Lucilia erected this for her well-deserving mother, Vesuccia Hermogenia.
EDCS-08400813.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Vesuccia Hermogenia by her mother, Vecilia Lucilia.

EDCS-08400821.translation: To good memory. Here rests in peace Calumniosa, handmaid of God, who lived 100 years; she was laid to rest on the day before the Nones of April.
EDCS-08400821.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for a deceased woman named Calumniosa, recording her age and date of burial.

EDCS-08400824.translation: To Apollo, willingly and deservedly, in fulfillment of a vow. Receptus and Crescens.
EDCS-08400824.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Receptus and Crescens.

EDCS-08400825.translation: Lucius Valerius Quartus Carpanto willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400825.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Valerius Quartus Carpanto.

EDCS-08400826.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Caius Cassius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400826.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Cassius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, dedicated to Jupiter.

EDCS-08400827.translation: Quintus Laberius fulfilled his vow to Liber Pater willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08400827.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Laberius dedicated to Liber Pater.

EDCS-08400829.translation: Sacred to the Divine Powers of the Emperor. Valerius Gracilis dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-08400829.summary: This is a dedication to the Imperial numen by Valerius Gracilis.

EDCS-08400832.translation: To Trittia. Lucius Iulius Martinus, son of Certus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400832.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Iulius Martinus, son of Certus, dedicated to Trittia.

EDCS-08400834.translation: Caius Iulius Iaso arranged for this to be made for Antho, trierarch to Caesar Livianus.
EDCS-08400834.summary: This inscription records that Caius Iulius Iaso arranged a construction or dedication for Antho, a trierarch serving Caesar Livianus.

EDCS-08400835.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius An[---], prefect of the fleet, at [---].
EDCS-08400835.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment for Marcus Aurelius An[---], a fleet prefect.

EDCS-08400836.translation: To Marcus Crassus, a veteran of the 19th Legion.
EDCS-08400836.summary: This is a dedication to a veteran of the 19th Legion, likely for a tombstone.

EDCS-08400837.translation: To Gaius Kafatius Capito, son of Gaius, of the Aniensis tribe, a veteran of the 9th Legion, and a father.
EDCS-08400837.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Kafatius Capito, a veteran of the 9th Legion, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-08400838.translation: To Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, first-rank centurion of the 9th Hispana Legion, who served twice as a duovir of the colony of Priamus.
EDCS-08400838.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Julius, a distinguished officer and former magistrate, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-08400839.translation: To Gaius Julius Apollonius, son of Apa, freedman, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-08400839.summary: This records a bequest or dedication made by Gaius Julius Apollonius, a freedman.

EDCS-08400840.translation: Kalavia, daughter of Marcus, centurion of the 8th Legion, rests here, in charge of religious rites.
EDCS-08400840.summary: This tombstone commemorates Kalavia, daughter of a centurion, who was responsible for religious matters.

EDCS-08400841.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Solicius Aurelianus, centurion of the 5th Macedonian Legion and the 1st Minervia Pia Fidelis Legion, and his freedman Numisius Chrestus, made this for themselves and their families while alive, for Numisia Caesia, daughter of Gaius Numisius, wife most dutiful, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-08400841.summary: This is a self-funded tombstone set up by a centurion and his freedman for themselves and their family, commemorating the centurion's wife.

EDCS-08400842.translation: To Gaius Valerius Placidus, soldier of the 14th Gemina Legion. His mother, Placida, daughter of Caius, made this. Frontage 4 feet, depth 11 feet.
EDCS-08400842.summary: This tombstone was erected by the mother of Gaius Valerius Placidus, a soldier of the 14th Legion, with dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-08400844.translation: To Caius Coelius Sestertius, freedman of Caius Coelius, and to Felix, freedman of Caius Coelius. Caius Coelius Faustus, freedman of Caius, a member of the board of six, erected this as a brother.
EDCS-08400844.summary: This tombstone honors two freedmen and was erected by their brother, who was also a freedman and a member of the local council.

EDCS-08400845.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Quintus Hirpidius Barbatus, member of the board of six, an Augustalis, who lived 30 years. Quintus Hirpidius Alphius and Hirpidia [erected this].
EDCS-08400845.summary: This tombstone is for Quintus Hirpidius Barbatus, a local official, who died at age 30.

EDCS-08400846.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Marcus Julius Euxinus, member of the board of six, an Augustalis.
EDCS-08400846.summary: This tombstone commemorates Marcus Julius Euxinus, a local official (Augustalis).

EDCS-08400847.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Licinius Eleuther made this for his patron, Publius Licinius Primus, an excellent six-man official of the imperial cult.
EDCS-08400847.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Licinius Primus, an imperial cult official, by his freedman Publius Licinius Eleuther.

EDCS-08400848.translation: His heirs, in accordance with his will, made this for Lucius Valerius Hermerotus, a six-man official of the imperial cult, and for his wife.
EDCS-08400848.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Valerius Hermerotus and his wife, made by their heirs as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-08400849.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His son made this for Lucius Veratius Encolpius, a six-man official of the imperial cult of Anienis.
EDCS-08400849.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Veratius Encolpius, an imperial cult official, made by his son.

EDCS-08400852.summary: A dedication or funerary marker for Gaius Julius Severus Petrucorius.

EDCS-08400853.summary: A dedication or funerary marker for CIL and his associate Quadratus.

EDCS-08400854.summary: A dedication or funerary marker for Albano, made by his father.

EDCS-08400855.translation: Annia Secunda, while alive, for herself.
EDCS-08400855.summary: A funerary dedication for Annia Secunda, made by herself while alive.

EDCS-08400856.translation: Quintus Atilius Phythee and Atilia made this for the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-08400856.summary: A dedication for the Sixth Cohort, made by Quintus Atilius Phythee and Atilia.

EDCS-08400857.translation: Bacchius, a centurion, for Urbana, his freedwoman.
EDCS-08400857.summary: A dedication by the centurion Bacchius for his freedwoman Urbana.

EDCS-08400858.translation: Agripina Prima, aged 40, made this for Baricbalus, her friend, and for herself, as heir according to the will.
EDCS-08400858.summary: A funerary dedication for Agripina Prima and her friend Baricbalus, made by Agripina as heir according to the will.

EDCS-08400859.translation: To Gaius Calvisius Rusticus and Gavia Modesta, parents.
EDCS-08400859.summary: This is a dedication to parents, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-08400860.translation: To Marcus Coelius Agricola, by his parents. He lived 8 years, 3 months, 8 days. He is buried here.
EDCS-08400860.summary: This epitaph records the death of a young boy, Marcus Coelius Agricola, and is set up by his parents.

EDCS-08400861.translation: To Hermes, for his dearest wife Venusta.
EDCS-08400861.summary: This epitaph is a dedication from a husband, Hermes, to his wife, Venusta.

EDCS-08400862.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Hirpidius Iulianus, IIvir, quaestor of the Pacensis, who lived 54 years, 12 days. Quintus Hirpidius, from his own funds, made this for his devoted patron.
EDCS-08400862.summary: This epitaph honors Quintus Hirpidius Iulianus, a local magistrate, and was erected by his patron.

EDCS-08400863.translation: To the departed spirits. Festina, for Iulia Restituta, her most beloved slave. From the money that remained from the funeral.
EDCS-08400863.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Iulia Restituta, made by her slave Festina using leftover funeral funds.

EDCS-08400865.translation: Caius Iulius Festus, while alive, made this for himself and his family: for his father Caius Iulius Asiaticus, and his mother Iulia Aglaidē, and his brother and heir Caius Iulius Gratus, and his wife Domitia Secundina, wife of Festus.
EDCS-08400865.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Caius Iulius Festus for himself and his family, including parents, brother, and wife.

EDCS-08400866.translation: Tiberius Iulius Cupitus is buried here. His freedmen and household staff gave this from their own funds.
EDCS-08400866.summary: This epitaph states that Tiberius Iulius Cupitus is buried here, and the monument was funded by his freedmen and household staff.

EDCS-08400867.translation: To Lucius Iulius Aniensis Iulianus, his mother Iulia Quintina, daughter of Quintus, and his wife Lucius Iulius Abascantus, made this while alive.
EDCS-08400867.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Iulia Quintina for her son Lucius Iulius Aniensis Iulianus and her husband Lucius Iulius Abascantus.

EDCS-08400868.translation: Caius Iulius Seneca for himself and for his father Marcus Iulius Exsuperatus, son of Turpa Nigra; his mother; his brothers Iulius Optatus, Lucius Iulius Ma, Lucius Iulius Gratus; his daughter Pulchra; his wife; and his son Caius Iulius Seneca; and his daughter Materna, daughter of Caius; for the Aniensia [family?].
EDCS-08400868.summary: This is a self-funded funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Seneca for himself and an extensive list of family members.

EDCS-08400869.translation: Quintus Iulius, son of Iulius, made this with his own money.
EDCS-08400869.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument or dedication made by Quintus Iulius for himself using his own funds.

EDCS-08400870.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Iulia Marcellina, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400871.translation: To Chilo, daughter of Vibius, wife of Iulia.
EDCS-08400871.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Chilo, daughter of Vibius and wife of Iulia, probably on a tombstone, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400872.translation: To Lucius Licinius Celsus, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, and to his mother Hospita, daughter of Caius, wife of Cosconia.
EDCS-08400872.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Celsus and his mother Hospita, likely on a tombstone, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400873.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Lutatius Verecundus, probably on a tombstone, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400874.translation: His brother Marcus Octavius had this made according to the will for his son Lucius Octavius of the Aniensis tribe.
EDCS-08400874.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus Octavius for his son Lucius Octavius, as stipulated in a will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400875.translation: Veratia Fausta, freedwoman of Marcus, made this for her husband Gaius Paccio, son of Lucius.
EDCS-08400875.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Veratia Fausta for her husband Gaius Paccio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400876.translation: Lucius Scaefius Primus made this for his wife, Petronia Posilla, daughter of Tertius, dutiful.
EDCS-08400876.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Lucius Scaefius Primus for his wife Petronia Posilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400877.translation: In memory of Silania Vera, who lived 6 years, 2 months, and 18 days. Silanius Gelasius and Iulia Charistosa for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-08400877.summary: This is a tombstone for Silania Vera, recording her age at death and erected by her parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400879.translation: Mother Titiana for her most dutiful daughter Nepotilla.
EDCS-08400879.summary: This inscription is a tombstone dedicated by Mother Titiana for her daughter Nepotilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400880.translation: The heirs made this for Lucius Trebonius, son of Lucius, according to the will.
EDCS-08400880.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the heirs for Lucius Trebonius, as specified in his will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400881.translation: To Valerius Calitus, son of Cai.
EDCS-08400881.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Valerius Calitus, son of Cai.

EDCS-08400882.translation: Veacaus, son of Tertius, killed overseas in Greece.
EDCS-08400882.summary: This inscription records the death of Veacaus, son of Tertius, who died in Greece.

EDCS-08400883.translation: Maxsuma, his mother, made this tomb for Gaius Vibius Ligur. The elders worked on the tomb, but the divine power intervened for the seven-year-old. Relatives and kin together raised and buried him, whom they nurtured. O, the hopes of mortals are not constant!
EDCS-08400883.summary: This funerary inscription, with Greek verse, records that Maxsuma dedicated a tomb to her son, Gaius Vibius Ligur, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-08400884.summary: This is a partial inscription likely intended as a funerary dedication.

EDCS-08400887.translation: To the departed spirits of Tilius.
EDCS-08400887.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Tilius, invoking the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits).

EDCS-08400893.translation: Lucius Illidius Restitus willingly and deservedly made this for Jupiter Depulsor.
EDCS-08400893.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Depulsor by Lucius Illidius Restitus.

EDCS-08400894.translation: Marcus Vibius Longus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08400894.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Marcus Vibius Longus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08400895.translation: To Carisius, son of Marcus, a centurion of the Tromentina tribe.
EDCS-08400895.summary: This is likely a funerary dedication to Carisius, a centurion belonging to the Tromentina tribe.

EDCS-08400897.translation: His mother made this memorial for her most dutiful son, Quintus Julius Felix. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-08400897.summary: This funerary inscription records that a mother made a memorial for her son, Quintus Julius Felix.

EDCS-08400898.translation: Manius Otacilius Onesimus, while alive, made this for himself and for his most excellent wife Otacilia Philonica, and for Valeria Sextillia and Otacilia Procula.
EDCS-08400898.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Manius Otacilius Onesimus commissioned his own tomb and those of his wife and two other women, likely family members.

EDCS-08400900.translation: To Lucius Magius Nedymus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-08400900.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Magius Nedymus, a freedman, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-08400901.translation: To the divine shades, for the memory of Quintus Solicius Maximus. Quintus Solicius Euhodus and Pompeia Enapodora, his parents, made this for their most dutiful son, who lived 12 years, 11 months, and 23 days.
EDCS-08400901.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Solicius Maximus, set up by his parents, dating to his childhood.

EDCS-08400902.translation: To the divine shades, for Titus Aevillius Sura, father. For Sextus Aevillius Secundus, son, aged 16. Titus Aevillius Secundus and Iulia Thalia made this at their joint expense for 6,000 sesterces.
EDCS-08400902.summary: This funerary monument was jointly funded by Titus Aevillius Secundus and Iulia Thalia for their son, Sextus Aevillius Secundus, who died at age 16.

EDCS-08400905.translation: For Titus Valerius Valens, aged 22. Titus Valerius Agathangelus and Iulia, his parents, made this for their most dutiful son, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08400905.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Titus Valerius Valens at age 22, set up by his parents who also intended it for themselves.

EDCS-08400906.translation: For Gaius Julius Reburrus, father. For Iulia Prima, daughter of Gaius, mother.
EDCS-08400906.summary: This inscription appears to commemorate a father, Gaius Julius Reburrus, and his daughter, Iulia Prima, possibly as a tomb marker.

EDCS-08400910.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Almahabus, Sextus Vindius Sabinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400910.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Mother Goddesses by Sextus Vindius Sabinus.

EDCS-08400912.translation: To Mars Giarinus, Sextus Iulius Firminus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400912.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars Giarinus by Sextus Iulius Firminus.

EDCS-08400913.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Ubelnabus, Sextus Licinius Successus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08400913.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Mother Goddesses by Sextus Licinius Successus.

EDCS-08400915.translation: For Lucius Attius Rufinus, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe. Lucius Clodius Posphorus.
EDCS-08400915.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or memorial for Lucius Attius Rufinus, possibly related to Lucius Clodius Posphorus.

EDCS-08400916.translation: To Jupiter Frugifer, Marcus Erucius Natalis.
EDCS-08400916.summary: This is a votive or dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Frugifer by Marcus Erucius Natalis.

EDCS-08400917.translation: Valeria Thematiliana and Lucius Valerius Certus made this for their nursling Lucius Valerius Lucinus, a most devoted son.
EDCS-08400917.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Valeria Thematiliana and Lucius Valerius Certus to their son, Lucius Valerius Lucinus.

EDCS-08400919.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Syagria, who died on the 21st day before the Kalends of February, in the eleventh indiction.
EDCS-08400919.summary: A funerary inscription for Syagria, recording her death date in the 11th indiction.

EDCS-08400920.translation: To the divine shades of Taetania Pacata, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-08400920.summary: A funerary dedication to Taetania Pacata, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-08400921.translation: Marcus Iulius Ianuarius willingly fulfilled his vow to Mars Rudianus, as deserved.
EDCS-08400921.summary: A votive inscription by Marcus Iulius Ianuarius to Mars Rudianus.

EDCS-08400923.translation: Paganus erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-08400923.summary: A funerary monument erected by Paganus at his own expense.

EDCS-08400924.translation: Cornelia Pia, daughter of Quintus, dedicated this to the divine shades while alive: for herself, for her most excellent and deserving husband Gaius Adreticius Victor, Voltian tribe, and for her son Gaius Adreticius Firminus, and for her son Sextus Adreticius Insequens, and for her son Titus Adreticius Vindicus, and for her grandson Gaius Adreticius Gratus, deceased at sixteen years of age, and for her grandson Gaius Adreticius Pius, and for her granddaughter Adreticia Pia, and for her grandson Titus Adreticius Avitus, and for her most deserving daughter-in-law Victimaria Iunia.
EDCS-08400924.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Cornelia Pia for herself and multiple family members, including her husband, sons, grandsons, and daughter-in-law.

EDCS-08400925.translation: Coilia Marcellina made this to the divine shades for her most devoted husband Lenti Pudentus.
EDCS-08400925.summary: A funerary dedication by Coilia Marcellina to her husband, Lenti Pudentus.

EDCS-08400926.translation: Valeria Marcella [made this] to the divine shades for her most excellent husband Lucius Valerius Virillio.
EDCS-08400926.summary: A funerary dedication by Valeria Marcella to her husband, Lucius Valerius Virillio.

EDCS-08400928.translation: Gnaeus Licinius Gratus fulfilled his vow to the Fates.
EDCS-08400928.summary: A votive inscription by Gnaeus Licinius Gratus to the Fates.

EDCS-08400929.translation: Eutychion, his freedman, [erected this] for Titus Domitius, son of Lucius, Pedullian tribe, of Arelate, who held all offices in his colony.
EDCS-08400929.summary: A funerary monument erected by the freedman Eutychion for Titus Domitius, a Roman citizen honored in his colony of Arelate.

EDCS-08400930.translation: To Gaius Julius Vitionus, Quintus Julius Superstes, for his brother, and Avita, daughter of Quintus Julius, for his excellent wife. Quintus Lucanius Insequens.
EDCS-08400930.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Lucanius Insequens for his brother and wife, Avita.

EDCS-08400931.translation: Aevillia Secundina, daughter of Maxumus, ordered by her will that this be made for herself and for Gaius Aevillius Receptus; Gaius Valerius Maxumus, aedile and heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-08400931.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Aevillia Secundina for herself and Gaius Aevillius Receptus, with the heir, Gaius Valerius Maxumus, overseeing the construction.

EDCS-08400932.translation: Julius Crispinus, for himself and his most devoted wife Paternus, his father Gaius Julius Secundinus, and his wife Prisca, this monument was erected while alive; it will not follow its heir.
EDCS-08400932.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Julius Crispinus for himself, his wife, and his father, stipulating that it is not inheritable.

EDCS-08400934.translation: To the god Aesculapius, Valerius Symphorus and Protis paid their vows willingly and deservedly for a bronze statue of Somnus, a gold torque made from two small dragons weighing [X] pounds, and a silver bracelet weighing [X] pounds, and a gold chain, on account of a remarkable effect of his divine power concerning them.
EDCS-08400934.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius by Valerius Symphorus and Protis for a statue of Somnus and other precious offerings, given in gratitude for divine intervention.

EDCS-08400936.translation: To Mars, Cicinus Carro, Lucius Pompeius Myrismus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08400936.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars by Lucius Pompeius Myrismus.

EDCS-08400938.translation: To the Mother of the Gods, Marcus Julius Hyacinthus, a IIIIIIvir Augustalis of the colony of Julia Augusta Apollinaris, paid his vow for a sacred offering; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08400938.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Mother of the Gods by Marcus Julius Hyacinthus, a local magistrate, for which the site was officially granted.

EDCS-08400939.translation: To the Mother of the Earth, Titus E V Viator.
EDCS-08400939.summary: This is a dedication to the Mother of the Earth by Titus E V Viator.

EDCS-08400941.translation: Annia Faustina, daughter of Titus Vitrasius Pollio, consul twice, praetor, quaestor, legate of the Emperor, pontifex, proconsul of Asia, wife of Nymphis Griselicis.
EDCS-08400941.summary: This inscription identifies Annia Faustina as the wife of Nymphis Griselicis and lists her husband's distinguished career, likely part of a larger dedication.

EDCS-08400942.translation: Quartus, son of Suramus, paid his vow willingly and deservedly to Osdiavis.
EDCS-08400942.summary: This is a votive dedication to Osdiavis by Quartus, son of Suramus.

EDCS-08400943.translation: To Silvanus, Diadumenus [dedicated this to] Symphoetus.
EDCS-08400943.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus by Diadumenus, possibly for Symphoetus.

EDCS-08400947.translation: Aulus Julius Saturninus, standard-bearer of the Tenth Legion Gemina, Pious, Faithful, made this (monument) for himself and his family, being a magistrate of the colony of Julia Augusta Apollinaris.
EDCS-08400947.summary: This is a dedication by Aulus Julius Saturninus, a standard-bearer and magistrate, for himself and his family.

EDCS-08400949.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Lucius Cornelius Festus, Augustalis.
EDCS-08400949.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Cornelius Festus, a member of a religious college.

EDCS-08400951.translation: To Julia Daphne, Decimus Julius Epaphroditus, a high priest, for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-08400951.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Decimus Julius Epaphroditus, a high priest, to his wife Julia Daphne.

EDCS-08400953.translation: To Julia Cosmia, for her most dutiful daughter Valeria Alexandria, aged 17.
EDCS-08400953.summary: This inscription records the death of Valeria Alexandria, aged 17, and is likely a funerary monument from her mother Iulia Cosmia.

EDCS-08400954.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Julia Eutychia.
EDCS-08400954.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Julia Eutychia.

EDCS-08400955.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Julia Lucilia, by Julius Amandus.
EDCS-08400955.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Julius Amandus to Julia Lucilia.

EDCS-08400957.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Saturnina, daughter of Verus; Verus her father, Vera her sister, Metilius for his wife Saturnina.
EDCS-08400957.summary: This is a complex funerary dedication involving multiple family members for Saturnina.

EDCS-08400958.translation: Vera, the daughter, arranged for this to be made by will for Veratius, son of Mocetimar, and for Grata, his wife, her parents.
EDCS-08400958.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Vera in accordance with her parents' will, honoring her father Veratius and mother Grata.

EDCS-08400961.translation: To the god Rudias, Lucius Lucretius Ileus, freedman of Lucius Lucretius, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-08400961.summary: This records a votive offering made by Lucius Lucretius Ileus to the god Rudias.

EDCS-08400965.translation: Quintus Atilius Prepon, freedman of Quintus Atilius, made this (monument) for himself and his family, while alive.
EDCS-08400965.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Quintus Atilius Prepon for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-08400966.translation: To Gnaeus Julius Epicefius Epaphroditus, freedman of Gnaeus Julius Aemilius.
EDCS-08400966.summary: This is a dedication to a freedman by his patron, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-08400968.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Valerius Messius ordered this to be made in his will for Titus Valerius Teretin(a?) Messius, Lucius Valerius Epu(l?)tus, and Gaius Valerius Valerianus, his sons, and for himself.
EDCS-08400968.summary: This is a funerary dedication commissioned by a father for his deceased sons and himself.

EDCS-08400971.translation: Julius Victorinus made this memorial for his grandfather Julius Apri, who deserved well, at the age of 3 years, 16 days.
EDCS-08400971.summary: This is a memorial erected by a grandson for his deceased infant grandfather, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-08400973.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld and for the memory of Statilia Patern(a?). She lived 17 years, 2 months, 18 days, 8 hours. Statilius Primus, a senior centurion, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-08400973.summary: This is a funerary monument for Statilia Patern(a?) and her family, dedicated by her husband, a former centurion.

EDCS-08400974.translation: To Gaius Terentius Fuscus. Cnaeus Turpilius Stephanus, heir, and Cthetus, freedman.
EDCS-08400974.summary: This is a dedication, likely on a tomb, from an heir and freedman to Gaius Terentius Fuscus.

EDCS-08400975.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Thallusa made this for herself and her family.
EDCS-08400975.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Iulia Thallusa and her family.

EDCS-08400976.summary: This is a simple dedication, possibly on a tomb or a public monument, by Quintus Atilius Phoenix.

EDCS-08400977.translation: To Lucia Caecilia Donata, daughter of Lucius, his wife, who deserved well. Valerius Philoserapis.
EDCS-08400977.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a husband to his deceased wife.

EDCS-08400978.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Patern(a?) as the wife of Quinctianus.

EDCS-08400980.translation: To the god Apollo. Lucius Aelius Nymphicus, a member of the board of six priests, a corporatus.
EDCS-08400980.summary: This is a dedication to the god Apollo by Lucius Aelius Nymphicus, an official in a collegium.

EDCS-08400981.translation: Titus Atilius Servatus willingly paid his vow, as he rightly should.
EDCS-08400981.summary: An epitaph recording a vow fulfilled by Titus Atilius Servatus, likely a freedman indicated by the name.

EDCS-08400982.translation: Vainnius Marcarita and Vainnius gave willingly.
EDCS-08400982.summary: An inscription recording a dedication by Vainnius Marcarita and another Vainnius.

EDCS-08400983.translation: Octavius Paternus dedicated this to the god Dolichenus, by his command, for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-08400983.summary: A dedication to the god Dolichenus by Octavius Paternus for his own and his family's well-being.

EDCS-08400984.translation: Philip, daughter of Hotarzabad, dedicated this to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Defender.
EDCS-08400984.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Propugnator by Philip, daughter of Hotarzabad.

EDCS-08400985.translation: Caius Ianuarius, apparitor of the same religion of the Great Mother of the Gods, Idaea Palatina...
EDCS-08400985.summary: An inscription mentioning Caius Ianuarius, an apparitor associated with the cult of the Great Mother of the Gods.

EDCS-08400988.translation: To Lucius Dudistius Novanus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, pontiff of the Laurentines, adorned with the ornaments of the colonia Aquensis, prefect of the Spanish cavalry, assistant to the census of the province of Lugudunensis, procurator of Augustus of the Cottian Alps, Dudistius Eglectus and Apthonetus [dedicated this] to their excellent patron.
EDCS-08400988.summary: A dedication to Lucius Dudistius Novanus, a distinguished official holding various military and administrative posts, by his clients Dudistius Eglectus and Apthonetus.

EDCS-08400989.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal security, Cornelia Secunda [dedicated this] to her most devoted husband, Quintus Gallius Euphemus, a member of the board of six (VI viri Augustales) and a corporatus.
EDCS-08400989.summary: An epitaph set up by Cornelia Secunda for her husband, Quintus Gallius Euphemus, who was a member of the Augustales and a corporatus.

EDCS-08400990.translation: To Gnaeus Valerius Pompeius Valerianus, son of Gnaeus, of the Quirinian tribe, honored with public equestrian status by the most sacred Emperors Antoninus and Verus Augusti, perpetual augur, for which honor he gave 10 million sesterces to the state, agonothete of the Iovian games, prophet, who excellently deserved well of them, the corporati centonarii of Massilia [dedicated this] to their patron, [a] place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08400990.summary: A dedication by the corporati centonarii of Massilia to their patron, Gnaeus Valerius Pompeius Valerianus, an imperial official and religious dignitary, who had endowed public games.

EDCS-08400992.translation: Etrilia Laeta, from Syriacus in Baetica, Hispania, Italicensis; this place was publicly granted to her in Massilia on the road to Aquae.
EDCS-08400992.summary: A record of a publicly granted plot of land in Massilia to Etrilia Laeta, who originated from Hispania.

EDCS-08400993.translation: To the Divine Shades, Secundus [dedicated this] to Publius Aelius Eutychetus, his fellow freedman.
EDCS-08400993.summary: An epitaph set up by Secundus for his fellow freedman, Publius Aelius Eutychetus.

EDCS-08400994.summary: This inscription is a name, likely for a funerary monument.

EDCS-08400995.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valerius Crescens and Calpurnia Onesime, her alumnae, (erected this) for Calpurnia Aris.
EDCS-08400995.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by alumnae for their patroness, Calpurnia Aris.

EDCS-08400996.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Carisius Pacatus (erected this) for his most devoted brother, Marcus Carisius Maximimus.
EDCS-08400996.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a brother for his deceased sibling, Marcus Carisius Maximimus.

EDCS-08400997.translation: (She) placed (this) for Cassia Primigenia, her wife.
EDCS-08400997.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife setting up a monument for her husband, Cassius Primigenius.

EDCS-08400998.translation: To the Divine Spirits. (Erected) for Cassia Fortunata, freedwoman of Aconis, most deserving patroness.
EDCS-08400998.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication for Cassia Fortunata, a deserving patroness.

EDCS-08400999.translation: To the Divine Spirits. P. Charite, wife of Quintus Cestius Ianuarius, placed (this) for herself while alive.
EDCS-08400999.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife, P. Charite, setting up a monument for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-08401000.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelianus, brother (erected this) for Lucius Cornelius Eutychianus.
EDCS-08401000.summary: This funerary inscription records a brother's dedication for Lucius Cornelius Eutychianus.

EDCS-08401001.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Panthia (erected this) for her husband, Cornelius Helpidianus.
EDCS-08401001.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife's dedication for her husband, Cornelius Helpidianus.

EDCS-08401002.translation: Clemens (erected this) for his father, Cornelius Saturninus, and his mother, Cina.
EDCS-08401002.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Clemens for his parents, Cornelius Saturninus and Cina.

EDCS-08401003.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cossutius Cerdon (erected this) for his most beloved freedwoman, Cossutia Epictesis.
EDCS-08401003.summary: This funerary inscription records a master's dedication for his beloved freedwoman, Cossutia Epictesis.

EDCS-08401004.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Cossutia Victoria, her husband Pompeius Abascantus [dedicated this] to his most devoted wife.
EDCS-08401004.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401005.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Crescens, who lived 7 years, 6 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-08401005.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a young child named Crescens, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401006.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus C. Polutimos [dedicated this] to his most devoted father, Polycarpus.
EDCS-08401006.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his father, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401007.translation: To Titus Domitius Asiaticus, his wife Lalihe, [dedicated this], to her most excellent husband and to herself.
EDCS-08401007.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lalihe to her husband Titus Domitius Asiaticus, and also for herself, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401008.translation: A. Domitius Ti. [son] to Domitia Domestica, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-08401008.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his mother, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401009.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Flavia Dionysia, Iulia Marcia [dedicated this] to her most excellent husband.
EDCS-08401009.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401010.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Titus Flavius Epicronitus, Lucius Flavius Iulianus [dedicated this] to his most dutiful father.
EDCS-08401010.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his father, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401012.summary: A brief funerary inscription identifying Flavia Iuliana as a granddaughter, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401013.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Flavia Marcella, Furius Callimorfus [dedicated this] to his incomparable wife.
EDCS-08401013.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401014.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Flavia Telesphoria, Flavius Frontinus, Sergius Libanus, and Flavia Frontis, her foster parents, and likewise Domitius Verinus and Valeria Rufiana, her educators.
EDCS-08401014.summary: A funerary dedication by various individuals, including foster parents and educators, to Flavia Telesphoria, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401015.translation: To Flavia Luciana, my wife, deceased.
EDCS-08401015.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to a deceased woman named Flavia Luciana, likely by her spouse.

EDCS-08401016.translation: To Fortunata, a well-deserving patroness, for herself, her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. In front 20 feet, in the field 24 feet.
EDCS-08401016.summary: This is a funerary dedication by an unknown person to their patroness Fortunata, specifying land dimensions for a burial plot.

EDCS-08401018.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Julius Iulianus, his most devoted father, by Iulius Annianus.
EDCS-08401018.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Iulius Annianus to his deceased father, Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-08401019.translation: To Titus Iulius Pompeianus. Setilia, mother, and Quintus Allius Sotericus, son, to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-08401019.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by a mother and son to their deceased relative, Titus Iulius Pompeianus.

EDCS-08401020.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Iulia Tycharus, by Iulius LIANNICINTER, aged 21.
EDCS-08401020.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Tycharus, aged 21, dedicated by Iulius LIANNICINTER.

EDCS-08401021.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Iulia Germana, his most devoted mother, by Marcus Statius Restitutus.
EDCS-08401021.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Marcus Statius Restitutus to his deceased mother, Iulia Germana.

EDCS-08401022.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Iulia Quintina, his most devoted daughter, by her mother, Cossutia Hygia.
EDCS-08401022.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Cossutia Hygia to her deceased daughter, Iulia Quintina.

EDCS-08401024.translation: To the Divine Shades. To ANRVNI, son of Secundus of Nicomedia.
EDCS-08401024.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to ANRVNI, son of Secundus, likely of Nicomedian origin.

EDCS-08401026.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Licyria Sebaste, his most deserving friend, by Iunia Tyrannis.
EDCS-08401026.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Iunia Tyrannis to her deceased friend, Licyria Sebaste.

EDCS-08401027.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lucius Lucilius Crispus, her best husband, and to Lucius Lucilius Gratus, her most devoted son, by Iulia Grata.
EDCS-08401027.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Iulia Grata to her deceased husband, Lucius Lucilius Crispus, and her son, Lucius Lucilius Gratus.

EDCS-08401028.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maccia Ianuaria and Licinia Carpime, wife, [set this up] for their most dutiful son, Titus Maccio Marcello.
EDCS-08401028.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother and wife for a deceased son and husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401030.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For their most dutiful son, Decimus Marcius Ingenuus, aged 22.
EDCS-08401030.summary: A funerary dedication for a young man named Decimus Marcius Ingenuus, specifying his age, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401031.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For their most devoted son, Marcius Lenaeus and Tyche.
EDCS-08401031.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their son, Marcius Lenaeus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401032.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Plaria Vera, his wife, and Quintus Minatius Tiridas [his father?] made [this] for Quintus Minatius Celer, son of Claudianus.
EDCS-08401032.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife and likely a father for Quintus Minatius Celer, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401033.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pompeia Musa [set this up] for her most sweet foster-son, Titus Pompeius Feliciano.
EDCS-08401033.summary: A funerary dedication by a woman named Pompeia Musa for her beloved foster-son, Titus Pompeius Feliciano, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401034.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Pomponia Primigenia, [by] Annia Felicula, her dearest sister.
EDCS-08401034.summary: A funerary dedication by a sister for her deceased sister, Pomponia Primigenia, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401035.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Porcius.
EDCS-08401035.summary: A very brief funerary dedication to someone named Porcius, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401036.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [This is from] the brothers Titus Publius Charito and Sextus Lucilius Flavianus [for] Titus Publius Rufinus.
EDCS-08401036.summary: A funerary dedication made by two brothers for another individual, Titus Publius Rufinus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401037.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quartia Irvatilla.
EDCS-08401037.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Quartia Irvatilla, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401041.translation: Valeria Amilla set [this up] for her most dutiful father, Lucius Valerius Felix.
EDCS-08401041.summary: A funerary monument erected by a daughter for her father, Lucius Valerius Felix, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401042.translation: Julia Marina made this for Gaius Valerius Graecinus, son of Gaius Valerius, and for her brother Iulius Ligur, and for herself and her family, while alive.
EDCS-08401042.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman, Julia Marina, dedicating a monument for herself, her family, and her deceased brother, Valerius Graecinus.

EDCS-08401043.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Valerius Rufinus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-08401043.summary: This is a self-made funerary dedication by Quintus Valerius Rufinus to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-08401044.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Eutychus for his most beloved daughter, Valeria Eutychia.
EDCS-08401044.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Marcus Valerius Eutychus to his daughter, Valeria Eutychia.

EDCS-08401045.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Primilla.
EDCS-08401045.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Valeria Primilla.

EDCS-08401047.translation: Iulia Zosima for her husband, Quintus Vibius Dexter.
EDCS-08401047.summary: This funerary inscription records Iulia Zosima dedicating a monument to her husband, Quintus Vibius Dexter.

EDCS-08401048.translation: To the departed spirits. Thallusa for her comrade Zosimus.
EDCS-08401048.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Thallusa to her companion, Zosimus.

EDCS-08401060.translation: Cypriana rests here in peace, who lived more or less 33 years.
EDCS-08401060.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the name and approximate age at death of Cypriana.

EDCS-08401061.translation: Here rests Eugenia of good memory, handmaid of God, who lived 46 years and departed on the sixth day before the Nones of March.
EDCS-08401061.summary: This Christian epitaph records the name, status as a "handmaid of God," age at death, and date of departure for Eugenia.

EDCS-08401062.translation: Noble Eugenia, born of illustrious blood, who lives by her merits, lies here entombed; leaving behind her body, heavy with life, so that she might better approach the heavenly homes; prudent in spirit, she always chose praiseworthy work with the weight of her character; rejoicing, she hastened to feed the hungry, offering feasts to the hungry; she loosed captives from unjust chains and restored the banished to their lands; with her mind intent on good things, throughout her whole life, she had a unique concern for noble deeds; her grandmother laid her to rest in her 60th year, not without pious tears.
EDCS-08401062.summary: This complex funerary poem praises the virtues of Eugenia, a noble woman who lived a life of charity and piety, and records her burial and the grief of her grandmother.

EDCS-08401063.translation: Eusebia, the devout and great handmaid of God, rests here in peace, who lived 14 secular years from her early age and, when she was chosen by God, served in the holy monastery of St. Cyricus for fifty years; she departed on the day before the Kalends of October, in the sixth indiction.
EDCS-08401063.summary: This Christian epitaph records the life and service of Eusebia, a devout handmaid of God who lived 14 secular years and then served in a monastery for 50 years, noting her date of departure and the sixth indiction.

EDCS-08401066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eliana Martini Iustina, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-08401067.translation: Menas made a vow, whose name is Bono; may he have rest in the future. My husband Menas lived well. And [for] wages... Here lies Gemula, whose name God knows.
EDCS-08401067.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gemula, who thanks Menas for a vow and expresses hopes for her husband Menas and herself, possibly from the late Roman period.

EDCS-08401068.translation: Here rests in peace Nymfidius, formerly of the guard, who lived for two years. He departed on the 8th day before the Kalends of [November], under Probino and Eusebio.
EDCS-08401068.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life and burial of Nymfidius, indicating his service in the guard and the consuls of the period.

EDCS-08401069.translation: Here rests in peace Spanilia, who lived for fifty-seven years. She departed on the seventh day before the Ides of May.
EDCS-08401069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Spanilia, stating her age at death and the date of her passing.

EDCS-08401070.translation: [...] Volasianus, son of Eutyches, [...] Fortunatus, who suffered the violence of the sea [...] may he refresh us, who has the power.
EDCS-08401070.summary: This fragmented inscription likely relates to a funerary monument or dedication, possibly mentioning victims of the sea and a deity like Fortunatus.

EDCS-08401071.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Here lies the faithful maiden Ulpia Dom[na], who lived 16 years, 11 months, 20 days. [Dedicated by] Lucius and Ulpia Agrippina to their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-08401071.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Ulpia Domna, a young girl, and provides details of her life and the dedication by her parents.

EDCS-08401072.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Urbece, good daughter of good memory Squelioles, who lived more or less 50 years. She departed on the Kalends of October, in the sixth indiction.
EDCS-08401072.summary: This epitaph records the death of Urbece, daughter of Squelioles, providing her approximate age and the date of departure within the sixth indiction.

EDCS-08401076.translation: Dexter to Bormanus, again, willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-08401076.summary: This is a short dedication, likely a votive offering or a marker of gratitude, from Dexter to Bormanus.

EDCS-08401077.translation: Eutyches willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Diana.
EDCS-08401077.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Eutyches fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-08401078.translation: Trebia Lucilla to Juno, from a vision, willingly.
EDCS-08401078.summary: This is a votive inscription where Trebia Lucilla dedicates something to Juno, prompted by a divine vision.

EDCS-08401079.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Protectors, Publius, for the Roman People.
EDCS-08401079.summary: This is a dedication to the Matres Conservatrices by Publius, likely on behalf of the Roman people, possibly an altar or votive stone.

EDCS-08401080.translation: Servatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-08401080.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Servatus, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401081.translation: Sextus Iulius Bacchylus, freedman of Sereni, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-08401081.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Iulius Bacchylus, a freedman, likely an altar or inscribed stone.

EDCS-08401084.translation: Caius Iulius Paternus [dedicated this] to Liber, the father.
EDCS-08401084.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater by Caius Iulius Paternus, possibly an altar or votive object.

EDCS-08401085.translation: Titus Flavius Iustus, by order, [dedicated this] to Mars Beladonus.
EDCS-08401085.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Beladonus by Titus Flavius Iustus, acting on divine command, likely a votive object or military dedication.

EDCS-08401086.translation: Titus Pompeius Felix willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Mother Goddesses.
EDCS-08401086.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Matres by Titus Pompeius Felix, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401087.translation: Iulia Minia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Matres Gerudatiae.
EDCS-08401087.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Matres Gerudatiae by Iulia Minia, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401088.translation: Sextus Annius Verus, son of Caius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-08401088.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mercury by Sextus Annius Verus, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401089.translation: Priscilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Mercury.
EDCS-08401089.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mercury by Priscilla, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401090.translation: [Syneros] willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-08401090.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mercury by Syneros, indicating the fulfillment of a promise, likely on a small altar or plaque.

EDCS-08401091.translation: Niceta gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Silvanus.
EDCS-08401091.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Niceta.

EDCS-08401092.summary: A very brief inscription, likely an offering or dedication by Statia Pthengis.

EDCS-08401093.translation: Publius Tallius Onesimus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08401093.summary: A votive inscription by Publius Tallius Onesimus.

EDCS-08401098.translation: Sextus Acutius Voltinia Aquila, praetor, to Acutus his father, to Ingenua his mother, to Severa his sister, to Rufus his brother, this monument shall not follow its heir.
EDCS-08401098.summary: A funerary monument set up by Sextus Acutius Voltinia Aquila for his family members, with a clause stating it is not inheritable.

EDCS-08401099.translation: Marcus Caelius Clemens, patron, to Marcus Caelius Florus, one of the six priests of the Augustales, his mother Caelia Restituta, his brother Verecundus, his sister Flora.
