EDCS-08401099.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Marcus Caelius Clemens for his relatives, including a member of the Augustales priesthood.

EDCS-08401100.translation: To Cae, daughter of Cnaeus, priestess of the Augustales, a matron of the second rank.
EDCS-08401100.summary: A dedication to Cae, daughter of Cnaeus, who held priestly and honorific titles.

EDCS-08401102.translation: Sextus Iulius Verinus, son of Sextus Iulius, flamen and aedile with distribution of games, made this monument for himself while alive, with his three sons and their children, to his father.
EDCS-08401102.summary: A funerary monument made by Sextus Iulius Verinus for himself, his sons, and his father, detailing his civic and priestly roles.

EDCS-08401103.translation: Sextus Punicius, freedman of the colony of Aquensis, member of the Augustales, member of the guild of fullers, and also a member of the guild of cloth workers, made this for himself and for his most dutiful wife Mercatia Rinilla while alive.
EDCS-08401103.summary: A funerary monument made by Sextus Punicius, a freedman with various guild and priestly affiliations, for himself and his wife.

EDCS-08401104.translation: To the Divine Shades, Pompeia Domestica, daughter of Lucius Pompeius Hermerotis, a scribe and a member of the Augustales, and her sister Veratia Nigrina, his wife, the heirs, erected this.
EDCS-08401104.summary: A funerary monument erected by Pompeia Domestica and Veratia Nigrina for Lucius Pompeius Hermerotis, who held civic and priestly offices.

EDCS-08401105.translation: Sextus Samicius Maximinus, aedile, decurion, quaestor of the public record office, curator, made this for his most excellent parents Sextus Samicius Verus and Iulia Syra, for his dearest wife Veratia Nice, and for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-08401105.summary: A funerary monument made by Sextus Samicius Maximinus for his parents, wife, and himself, detailing his magistracies and curatorial roles.

EDCS-08401106.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Valgius Victorinus, one of the six priests of Augustus, and also from the college of the fullers. His most devoted wife, Iulia Marcina, [dedicated this].
EDCS-08401106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, who held priesthood and guild positions, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08401107.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Veratius Threption, son of Gaius Veratius Paternus, Roman knight and priest of Augustus. His most devoted father, Gaius Veratius, dedicated this.
EDCS-08401107.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a father to his son, a Roman knight and priest, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08401108.translation: To Lucius Virilius Gratinianus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, Roman knight.
EDCS-08401108.summary: This is an epitaph honoring Lucius Virilius Gratinianus, a Roman knight, possibly for a public monument or burial, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08401109.translation: Cisius, as aedile and prefect, in place of a duovir, for himself and his family.
EDCS-08401109.summary: This inscription records Cisius holding multiple magistracies, including aedile and prefect acting as a duovir, for himself and his family, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08401135.translation: Young man, please halt your steps; traveller, I beg you, so that through this inscription you may know me. Thus envious fate decreed. I lived scarcely twenty years, but more than that. I was whole, always innocent, approved with a pious mind. I was well-taught in the skillful games of youth. I was also handsome, surrounded by various arms. Often I jousted with beasts; moreover, I was a physician in the life I lived, and a companion to those who were usually wont to sacrifice victims to the gods at sacred rites, and to those who, in the new season, with woven spring flowers, refresh the images of the gods. If you seek my name, the inscription tells you the truth: Sextus Julius Felicissimus. Felix and Felicitas made this for their incomparable pupil. You, whoever you are who read this epitaph, learn who I was when buried, what vows I had, what glory. I lived twenty years, with months completed, and powerful in virtue, and handsome in the flower of youth, and I was preferred above others, loved by the praising crowd. Why do you grieve my losses? The order of fate cannot be overcome. The affairs of men are thus, like citrons: they either fall when ripe, or are picked before they are ripe.
EDCS-08401135.summary: This is a lengthy funerary epitaph for Sextus Julius Felicissimus, a young man who excelled in games, was a physician, and participated in religious rites, lamenting his premature death and reflecting on the transience of life, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08401136.translation: For burial: to Lucius Pompeius Silvanus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Tolosa.
EDCS-08401136.summary: This is an inscription indicating a burial plot or dedication for Lucius Pompeius Silvanus, a citizen from Tolosa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401142.translation: For Sextus Aemilius Paullus, father; for Aemilia Regilla, daughter of Quintus, mother; for Sextus Aemilius Paullinus, brother; for Titus Aemilius Burrus, brother. Gaius Aemilius Vastus [dedicated this] for his family.
EDCS-08401142.summary: This is a family tomb inscription dedicated by Gaius Aemilius Vastus to honor his father, mother, and brothers, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401143.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Calpurnia Alexandria made this for her well-deserving pupil, Albucio Liguri.
EDCS-08401143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a teacher to a deserving pupil, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401145.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely begins the name and filiation or titles of Quintus Atilius, possibly a dedication or epitaph, date uncertain due to fragmentation.

EDCS-08401146.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Avilia Gemina, patroness. Avilia Iusta made this for her excellent patroness.
EDCS-08401146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a freedwoman to her patroness, Avilia Gemina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401147.translation: Euangelus and Docimus made this here for their friend, Beryllus, slave of Urbina, daughter of Lucius Vergilius.
EDCS-08401147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Euangelus and Docimus to their friend Beryllus, a slave.

EDCS-08401148.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Asper, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08401148.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Cornelius Asper, son of Quintus.

EDCS-08401149.translation: Marcus Cornelius Secundus to his brother Lucius Cornelius Festus.
EDCS-08401149.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Cornelius Secundus to his brother Lucius Cornelius Festus.

EDCS-08401150.translation: Quintus Cornelius Gratus to his parents.
EDCS-08401150.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Cornelius Gratus to his parents.

EDCS-08401151.translation: To Cornelius Hospes and Pompeia Aquila, Certo Eutactus Deuteros.
EDCS-08401151.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Cornelius Hospes and Pompeia Aquila by Certo Eutactus Deuteros.

EDCS-08401152.translation: To Cornelius Sinnio V M, to Firma, daughter of Philoxenus, to Marcus Cornelius Aquilo, while alive.
EDCS-08401152.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Cornelius Sinnio V M, Firma daughter of Philoxenus, and Marcus Cornelius Aquilo during his lifetime.

EDCS-08401153.translation: To the Divine Manes of Cornelia Eutychia.
EDCS-08401153.summary: This is a dedication to the Divine Manes of Cornelia Eutychia.

EDCS-08401154.translation: Sextus Cornelius Hilarus, freedman of Sextus, [dedicated this] to Cornelia Tertia, daughter of Spurius, to Vibius, son of Exciggorigis.
EDCS-08401154.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Sextus Cornelius Hilarus to Cornelia Tertia and Vibius.

EDCS-08401155.translation: To the Divine Manes of Cornelia Secunda, dearest wife, Quintus Iulius Earinus, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-08401155.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Iulius Earinus to his dearest wife, Cornelia Secunda.

EDCS-08401156.translation: To the Divine Manes of Cornelia Valerilla, aged 18, Caesonius Hermes to his dearest wife.
EDCS-08401156.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Caesonius Hermes to his dearest wife, Cornelia Valerilla, who died at 18.

EDCS-08401157.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Gratina to her most devoted mother, Duccia Gratilla.
EDCS-08401157.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from a daughter to her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401158.translation: Sozomenus [set this up] for Lucius Frontonius Trypho.
EDCS-08401158.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary or a dedication of a tomb, from Sozomenus to Lucius Frontonius Trypho.

EDCS-08401159.translation: Marcus Geminius Nasica [set this up] for his brothers, Gaius Geminius Censor and Lucius Geminius Messius.
EDCS-08401159.summary: This inscription records a dedication from a brother to his two brothers, likely funerary, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401161.translation: Titus Iulius Natalis [set this up] for Gaius Iulius Avitus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-08401161.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary, from Titus Iulius Natalis to Gaius Iulius Avitus, possibly a relative, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401162.translation: Caius Cornelius Valentinus and Quintus Cornelius Servatus [set this up] for their friend Sextus Iulius Saturninus.
EDCS-08401162.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary, from two friends to Sextus Iulius Saturninus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401163.translation: Antonia Aristophania to her incomparable friend, Iulia Chrestes.
EDCS-08401163.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from one friend to another, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401164.translation: Phile made [this] for her daughter, Iulia Secunda, aged 12.
EDCS-08401164.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from a mother to her young daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401168.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maternia Grata to her most devoted father, Quintus Maternus Marcinus.
EDCS-08401168.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from a daughter to her father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401169.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Muses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401171.translation: Pompeia Banonae, most devoted freedwoman, [set this up] for her master, Lucius Pompeius Vitalis.
EDCS-08401171.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary, from a freedwoman to her former master, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401172.translation: Pompeia Compse made this for herself and her daughter Pieris while alive.
EDCS-08401172.summary: A funerary dedication by Pompeia Compse for herself and her daughter, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401173.translation: To Gaius Iunius Priscianus, Aelia Servilia, his most excellent wife.
EDCS-08401173.summary: A dedication from a wife to her husband, Gaius Iunius Priscianus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-08401174.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Victoriana and Mansuetus for Prothida.
EDCS-08401174.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Underworld) for Prothida by Victoriana and Mansuetus, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401175.translation: Sergia Optata, daughter of Lucius, made this for her father Sergius, her mother Maxuma, her sister Fusca, her sister Maxumilla, and her brother Veratio, while alive, for herself and her family.
EDCS-08401175.summary: A funerary dedication by Sergia Optata for multiple family members, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401177.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Lucius Valerius Eutichion to his incomparable wife, Severia Secundina.
EDCS-08401177.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Underworld) from Lucius Valerius Eutichion to his wife Severia Secundina, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401179.translation: Sextus Valerius Mansuetus, freedman of Martialis.
EDCS-08401179.summary: An epitaph identifying Sextus Valerius Mansuetus as the freedman of Martialis, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401180.summary: A dedication or epitaph for Vatinia Servata, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401181.translation: Titus (?) Aelius (?) Cin(i)us, son of Sextus, Severus, from Rome.
EDCS-08401181.summary: An abbreviated funerary or commemorative inscription for a Roman citizen named T. Aelius Cin(i)us Severus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401182.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Marcus (?) Foebus.
EDCS-08401182.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Underworld) for a person named Foebus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08401197.translation: Here rests in peace Adiutor, who after receiving penance, departed to the Lord, aged 65 years, 7 months, and 15 days, buried on the 9th day before the Kalends of January, during the consulship of the most illustrious Anastasius.
EDCS-08401197.summary: A Christian epitaph recording the death and age of Adiutor, dated by the consulship of Anastasius (likely 5th or 6th century AD).

EDCS-08401198.translation: And to the notary Bishop Basil, he lived 23 years, 8 months, 2 days. He passed away on the Nones of October during the consulship of Asterius.
EDCS-08401198.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and death of an individual, identified by his profession and religious title, with the date specified by the consul.

EDCS-08401199.translation: Here lies Dextrianus, alas, taken by death, dear to all, hated only by the malevolent, and not without reason called by that name. For by Your gift, Christ, he now stands by Your side among the lambs, hoping for eternity, which You, Lord, grant. He was endowed with prudence and beauty of form; he possessed nothing else but faith, alongside goodness. Nor did he lack elegant modesty coupled with reverence. He completed twice eleven months and years, a beautiful and innocent boy, always approved with a pious mind. We mourn you, pitiable child, because all that is good is short-lived. He departed from the world to seek the stars on the third day before the Nones of June, which is in the first indiction.
EDCS-08401199.summary: This is a lengthy Christian funerary inscription for a young boy named Dextrianus, detailing his virtues, piety, and the circumstances of his death, with the date specified by the indiction.

EDCS-08401201.translation: The pagans of the district of Lucretius, who are within the boundaries of Arles, in the place called Gargarius, to Quinto Cornelio Marcelli, freedman, a member of the IIIIvirate Augustalis of the colony Iulia Paterna Arelate, on account of his honor, who made our grievance known to the Most Sacred Prince Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, three times in Rome; he pursued our grievance through the presidents of the province for many years at his own expense, and for this reason he granted us the expenses which he incurred, so that the benefits of the Most Sacred Prince Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus Pius of all ages might endure and remain, by which we might enjoy the waters and the bathhouse freely, which had been taken away from the pagans, which they had used for more than 40 years.
EDCS-08401201.summary: This is a dedication from the pagans of a district near Arles to a freedman who successfully pursued a grievance concerning water and bath access before imperial officials and the emperor, ensuring its continued availability. The inscription dates to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-08401203.translation: Caius Clodius Pulcher, son of Cirrus.
EDCS-08401203.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying an individual by name and filiation.

EDCS-08401205.translation: Lucius Nigidius Optatus from the tribe Teretina and Valeria Secunda, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-08401205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying two individuals by name and filiation/tribe.

EDCS-08401206.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Messia Magna and Memmia Valeria erected this for Quintus Messius Fabricius, father; Quintus Messius Valerianus, son; Quintus Cominius Valerianus, grandson, a soldier of the praetorian guard, an equestrian; and Messia Magna, daughter; and for Memmia Valeria, mother, who were ordered by the will of their husband Messius Fabricius.
EDCS-08401206.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a wife and daughter to honor deceased family members, including a father, son, grandson (a soldier), and mother, as dictated by a will.

EDCS-08401207.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pompeia Calliste placed this for her most pious patrons.
EDCS-08401207.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a freedwoman (or someone in a client relationship) to her patrons.

EDCS-08401209.summary: This is a short funerary epitaph identifying an individual by name and filiation, possibly of Germanic origin.

EDCS-08401210.translation: To Titus, brother, and to his brother Gaius Servilius Firmus, Titus son of Hospes.
EDCS-08401210.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying three individuals, Titus, his brother Gaius Servilius Firmus, and the dedicator Titus son of Hospes, indicating familial relationships.

EDCS-08401211.translation: For Servilius Barbarus, son of Titus, father; Quieta, mother; Servatus, brother; and Sura, brother.
EDCS-08401211.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating the monument to multiple family members: father, mother, and two brothers.

EDCS-08401212.translation: Gnaeus Maelius Pudens made this, while alive, for his father Gnaeus Maelius Flavus.
EDCS-08401212.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gnaeus Maelius Pudens for his father, Gnaeus Maelius Flavus.

EDCS-08401213.translation: To Plotia Procula, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-08401213.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Plotia Procula, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-08401214.translation: To his mother Prima, daughter of Sanvillius.
EDCS-08401214.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Prima, daughter of Sanvillius, by her son.

EDCS-08401216.translation: Marcus Saenius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-08401216.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Saenius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe.

EDCS-08401217.translation: Valeria Pia, daughter of Saenius Cnaeus.
EDCS-08401217.summary: This inscription identifies Valeria Pia, daughter of Saenius Cnaeus.

EDCS-08401219.translation: Orestus, with piety, with the consent of all, made this for himself.
EDCS-08401219.summary: This funerary inscription records that Orestus made this monument for himself out of piety.

EDCS-08401220.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Aemilius Syrus for himself and for A[...] Dudistia.
EDCS-08401220.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Aemilius Syrus for himself and his wife, Dudistia A[...].

EDCS-08401221.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Vrittius Avitus for his most dutiful wife, Iulia Marcellina.
EDCS-08401221.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Vrittius Avitus for his wife, Iulia Marcellina.

EDCS-08401222.translation: Lucius Ausc made this for Secundus.
EDCS-08401222.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Ausc for Secundus.

EDCS-08401224.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Quintus Catius Lucullus.
EDCS-08401224.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Catius Lucullus.

EDCS-08401225.translation: Alphios, a slave, willingly erected this in fulfillment of a vow to the genius of the Annii Macri and Liciniani.
EDCS-08401225.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by a slave named Alphios to the genius of the Annii Macri and Liciniani.

EDCS-08401226.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Veratius Firmus.

EDCS-08401227.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caecilia Euphraenusa, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-08401229.translation: To the departed spirits of Flavia Panthia.
EDCS-08401229.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed spirit of Flavia Panthia, intended for a tomb.

EDCS-08401231.translation: His parents, Cnaeus, to Gaius Victor.
EDCS-08401231.summary: This inscription is a dedication by parents named Cnaeus to their son Gaius Victor, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-08401236.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of C. C., son of Augustus, possibly on a tomb or other dedication.

EDCS-08401237.translation: We grieve that she is gone and desire that she serve her worthy ashes for a long time. Reader, if by chance you seek the name of the deceased, you will learn it by reading downwards from the top. She had already lived thirty years when her soul departed to the stars in her pious youth, on the fifth day before the Kalends of November, in the consulship of the most distinguished Messala.
EDCS-08401237.summary: This epitaph records the death of a woman who lived thirty years, and her soul departed on November 27th during the consulship of Messala.

EDCS-08401238.translation: To Aurelia Actula, daughter of Secundus.
EDCS-08401238.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aurelia Actula, daughter of Secundus, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-08401239.translation: To Apollo, Titus Aelius Glaphyrus willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08401239.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication made by Titus Aelius Glaphyrus to Apollo.

EDCS-08401242.translation: Agathopus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08401242.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Agathopus.

EDCS-08401243.translation: M. Amoena, freedwoman of Pompeia, willingly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-08401243.summary: This inscription records a woman, M. Amoena, paying a vow to Iboita, likely a local deity, with the payment made willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-08401244.translation: Sextus Pompeius Theophilus, freedman of Proculus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Iboita.
EDCS-08401244.summary: This inscription records Sextus Pompeius Theophilus, a freedman, paying a vow to Iboita, indicating a fulfillment of a religious obligation.

EDCS-08401245.translation: Decimus Ratius Bassus, freedman of Decimus, paid his vow to Iboita.
EDCS-08401245.summary: This inscription records Decimus Ratius Bassus, a freedman, fulfilling a vow to Iboita.

EDCS-08401248.translation: Valeria Nicephoris, with her patron, made this for herself and her freedman while alive.
EDCS-08401248.summary: This funerary inscription records Valeria Nicephoris dedicating a monument for herself and her freedman, with the dedication likely made to an Augustalis official.

EDCS-08401249.translation: Basilius Iustinus made this for his wife Tiae Paternae, and for himself and his descendants, while alive.
EDCS-08401249.summary: This funerary inscription records Basilius Iustinus dedicating a monument for his wife, himself, and his descendants, indicating a testament to family legacy.

EDCS-08401250.translation: They willingly and deservedly paid their vow to Jupiter for Valerius, the neighbour.
EDCS-08401250.summary: This inscription records a vow being paid to Jupiter on behalf of Valerius, likely by his neighbours, suggesting a collective act of religious observance.

EDCS-08401251.translation: Valeria Eleutheris paid her vow to the Parcae willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08401251.summary: This inscription records Valeria Eleutheris paying a vow to the Parcae (Fates), indicating a personal religious devotion.

EDCS-08401252.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying Celtilla as the daughter of Atton.

EDCS-08401253.translation: Lucius Donnius Flavus, son of Caius, flamen of Rome and Augustus, ordered this to be made by his will, under the supervision of Caius Donnius Vena and Caius Attius Rufus.
EDCS-08401253.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by Lucius Donnius Flavus, a flamen of Rome and Augustus, to be executed after his death according to his will.

EDCS-08401254.translation: To the Divine Shades: Heliadis, a most devoted companion, for Antiochus, of Caesar's 40th legion from Gaul.
EDCS-08401254.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Shades, likely a funerary monument for Heliadis, a devoted companion of Antiochus, a soldier in the 40th legion.

EDCS-08401255.translation: Mary, virgin, minister of the temple of Jerusalem
EDCS-08401255.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for a virgin named Maria, who served as a minister in the temple of Jerusalem.

EDCS-08401256.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Helene. Valeria Helene, to her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08401256.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Valeria Helene for her devoted mother, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401257.summary: This is a dedication to the deified emperor Germanicus, possibly erected by a private individual or a community.

EDCS-08401258.translation: Vercilla Solimari, daughter, on her own property.
EDCS-08401258.summary: This inscription records Vercilla Solimari, daughter of Solimarus, and indicates she owned the land where it was placed.

EDCS-08401259.translation: To Juno. Verrius Crescens and Tillia Attica.
EDCS-08401259.summary: This is a votive offering to the goddess Juno, dedicated by Verrius Crescens and Tillia Attica.

EDCS-08401261.translation: By order, Titus Attius Quartus of Cailaro willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08401261.summary: This inscription records Titus Attius Quartus of Cailaro fulfilling a vow, likely in thanks for a divine favor.

EDCS-08401264.translation: To the spirit of our Titus. Urbinus, the slave.
EDCS-08401264.summary: This is a dedication to the 'genius' or protective spirit of a person named Titus, made by his slave Urbinus.

EDCS-08401265.translation: To the gods below, or to the Fates. Quartia, daughter of Lucius, from Anisera, set up this offering willingly and deservedly, by vow, or vision, or warning.
EDCS-08401265.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quartia, daughter of Lucius, from Anisera, to the underworld deities or the Fates, prompted by a divine sign or fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08401266.translation: To Neptune Augustus. Lucius Veratius Verus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08401266.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Neptune Augustus by Lucius Veratius Verus, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-08401267.translation: Attia Prima, to her own relatives.
EDCS-08401267.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary marker or dedication made by Attia Prima for her close family members.

EDCS-08401268.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Titus Flavius Severus paid his vow for his father Titus Flavius Onesimus.
EDCS-08401268.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus, where Titus Flavius Severus fulfilled a vow for his father, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08401269.translation: Martialis Silvestri willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08401269.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus, made by Martialis Silvestri, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401270.translation: Clymenus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08401270.summary: This is a votive inscription by Clymenus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401273.translation: To the son of the deified Constantius, the most invincible prince, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08401273.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring a son of Emperor Constantius, likely erected by local town councillors in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08401275.translation: To [---] ino, son of Caius, from [---]torius.
EDCS-08401275.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording names and possibly a filiation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401279.translation: To the peacefully resting Flavius Memorius, a most perfect man, who served among the Joviani for 28 years, a protector domesticus for 6 years, prefect of the lance-bearers and spearmen of the prince for 3 years, comes ripae for 1 year, comes of Mauretania Tingitana for 4 years; he lived 75 years. Praesidia, his wife, [erected this] for her most sweet husband.
EDCS-08401279.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Flavius Memorius, a high-ranking official, detailing his military and administrative career, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-08401280.translation: Geminus, an inhabitant of Agrippinensis by name, lies here; he was worthy to be honored as governor and administrator of the accounts of five provinces; he died faithfully after 38 years, 2 months, and 6 days. His citizens adorned his distinguished glory with this tomb.
EDCS-08401280.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Geminus, a prominent official from Cologne, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401281.translation: Terentius Museus erected this tomb for Hydria Tertulla, a most illustrious woman, his most loving wife, and for Axía Aelianna, his most sweet daughter.
EDCS-08401281.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Terentius Museus for his wife and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401283.translation: Lucius Marcus Aurelius Difilus, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, and Marcus Aurelius Priscus erected [this] for their most dutiful father.
EDCS-08401283.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by two sons for their father, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401284.translation: To the Divine Shades. Garinia Emerita erected [this] for Aurelius Valentinus, a standard-bearer of Legio XX, who lived 50 years, her most deserving husband.
EDCS-08401284.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for Aurelius Valentinus, a standard-bearer of Legio XX, by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401285.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Carsia Titia, daughter of Titus Carsi Teretina Certinus, a veteran of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, erected this for her most devoted father.
EDCS-08401285.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a daughter for her deceased father, a veteran of Legio XX Valeria Victrix.

EDCS-08401286.translation: To Clodia Ingenua, daughter of Marcus, and to Licinius Faustus, a soldier of the Praetorian Guard, son of Lucius, and to Licinia Ingenua, daughter of Lucius, and to Clodius Continens, son of Marcus.
EDCS-08401286.summary: This inscription lists multiple individuals, likely family members, with one identified as a soldier of the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-08401287.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cocceia Valentina, to her most devoted husband, Lucius Domitius Domitianus, formerly trierarch of the fleet of Germania Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-08401287.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, who served as a trierarch in the German fleet.

EDCS-08401288.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius erected this for his most devoted patron, Titus Flavius Iuvenalis, a veteran of Legio XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-08401288.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a patron to his deceased client, a veteran of Legio XXII Primigenia.

EDCS-08401289.translation: Nero erected this for himself while alive, and for Silvana Patricia, his lady and most devoted wife, through whose benefit he lived for 30 years after his discharge, without trouble.
EDCS-08401289.summary: This is a self-dedicatory tomb inscription, also commemorating Nero's wife and his peaceful life after military service.

EDCS-08401290.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fannius Paternus, a soldier of Legio XXX Ulpia Victrix, erected this for his incomparable wife, Iustinia Lupula.
EDCS-08401290.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a soldier of Legio XXX Ulpia Victrix to his deceased wife.

EDCS-08401291.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Severus, Titus Iulius Valentinus, and Iulia Valentina, siblings, and Titus Licinius Rusticus, a centurion of Legio III Augusta, her husband, [erected this] for Licinia Magna, a most esteemed matron.
EDCS-08401291.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by siblings and a husband to the deceased Licinia Magna, a respected matron.

EDCS-08401292.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Nonia Venustilla, sister and heir, and for herself while alive, erected this for her incomparable brother and for herself. Lucius Noni Secundus, son of Lucius, a signifer of Legio XII Augustae Fulminatae, lived for 29 years and 4 months, served for 14 years. She arranged for this to be made in front for 10 feet, in the field for 15 feet.
EDCS-08401292.summary: This funerary epitaph, commissioned by a sister for her deceased brother and herself, records the brother's military service as a signifer in Legio XII Augustae Fulminatae and specifies tomb dimensions.

EDCS-08401293.translation: sentius Saturninus, from the fleet of Britannia Philippiana, of Afer origin, from the colony of Septimia Libera Thysdritana, by his will.
EDCS-08401293.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording an individual named Sentius Saturninus, who was from Britannia Philippiana, of Afer origin, and from the colony of Septimia Libera Thysdritana, and who made arrangements by his will.

EDCS-08401294.translation: Vettitia Valeria erected this for herself while alive, and for her incomparable husband, Marcus Superinius Fruendus, a retired soldier of Legio I Minerviae.
EDCS-08401294.summary: This is a self-dedicatory tomb inscription, also commemorating Vettitia Valeria's deceased husband, a retired soldier of Legio I Minerviae.

EDCS-08401296.translation: To the divine spirits. Sextus Alfius Vitalis Forens, a six-member of the Augustales, a corporatus of the colony of Julia Paterna of Arelate, and Alfia Epauxesis, his freedwoman freedwoman and wife, who lived 40 years, 2 months, and 27 days; he made (this monument) for himself and his descendants while alive.
EDCS-08401296.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Sextus Alfius Vitalis Forens to himself, his wife Alfia Epauxesis, and their descendants, dating to the Roman Imperial period based on the mention of civic offices.

EDCS-08401297.translation: To the divine spirits. For Caecilia Aprulla, daughter of Decimus, designated priestess of the colony of Dea Augusta Vocontiorum, who died at 14 years, 2 months, and 5 days; her husband erected (this monument) for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-08401297.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a husband for his deceased wife, Caecilia Aprulla, who held the designation of priestess in the colony of Dea Augusta Vocontiorum, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401298.translation: Publicly dedicated to Titus Callonius Niger, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-08401298.summary: This inscription records the public dedication of something to Titus Callonius Niger, indicating his prominence within the community, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401299.translation: To Gnaeus Cornelius Optatus, son of Gnaeus, of the Teretina tribe, twice-man, priest, and maritime shipowner of Arelate, their patron.
EDCS-08401299.summary: This dedication honors Gnaeus Cornelius Optatus, a prominent citizen of Arelate who held various civic and religious offices, including patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401300.translation: To the divine spirits. Of Sextus Domitius Blastus, in front 20 feet.
EDCS-08401300.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Domitius Blastus, specifying a plot of land measuring 20 feet in front, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401301.translation: Gaius Fabius Hermes, freedman of Gaius, six-member of the Augustales of the colony of Julia Paterna of Arelate, made (this monument) while alive for himself, his family, and for Gaius Fabius Secundus, son of Lucius, his patron, and for Lucius Fabius Primus, son of Lucius, his brother; this monument shall not follow my heir.
EDCS-08401301.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Fabius Hermes, an Augustalis of Arelate, for himself, his family, his patron, and his brother, stipulating that his heir is not bound to it, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401302.translation: To the divine spirits. For Julia Tertullina, daughter of Caius, priestess of the colony of Apta; Lucius Vallius Atilianus, her grandson.
EDCS-08401302.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Lucius Vallius Atilianus to his grandmother, Julia Tertullina, who was a priestess of the colony of Apta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401303.translation: To the divine spirits. For Quintus Iulius Memorius, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, aedile, twice-man, priest of the colony of Julia Arelatensis; Iulius Licinianus, (his) dearest parent.
EDCS-08401303.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Iulius Licinianus to his father, Quintus Iulius Memorius, who held several civic and religious offices in the colony of Julia Arelatensis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401305.translation: To Iulius Marullus, son of Marcius, of the Teretina tribe, duovir.
EDCS-08401305.summary: This inscription records Iulius Marullus, son of Marcius, who held the office of duovir in his tribe, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401306.translation: To the divine spirits. For Lucius Pacullius Ephoebicus, a six-member of the Augustales of Arelate; Aurelia Eutychia, his wife.
EDCS-08401306.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelia Eutychia for her husband, Lucius Pacullius Ephoebicus, a member of the Augustales in Arelate, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08401307.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Paquius Epigonus, with his children, [erected this] to his most deserving patron, Gaius Paquius Optatus, freedman of Pardalus, Sextumvir Augustalis of the colony Iulia Paterna Arelatensis, patron of the same colony, and also patron of the shipwrights, sailors and blanket-makers.
EDCS-08401307.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Gaius Paquius Optatus, a patron of various guilds, erected by his freedman and his children.

EDCS-08401308.translation: By decree of the decurions, the Sextumviri Augustales and fellow citizens of Arles, to Pompeianus, son of Marcus Teretina, holder of the quinquennial office, flamen, and pontifex, their patron who served them most deservedly, who, content with the honor of this statue, remitted the expense to the public treasury.
EDCS-08401308.summary: This inscription records a statue erected by the citizens of Arles to their patron Pompeianus, who declined further public expenditure after receiving this honor.

EDCS-08401309.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Publicius Eutychus, Sextumvir Augustalis of the colony Iulia Paterna Arelatensis.
EDCS-08401309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Publicius Eutychus, a Sextumvir Augustalis of Arles.

EDCS-08401310.translation: Satria Firma, priestess, dedicated [this] to the goddess IARCO.
EDCS-08401310.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess IARCO by a priestess named Satria Firma.

EDCS-08401311.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Secundia Tatianae, daughter, to her most devoted father, Lucius Secundius Eleutherus, shipowner of Arles, and also a corporately recognized Sextumvir Augustalis of the colony Iulia Paterna Arelatensis.
EDCS-08401311.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Secundius Eleutherus, a shipowner and Sextumvir Augustalis of Arles, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-08401312.translation: To Publius Sextius Florus, Sextumvir Augustalis of the colony Iulia Aquis, and to his most devoted wife Valeria Spuria Flassina, and to Sextus Valerius Proculinus and his family.
EDCS-08401312.summary: This inscription records the dedication by Publius Sextius Florus, a Sextumvir Augustalis of Arles and Aquae, to his wife and family.

EDCS-08401313.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucia Statia Firma, of the colony of Arles, her most devoted neighbor's daughter.
EDCS-08401313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucia Statia Firma, described as a devoted neighbor's daughter from Arles.

EDCS-08401314.translation: To Marcus Surius Albinus, son of Gaius Voltinius, aedile of Apata, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-08401314.summary: This inscription records a posthumous dedication to Marcus Surius Albinus, an aedile of Apata, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-08401315.translation: Valeria Urbana, priestess, [dedicated this] for herself, and for her husband Sextus Mantius Eros, and for Octavia Hilara, friend, and for Charis, freedwoman.
EDCS-08401315.summary: This is a dedication made by Valeria Urbana, a priestess, for herself and several other individuals including her husband, friend, and freedwoman.

EDCS-08401316.translation: To the Divine Spirits. In honor of the wife of Gaius Iulius Fortunatus, Sextumvir Augustalis, Veria Filtata, friend, sadly dedicated [this].
EDCS-08401316.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring the wife of Gaius Iulius Fortunatus, a Sextumvir Augustalis, erected by her friend Veria Filtata.

EDCS-08401317.translation: To Marcus Vibius Frontonius, son of Marcus, of the Pollia tribe, the freedman Eros.
EDCS-08401317.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Vibius Frontonius, who held the office of aedile, set up by his freedman Eros.

EDCS-08401318.translation: To Sextus Voconius Gemellus, son of Sextus, of the Poblilia tribe, aedile. Voconius Restitutus, son of Sextus, of the Poblilia tribe, and his sister Voconia Prima, daughter of Sextus, made this at their own expense.
EDCS-08401318.summary: This is a dedication made at the expense of Voconius Restitutus and his sister Voconia Prima for Sextus Voconius Gemellus, who was an aedile.

EDCS-08401335.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication for Felix Apolastos, likely a freedman given his name.

EDCS-08401337.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Baebia Politice, his most devoted wife, the foreman Baebia Politice, aged 40, of the Galliarum quadragesima, formerly a slave of our Augusti, to Apronianus.
EDCS-08401337.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Apronianus, a foreman and husband, by his devoted wife Baebia Politice.

EDCS-08401338.translation: and for the eternal rest of Marcus Atinius Saturninus, an attendant of the ship-owners' station.
EDCS-08401338.summary: This inscription, likely a continuation of a previous one, commemorates Marcus Atinius Saturninus, an attendant for ship-owners.

EDCS-08401339.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Lucius Aventinius Avitianus, master craftsman of timber, of the colony of Julia Paterna Arelatis, chief of the same guild, first master craftsman of timber; Tossius Marcus Cosna, to his deserving master.
EDCS-08401339.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Aventinius Avitianus, a master timber craftsman and guild leader, set up by Tossius Marcus Cosna.

EDCS-08401340.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Avillia Grata, his most excellent wife, by Allatus, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-08401340.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Avillia Grata by her husband Allatus, a freedman of Augustus.

EDCS-08401341.translation: Aurelius Septimius Demetrianus, a sailor from Druenticus, set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-08401341.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Aurelius Septimius Demetrianus, a sailor, for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-08401342.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Quintus Candidus Benignus, master craftsman of timber of the guild of Arelatis, whose skill in craftsmanship was highest, whose study of doctrine, modesty, who all great craftsmen always called a master, none was more learned, none could surpass him, who knew how to make waterworks or direct their course, he was a sweet companion, who knew how to feed friends, teachable in intellect and application, and kind in spirit; for his most sweet father by Candidia Quintina and for her most dear husband by Valeria Maxsimina.
EDCS-08401342.summary: This complex funerary epitaph commemorates Quintus Candidus Benignus, a highly skilled master craftsman of timber and waterworks, by his daughter Candidia Quintina and wife Valeria Maxsimina.

EDCS-08401343.translation: Coelius D., a naval architect, for himself and for Coelius Monimius, his mother, and for Frontonius Nic[...]
EDCS-08401343.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Coelius D., a naval architect, for himself, his mother, and another individual named Frontonius.

EDCS-08401344.translation: To Decumanus, companion of the partners, [erected by] Prima, daughter of Vercillus, to her devoted life partner.
EDCS-08401344.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Prima to her deceased partner, Decumanus, likely a soldier or member of a collegium.

EDCS-08401345.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Iulius Hermes, to his devoted teacher, Dionysius the doctor.
EDCS-08401345.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulius Hermes to his master, Dionysius, a physician.

EDCS-08401346.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Titus Flavius Inventus, to Titus Flavius, patron most devoted, of the guild of carpenters of the Arles body.
EDCS-08401346.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from Titus Flavius Inventus to his patron, Titus Flavius, a leader in the carpenters' guild in Arles.

EDCS-08401347.translation: Lucius Granius Romanus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe. Marcus Iulius Olympus, merchant of the gladiatorial troupe, erected [this] to the deserving Lucius Granius Victor, his grandfather, for his merits.
EDCS-08401347.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Iulius Olympus for his grandfather, Lucius Granius Victor, a merchant involved with gladiators.

EDCS-08401348.translation: Lucius Iulius Augustalis, carpenter of the guild of Arles. Lucius Iulius Trophimus, his most unhappy father.
EDCS-08401348.summary: This inscription records the death of Lucius Iulius Augustalis, a carpenter, commemorated by his grieving father, Lucius Iulius Trophimus.

EDCS-08401349.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Iulius Eumenes lived 22 years. Iulia Agrippina, patroness, and [his] mother, the most unhappy, erected [this] to [their] corporately-enrolled waterskin maker, because of what you owed us and [what you should have done].
EDCS-08401349.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Eumenes, a waterskin maker, commemorating his life and expressing a grievance from his patroness and mother.

EDCS-08401350.translation: Caius Iulius Pot, colleague of the shipwrights' guild of the Arles body, curator of the same body. He and Severa, living, erected [this] for themselves and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08401350.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Pot and Severa for themselves, likely to commemorate their shared status within the shipwrights' guild in Arles.

EDCS-08401351.translation: To Lucius Iulius Secundus, a waterskin maker of the guild of the colony of Julia Paterna Arelatis, who bequeathed 200 denarii to them in his will, so that annually from the interest a sacrifice might be made to him. Also, Nauta Druenticus, corporately-enrolled, from Mogituma, Epipodius, his natural son, to his most devoted father.
EDCS-08401351.summary: This inscription records a bequest for funerary rites for Lucius Iulius Secundus, a waterskin maker, and also commemorates Nauta Druenticus, honored by his son.

EDCS-08401352.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Sextus Iulius Valentinus, a stonemason of the Almantica masons. They erected [this] from his funeral and for Pompeia Gratinia, his incomparable wife.
EDCS-08401352.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Sextus Iulius Valentinus's colleagues and his wife, Pompeia Gratinia, to his memory.

EDCS-08401353.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Iunia Valeria, [to her] most cherished pupil, Marcus Iunius Messianus, waterskin maker of the Arles guild, who held the office of master of the same guild four times, who lived 28 years, 5 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-08401353.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iunia Valeria to her beloved pupil, Marcus Iunius Messianus, a waterskin maker and four-time master of his guild.

EDCS-08401354.translation: To the Divine Manes. The Arles colleagues of Maximinus Festus, a weaver, erected this tombstone.
EDCS-08401354.summary: This is a tombstone erected by the colleagues of Maximinus Festus, a weaver from Arles, to his memory.

EDCS-08401355.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Metellia Prota, his mother, from Bilbilis. For Lucina.
EDCS-08401355.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Metellia Prota, mother of Lucina, who was from Bilbilis.

EDCS-08401356.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Lucius Pomponius Lucidus, a carpenter, a member of the guild. The Arles members of the guild, from his funerary fund.
EDCS-08401356.summary: This tombstone was erected from the funerary fund of Lucius Pomponius Lucidus, a carpenter and guild member from Arles.

EDCS-08401357.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Primigenius, an actor of the Eudoxus troupe.
EDCS-08401357.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Primigenius, an actor from the Eudoxus theatrical company.

EDCS-08401358.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Publicius Bellicus, a member of the colony of Julia Paterna Arelas, a carpenter, and also a magistrate. Venucia Priscilla, his incomparably beloved wife, and Venucia Priscilla herself, made this while alive for herself.
EDCS-08401358.summary: This tombstone was erected by Venucia Priscilla for her husband Gaius Publicius Bellicus, a carpenter and magistrate of the colony of Arelas, and also for herself.

EDCS-08401359.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iunia Melpomene, wife of Marcus Adretonus Avitus, Hortensius freedman and heir, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08401359.summary: This tombstone was commissioned by Iunia Melpomene for her husband Marcus Adretonus Avitus, a freedman and heir.

EDCS-08401360.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Sextus Aebutius Hermes. Aebutia Calliope to her most virtuous husband.
EDCS-08401360.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Aebutia Calliope to her husband Sextus Aebutius Hermes.

EDCS-08401361.translation: To Aelianus Hilarius, freedman, freedwoman A[---]
EDCS-08401361.summary: This monument fragment appears to be dedicated to Aelianus Hilarius and a freedwoman, possibly his wife.

EDCS-08401362.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Titus Aelius Narcissus. Damas, his freedman.
EDCS-08401362.summary: This tombstone was erected by Damas, a freedman, for his master Titus Aelius Narcissus.

EDCS-08401363.translation: To Aelia Aeliana. You who know the fate and sorrow of the girl from the letter, say many call this sarcophagus a house because it consumes the limbs, but this home, enclosed and decent, must be called a hive. Oh, what an unspeakable and unworthy thing, a distinguished girl lies here. This is more than sorrow: a beautiful girl has been snatched away. She lived as a virgin when she was pleasingly mature; her parents had announced her wedding, they rejoiced in their hopes. For she lived 17 years, 7 months, and 18 days. Oh, happy father who did not see such grief. The wound remains fixed in his chest. Geron, her father, holding his daughter by the hand, along with her mother, Dionysias, clings to her.
EDCS-08401363.summary: This epitaph laments the death of Aelia Aeliana, a young maiden who died at 17 years, 7 months, and 18 days, just before her wedding, and was buried in a sarcophagus.

EDCS-08401365.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aemilius and Valerius Aemilius Vitulus, father and son, set this up for their dearest sister.
EDCS-08401365.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits by a father and son for their sister.

EDCS-08401366.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Julius Eutyches set this up for his most devoted and most deserving wife, Aemilia Condita.
EDCS-08401366.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Julius Eutyches to his wife, Aemilia Condita.

EDCS-08401367.translation: To the Divine Spirits and to security. For Aemilia Eucarpia, Caius Paquius Pardalas set this up for his most beloved wife; she lived 41 years, 8 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-08401367.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Aemilia Eucarpia and is dedicated by her husband, Caius Paquius Pardalas.

EDCS-08401368.translation: For Albia Lucilia, his most devoted wife, Aurelius Philocrates and her sister Nigrina set this up.
EDCS-08401368.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelius Philocrates for his devoted wife Albia Lucilia and her sister Nigrina.

EDCS-08401369.summary: This is a simple memorial inscription for Albina.

EDCS-08401370.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Alfenia Attalis, Marcus Coelius Antoninus, his incomparable wife.
EDCS-08401370.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Marcus Coelius Antoninus to his unparalleled wife, Alfenia Attalis.

EDCS-08401371.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Alfia Philete and Philippus, with their son, made this for their wife.
EDCS-08401371.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Alfia Philete and Philippus for their wife and son respectively.

EDCS-08401372.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fortunata, his freedwoman, and Asterge and Faustinus Dionysius and Oclatius Fortunatus, and his son, made this for the excellent young slave Amazon, with great care.
EDCS-08401372.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by multiple freedmen and others for the young slave Amazon.

EDCS-08401373.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Bucamia Aphrodite made this for her most beloved husband, Antio Antonio.
EDCS-08401373.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Bucamia Aphrodite for her husband, Antio Antonio.

EDCS-08401374.translation: Antonius Tr [---] for his wife, Iniacra, p[---]
EDCS-08401374.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription initiated by Antonius Tr for his wife Iniacra.

EDCS-08401375.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Vifidius Quartus to his most dutiful mother, Antonia Antulla, daughter of Decimus Quintus.
EDCS-08401375.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Vifidius Quartus to his mother.

EDCS-08401376.translation: Apatia made this for her most beloved Marino Virginio, aged 37.
EDCS-08401376.summary: This funerary epitaph records the maker and the deceased, Marino Virginio, with his age.

EDCS-08401377.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aquilina. Rubria Acte, the nurse, for the girl of one and a half years.
EDCS-08401377.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Aquilina by her nurse.

EDCS-08401378.translation: To eternal peace and quiet and eternal memory. For Quintus Aristius Chresimus the Younger, who lived 3 months and 7 days. Ateria Chreste to her brother and Aristius Chresimus and Aristia Helpis to their son, most beloved. Good journey!
EDCS-08401378.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Quintus Aristius Chresimus the Younger, listing his age and dedicators, likely family members.

EDCS-08401379.translation: The bones of the Asiatici Confuleiorum are placed here.
EDCS-08401379.summary: This inscription records the burial place of the Asiatici Confuleiorum family.

EDCS-08401380.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Maspetia Vera erected this to her most devoted father, Quintus Asicius Verus, and to her mother, Maspetia Onesima, while alive.
EDCS-08401380.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Maspetia Vera for her parents, Quintus Asicius Verus and Maspetia Onesima.

EDCS-08401381.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Epaphroditus, fellow freedman of Caius Valerius Valerianus, made this from his own resources for his excellent freedman.
EDCS-08401381.summary: This funerary epitaph records Epaphroditus honoring his fellow freedman, Valerianus.

EDCS-08401382.translation: To their parents, alive, Sedulus and Securus, sons of Aulus Asvius Sedatus and Pompeia Graphini, made this.
EDCS-08401382.summary: This funerary epitaph records the sons Sedulus and Securus honoring their living parents.

EDCS-08401383.translation: Octavia Catullina made this for her pious husband, Sextus Attius Felix.
EDCS-08401383.summary: This funerary epitaph records Octavia Catullina dedicating a monument to her husband Sextus Attius Felix.

EDCS-08401384.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Attius and Afrodisia dedicated this to their most excellent and upright father, Caius Attius Secundus.
EDCS-08401384.summary: This funerary epitaph records Caius Attius and Afrodisia honoring their father, Caius Attius Secundus.

EDCS-08401385.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Sextus Attius Diadumenus, a boy of outstanding character and talent, who died in his thirteenth year. Lucius Annianius Mercurinus, to his sweetest brother.
EDCS-08401385.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy named Sextus Attius Diadumenus and is dedicated by his brother.

EDCS-08401386.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelius Eusebius, who lived 20 years, 6 months, and 15 days. Aurelius Alexander, to his incomparable son.
EDCS-08401386.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Aurelius Eusebius, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-08401387.translation: To Aulus Babbius, son of Marcus, Benigna from the tribe Teretina.
EDCS-08401387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Aulus Babbius, son of Marcus, and mentioning his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-08401388.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating the name of the deceased, Baebia Pieris.

EDCS-08401389.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to a father named Caecilianus and a person named Procula.

EDCS-08401390.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Caenius Macrinus, [his] brother, and Marcus Publicius Saturninus, [her] husband, [dedicated] to Caenia Primilla, most devoted to both.
EDCS-08401390.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Caenia Primilla by her brother and husband.

EDCS-08401391.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Caesonius Chariton, who died at the age of two years and 9 days. Marcus Caesonius Charitinus, to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08401391.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a very young child, Marcus Caesonius Chariton, and is dedicated by his father.

EDCS-08401392.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Stines Caesonius Callistianus, [his] incomparable wife.
EDCS-08401392.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Stines Caesonius Callistianus by her husband.

EDCS-08401394.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Calvis and Sextus Ca. [dedicate this to] Calvisia Iusta. They lie here.
EDCS-08401394.summary: This funerary inscription records that Calvis and Sextus Ca are dedicating a monument to Calvisia Iusta.

EDCS-08401395.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to eternal memory. For Candidia Quintina, who lived 30 years, 8 months, and 15 days. Iulius Quintinus, mother, and Valeria Maximina, mother, to their most unhappy daughter, placed this and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08401395.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Candidia Quintina by her mother and grandmother, noting her lifespan and the location of burial.

EDCS-08401396.translation: To Publius Canius, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-08401396.summary: This is a funerary dedication naming an individual and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-08401397.translation: To Gaius Cassius Exoratus, from Iulia Tittula, his wife.
EDCS-08401397.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a wife to her husband, naming both individuals.

EDCS-08401398.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sextus Iulius Polycarpus, freedman, erected this monument with utmost unhappiness to Cellia Panthia, an incomparable woman, at the age of 28.
EDCS-08401398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cellia Panthia, erected by her freedman, indicating her exceptional qualities and the dedicator's grief.

EDCS-08401399.translation: Eutychus erected this to Cemellus, his fellow slave and well-deserving companion, son of Tiberius Claudius Pocius.
EDCS-08401399.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a fellow slave to a deceased companion, indicating a close relationship and good service.

EDCS-08401400.translation: To the Divine Shades. The funeral of Censorinus, aged 49, his most deserving patron.
EDCS-08401400.summary: This inscription records the funeral arrangements or dedication for a highly deserving patron named Censorinus.

EDCS-08401401.translation: To the Divine Shades. Edia Tocidia made this for her sweetest and most dutiful son, Quintus Cepidius Marcellus, who lived 18 years, 3 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-08401401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man, Quintus Cepidius Marcellus, erected by his grieving mother.

EDCS-08401402.translation: Eternal peace to our sweetest and most innocent daughter, Chrysogone the Younger, Siricio, who lived 3 years, 2 months, and 27 days. Valerius and Chrysogone, parents of their most rare daughter, most desired at all times of her life.
EDCS-08401402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a very young child, Chrysogone the Younger, erected by her parents who express profound grief and love.

EDCS-08401403.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Claudius, freedman of Tiberius, aged 8 years and 10 days. Dapnus IIT Claudius.
EDCS-08401403.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young child, likely a freedman's son, with the names of associated individuals.

EDCS-08401404.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Cominius Severinus, the patron, arranged for this to be set up and dedicated under the axe to his most dutiful freedman, Quintus Cominius Hermophilus.
EDCS-08401404.summary: This funerary inscription is from a patron to his freedman, indicating a well-established and dutiful relationship, with the monument being erected and dedicated.

EDCS-08401405.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aminta Faustina erected this for her daughter, Cominia Flavia, who lived 17 years and 7 months. Most unhappy mother.
EDCS-08401405.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman, Cominia Flavia, erected by her grieving mother.

EDCS-08401406.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cominius Tullus, aged 18, in his native land. Atgia and Reburra, his mother, set this up for their son.
EDCS-08401406.summary: This is a tombstone for Cominius Tullus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-08401407.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Attius Euangelus set this up for his father, Lucius Cornelius Eutyches, who lived 80 years, and for his son Cornelius Iulianus, most sweet infant, who lived 3 years, 2 months, 17 days. If prudent, if wise, if as if not given. Cornelius Eutyches [set this up] for his most loving son.
EDCS-08401407.summary: This tombstone was erected by Marcus Attius Euangelus for his father Lucius Cornelius Eutyches and his infant son Cornelius Iulianus.

EDCS-08401408.translation: For Decimus Cornelius Valerianus of the Teretina tribe, deceased, aged 19 years, 10 months, 5 days. Cornelius Licinianus and Valeria Materna [set this up] for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-08401408.summary: This tombstone records the death of Decimus Cornelius Valerianus and was set up by his parents.

EDCS-08401409.translation: Sextus Valerius Severus made this for his wife Cornelia Clintae, pious, and for himself, during his lifetime.
EDCS-08401409.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sextus Valerius Severus for his wife Cornelia Clintae and himself.

EDCS-08401410.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelia Iacaena, set up by herself during her lifetime; her heirs took care to have it built.
EDCS-08401410.summary: This tombstone was set up by Cornelia Iacaena for herself during her lifetime, with her heirs completing the construction.

EDCS-08401411.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Lucilia and Cornelia Hippolyte, sisters, set this up for themselves during their lifetime.
EDCS-08401411.summary: Two sisters, Cornelia Lucilia and Cornelia Hippolyte, erected this tombstone for themselves.

EDCS-08401412.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelia Pia, daughter of Lucius. Her father [set this up] for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-08401412.summary: This tombstone was dedicated to Cornelia Pia by her father.

EDCS-08401413.translation: Cornelia Sedata, daughter of Lucius, pious, made this for herself and for Cornelia Optata, freedwoman, aged 20, pious, during her lifetime.
EDCS-08401413.summary: Cornelia Sedata made this funerary monument for herself and her freedwoman Cornelia Optata.

EDCS-08401414.translation: For Cornelia Soszomene, deceased, aged 25, incomparable soul. Aulus Annius Perpetuus [set this up] for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-08401414.summary: This tombstone was erected by Aulus Annius Perpetuus for his wife Cornelia Soszomene.

EDCS-08401415.translation: Cornelia TERIV [set this up] during her lifetime for herself and for Lucius Virtius Eutactus.
EDCS-08401415.summary: Cornelia TERIV erected this funerary monument for herself and Lucius Virtius Eutactus.

EDCS-08401416.translation: Cornelia Tertia, daughter of Lucius, made this while alive for her son Firmanus Quietus and her freedman Asbolinus and her freedman Titus Veturius Quartio and Aulus Adius Frontonus.
EDCS-08401416.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cornelia Tertia for her son, freedmen, and others.

EDCS-08401417.translation: To the Divine Manes of Marcus Cossutius Rufio and Cossutia Soteria.
EDCS-08401417.summary: This dedication is to the Divine Manes of Marcus Cossutius Rufio and Cossutia Soteria, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-08401418.translation: Gaius Crassus Agnelius placed this for his most beloved wife Afnia Primitiva.
EDCS-08401418.summary: Gaius Crassus Agnelius erected this monument for his wife Afnia Primitiva.

EDCS-08401419.translation: To the Divine Manes of Sextus Davarius Martiolus; Peregrinia Iulia [erected this] for her dearest husband.
EDCS-08401419.summary: Peregrinia Iulia dedicated this monument to the Divine Manes of her husband Sextus Davarius Martiolus.

EDCS-08401420.translation: Quintus Dellius Neo, son of Quintus, made this monument while alive for himself and his family; my heir shall not succeed to this.
EDCS-08401420.summary: Quintus Dellius Neo erected this self-funded monument for himself and his family, specifying inheritance terms.

EDCS-08401421.translation: To the Divine Manes of Domitia Martina, a most excellent and most holy wife; Tiberius Iulius Iulianus [erected this].
EDCS-08401421.summary: Tiberius Iulius Iulianus dedicated this monument to his wife Domitia Martina.

EDCS-08401422.translation: To the Divine Manes of Egnatia Avita; Statia Titulla [erected this] for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08401422.summary: Statia Titulla dedicated this monument to her mother Egnatia Avita.

EDCS-08401423.translation: To the Divine Manes of Euterpe, freedwoman of Flavia Carpima; Flavia Spes [erected this].
EDCS-08401423.summary: Flavia Spes dedicated this monument to the Divine Manes of her freedwoman Euterpe.

EDCS-08401424.translation: To the Divine Manes of Fabia Tertulla; Domitia Fabiana, daughter of Lucius, [erected this] for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08401424.summary: Domitia Fabiana dedicated this monument to her mother Fabia Tertulla.

EDCS-08401425.translation: [nephews] of their father and Fadianus the freedman.
EDCS-08401425.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to nephews and a freedman, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-08401426.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pompeius Fortunatus to Flavia, his sweetest wife, and to Flavia Re, his most obedient mistress.
EDCS-08401426.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia and Flavia Re, set up by Pompeius Fortunatus.

EDCS-08401427.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Maria Fronima, daughter of Flaviaes Dafnenis, incomparable, who lived 7 years, 8 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-08401427.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Fronima, daughter of Flaviaes Dafnenis, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-08401429.translation: To Hilara Appia, a pious mother, from Quintus Appaeus Faus.
EDCS-08401429.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hilara Appia, set up by her son Quintus Appaeus Faus.

EDCS-08401430.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Hostilius, son of Silvanus, aged 24 years, 2 months, and 15 days. His miserable mother, in eternal mourning, and his stepmother, by their kindness.
EDCS-08401430.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Hostilius, recording his age and dedicated by his mother and stepmother.

EDCS-08401432.translation: To Indius Faustinianus, who lived 17 years, 4 months, and 10 days. His most unhappy mother, Sexta Ursula, placed this, and his brothers placed this.
EDCS-08401432.summary: A funerary epitaph for Indius Faustinianus, detailing his age and erected by his mother and brothers.

EDCS-08401433.translation: To Quintus Julius Adventus, from Priscus his brother.
EDCS-08401433.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Julius Adventus, set up by his brother Priscus.

EDCS-08401434.translation: To Flora, his most dutiful tent-mate, from Sextus Julius Communis.
EDCS-08401434.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flora, dedicated by Sextus Julius Communis, who was her contubernalis.

EDCS-08401435.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Julius Eorticus and Lucia Iulia Melitine set this up for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08401435.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Eorticus and Lucia Iulia Melitine, a couple who commissioned their own tomb.

EDCS-08401436.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Julius Florus, who lived 39 years, 11 months, and 16 days. Iulia Olympias placed this for her most dutiful husband.
EDCS-08401436.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Julius Florus, detailing his age and erected by his wife Iulia Olympias.

EDCS-08401437.translation: To Julius Philon, who lived 18 years and 6 months.
EDCS-08401437.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Philon, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-08401438.translation: To Julius E., son of Optimus, Agatho.
EDCS-08401438.summary: This is an epitaph for Agatho, son of Julius E., from the Roman province of Africa, dated to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08401439.translation: To the Divine Manes. Carus to his dearest comrade-in-arms, Julia Aphrodisias.
EDCS-08401439.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a soldier to his beloved partner, Julia Aphrodisias.

EDCS-08401440.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Veratius Protoctetus to his dearest wife Julia Amabilis and to his most dutiful daughter Julia Sabina, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-08401440.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Veratius Protoctetus to his wife Julia Amabilis and their daughter Julia Sabina.

EDCS-08401441.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Turranius Eutyches to his most chaste and most deserving wife, Julia Castina.
EDCS-08401441.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Titus Turranius Eutyches to his wife Julia Castina.

EDCS-08401442.translation: To Julia Fortunata, his sweetest foster-daughter, who lived 16 years and 4 months. Valerius Valerianus and Julia Crescentina [dedicated this].
EDCS-08401442.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Julia Fortunata at the age of 16 years and 4 months, dedicated by her foster parents.

EDCS-08401443.translation: To the Divine Manes. [A word is missing]. to his incomparable wife, Julia Grata, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-08401443.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Julia Grata, wife of 'Atinus', who lived 40 years.

EDCS-08401444.translation: Julia Iusta to Mysticus, a slave, aged 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08401444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mysticus, a slave who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-08401445.translation: To the Divine Manes. Oh grief, what tears have made this tomb for Julia Lucina, dearest mother, flower of youth! She lies here, buried within the stone. Oh, if only that spirit could be restored, so she might know what grief it is! She lived 27 years, 10 months, and 13 days. Julia Parthenope, unhappy mother, placed this.
EDCS-08401445.summary: This is an emotional funerary epitaph by Julia Parthenope for her daughter Julia Lucina, who died at the age of 27 years, 10 months, and 13 days.

EDCS-08401446.translation: To Julia Secconis, daughter of M. From her sons Quietus and Verus, out of duty.
EDCS-08401446.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by sons Quietus and Verus to their mother, Julia Secconis.

EDCS-08401447.translation: Julia Parthenope erected this for herself while alive.
EDCS-08401447.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Julia Parthenope during her lifetime.

EDCS-08401448.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cornelius Hiberus to his dearest wife, Iulia Politica.
EDCS-08401448.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Politica, dedicated by her husband Lucius Cornelius Hiberus.

EDCS-08401449.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Messius Sym to his excellent wife, Iulia Serena.
EDCS-08401449.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Serena, dedicated by her husband Messius Sym.

EDCS-08401450.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Iulius Dorus to his most dutiful daughter, Iulia Servata, who died at the age of 18 years, 7 months.
EDCS-08401450.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Servata, who died at the age of 18 years and 7 months, dedicated by her father Sextus Iulius Dorus.

EDCS-08401451.translation: Peace be with you. Aurelius Clemens to his sweetest and incomparable wife, Iulia Supera.
EDCS-08401451.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Supera, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Clemens.

EDCS-08401452.translation: To Iulia, daughter of Lucius, Tyrrania, who lived 20 years, 8 months, who was an example to other women in both character and discipline. Autarcius to his daughter-in-law, Laurentia, his wife.
EDCS-08401452.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tyrrania, daughter of Lucius, and possibly her mother Laurentia, with a dedication from Autarcius.

EDCS-08401453.translation: Peace be with you. Lucius Septimius Primitivus to his incomparable wife, Iulia Valeria Serenilla, who lived with him for 19 years and 38 days. For a deserving person.
EDCS-08401453.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Valeria Serenilla, dedicated by her husband Lucius Septimius Primitivus, who they lived together for 19 years and 38 days.

EDCS-08401454.translation: Peace be with you. To Iulia Victoria, incomparable wife of Marcus Aurelius Asclepiodotus.
EDCS-08401454.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Victoria, dedicated by her husband Marcus Aurelius Asclepiodotus.

EDCS-08401455.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Publius Aelius Protus' most dutiful granddaughter, Iulia Urania, who died at the age of 22 years and 22 days, and to Titus Flavius Achilleus' incomparable wife.
EDCS-08401455.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Urania, dedicated by her grandfather Publius Aelius Protus and her husband Titus Flavius Achilleus.

EDCS-08401456.translation: Iulia Zosime placed this for her most dutiful husband, Gaius Pomponius Polycarpus, and for herself while living.
EDCS-08401456.summary: A funerary epitaph placed during their lifetime by Iulia Zosime for herself and her husband Gaius Pomponius Polycarpus.

EDCS-08401457.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Iulia Iunia. In the 27th year.
EDCS-08401457.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Iulia Iunia, potentially dating to the 27th year of an unknown regnal period.

EDCS-08401458.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vellia Phronime and Vellia Comazusa set this up for their son Tiberius Iunius Amandus.
EDCS-08401458.summary: A funerary dedication by two women to their son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401459.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iunia Marina set up this altar for her most beloved husband, Gaius Iunius Iunianus.
EDCS-08401459.summary: A funerary altar dedicated by a wife to her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401460.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iunia Modesta, aged 30. Corintius Iunius founded this tomb. Cultured Modesta, your beautiful body, alas, taken by fate in its thirtieth year, was guarded in this tomb by a dutiful [---].
EDCS-08401460.summary: A metrical epitaph for Iunia Modesta, commemorating her youth and beauty, set up by Corintius Iunius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401461.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iunius Blastus for his daughter Iunia Stratonice, most innocent soul.
EDCS-08401461.summary: A funerary dedication by a father to his daughter, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401462.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Damis, an innocent young man. Carisi Amphion, Alexander, and Heracla, fellow freedmen.
EDCS-08401462.summary: A funerary dedication by fellow freedmen to an innocent young man named Damis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401463.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Here lies lifeless Laberia Felicia. Attius S. purchased this resting place for her husband, with whom she lived chastely for [---] years, after her death.
EDCS-08401463.summary: A funerary inscription for Laberia Felicia, set up by her husband Attius S. after her death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401464.translation: He made this, while alive, for his wife Campana, daughter of Lucius Domitius Iucundus.
EDCS-08401464.summary: A funerary epitaph for Campana, set up by her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401465.translation: Licinia Secunda, daughter of Sextus Licinius, rests here.
EDCS-08401465.summary: A simple epitaph for Licinia Secunda, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401466.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Liguria Euangelia.
EDCS-08401466.summary: A funerary dedication to Liguria Euangelia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401467.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Liguria Euphrosyne. Lucius Cornelius Eutyches for his incomparable wife, and Lucius Cornelius Iulianus, Lucius Cornelius Euphrosynus, and Marcus Attius Euangelus for their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08401467.summary: A complex funerary dedication by a husband and sons to Liguria Euphrosyne, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08401468.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Magus Martialis.
EDCS-08401468.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Magus Martialis, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-08401469.translation: To Gaius Marius, son of Marcus, his chosen heir.
EDCS-08401469.summary: This inscription records a bequest to Gaius Marius, son of Marcus, as stated in a will.

EDCS-08401470.translation: Eternal peace to you, most deserving. Martius Victor, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-08401470.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Martius Victor to a highly esteemed woman, likely his wife.
