EDCS-08500914.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Atilius Firmus, tribune of soldiers of the legion, his daughter Secundina.
EDCS-08500914.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a military tribune by his daughter.

EDCS-08500916.translation: For Marcus Arruntius Verecundus, freedman of Marcus, a member of the college of the six (IIIIII viro), husband of Pupae Cilae, and son of Fulviae Pupae Secundinae, the heirs by will.
EDCS-08500916.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Arruntius Verecundus, specifying his status as a freedman and member of a college, and mentions his wife and daughter as heirs.

EDCS-08500917.translation: Titus Licinius Maxumus made this at his own expense when he was aedile.
EDCS-08500917.summary: This is a dedication by Titus Licinius Maxumus, who funded the construction of a public work during his term as aedile.

EDCS-08500926.translation: On the left, ten units from the decumanus, ten units from the cardo, from the tributary twelve. Colonus Varius Calidus counts twenty asses and twenty-six denarii for eight asses. Appuleia Paulla counts forty-two for eight asses and twenty-one denarii for sixteen asses. Valerius Secundus counts four for eight asses and two denarii.
EDCS-08500926.summary: This inscription details a financial transaction or accounting related to land division and payments, possibly in a military or colonial context.

EDCS-08500928.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription possibly recording a person's origin and an action they took.

EDCS-08500930.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Anicia Tryphaera, Caius Lucceius Marcus [dedicated this] to his wife. Be well.
EDCS-08500930.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Anicia Tryphaera.

EDCS-08500933.translation: Lucius Aurelius Lucanus, Titus Fufius Modostus, Lucius Aurelius Verus.
EDCS-08500933.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly of officials, landowners, or donors, with no further context provided.

EDCS-08500935.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cassia Maxima, Licinius Hylas [dedicated this] to his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-08500935.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Licinius Hylas to his wife, Cassia Maxima.

EDCS-08500936.summary: This is a simple dedication or identification of a woman named Cati Secunda, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-08500937.summary: This is a dedication to an individual named Felix, son of Cassius.

EDCS-08500940.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Paterna, mother, and Togius Paullinianus, father, (erected this) for their most dutiful daughter Iulia Paullina.
EDCS-08500940.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Paullina, set up by her parents, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08500941.translation: Marcus Licinius Festus. This monument will not pass to his heir.
EDCS-08500941.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Licinius Festus, specifying that the monument is not inherited, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500942.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maternus Solidumarius.
EDCS-08500942.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph for Maternus Solidumarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500944.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Minicius Valerianus (erected this) for his most devoted grandfather Lucius Minicius Firmus.
EDCS-08500944.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Minicius Firmus, dedicated by his devoted grandson, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500945.translation: Paulia Ilias, wife of Titus Sextilius VI, rests here, to one who deserved well.
EDCS-08500945.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paulia Ilias, wife of Titus Sextilius VI, indicating she rests in a tomb deserving of her memory, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500950.translation: Spurius Severus made this alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-08500950.summary: An epitaph for Spurius Severus, who built his own tomb during his lifetime for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500951.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Valerius Julianus and Byria Severila, parents, (erected this) for their dearest son Lucius Valerius Severianus, aged [some years].
EDCS-08500951.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Severianus, set up by his parents, indicating his age was recorded, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500966.translation: To Apollo. Marcus Licinius Maxsuminus gladly paid his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-08500966.summary: A votive inscription to Apollo by Marcus Licinius Maxsuminus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500967.translation: To Belus, ruler of Fortune and master of the mind, Sestos dedicated an altar in Apamea, remembering the wise; the altar will rejoice which he gave and willed.
EDCS-08500967.summary: A bilingual inscription (Greek and Latin) from Apamea, where Sestos dedicates an altar to Belus (identified with Fortuna), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500968.translation: To Diana Augusta. Marcus Fufius Maternus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08500968.summary: A votive inscription to Diana Augusta by Marcus Fufius Maternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500970.translation: Marcus Licinius Goas willingly fulfilled his vow to Dullovia, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500970.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Licinius Goas to the deity Dullovia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500971.summary: This is a short funerary or dedication inscription, identifying Cornelia as the daughter of Fatis, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500973.translation: Calomallus Vasiensis, a scribe, [dedicated this] to the Genius of the Forum.
EDCS-08500973.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Calomallus Vasiensis, identified as a scribe, to the Genius Forensis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500974.translation: Valerius Hermes [dedicated this] to the Genius of Publius Terentius Aquila.
EDCS-08500974.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Valerius Hermes to the Genius of Publius Terentius Aquila, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500975.translation: Ioventius, son of Daver, willingly fulfilled his vow to Juno Regina, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500975.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ioventius to Juno Regina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500976.translation: Proclus willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500976.summary: This is a votive inscription by Proclus to Jupiter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500977.translation: Marcus Iulius F. willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Depulsor, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500977.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Iulius F. to Jupiter Optimus Depulsor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500978.translation: Servandus willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Depulsor, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500978.summary: This is a votive inscription by Servandus to Jupiter Depulsor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500979.translation: Marcus Domitius Festus, according to his vow.
EDCS-08500979.summary: This inscription records a fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Domitius Festus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500980.translation: Attica, freedwoman of Gaviatia Quinti, willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, as was deserved.
EDCS-08500980.summary: This is a votive inscription by Attica, freedwoman of Gaviatia Quinti, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500981.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius Nor Mur [dedicated it].
EDCS-08500981.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Nor Mur.

EDCS-08500982.translation: Ennius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Luna.
EDCS-08500982.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Luna by Ennius.

EDCS-08500983.translation: Litugena's daughter, Paterna, [dedicated it] to Luna.
EDCS-08500983.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Luna by Paterna, daughter of Litugena.

EDCS-08500985.translation: Titus Agileius Rufus, son of Quintus, and Sextus Agileius Pedo, son of Quintus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Mars.
EDCS-08500985.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars by two individuals, Titus Agileius Rufus and Sextus Agileius Pedo, likely brothers.

EDCS-08500986.translation: Lucius, son of Ceionius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08500986.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars by Lucius, son of Ceionius.

EDCS-08500987.translation: Titus Cornelius Pegasus, in fulfillment of a vow, [dedicated this] to Mars.
EDCS-08500987.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars by Titus Cornelius Pegasus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08500988.translation: Sextus Erucius Maxumus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08500988.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars by Sextus Erucius Maxumus.

EDCS-08500989.translation: Masso gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08500989.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars by Masso.

EDCS-08500990.translation: Sextus Cornelius Sacratus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars, the god of the Albioriges.
EDCS-08500990.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars Albiorix by Sextus Cornelius Sacratus.

EDCS-08500991.translation: Tacitus [dedicated this] to Mars and Vasionus.
EDCS-08500991.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and Vasionus by Tacitus.

EDCS-08500993.translation: Quintus Abudius Theodotus, freedman of Frontonis, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500993.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Matres (Mothers) by Quintus Abudius Theodotus, a freedman, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500994.translation: Adcultus, son of Vassedonis, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500994.summary: This votive inscription to the Matres was made by Adcultus, son of Vassedonis, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500995.translation: Catius Mansuetus and his brothers paid their vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500995.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Matres by Catius Mansuetus and his brothers, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500996.translation: Sextus Coelius Ingenuus, freedman of Nigri, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500996.summary: This votive inscription to the Matres was made by Sextus Coelius Ingenuus, a freedman, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500997.translation: Caius Dunatius Gratus, prefect of the June township, gave this from his own funds.
EDCS-08500997.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Caius Dunatius Gratus, prefect of the pagus (township) Iunius, providing funds for a monument, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08500998.translation: Lucius Epidius Murranus, freedman of Luci, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500998.summary: This votive inscription to the Matres was made by Lucius Epidius Murranus, a freedman, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500999.translation: Euneos, freedman of Sexti Afrani, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-08500999.summary: This votive inscription to the Matres was made by Euneos, a freedman of Sextus Afranius, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501000.translation: Ingenua paid her vow to the Mothers, wife of Solimutus, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501000.summary: This votive inscription to the Matres was made by Ingenua, wife of Solimutus, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501002.translation: Cominia E paid her vow to Mercury, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501002.summary: This votive inscription to Mercury was made by Cominia E, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501003.translation: Erucia Prisca dedicated this monument from her own funds.
EDCS-08501003.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Erucia Prisca funding a monument, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501004.translation: Sextus, freedman of Marcellus, a vow to Mercury.
EDCS-08501004.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Sextus, a freedman of Marcellus, to the god Mercury.

EDCS-08501005.translation: Sextus Marius [made an offering] to Mercury.
EDCS-08501005.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Sextus Marius to the god Mercury.

EDCS-08501007.translation: Messius, son of Sennon, [made an offering] to Mercury.
EDCS-08501007.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Messius, son of Sennon, to the god Mercury.

EDCS-08501008.translation: Scotaus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08501008.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Scotaus to the god Mars.

EDCS-08501009.translation: Sextus Silvius Silvester Iccianus [made an offering] to Mercury.
EDCS-08501009.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Sextus Silvius Silvester Iccianus to the god Mercury.

EDCS-08501010.translation: Attia made a vow to Minerva.
EDCS-08501010.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Attia to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-08501011.translation: Erucia Alliola willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Minerva.
EDCS-08501011.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Erucia Alliola to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-08501012.translation: Marcus willingly and deservedly returned his vow to Minerva.
EDCS-08501012.summary: This inscription records a vow returned by Marcus to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-08501013.translation: Paterna, daughter of Paternus, willingly and deservedly placed [this] at the order [of the deity].
EDCS-08501013.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Paterna, daughter of Paternus, to Minerva.

EDCS-08501014.translation: Lucius Apronius Chrysomallus dedicated [this] because of the Persian rank.
EDCS-08501014.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Apronius Chrysomallus, likely related to a military promotion.

EDCS-08501016.translation: Lucius Carenus Carus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-08501016.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nymphs by Lucius Carenus Carus.

EDCS-08501017.translation: Maternus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-08501017.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nymphs by Maternus.

EDCS-08501018.translation: Maternus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the August Nymphs.
EDCS-08501018.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the August Nymphs by Maternus.

EDCS-08501019.translation: Titus Gingetius Dionysius, in accordance with his vow, to the August and Bountiful Nymphs.
EDCS-08501019.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the August and Bountiful Nymphs by Titus Gingetius Dionysius.

EDCS-08501020.translation: Lucceius Fuscus paid his vow to his own next of kin.
EDCS-08501020.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the dedicator's next of kin by Lucceius Fuscus.

EDCS-08501021.translation: Potita, daughter of Cai Codonius, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to her own next of kin.
EDCS-08501021.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the dedicator's next of kin by Potita.

EDCS-08501022.translation: Seneca, daughter of Secundus, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to her own next of kin.
EDCS-08501022.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the dedicator's next of kin by Seneca.

EDCS-08501023.translation: Gaius Asicius Silvinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08501023.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus by Gaius Asicius Silvinus.

EDCS-08501024.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus by Attius.

EDCS-08501025.translation: Publius Iccius Veratianus, by order, [dedicated this] to the god Silvanus.
EDCS-08501025.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus by Publius Iccius Veratianus, made by order.

EDCS-08501026.translation: Marcus Homullus, freedman of Vasionius, [dedicated this].
EDCS-08501026.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Homullus, a freedman, to a person named Vasionius.

EDCS-08501027.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vasionius, identified as a river.

EDCS-08501029.translation: To the goddess Victoria, the established one.
EDCS-08501029.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Victoria.

EDCS-08501030.translation: To Victoria, fortunate; M., S. [dedicated this].
EDCS-08501030.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Victoria by an individual or individuals whose names are abbreviated.

EDCS-08501031.translation: The Vinturi people gladly fulfilled their vow as deserved.
EDCS-08501031.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by the Vinturi people.

EDCS-08501032.translation: Quintus Terentius gladly and deservedly dedicated an altar to Vulcan.
EDCS-08501032.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Vulcan by Quintus Terentius.

EDCS-08501033.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Messius.

EDCS-08501037.translation: Primus, Secundus, and Titus, sons of Dem, gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-08501037.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Primus, Secundus, and Titus, sons of Dem.

EDCS-08501038.translation: Sedatus Sacrini gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08501038.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Sedatus Sacrini.

EDCS-08501039.translation: To Victorius Favoris, he fulfilled his vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501039.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Victorius Favoris.

EDCS-08501040.translation: The equestrian officer discharged his vow to Augustus, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501040.summary: This is a dedication made by an equestrian officer to Augustus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501041.translation: Virius made this for Sumelus Voretus.
EDCS-08501041.summary: This is a funerary or dedication inscription recording the maker and recipient, possibly from the Roman period.

EDCS-08501042.translation: The Augustales of Vasienses [honored] the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, unconquered, pious, fortunate.
EDCS-08501042.summary: This is an honorary inscription by the Augustales of Vasienses to Emperor Gallienus, likely dating to his reign (253-268 CE).

EDCS-08501044.translation: [He served as] augur, legate pro praetore of the army of Germania Inferior, legate pro praetore of the province of Aquitania, [and] praetor.
EDCS-08501044.summary: This inscription lists the high-ranking imperial offices held by an individual, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501046.translation: For the eternal memory of Macia Severina, Aurelius Valerianus, a citizen of the Vergellenses and brother of Macia Severina, tribunes of the Second Italica Legion, made this for his wife and himself while alive.
EDCS-08501046.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelius Valerianus for his wife Macia Severina and himself, indicating their civic status and his military connection, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501047.translation: The Vasienses Vocontiorum [dedicated this to] Gaius Sappius Voltinus Flavus, son of Gaius, prefect of the Iuliensians, tribune of the soldiers of the XXI Rapax Legion, prefect of the Thracian Wing, prefect of the bank of the Euphrates River, who by his will bequeathed 1,200,000 sesterces to the republic of the Iuliensians, to be managed with 40,000 sesterces interest; he also bequeathed 50,000 sesterces for the portico before the baths, to be adorned with marble.
EDCS-08501047.summary: This inscription details the bequests made by Gaius Sappius Voltinus Flavus, a prominent official with military and administrative roles, to the community of Iuliensians and for public works, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501049.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying Marcus Calpurnius Tutor as a prefect, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501050.translation: The Vasienses Vocontiorum [honored] their patron Sextus Afranius Burrus, son of Sextus Voltinus, tribune of the soldiers, procurator of Augusta, procurator of Tiberius Caesar, procurator of the deified Claudius, prefect of the Praetorium, with consular ornaments.
EDCS-08501050.summary: This honorary inscription from the Vasienses Vocontiorum honors Sextus Afranius Burrus for his various high-ranking military and administrative positions, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501051.translation: For Bellica, priestess of the deified Augusta...
EDCS-08501051.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription honoring a priestess named Bellica, dedicated to the deified Augusta, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501052.translation: ...daughter of Au, priestess of the Vasienses Vocontiorum, her heirs Callistus and his freedmen arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-08501052.summary: This is a funerary or honorary inscription concerning a priestess of the Vasienses Vocontiorum, whose heirs arranged for its erection, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501053.translation: To Catia, daughter of Titus, called Servata, priestess of Julia Augusta of the Vasienses Vocontii, Titus Catius Severus, her brother and heir, had this made by his will, to her husband Quinto Secundio Zmaragdo, a Severi Augustalis.
EDCS-08501053.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a brother and heir for his sister and her husband, a Severus Augustalis, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501054.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a Severus Augustalis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501055.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying a person named Festus, possibly a Severus, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-08501056.translation: To the priestess of the Vocontii, also priestess of Iulia, from V. Iulius Politicus and He, her excellent patronesses.
EDCS-08501056.summary: This is a dedication from patronesses to a priestess of the Vocontii and Iulia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501057.translation: Titus Iulius Pothinus, Severus Augustalis, Seren[--].
EDCS-08501057.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honoring Titus Iulius Pothinus, a Severus Augustalis, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-08501058.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lucius Laelius Fortunatus, prefect of the garrisons and private affairs of the Vocontii, priest of Augustus, from Lucius Laelius Olympus, his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08501058.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a son to his father, who held significant administrative and religious positions in the Vocontii region, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501059.translation: To Marcianus, prefect of the Vasienses.
EDCS-08501059.summary: This is a dedication to Marcianus, who was the prefect of the Vasienses, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-08501060.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Quintus Passerius Tertius, Severus Augustalis of the Vasienses. Quintus Passerius Valentinus and Quintus Passerius Fortunatus, his freedmen, [erected this] to their most excellent patron, by his will.
EDCS-08501060.summary: This is a funerary dedication by freedmen to their patron, a Severus Augustalis of the Vasienses, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501062.translation: To Quintus Pompeius Voltinius, son of It, priest of Augustus. Valerius Tetricus Anullinus.
EDCS-08501062.summary: This inscription records the dedication by Valerius Tetricus Anullinus to Quintus Pompeius Voltinius, a priest of Augustus, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-08501063.translation: Prefect of engineers, priest of the deified Augustus, pontiff. lia Pompullina.
EDCS-08501063.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a prefect of engineers and a priest of Augustus, possibly dedicated by lia Pompullina, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-08501064.translation: Quintilia Patera, daughter of Vasiensium Vocontiorum, made this monument for herself and her family while alive.
EDCS-08501064.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Quintilia Patera for herself and her family.

EDCS-08501065.translation: Quintus Domitius Rufus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, prefect of craftsmen and twice prefect of Vasiensium, ordered the stage to be adorned with marble in his will; the state restored it after it had been consumed by age.
EDCS-08501065.summary: This inscription records the testament of Quintus Domitius Rufus, who willed funds for the stage decoration, and the subsequent restoration of the stage by the state.

EDCS-08501066.translation: To the departed spirits of Valerius Maximus, son of Valerius, 13 years old, prefect of the vigintivirate of the pagus Deobensis. His mother Valeria and his husband Cassius Eros.
EDCS-08501066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the 13-year-old Valerius Maximus, who held a local magistracy, set up by his mother and husband.

EDCS-08501067.translation: Lucius Veratius Rusticus, aedile of the pagus Baginensis, as a legate of the beneficiaries, from many sources and broken bronze.
EDCS-08501067.summary: This inscription records Lucius Veratius Rusticus, aedile of the pagus Baginensis, dedicating something using various resources.

EDCS-08501071.translation: To the departed spirits of Aurelius Maxsimus, who met his fate, of the citizenship of Vasiensium, whom his father and mother handed over for burial.
EDCS-08501071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Maxsimus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-08501072.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name and perhaps a cognomen, possibly indicating ownership or a grave.

EDCS-08501073.translation: [---]us, son of Lucius, Voltinia Festus and [---] with 350 sesterces into this portico.
EDCS-08501073.summary: This inscription records a donation of 350 sesterces by Festus, son of Lucius, for a portico.

EDCS-08501074.translation: For Decimus Sallustius Acceptus, the stone workers, for his burial.
EDCS-08501074.summary: This inscription is a dedication by stone workers for the burial of Decimus Sallustius Acceptus.

EDCS-08501075.translation: For Decimus Valerius Valentinus, a tessellarius, by Quinta Centonia, according to his will.
EDCS-08501075.summary: This inscription records a provision made according to the will of Decimus Valerius Valentinus for a tessellarius.

EDCS-08501078.translation: Lucius Abudius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-08501078.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Abudius.

EDCS-08501079.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Aelia Prima.
EDCS-08501079.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aelia Prima.

EDCS-08501080.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Albius Lucinulus, a most wonderful youth, who lived 15 years, 4 months, and 5 days. Albius Gratus and Attia Lucinula dedicated (this monument) to their most devoted son, under the axe.
EDCS-08501080.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents to their deceased son, including his age at death and the date of dedication.

EDCS-08501082.summary: This is a funerary dedication, likely for Gaius Antius Rufinus.

EDCS-08501083.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Asprius Servatus. Satrius Asprius (dedicated this) to his father and his own family, 25 feet in front, 25 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501083.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son to his father and family, specifying land dimensions.

EDCS-08501084.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Titus Atilius Ingenuus, most devoted son. Iulia Ingenua, mother, and Quintus Iulius Marcellinus Laena, husband.
EDCS-08501084.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother and husband to Titus Atilius Ingenuus.

EDCS-08501085.translation: To the spirits of the departed and eternal memory of Atilia Avitiana. Titus Sallustius Marcellianus (dedicated this) to his excellent mother.
EDCS-08501085.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son to his mother, Atilia Avitiana.

EDCS-08501087.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Iulia Iulia, most devoted daughter. Iulia Titulla, mother, (dedicated this). Who lived 29 years, 4 months, and 4 days, dedicated under the axe.
EDCS-08501087.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother to her deceased daughter, including her age at death and the date of dedication.

EDCS-08501088.translation: To Titus Blaesius, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe. Cupito, to Gaius Blaesius, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe. Epatus, son of Lituccus, mother.
EDCS-08501088.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial, possibly funerary, for Titus Blaesius and Gaius Blaesius, with a mention of their mother.

EDCS-08501089.summary: This is an inscription indicating a dedication or memorial made by Bullonius during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501090.translation: Of Quintus Cafatius Primus and Capella Decuma, daughter. 20 feet in front, 15 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501090.summary: This inscription likely records land dimensions associated with Quintus Cafatius Primus and Capella Decuma.

EDCS-08501091.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Titus Camullus Telesphorus, his most excellent son, and to Iustinia Valeria, his most excellent wife. [Erected] by Camullius Onesimus.
EDCS-08501091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Titus Camullus Telesphorus and his wife, erected by Camullius Onesimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501092.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Placed by his sister and mother to the most dutiful Ca[---] Carus.
EDCS-08501092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Carus, erected by his sister and mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501093.translation: Caresus, son of Adulus, [measured] 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501093.summary: This inscription records land dimensions (12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field) belonging to Caresus, son of Adulus, likely a property boundary marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501094.translation: Castus [erected this] for himself, for his wife Iulia Cita, and for his family.
EDCS-08501094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Castus for himself, his wife Iulia Cita, and their family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501095.translation: Of Titus Catius Modestus: 25 feet in the field, 25 feet in front.
EDCS-08501095.summary: This inscription records land dimensions (25 feet in front, 25 feet in the field) belonging to Titus Catius Modestus, likely a property boundary marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501096.translation: For eternal memory, to the Spirits of the Underworld. Of Craxus Honorius: 40 feet in front, 140 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501096.summary: This funerary inscription records the dimensions of a plot (40 feet in front, 140 feet in the field) belonging to Craxus Honorius, dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501098.translation: Of Lucius Duvius Laurentis: an area of 14 feet in front and 27 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501098.summary: This inscription records land dimensions (14 feet in front, 27 feet in the field) belonging to Lucius Duvius Laurentis, likely a property boundary marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501100.translation: To Quintus Gellius, son of Quintus, of the Voltinian tribe.
EDCS-08501100.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Gellius, son of Quintus, mentioning his tribal affiliation (Voltinia), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501101.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gratinus [erected this] for Grata and for his family.
EDCS-08501101.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Grata and the family of Gratinus, addressed to the Spirits of the Underworld, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501102.translation: Licnus gives [this] to Helena, slave of Titinius.
EDCS-08501102.summary: This inscription records a donation by Licnus to Helena, a slave belonging to Titinius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501103.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Caius Iucundius Severus, who died at the age of 29 years, 8 months, and 4 days. Caius Iucundius Severianus, to his most devoted son.
EDCS-08501103.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deceased son, Caius Iucundius Severus, providing his age at death.

EDCS-08501104.translation: He made this for his son, Julius Pompeius.
EDCS-08501104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating that a monument was erected by an unnamed person for their son, Julius Pompeius.

EDCS-08501105.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Julius Tertius, the heir, made this for Julius Secundio.
EDCS-08501105.summary: This funerary epitaph records that the heir, Titus Julius Tertius, erected the monument for Julius Secundio.

EDCS-08501106.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Julius Severianus, who died at the age of 4 years, 8 months, and 18 days. Julius Maximinus and Valeria Valeriana, most unhappy parents, erected this for their son.
EDCS-08501106.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Julius Severianus, detailing his young age at death and identifying his parents as the dedicants.

EDCS-08501107.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Iulius Natus, [set this up] to his mother, Julia Cupita, and his brother, Marcus Iulius Paternus.
EDCS-08501107.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Iulius Natus setting up a monument in honor of his mother, Iulia Cupita, and his brother, Marcus Iulius Paternus.

EDCS-08501108.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Iulia Rufina, his excellent freedwoman. Iulia Primella.
EDCS-08501108.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Iulia Primella to her freedwoman, Iulia Rufina, described as 'excellent'.

EDCS-08501110.summary: This inscription is simply the name of Marcus Licinius Goas, likely part of a larger inscription or a simple tomb marker.

EDCS-08501111.translation: Lucius Licinius, son of Aulus, [from] Se[...].
EDCS-08501111.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Lucius Licinius, son of Aulus, with an incomplete place name.

EDCS-08501113.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmented name, possibly Titus Livius, followed by the name C. Suillius and an incomplete element.

EDCS-08501114.summary: This inscription is a name, Titus M. Paternus, likely a tombstone or dedication fragment.

EDCS-08501115.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Marcianus to his most excellent mother Marcellina, daughter of Maternus.
EDCS-08501115.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcianus to his mother.

EDCS-08501116.translation: Publius Valerius Marcellus to his father Marcello, from Valeria Marcella.
EDCS-08501116.summary: A funerary dedication from Valeria Marcella to her father Marcello.

EDCS-08501117.translation: Marcianus and Secundinus set this up in memory of their father Patruvius, dearest to them, aged sixty years.
EDCS-08501117.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcianus and Secundinus for their father.

EDCS-08501118.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Caius Aelius Pitymias to his fellow freedman Caius Iulius Archias.
EDCS-08501118.summary: A funerary dedication from Caius Aelius Pitymias to his fellow freedman.

EDCS-08501119.summary: A simple dedication to Maternus, son of Maternus.

EDCS-08501120.translation: Materna, daughter of Paetus, made this monument for herself and her family: for her husband Sextus Gennaeus Soricus, for her son Sextus Gennaeus Maternus, for her daughter Gennaea, daughter of Sextus, and for her daughter Lucilla, daughter of Sextus. It shall not be inherited by an heir.
EDCS-08501120.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Materna for herself and her family, stipulating it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-08501121.summary: A simple dedication to Mattus, son of Lutevus.

EDCS-08501122.translation: The son of Maximuus gives this gift.
EDCS-08501122.summary: A dedication recording a gift made by the son of Maximuus.

EDCS-08501124.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Victorinus, son of Lucinus, to his dearest wife Musa, freedwoman of Granianus, and to Chrysippus, their son.
EDCS-08501124.summary: A funerary dedication to a wife and son by Victorinus.

EDCS-08501126.translation: ola for herself and C. Paanus, son of Fr[---].
EDCS-08501126.summary: A partial inscription likely recording a funerary dedication or endowment.

EDCS-08501127.translation: This monument will not pass to an heir for my daughter Vroia and son [or for my son Vroius and daughter].
EDCS-08501127.summary: This funerary inscription is a testament to the deceased's property and inheritance, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501129.translation: Primulus made this for his own [family] while he was alive.
EDCS-08501129.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Primulus for his family during his lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501130.translation: Of Primulus, son of Primus, 25 feet in the field, 12 feet in the front.
EDCS-08501130.summary: This inscription records land dimensions, likely associated with a property boundary or funerary plot, belonging to Primulus, son of Primus.

EDCS-08501131.translation: For Primula, daughter of Restitutus. [Height] V.
EDCS-08501131.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primula, daughter of Restitutus, with a measurement of five units, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501132.translation: For Sextus Quintius Sextianus, his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08501132.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a son to his father, Sextus Quintius Sextianus, highlighting filial piety, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501134.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Rutilius Magiacus, possibly funerary or dedicatory, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501136.translation: Of Sabina, daughter of Atilius. 26.5 feet in the front, 13 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501136.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land or a tomb belonging to Sabina, daughter of Atilius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501138.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Sappia, daughter of Gaius, Firmina.
EDCS-08501138.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sappia, daughter of Gaius, also known as Firmina, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501139.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Sapricia, an innocent soul. Marinus [erected this] to his wife and Marina to his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08501139.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Marinus to his wife Sapricia and by Marina to her mother Sapricia, emphasizing innocence and duty, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501140.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Saturnina Paterna Marcellina, daughter of Faustus. 40 feet in the front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-08501140.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturnina Paterna Marcellina, daughter of Faustus, including plot dimensions, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501141.translation: To Secundina, my wife, and A...
EDCS-08501141.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Secundina and another individual, with the recipient's name incomplete.

EDCS-08501143.translation: Pompeia Festa to her mother Severa...
EDCS-08501143.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Pompeia Festa to her mother Severa, with the mother's name possibly continuing.

EDCS-08501144.translation: To the divine shades, in memory of Severianus, most dutiful...
EDCS-08501144.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the memory of Severianus, described as most dutiful.

EDCS-08501145.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Sextiola's excellent father, the dedicator being implied.

EDCS-08501146.summary: This inscription likely records the fulfillment of vows by Taurus, possibly related to a construction project.

EDCS-08501147.translation: Sextinius Sorex has been buried here.
EDCS-08501147.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Sextinius Sorex.

EDCS-08501148.translation: To the divine shades, Veratianus dedicated to his most beloved wife Quintia Titia, freedwoman of Quintus, under the axe.
EDCS-08501148.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Veratianus to his wife Quintia Titia, indicating the burial was performed 'sub ascia' (under the axe), a ritualistic method.

EDCS-08501149.translation: In the front, 16.5 feet; in the field, 20 feet of Valentis Aticus.
EDCS-08501149.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to Valentis Aticus.

EDCS-08501150.translation: To the divine shades, Valerina, daughter of Valerius, [dedicated this] for herself and her family.
EDCS-08501150.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Valerina, daughter of Valerius, for herself and her kin.

EDCS-08501151.summary: This inscription appears to be an abbreviation related to a collegium or association, possibly indicating foundation or membership.

EDCS-08501153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Licinia Vera set this up for her dearest son, Marcus Valerius Severus.
EDCS-08501153.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son.

EDCS-08501154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Pompeia Demetria, dearest daughter.
EDCS-08501154.summary: A funerary epitaph for a daughter.

EDCS-08501155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Firmina made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-08501155.summary: A funerary epitaph made by Valeria Firmina for herself.

EDCS-08501156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextilianus and Valeria Vincentia set this up for their most devoted mother and incomparable wife, Valeria Severina.
EDCS-08501156.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband and child for their wife and mother.

EDCS-08501157.translation: Vallius Lyricus for himself and his family.
EDCS-08501157.summary: An epitaph for Vallius Lyricus and his family.

EDCS-08501158.summary: A dedication by Gaius V. R. to Vardigia.

EDCS-08501159.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Verina, daughter of Aventinus. Carintianus set this up for his wife, and Veratianus and Severiana for their mother.
EDCS-08501159.summary: A funerary epitaph for Verina, set up by her husband and children.

EDCS-08501160.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Verina, daughter of Verus.
EDCS-08501160.summary: A funerary epitaph for Verina, daughter of Verus.

EDCS-08501185.translation: For the son Ato and Tricce.
EDCS-08501185.summary: A dedication to Ato and Tricce, likely children.

EDCS-08501187.summary: A dedication by Sextus Co for VLLI OLI.

EDCS-08501205.translation: Rusticus made this according to his vow. My good-memory mother Stephania died on the 14th day before the Kalends of November, a Monday, on the 17th day of the moon. She lived for 46 years, during the consulship of Severus and Iordanus. Rusticus made this according to his vow.
EDCS-08501205.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Stephania, her age, the date, and the maker of the monument, Rusticus, likely dating to the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-08501206.translation: Pyminius of good memory rests in peace. He lived 25 years, 3 months, and 27 days. He departed on the Kalends of June, after the consulship of Veri Venantius, a most distinguished consul. Peace be with you forever.
EDCS-08501206.summary: This epitaph commemorates Pyminius, recording his age at death and the date of his passing, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08501207.translation: This distinguished inscription, no less worthy than his ancestors, when Pantagathus left the fragile use of life, preferred to commit his body to his own land rather than seek a place through prayers, if rest is to be sought from great patrons. The most holy Vincentius, with his peers and companions, seeks these approaches and protects the house and master, providing light from the light of truth from darkness. If you should happen to ask what service he rendered, and what good he accomplished, when placed in the light above, you will find that he gave just laws and sanctioned them with his judgments. For he was called guardian and ruler of the homeland and ruled the homeland with the freedom of spirit, generous to friends and sparing of himself, and faithful to his lords. First, as the order of life demands, his fame transmits him to the eternal world. Envious death took him from worldly affairs in the tenth lustrum. For after laying down the symbols of a senator, he spent his life.
EDCS-08501207.summary: This complex funerary epitaph praises Pantagathus and Vincentius, highlighting their civic virtues, piety, and service, likely dating to the late Roman period (4th-5th century CE).

EDCS-08501208.translation: Eripius the presbyter of holy memory rests in peace here. He lived 27 years, 10 months, and 18 days. He died on the 8th day before the Kalends of February, after the second consulship of Agapitus, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-08501208.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Eripius, a presbyter, including his age and date of death, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08501209.translation: Amicus the deacon of good memory rests in peace here. He lived 30 years. He died on the 3rd day before the Ides of January, after the second consulship of Paulinus the Younger, in the fourteenth indiction.
EDCS-08501209.summary: This epitaph records the death of Amicus, a deacon, his age, and the date of his passing, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08501210.summary: A simple epitaph or dedicatory inscription for Florentiole, likely from the 4th-6th century CE.

EDCS-08501211.translation: Neglicia, a Christian woman, peace be with you.
EDCS-08501211.summary: A short epitaph for Neglicia, identifying her as Christian, likely from the 4th-6th century CE.

EDCS-08501212.translation: Papitus of good memory rests in peace here. He lived 50 years and 8 months. He died on the fourth day before the Kalends of August. NB BS CONI COSVC [This part is unclear and likely contains abbreviations or corruptions].
EDCS-08501212.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Papitus, noting his age and the date of his death, with unclear abbreviations, likely from the 4th-6th century CE.

EDCS-08501213.translation: Pelagius the presbyter rests in peace here. He lived [age missing] years. He died [date missing].
EDCS-08501213.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the name and office of Pelagius, a presbyter, but lacks details of his lifespan or death date, likely from the 4th-6th century CE.

EDCS-08501214.translation: Stafili, peace be with you in God. Hail, farewell.
EDCS-08501214.summary: A brief epitaph or farewell message for Stafili, invoking God's peace, likely from the 4th-6th century CE.

EDCS-08501215.summary: This is a personal inscription, possibly funerary or a blessing, addressed to Susomine.

EDCS-08501216.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Teto, associated with Concordia.

EDCS-08501218.translation: To Titulus, of two brothers, the genuine ones.
EDCS-08501218.summary: This is a dedicatory or funerary inscription honoring Titulus, described as genuine among two brothers.

EDCS-08501223.translation: To the divine shades of Titus Viriatius Temporinus, aedile of the Vocontii, and Viriatia Pia. Titus Viriatius Priscus, senator of the Vocontii, and Sextus Viriatius Sequens, the heirs.
EDCS-08501223.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by heirs for Titus Viriatius Temporinus, an aedile, and Viriatia Pia, likely his family members, from the Vocontii tribe.

EDCS-08501225.translation: To the divine shades of Viator, son of Sabinus, for Julia Maria, his dearest wife, set up by her husband.
EDCS-08501225.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a husband for his beloved wife, Julia Maria.

EDCS-08501226.translation: Us Tacitus paid his vow willingly and deservedly with his own money.
EDCS-08501226.summary: This is a votive inscription where Tacitus fulfilled a vow made with his own funds.

EDCS-08501230.translation: To Silvanus, Gaius Julius Thallus, according to his vow.
EDCS-08501230.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Gaius Julius Thallus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501231.translation: Publius Julius Gratus restored (this) according to his vow.
EDCS-08501231.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the restoration of something by Publius Julius Gratus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501233.translation: Claudius Postumus Dardanus, a distinguished man of patrician rank, former consular governor of the province of Vienne, former master of the office of petitions, former quaestor, former praetorian prefect of the Gauls, and Nevia Galla, a most distinguished and illustrious woman, matron of the family, have provided for public use the roads by cutting the sides of both mountains, and they have given walls and gates, which they wanted to be established on their own land for the protection of all. With the assistance also of the distinguished Claudius Lepidus, count and brother of the aforementioned man, former consular governor of Germania Prima, former master of the memorial office, former count of the privy purse, so that their zeal for the safety of all and a public inscription of their devotion might be shown.
EDCS-08501233.summary: This is a monumental inscription recording the public works (roads, walls, gates) provided by Claudius Postumus Dardanus and Nevia Galla, with the help of Claudius Lepidus, to benefit the public, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-08501234.translation: To Fortuna, Hedone Marina, a freedwoman, paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501234.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Fortuna by Hedone Marina, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08501235.translation: Sextus Marius Montanus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08501235.summary: This is a votive inscription from Sextus Marius Montanus to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501238.translation: To the departed spirits of Quinctus Caetronius, son of Quinctus, of the Voltinian tribe, Titullus the veteran of the VI praetorian cohort, former chief magistrate, pontiff of the colony of Ariminum, prefect of the pagus Epoti, flamen of Augustus, and curator of public works, his heirs, in accordance with his will, dedicated this to the August Goddess Vocontiorum.
EDCS-08501238.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the dedication by the heirs of Titullus, a prominent local official and veteran, to the August Goddess Vocontiorum.

EDCS-08501239.translation: Here rests in good memory John, a most distinguished man, who lived fifty years, in the second indiction.
EDCS-08501239.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and resting place of a distinguished man named John, likely from the Late Roman or Early Christian period.

EDCS-08501240.translation: Pompeia Lucilla gladly and deservedly paid her vow to Allobrogus.
EDCS-08501240.summary: This is a votive inscription from Pompeia Lucilla to the god Allobrogus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501241.translation: Cornelia Materna gladly and deservedly paid her vow to Isis.
EDCS-08501241.summary: This is a votive inscription from Cornelia Materna to the goddess Isis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501243.translation: Sextus Marius Montanus paid his vow to Mars.
EDCS-08501243.summary: This is a votive inscription from Sextus Marius Montanus to the god Mars, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501244.translation: Marcus Iulius Maternianus [dedicated this] in accordance with his vow to the Unconquered Sun God.
EDCS-08501244.summary: This is a votive inscription from Marcus Iulius Maternianus to the Unconquered Sun God (Sol Invictus), likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501245.translation: Decimus Roccius Titullus paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08501245.summary: This is a votive inscription from Decimus Roccius Titullus to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501246.translation: Victor Vitalis, son of Victor, gladly and deservedly gave this offering to Victory Augusta.
EDCS-08501246.summary: This is a dedication from Victor Vitalis to the goddess Victoria Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501247.translation: Caius Lucceius Apolaustus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Novatianus, while he was safe.
EDCS-08501247.summary: This is a votive inscription from Caius Lucceius Apolaustus to Novatianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501248.translation: Erus gladly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-08501248.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Erus, likely in thanks to a deity.

EDCS-08501255.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Attilia Verina and Vassata and Avita, parents.
EDCS-08501255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Attilia Verina, Vassata, and Avita, set up by their parents.

EDCS-08501256.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Aelia Fortunata, mother, arranged for this to be set up for Baebia Gratiana.
EDCS-08501256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia Gratiana, erected by her mother Aelia Fortunata.

EDCS-08501257.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Titus Valentinius Messianus, alive, for Iulia Marcina.
EDCS-08501257.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Marcina, set up by Titus Valentinius Messianus during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501258.translation: To the Divine Spirits and for eternal memory: Servata, daughter of Catullus, for her most dutiful son Paternus Paulus, and for her husband Lucius Eppius Fortunatus, set up while alive.
EDCS-08501258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Paternus Paulus and Lucius Eppius Fortunatus, set up by Servata, wife and mother.

EDCS-08501259.translation: Victorinus, son of Mogetus, and Marina, freedwoman, heir.
EDCS-08501259.summary: This inscription appears to record an inheritance or a funerary dedication involving Victorinus, his father Mogetus, and Marina.

EDCS-08501262.translation: Erus gladly fulfilled his vow to Mars Augustus, as was deserved.
EDCS-08501262.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Erus to Mars Augustus.

EDCS-08501264.translation: Lucius Maternius Optatus, sacred to Vulcan Augustus.
EDCS-08501264.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Lucius Maternius Optatus to the god Vulcan Augustus.

EDCS-08501265.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Urbanicius the abbot, noble by birth, but blessed by deeds, chaste, sober, kind.
EDCS-08501265.summary: This is a funerary inscription praising the deceased Urbanicius, an abbot, highlighting his virtues.

EDCS-08501268.translation: To the August Goddess Andarta: Lucius Carisius Serenus, one of the six priests of Augustus, gladly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-08501268.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Lucius Carisius Serenus, a local magistrate, to the goddess Andarta.

EDCS-08501269.translation: To the August Goddess Andarta, Titus Dexius Zosimus.
EDCS-08501269.summary: An offering to the goddess Andarta by Titus Dexius Zosimus.

EDCS-08501270.translation: To the August Goddess Andarta, Quintus Iulius Antoninus; to the August Goddess Andarta, Marcus Iulius Theodorus.
EDCS-08501270.summary: Offerings to the goddess Andarta by Quintus Iulius Antoninus and Marcus Iulius Theodorus.

EDCS-08501271.translation: To the Goddess Andarta Augusta, Sextus Plutatius Paternus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08501271.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Andarta Augusta by Sextus Plutatius Paternus.

EDCS-08501272.translation: To the August Goddess Andarta, Marcus Pompeius Primitivus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08501272.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Andarta Augusta by Marcus Pompeius Primitivus.

EDCS-08501273.translation: To Bormanus and Bormana, Publius Sappinius Eusebes willingly fulfilled his vow as was fitting.
EDCS-08501273.summary: A vow fulfilled to the gods Bormanus and Bormana by Publius Sappinius Eusebes.

EDCS-08501274.translation: To Queen Isis, Birria Secundilla, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08501274.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Isis Regina by Birria Secundilla.

EDCS-08501275.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Epaphroditus, freedman of Caius Venaesius Hermagoras and Venaesia Attica, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08501275.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Epaphroditus, a freedman.

EDCS-08501276.translation: Cinna, as aedile for the second time, gives from his own means to the Lares.
EDCS-08501276.summary: An offering to the Lares by Cinna, who was aedile for the second time.

EDCS-08501277.translation: Verri Albini Evecus set up this monument in fulfillment of his vow to Mars Masucicus.
EDCS-08501277.summary: A monument dedicated to Mars Masucicus by Verri Albini Evecus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501278.translation: The curators erected (this) for the August God Mars.
EDCS-08501278.summary: An erection for the god Mars Augustus, commissioned by the curators.

EDCS-08501282.translation: Novellus Iovincati willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-08501282.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501283.translation: Masidia Ingenua willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08501283.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501284.translation: Paternus' freedman, according to his vow, to Vulcan.
EDCS-08501284.summary: This is a votive inscription to Vulcan, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501285.summary: This is a dedication or identification of an individual, R. Pompeius Lucus, who served as a priest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501286.summary: This inscription likely identifies Statilius Carpophorus, possibly in relation to the imperial cult, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501288.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Titus, beneficiary, made this for his friend Valentinius Fortunatus, soldier of the Ist Legion Minervia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-08501288.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a soldier of the Ist Legion Minervia, erected by a colleague, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501289.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ambidavus, flamen of the deified Augusti, made this for his family and himself while alive.
EDCS-08501289.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a priest of the imperial cult, who set it up for himself and his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501290.summary: This inscription identifies Felix as the prefect of the Vocontii, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08501292.translation: Lucius Iulius Iulianus made this for his dearest father, Lucius Iulius Cladaeus, Augustalis seviral, and for his mother, Iulia Carpima, while alive.
EDCS-08501292.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a son for his parents, including an Augustalis seviral, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501293.translation: Marcus Iulius Regulus, freedman and heir, [made this] for his excellent patron, Marcus Iulius Theodorus, Augustalis seviral of the goddess Augusta of the Vocontii.
EDCS-08501293.summary: This is an epitaph honoring a patron who was an Augustalis seviral, set up by his freedman and heir, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501295.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Venaesia Euthychis, patroness and heir, made [this] for Caius Venaesius Fortunatus, one of the six priests of the Augustan cult.
EDCS-08501295.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Venaesius Fortunatus, set up by Venaesia Euthychis.

EDCS-08501296.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-08501297.translation: For Sextus Vencius Iuventianus, priest of the deified Augustus, also priest and curator of the Villian gladiatorial games, who was appointed to the curia of the people of Lugudunum in the name of citizenship, by the most splendid order of that city, the order of the Vocontii, by common consent and petition of the people, on account of his exceptional generosity in presenting public shows.
EDCS-08501297.summary: This is an honorary inscription from the Vocontii people of Lugudunum honoring Sextus Vencius Iuventianus for his generosity in sponsoring gladiatorial games.

EDCS-08501304.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerianus the scribe made [this] for his dearest wife Marcellina and for himself while alive.
EDCS-08501304.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcellina, made by her husband Valerianus, who also provided for himself.

EDCS-08501305.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camasius the butcher made [this] for his dearest wife Martina.
EDCS-08501305.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Martina, made by her husband Camasius.

EDCS-08501306.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Iphigenia, perfumer, lived 9 years, 11 months, and 23 days, and [they] made [this] for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08501306.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Pompeia Iphigenia, a perfumer, who died young, and for whom her family provided the tomb.

EDCS-08501307.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Myron, slave of the Vocontii, and Verina for their dearest son Severus, who lived 1 year and 10 days.
EDCS-08501307.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Severus, the son of Myron (a slave of the Vocontii) and Verina.

EDCS-08501308.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Silvanus the secutor. By Valerius Proculus.
EDCS-08501308.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Silvanus, a secutor (type of gladiator), made by Valerius Proculus.

EDCS-08501309.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Verus Severianus, a banker, aged 29. Severinus and Iulia, most unhappy parents, made [this] for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08501309.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Verus Severianus, a 29-year-old banker, made by his grieving parents who also provided for themselves.

EDCS-08501310.translation: [---]us, slave of the Vocontii, made [this] for himself and his family.
EDCS-08501310.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a slave of the Vocontii and his family, likely made by himself.

EDCS-08501313.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Achilleus the slave.
EDCS-08501313.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a slave named Achilleus.

EDCS-08501314.translation: Titus Admatius Verinus arranged for this to be made in accordance with his will.
EDCS-08501314.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Titus Admatius Verinus arranged for the monument to be built according to his will.

EDCS-08501315.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Alleticia Trophime made this, while alive, for her foster-daughter Alleticia Doridis and for herself.
EDCS-08501315.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Alleticia Trophime for her foster-daughter and herself.

EDCS-08501317.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Livius Docimus and Atilia Stasime, parents, for their most devoted son, Gaius Atilius Decuminus.
EDCS-08501317.summary: A funerary inscription from parents for their son, Gaius Atilius Decuminus.

EDCS-08501318.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Chrysis made this, while alive, for her dearest husband, Lucius Atilius Dionysus, and for herself.
EDCS-08501318.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Iulia Chrysis for her husband and herself.

EDCS-08501320.translation: Porcius Uderulus for his mother, Aventina, daughter of Carinus.
EDCS-08501320.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Porcius Uderulus for his mother, Aventina.

EDCS-08501322.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Acne made this, while alive, for his dearest son, Carinianus Valerianus, aged 15, and for himself.
EDCS-08501322.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Acne for his son, Carinianus Valerianus, and himself.

EDCS-08501323.translation: Coelia Marcellina for her son, Paternianus, aged 17.
EDCS-08501323.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Coelia Marcellina for her son, Paternianus.

EDCS-08501324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Philumene for her dearest son, Charmides, freedman of Attia, aged 18.
EDCS-08501324.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Philumene for her son, Charmides.

EDCS-08501325.translation: Freedman of Gaius Laelius Atticus, tax collector of the hundredth tax.
EDCS-08501325.summary: An inscription identifying an individual as a freedman of Gaius Laelius Atticus, a tax collector.

EDCS-08501326.translation: Titus Coelius Asiaticus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-08501326.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Coelius Asiaticus to himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501327.translation: Cuncius Cinnamus and his wife Baburia Fuscina made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08501327.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Cuncius Cinnamus and his wife Baburia Fuscina to themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-08501328.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Marcus Eutychius Sabinianus, Eutychia Sabina [dedicated this] to her most excellent father.
EDCS-08501328.summary: This funerary inscription records Eutychia Sabina's dedication to her father, Marcus Eutychius Sabinianus.

EDCS-08501329.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Faustinus Homuncio, his daughter Carilla dedicated [this] to her father.
EDCS-08501329.summary: This funerary inscription records Carilla's dedication to her father, Faustinus Homuncio.

EDCS-08501330.translation: [dedicated by] the son of Felicissimus to his brother.
EDCS-08501330.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication from a brother to his deceased brother.

EDCS-08501332.translation: Fortunia [dedicated this] to the most dutiful DR.
EDCS-08501332.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication by Fortunia to a person referred to as DR.

EDCS-08501333.translation: Gratilla, daughter of Gratinus Mercator, [dedicated this] to her dearest brother.
EDCS-08501333.summary: This funerary inscription records Gratilla's dedication to her brother.

EDCS-08501335.translation: While alive, Iulianusolanus, a physician of the Vocontii, made this cinerary urn for himself.
EDCS-08501335.summary: This is a funerary monument, likely a cinerary urn, commissioned by Iulianusolanus, a physician, for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501336.translation: [dedicated by] Vilius Ianuarius to his most excellent wife, Flamina.
EDCS-08501336.summary: This funerary inscription records Vilius Ianuarius' dedication to his wife, Flamina.

EDCS-08501337.translation: Iullus and Marcella [dedicated this] to their son Ianuarius.
EDCS-08501337.summary: This funerary inscription records Iullus and Marcella's dedication to their son, Ianuarius.

EDCS-08501338.translation: Ioventio, daughter of Adrumetus, Verecunda, daughter of Vercatus, his wife, made this while alive for herself.
EDCS-08501338.summary: This is a funerary monument made by a wife for herself and her husband, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501339.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Iulianus, son of Verina, and for his wife Glycera. Verina, their daughter, made this for her most excellent parents while alive.
EDCS-08501339.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a daughter for her deceased parents, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501340.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Iulius and Iulianus.
EDCS-08501340.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to two individuals, likely family members, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501341.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Lucius Iulius Onesimus. Iulia Secundina made this for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-08501341.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501342.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Paternus and Iulia Pompeia. Iulius Paezon and Iulia Paternilla, their most dutiful children, made this for their dearest parents.
EDCS-08501342.summary: This funerary inscription records children setting up a monument for their parents, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501343.translation: For Quintus Iulius Quadratus, son of Quintus. Quintus Iulius Florus made this for his father.
EDCS-08501343.summary: This funerary inscription records a son setting up a monument for his deceased father, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501344.translation: For Julius Veinus. Iulius Verinus made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-08501344.summary: This funerary inscription records a son setting up a monument for his deceased father, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501346.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Iulia, daughter of Iullinus, and for Iullinus Victorinus, her son.
EDCS-08501346.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates the monument to a mother and son, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501347.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Iulia Acme, who lived 27 years. Caius Iulius Asclepiades made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-08501347.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband setting up a monument for his wife, including her age at death, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501348.translation: ... and Iulia Carina, parents, made this for their children and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08501348.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment where parents are dedicating a monument for themselves and their children, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501349.translation: Gaius Venaesius Asclepiodorus made this for his incomparable wife Iulia Eutychia and for himself while alive.
EDCS-08501349.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Venaesius Asclepiodorus for his wife Iulia Eutychia and himself.

EDCS-08501351.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Sextus Pompeius Eutyches for his wife Iulia Philumene and Iulia Calinice for her most devoted freedwoman.
EDCS-08501351.summary: This inscription is a joint dedication to the spirits of the underworld by Sextus Pompeius Eutyches for his wife Iulia Philumene and Iulia Calinice for her freedwoman.

EDCS-08501352.translation: Titus Iulius Agathangelus for his dearest wife Iulia Servanda.
EDCS-08501352.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Titus Iulius Agathangelus for his wife Iulia Servanda.

EDCS-08501353.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: His mother made this for Marcella and for Secundinus, son of Papius.
EDCS-08501353.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her children, Marcella and Secundinus.

EDCS-08501355.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Verus and Severus for their father Caius Mellinius Secundinus.
EDCS-08501355.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld by Verus and Severus for their father, Caius Mellinius Secundinus.

EDCS-08501356.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Mellinius Alexander and Valeria Martina for their dearest son Mellinius Severianus.
EDCS-08501356.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the underworld by Mellinius Alexander and Valeria Martina for their son, Mellinius Severianus.

EDCS-08501357.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Gratilla, daughter, for Mercator, son of Porcius.
EDCS-08501357.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld by Gratilla for Mercator.

EDCS-08501358.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Severia Vitalis for her husband Marcus Numisius Primus.
EDCS-08501358.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Severia Vitalis for her husband Marcus Numisius Primus.

EDCS-08501359.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: [DO] for Oporinus, her companion in barracks.
EDCS-08501359.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld by an unnamed individual for their companion in barracks, Oporinus.

EDCS-08501360.translation: Quintus Iulius Dioscorus for his companion in barracks.
EDCS-08501360.summary: This inscription records Quintus Iulius Dioscorus dedicating something for his companion in barracks.

EDCS-08501361.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcellus Mercatoris, son of Marcellus, [erected this] to his mother and brother.
EDCS-08501361.summary: This funerary inscription records a son dedicating a monument to his mother and brother.

EDCS-08501362.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Pompeius Hermeros, a devoted father, had lost his son Lucius Pompeius Faustinus, aged 20, after the death of his brother Severianus.
EDCS-08501362.summary: This funerary inscription records a father mourning his son who died after his brother.

EDCS-08501363.translation: Pompeia, daughter of Titus, aged 26. Pompeius V[erecundus?].
EDCS-08501363.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Pompeia, daughter of Titus, aged 26.

EDCS-08501365.translation: [This monument is for] Aemmius Pothinus, to his most holy wife.
EDCS-08501365.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband dedicating a monument to his wife.

EDCS-08501366.translation: To the Divine Manes. Euhemer and Philocyrius, sons of Primanius.
EDCS-08501366.summary: This funerary inscription records two sons dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-08501367.translation: To the Divine Manes. Atisia Paulina [erected this] to her most excellent husband, Marcus Primus Messoris.
EDCS-08501367.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife dedicating a monument to her husband.

EDCS-08501368.translation: Loes, Cnaeus, his freedman Zelus, and his freedwoman Prisca.
EDCS-08501368.summary: This inscription lists individuals, likely freedmen and freedwomen, possibly associated with a household or family.

EDCS-08501371.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pusinna, wife of Publicus Nitoris.
EDCS-08501371.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife dedicated to the Divine Manes by her husband.

EDCS-08501372.translation: To the Divine Manes. Paternus, a father, [erected this] to his son Pupi Paternus.
EDCS-08501372.summary: This funerary inscription records a father dedicating a monument to his son.

EDCS-08501373.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quarti, daughter of Quartinus.
EDCS-08501373.summary: This funerary inscription records a daughter dedicated to the Divine Manes by her father.

EDCS-08501374.translation: Voltinia, daughter of Sextus, to her father, Quintilla, to her husband.
EDCS-08501374.summary: This epitaph records the dedication by Quintilla, daughter of Sextus and belonging to the Voltinian tribe, to her father and husband.

EDCS-08501375.translation: Decimus, of the Romani tribe, to Verulus, of the Romania tribe, a freedman, to his patron.
EDCS-08501375.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Decimus, a freedman belonging to the Romani tribe, to his patron Verulus, of the Romania tribe.

EDCS-08501376.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Mercatilla, daughter of Secundinus, to her dearest husband, Saturninus, son of Secundinus.
EDCS-08501376.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Mercatilla to her husband Saturninus, both children of a Secundinus.

EDCS-08501377.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Pompeia Servatilla to her dearest son, Servatus, son of Secundinus.
EDCS-08501377.summary: This funerary inscription records Pompeia Servatilla dedicating a monument to her son, Servatus.

EDCS-08501379.translation: Maturus, son of Solitus, and Temporina made this for their father.
EDCS-08501379.summary: This inscription records Maturus and Temporina dedicating a monument to their father.

EDCS-08501380.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Sollius Calendio, by nationality a Rei, dedicated this to his dearest wife Sollia Sabina, and consecrated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501380.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Lucius Sollius Calendio, of the Rei ethnicity, to his wife Sollia Sabina, with the special rite 'sub ascia'.

EDCS-08501381.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Vera to her excellent father, Successus Vitalis, son of Vitalis.
EDCS-08501381.summary: This funerary inscription records Vera dedicating a monument to her father, Successus Vitalis.

EDCS-08501382.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Mercator, son of Secundus, and Victorina, daughter of Temporinus, to their most dutiful son, Valerius.
EDCS-08501382.summary: This funerary inscription records Mercator and Victorina dedicating a monument to their son, Valerius.

EDCS-08501383.translation: Caius Pompeius Valerianus to his wife, Valeria.
EDCS-08501383.summary: This inscription records Caius Pompeius Valerianus dedicating a monument to his wife, Valeria.

EDCS-08501384.translation: Aventina Secundiae, daughter of Secundus, to her daughter, Valeria, daughter of Valentinus.
EDCS-08501384.summary: This inscription records Aventina Secundiae dedicating a monument to her daughter, Valeria.

EDCS-08501385.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Martinus, her brother, made this tomb for Valeria, daughter of Valerius, in accordance with her will.
EDCS-08501385.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a brother for his sister, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501386.translation: For Valeria Vera, deceased at six years of age. Lucius Valerius Veratianus, for his incomparable daughter.
EDCS-08501386.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl, likely dating to the Imperial period, set up by her father.

EDCS-08501387.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vera, her mother, for her most excellent son, Veratius Albinus.
EDCS-08501387.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a mother for her son, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501388.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Veratius Verinus, son of Marcellus, most dutiful, and for himself, erected during his lifetime.
EDCS-08501388.summary: A funerary monument erected by a man for himself and his son, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501390.translation: For Aes, his most dutiful daughter, for Verinus, his dearest sister, and for himself, erected during his lifetime, deceased at twenty-two years of age.
EDCS-08501390.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to a daughter and sister, also for the dedicator and his sister, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501391.translation: For Verini Pupi, son of Pupi. Vera Pupi, daughter of Pupi, for her dearest brother.
EDCS-08501391.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a sister for her brother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501392.translation: To the spirits of the departed and for security. Quintus Verrius Bassus, during his lifetime, made this for himself and for his daughter Carilla.
EDCS-08501392.summary: A funerary monument erected by a man for himself and his daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501393.translation: The ground of burial for Sextus Vervinius Lepidus, measuring sixty feet in length and sixty feet in width.
