EDCS-08501393.summary: A boundary marker for a burial plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501395.translation: To Victoria, to Victorina, to Victor.
EDCS-08501395.summary: A dedication to three individuals, likely part of a larger funerary or honorary monument, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501396.translation: To the spirits of the departed and for the prosperity of Urbicilla. Iulius Asclepiades, for his dearest and most dutiful wife, and for himself, erected during his lifetime.
EDCS-08501396.summary: A funerary monument erected by a husband for his wife and himself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501397.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Vrittia Primigenia.
EDCS-08501397.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Vrittia Primigenia.

EDCS-08501399.translation: To Gaius Julius Icarus, his father Gaius Julius Isidorus dedicated me, his young son, and this modest tomb; this house will forever cover us both. The father dedicated his child, fashioned a stone tomb for both.
EDCS-08501399.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a father for his son, Gaius Julius Icarus, and himself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501403.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Vana, to her most beloved husband Quintus Iulius Ic and to herself, made while alive.
EDCS-08501403.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Vana for her husband Quintus Iulius Ic and herself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501415.translation: Here Dalmata, redeemed by Christ's death, rests in peace and joyfully awaits the day of future judgment, with the saints interceding.
EDCS-08501415.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the death and burial of Dalmata, a redeemed individual awaiting judgment.

EDCS-08501416.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Desiderius the deacon, who lived 31 years, died on the 3rd day before the Ides of April, in the 12th indiction.
EDCS-08501416.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for Desiderius, a deacon, giving his age at death and the date of his passing.

EDCS-08501418.translation: An area 33 feet wide. Valeria Secundina, according to her vow. Place for a monument. ON.
EDCS-08501418.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot purchased by Valeria Secundina according to a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501420.translation: Sabinus Coelius paid his vow to the Matres, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501420.summary: This is a dedication recording Sabinus Coelius fulfilling a vow to the Matres, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501421.translation: To the spirits of the departed and eternal memory, for Severiola, incomparable wife, RAT placed this monument.
EDCS-08501421.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Severiola, placed by her husband RAT, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501423.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gellius Paulina, Lucius Caresius Severianus made this for his most excellent wife and for himself while alive.
EDCS-08501423.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Caresius Severianus for his wife Gellius Paulina and himself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501424.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Quintus Julius Quintianus, son of Quintilianus, deceased at the age of 2 years, 7 months, and 2 days, Julius Quintilianus and Attia Avita to their sweetest and most missed son at all hours.
EDCS-08501424.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents Julius Quintilianus and Attia Avita for their young son Quintus Julius Quintianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08501427.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Rufia, daughter of Suegius, daughter of Cai, paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501427.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Mercury by Rufia, daughter of Suegius.

EDCS-08501430.translation: To all the gods. Nonnus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501430.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to all the gods by Nonnus.

EDCS-08501432.translation: To Lucius Voturius Maximus, aedile of the Aletani district, son of Cai Voturius Avitus.
EDCS-08501432.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Voturius Maximus, an aedile of the Aletani district.

EDCS-08501433.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Catia Paternus, deceased, aged 5 years, 2 months, and 19 days. Sextus Pat[...]
EDCS-08501433.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Catia Paternus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-08501434.translation: To the Mother Goddess. Marcus Lusius Rhodon paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501434.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the Mother Goddess by Marcus Lusius Rhodon.

EDCS-08501435.translation: Of Spartus, son of Cassilius, of Ritucai, wife.
EDCS-08501435.summary: This inscription likely designates ownership or burial place for Spartus and his wife.

EDCS-08501436.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Verilla set up and dedicated this monument, marked by an axe, for her husband Tertinius Maximianus.
EDCS-08501436.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Verilla to her husband, Tertinius Maximianus, with the monument marked by an axe.

EDCS-08501437.translation: Sextus Sergius Tiro returned his vow to the Mother Goddess willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08501437.summary: This inscription is a votive offering returned to the Mother Goddess by Sextus Sergius Tiro.

EDCS-08501438.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Julius Felix for his dearest wife Verilla, daughter of Veratus Flavianus.
EDCS-08501438.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius Felix to his wife, Verilla.

EDCS-08501439.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Atticianus and Maternatius for their excellent father Atticinus, son of Aventinus.
EDCS-08501439.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Atticianus and Maternatius to their father, Atticinus.

EDCS-08501440.translation: To the Conservatrices, Volusius Valerianus, praetor of the Vocontii.
EDCS-08501440.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Conservatrices' by Volusius Valerianus, who held the office of praetor for the Vocontii tribe.

EDCS-08501441.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal rest of Marcus Primanius Iulianus, deceased at the age of 3 years, 11 months, and 10 days, Marcus Primanius and his mother Iulia Veratia [dedicated this].
EDCS-08501441.summary: This epitaph records the untimely death of a young child, Marcus Primanius Iulianus, and was dedicated by his father and mother.

EDCS-08501442.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Iulius Servatus [dedicated this].
EDCS-08501442.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Iulius Servatus.

EDCS-08501443.translation: Cnaeus Iulius made this for himself, alive, as a most dutiful son of Dionysius.
EDCS-08501443.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected during the lifetime of Cnaeus Iulius, in honour of his father Dionysius.

EDCS-08501444.translation: Frontia Marciane, daughter [of...], and her son-in-law Claudius Primanus arranged for this to be set up and dedicated it under the axe for the Divine Shades and eternal rest of Segudia Maximilla.
EDCS-08501444.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated to Segudia Maximilla, was set up and consecrated by her daughter and son-in-law.

EDCS-08501446.translation: Here lie in good peace, Rodanics II. In this tomb lies Ferreola, who died in good peace.
EDCS-08501446.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Rodanics II and Ferreola, who are described as resting in peace.

EDCS-08501448.translation: Pupa, companion of Vitalis, son of Quartonis, made this for herself and her husband while alive.
EDCS-08501448.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Pupa, the companion of Vitalis, for herself and her husband.

EDCS-08501449.translation: To the Divine Shades, Cassius Severianus and Vettia Matronilla, parents [of this infant].
EDCS-08501449.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Shades for an infant, set up by its parents Cassius Severianus and Vettia Matronilla.

EDCS-08501451.translation: Iulia Secunda gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to her mother.
EDCS-08501451.summary: This inscription records Iulia Secunda fulfilling a vow to her mother.

EDCS-08501453.translation: To the Divine Shades, Aemilia Exorata, daughter of Sextus and wife of Gaius Cornaiius Sedulus, [dedicated this] to her most excellent husband.
EDCS-08501453.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Cornaiius Sedulus, set up by his wife Aemilia Exorata.

EDCS-08501454.summary: This is a dedication from a mother to her daughter.

EDCS-08501455.summary: This is a dedication to the son of Primus.

EDCS-08501456.translation: To the Divine Shades. The heir arranged for the monument to be made for Rutilius Calvus, a veteran of the Third Legion.
EDCS-08501456.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the heir for Rutilius Calvus, a veteran of the Third Legion.

EDCS-08501458.translation: Baebia Eroe made this monument for her relatives.
EDCS-08501458.summary: This monument was made by Baebia Eroe for her own family members.

EDCS-08501459.summary: This marks a monument dedicated to Lucius Antonius.

EDCS-08501460.summary: This epitaph indicates that a veteran is buried here.

EDCS-08501461.translation: Gaius Julius Pastor made this while alive for his wife, Tertulla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-08501461.summary: This is a funerary monument built by Gaius Julius Pastor for his wife, Tertulla, during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501462.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Severius Julianus [dedicated this] to his most dutiful mother, Suaduilia Iulianena.
EDCS-08501462.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gaius Severius Julianus to his mother, Suaduilia Iulianena.

EDCS-08501464.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Aucius Macrinus.
EDCS-08501464.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Aucius Macrinus.

EDCS-08501467.translation: To Mercury. Cassius and his companions willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-08501467.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Cassius and others to the god Mercury.

EDCS-08501470.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and to the eternal memory of Petronius Castus, a veteran honorably discharged from the First Minervian Legion, formerly optio to a procurator of 200,000 sesterces, and of his wife Vitalinia Flora; they had this erected for themselves while alive and dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501470.summary: This funerary epitaph honors a veteran and his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501471.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Pius, son of Tiberius, of the Galeria tribe, a decurion of Lugudunum, honored with the rank of duumvir, who died at Rome while on a legation; Claudius Spendon and Cestia C, his parents, for their son Tiberius.
EDCS-08501471.summary: This funerary dedication is for Tiberius Claudius Pius, a local magistrate from Lugudunum, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501472.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Quintus Julius Aper, a citizen of Lugudunum, a Sexvir [of the city], who lived 17 years, 9 months, and 29 days, his body rests here; the mausoleum was made by his mother Vindauscia Euvanelis for her dearest son and dedicated beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501472.summary: This epitaph records the death of a young man, Quintus Julius Aper, and was erected by his mother to form a mausoleum, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501474.translation: Marcus Junius Secundus, Caius Valerius Terentianus, Caius Valerius Decuminus, and Quarta Sextilla gave these steps from their own funds.
EDCS-08501474.summary: This inscription records a donation of steps by four individuals, likely for a public building or monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501475.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Lucius Liberalinius Florentinus, a taster; Sergius Liberalinius Gallicanus erected this for his most dutiful father beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501475.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Lucius Liberalinius Florentinus, a 'taster', and was erected by his son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501476.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Valentinus VIBLIC[---], son of C. VA[---]nus and Attia Victorina, their most dutiful son, who lived 21 years, 5 months, and 8 days; they dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501476.summary: This epitaph commemorates Valentinus, son of VIBLIC[---] and Attia Victorina, and was erected by his parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501477.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Verulia Aventina Vocontia, to her incomparable wife.
EDCS-08501477.summary: This funerary dedication is from Marcus Barbarius Perpetuus to his wife Verulia Aventina Vocontia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501478.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Lucius Aelius Euelpistianus, who lived 21 years, 11 months; Aelius Euelpistus made this for his dearest son and for himself and his descendants, and dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501478.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Aelius Euelpistianus and was erected by his father for him, himself, and descendants, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501480.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and to the memory of Crescentina, aged 5 years and 7 days; Lucius Albanius Achillaeus [erected this] for his foster-child.
EDCS-08501480.summary: This funerary dedication commemorates a young child, Crescentina, and was erected by her foster-father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501481.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Sextus Decidius Hilarus, who lived 20 years, 5 months, and 10 days; his brothers Decidius Hilarianus and Hilarus, and his sister and heir Iulia Vitellia, made this according to his will and dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-08501481.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by the brothers and sister of Sextus Decidius Hilarus, honoring his memory and following his testamentary instructions, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501482.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Donnia Quintilla and her son Cassius Valentinus, who lived 16 years, 6 months, and 12 days. Cassius Valerius dedicated [this monument] to his dearest wife and most dutiful son, under the axe.
EDCS-08501482.summary: A funerary dedication by Cassius Valerius for his wife Donnia Quintilla and son Cassius Valentinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501484.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iulius Maternus.
EDCS-08501484.summary: A funerary dedication to Iulius Maternus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501485.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Lucius Iulius Maternus. Valeria Materna [dedicated this] for herself and Aelius Achillis.
EDCS-08501485.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Iulius Maternus, also mentioning Valeria Materna and Aelius Achillis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501486.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iulia Decumilla. Titus Iulius Paternus made [this monument] for his dearest wife and for himself, and dedicated it under the axe while alive.
EDCS-08501486.summary: A funerary dedication made by Titus Iulius Paternus for his wife Iulia Decumilla and himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501487.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iulia Marcella. Lucius Severius Severianus [dedicated this] to his dearest wife. Tiberius Severius.
EDCS-08501487.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Severius Severianus for his wife Iulia Marcella, also mentioning Tiberius Severius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501488.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iulia Marullina. Quintus Saedius Athamallus dedicated [this monument] to his dearest wife under the axe.
EDCS-08501488.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Saedius Athamallus for his wife Iulia Marullina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501489.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld and to the memory of Iulia Valeria. Mustius Iulianus took care to have [this monument] made for his excellent wife, and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501489.summary: A funerary dedication commissioned by Mustius Iulianus for his wife Iulia Valeria, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501490.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Iustina.
EDCS-08501490.summary: A funerary dedication to Iustina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501491.translation: For Titus Pompeius Hilarus, freedman of Veriuccus. Titus Pompeius Bassus for his father and himself.
EDCS-08501491.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Pompeius Bassus for his freedman Titus Pompeius Hilarus and himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501492.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Titus Pompeius Severus. Titus Pompeius Primitivus and Valeria Severina dedicated [this monument] to their sweetest son, under the axe.
EDCS-08501492.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Pompeius Primitivus and Valeria Severina for their son Titus Pompeius Severus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501493.translation: To the spirits of the departed and for the eternal memory of Quintus Pontenius Bassianus, who lived 25 years, 8 months, and 4 days. Carisia Bassiana, a grieving mother, had this made for her most dutiful son and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501493.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Pontenius Bassianus, set up by his mother.

EDCS-08501494.translation: To Primo, son of Br, by the decision of Publius Cossutius S.
EDCS-08501494.summary: A funerary dedication for Primo, possibly by a relative or friend.

EDCS-08501496.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Lucanius ThrHeption to his most holy wife Severina, and their daughter Maximina to her incomparable mother, and for themselves, their descendants, while alive.
EDCS-08501496.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Decimus Lucanius ThrHeption and his daughter to his wife and their mother, Severina.

EDCS-08501497.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tiberius to his dearest son, who lived [---] years. Dedicated under the axe.
EDCS-08501497.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son, set up by his father Tiberius.

EDCS-08501498.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelius Aelianus to his most holy wife, Vindauscia Petrusonia.
EDCS-08501498.summary: A funerary dedication by Coelius Aelianus to his wife, Vindauscia Petrusonia.

EDCS-08501499.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Volusius Apollinaris. Veratia Gellia, his wife.
EDCS-08501499.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Volusius Apollinaris, set up by his wife Veratia Gellia.

EDCS-08501502.translation: [...] who lived 60 years, died peacefully on the 8th day before the Kalends of August, in the consulship of Maximinus, most illustrious man.
EDCS-08501502.summary: A funerary epitaph recording a death date and the consul Maximinus, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501504.translation: To Quintus Valerius, son of Caius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Macedon, flamen of the youth, quaestor of the colony of Viennensium, duovir of the treasury, augur, a commissioner for public places. To this man, the divine Hadrian offered the broad stripe with the quaestorship and accepted his request for exemption. The villagers of Boxsanus and Noiomagenses [dedicate this] to their patron.
EDCS-08501504.summary: A dedication by villagers to their patron Quintus Valerius, listing his numerous civic and religious offices in the colony of Viennensium, and noting an honor from Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-08501505.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Iulia and Sabina, daughter. Set up by herself while alive, and dedicated under the axe.
EDCS-08501505.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Iulia and her daughter Sabina, set up by Valeria Iulia herself.

EDCS-08501508.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Leubatena, who lived 45 years, died on the 14th day before the Kalends of February, in the consulship of Volusianus, most illustrious man.
EDCS-08501508.summary: A funerary epitaph for Leubatena, recording her age at death and the consul Volusianus, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501512.translation: Constantiola rests here in peace, who lived 20 years and 3 months. She departed on the 22nd of February, after the third consulship of Leo.
EDCS-08501512.summary: A funerary inscription for Constantiola, dating to the period after the third consulship of Leo (likely 470s AD), recording her lifespan and death date.

EDCS-08501513.translation: Here rests in peace the young man of good memory, Levanius, who lived 16 years and 6 months. He departed from this life on the 22nd of February, after the consulship of the most illustrious men, Florentius and Anthemis.
EDCS-08501513.summary: A funerary inscription for the young man Levanius, who died on February 22nd after the consulship of Florentius and Anthemis (455 AD), recording his age and death date.

EDCS-08501519.translation: To the departed spirits. Alcius Sabinianus dedicated this monument to his incomparable wife Selia Sexta, who lived with him for 23 years, 5 months, and 21 days. Her brother Sextinus also took care of its erection, and they dedicated it under the chisel.
EDCS-08501519.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Selia Sexta by her husband Alcius Sabinianus and her brother Sextinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, detailing her lifespan and the dedicators.

EDCS-08501521.translation: Receive, Placidia, appeased one, who served you with faith, piety, and power, filled with the fruits of her thirtieth widowhood, she attained the third place in martyrdom; she lived sixty years.
EDCS-08501521.summary: An epitaph likely for a Christian woman named Placidia, possibly a martyr, who lived to sixty years and had experienced thirty years of widowhood.

EDCS-08501526.translation: To the departed spirits. Trebellia Tyche to her patron Quintus Trebellius Macareus, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-08501526.summary: A funerary dedication from Trebellia Tyche to her patron Quintus Trebellius Macareus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501527.translation: To Marcus Apronius Eutropus, physician, Augustalis decurion, and to his wife Clodia Apronia Clodilla, his best parents.
EDCS-08501527.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Apronius Eutropus and his wife Clodia Apronia Clodilla, honoring their roles as parents and his profession as a physician and Augustalis decurion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501528.translation: To the departed spirits. For Contessia Martina, who died at 5 years and 3 months. Caius Titius Sedulus and Contessia Severina, patrons of their dearest foster-daughter, dedicated this.
EDCS-08501528.summary: A funerary inscription for a young child, Contessia Martina, who died at the age of five years and three months, dedicated by her patrons, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501531.translation: To Marcus Sextius Atticus, freedman of Primus.
EDCS-08501531.summary: A simple dedication or epitaph for Marcus Sextius Atticus, a freedman of Primus.

EDCS-08501533.translation: Dedicated to Apollo Augustus by Nattia Severa, daughter of Soillus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08501533.summary: A dedication to Apollo Augustus, made by Nattia Severa in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08501538.translation: To the Genius and Honor of the 'utricularii' (boatmen/water carriers). Aurelius Eutyches and Antonius Pelagius, immunes (tax-exempt soldiers/officials), dedicated this from their own funds. Aurelius Marinus also granted the site by decree of the 'utricularii'.
EDCS-08501538.summary: A dedication to the Genius and Honor of the 'utricularii' by Aurelius Eutyches and Antonius Pelagius, with the site granted by Aurelius Marinus, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08501540.translation: To Jupiter, from a vow, Sentius Sabinus and Sentius Amabilis.
EDCS-08501540.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by two individuals, Sentius Sabinus and Sentius Amabilis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501541.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, from a vow, Lucius Crepereius Marinus.
EDCS-08501541.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Lucius Crepereius Marinus.

EDCS-08501542.translation: Lucius Rufius Rufinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Augusta Venus.
EDCS-08501542.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Lucius Rufius Rufinus to Jupiter Best and Greatest and Venus Augusta.

EDCS-08501547.translation: Decimus Dimarius Messulus restored this to the August Mother from a vow.
EDCS-08501547.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something to the August Mother by Decimus Dimarius Messulus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08501548.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Quartus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the August Mother.
EDCS-08501548.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gnaeus Pompeius Quartus to the August Mother.

EDCS-08501549.translation: To the August Mother, Caius Titius Sedulus from a vow.
EDCS-08501549.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the August Mother by Caius Titius Sedulus.

EDCS-08501551.translation: Sacred to Mercury, Lucius Taietius Chresimus, Augustalis *IIIIII vir*, from a vow.
EDCS-08501551.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury by Lucius Taietius Chresimus, an Augustalis *IIIIII vir*, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501552.translation: Sacred to August Mercury, Publicius Avinis.
EDCS-08501552.summary: This inscription records a dedication to August Mercury by Publicius Avinis.

EDCS-08501553.translation: To August Mercury, having undertaken a vow, Titus Laetorius Duellus Antestianus and Laetorius Numida Antestianus, father and son, give.
EDCS-08501553.summary: This inscription records a dedication to August Mercury by Titus Laetorius Duellus Antestianus and his son Laetorius Numida Antestianus, fulfilling an undertaken vow.

EDCS-08501556.translation: To the Chthonic Gods, Pluto and Proserpina, Cornelia, daughter of Gaius, from a vow.
EDCS-08501556.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Pluto and Proserpina by Cornelia, daughter of Gaius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08501557.translation: To Silvanus Augustus Primigenius, the freedman of Victor, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08501557.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by a freedman, dated by the names of the deities and the honorific 'Augustus'.

EDCS-08501559.translation: Gellia Iucunda willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the god Sucellus.
EDCS-08501559.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Sucellus by Gellia Iucunda.

EDCS-08501561.translation: Sapavidus and Campana set these up for the Holy Virgins.
EDCS-08501561.summary: This is a dedication to the 'Holy Virgins' by Sapavidus and Campana.

EDCS-08501562.translation: In the second consulship of the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, from the temple offerings.
EDCS-08501562.summary: This inscription records the building of something under the consuls of Trajan's second year, possibly indicating a public work funded by temple offerings.

EDCS-08501576.translation: To Gaius Bellicus Calpurnius Torquatus, most excellent consul, citizen, patron of the Viennenses.
EDCS-08501576.summary: This is a dedication to the consul Gaius Bellicus Calpurnius Torquatus, honoring him as patron by the people of Vienne.

EDCS-08501577.translation: To Marcus Julius Atticus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, procurator of two provinces.
EDCS-08501577.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Julius Atticus, son of Gaius, who served as procurator of two provinces.

EDCS-08501582.translation: To Quintus Valerius Lupercus, son of Quintus, of the Voltinian tribe, urban quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor-designate. Quintus Pullius Secundus with his wife Flavia Tertia and sons Pullius Secundus and Priscus and daughter Pullia Prisca. Pullius Gallus with his wife.
EDCS-08501582.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Lupercus, listing his career, and also mentions his family members and another family, Pullius Gallus and his wife.

EDCS-08501589.translation: To the most excellent patron, from the freedmen Bellinus and Ninnus.
EDCS-08501589.summary: This is an inscription from two freedmen, Bellinus and Ninnus, dedicated to their patron.

EDCS-08501590.translation: To Marcus Coelius Lectus, son of Cnaeus, of the Voltinian tribe, prefect of the engineers, tribune of the III Gallica legion, duovir of the treasury, pontiff, aedile.
EDCS-08501590.summary: This inscription details the career of Marcus Coelius Lectus, including military and civic positions.

EDCS-08501592.translation: To Decimus Julius Capito, son of Decimus, of the Voltinian tribe, flamen of the youth, duovir of public places, duovir of the treasury, augur, prefect of the engineers, tribune of the II Adiutrix legion, censor of the federated civitas of the Remi. The place was publicly granted at Remi by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08501592.summary: This inscription records the public grant of a place to Decimus Julius Capito in the civitas of the Remi, listing his numerous civic and religious offices.

EDCS-08501594.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Minnius Vestinus, a retired centurion of the 13th Urban Cohort, a councilor of Lugudunum, erected and dedicated this under the axe for his dearest: his father Venustus, a councilor of the civitas of the Sogiontii, and his brother Sextus Minnius Valerianus, son of the same, and his cousin Sextus Minnius Quintilianus.
EDCS-08501594.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Minnius Vestinus to honor his father and other relatives, likely in the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-08501595.translation: Marcus Valerius Rufus, Publius Tincius Pacatus, Lucius Tincius Tertius, Lucius Tarius Rufus, Sextus Valerius Venator, Valerius Crispinus, and Sextus Calvisius Quietus, friends, [erected this] for Gaius Passierus Afro, son of Publius, of the Voltinia tribe, a military tribune of the 22nd Legion, a quaestor, a flamen of the deified Augustus, a flamen of Germanicus Caesar, and a prefect of the engineers.
EDCS-08501595.summary: This monument was dedicated by friends to Gaius Passierus Afro, a prominent local official and priest, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501600.translation: The carpenters of Vienna, for their patron Lucius Aquilius Severianus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, aedile, duovir, treasurer, and master carpenter.
EDCS-08501600.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the carpenters' guild to their patron, Lucius Aquilius Severianus, a local magistrate, probably in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501602.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Vibrius Eutyches for his most devoted wife, Aurelia Prima, a member of the Augustan priestly college.
EDCS-08501602.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Vibrius Eutyches for his wife Aurelia Prima, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501604.translation: This place was granted by decree of the councilors to Domitius, son of Sextus, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-08501604.summary: This inscription records the allocation of public space by local councilors to Domitius, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501613.summary: This brief inscription records a donation by someone named Justinus, without further context.

EDCS-08501614.translation: Lucius Litugius, son of Sextus, of the Laena family, quaestor of the colony of Viennenses.
EDCS-08501614.summary: This is a dedication or record of office for Lucius Litugius, a quaestor in the Roman colony of Viennenses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501615.translation: For Aulus Lucilius Cantabrus, quaestor of the colony of Viennenses.
EDCS-08501615.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Lucilius Cantabrus, who held the office of quaestor in the colony of Viennenses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501617.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication by the daughter of a prefect named Marcellus, the exact nature of which is lost.

EDCS-08501619.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gaius Maximius Paterno, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, a councilor and wine merchant of Vienna, and for his most sacred patron, Gaius Maximius, by Maximia Secundilla, for her most dutiful father.
EDCS-08501619.summary: This funerary monument honors Gaius Maximius Paterno, a councilor and wine merchant, and his patron Gaius Maximius, dedicated by Maximia Secundilla, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08501620.translation: To Lucius Porcius Latinus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, honored with the public equestrian rank, prefect of the corps of artisans (twice), aedile (thrice), overseer of public properties, his daughter Porcia Tutela, daughter of Titus, restored [this].
EDCS-08501620.summary: This epitaph honors Lucius Porcius Latinus, detailing his public career and family, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08501621.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal peace, Caius Rusonius Myron, twice duovir Augustalis of the colony of Copia Augusta Lugudunensium, also a sagarius, also honored as a centonarius and a full member of the sagarii, the heir of his freedman of excellent example, by his order, alone placed and dedicated this under the axe.
EDCS-08501621.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by an heir for Caius Rusonius Myron, a prominent citizen and member of various collegia in Lugudunum, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501622.translation: Voltinia, daughter of Sammonis, priestess of Mars.
EDCS-08501622.summary: This is a short dedication or identification of Voltinia, daughter of Sammonis, as a priestess of Mars, possibly from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08501624.translation: To the Divine Shades, Porcia Tutela, heir, to her most excellent friend Caius Terentius Viator, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, twice duovir of the treasury.
EDCS-08501624.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Porcia Tutela for her friend Caius Terentius Viator, a former official in the treasury, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08501625.translation: To Decimus Titius Iustus, son of Decimus, of the Voltinia tribe, twice duovir with the power of jurisdiction, priest of the youth, his wife Cassia Prisca, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-08501625.summary: This inscription records the dedication of Decimus Titius Iustus, a civic official and priest of the youth, by his wife Cassia Prisca, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08501634.translation: The friends of the corps of artisans decreed that an altar should be erected to him, namely Attius Saturninus and Cassius T...ianus, on the very day he died, because they committed fraud concerning his funeral.
EDCS-08501634.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a decree by friends of the artisans' guild to erect an altar for a deceased individual, likely due to a dispute over funeral arrangements, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501635.translation: To the Divine Shades, to Lucius Caecilius Pison, a citizen of Arausio. Lucius Caecilius Marianus and Valerius Orphitus, sons, to their most devoted father, and Valeria Maritima, to their incomparable wife.
EDCS-08501635.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by sons and a wife to Lucius Caecilius Pison, a citizen of Arausio, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08501636.translation: To the Divine Shades. Krates of Tralles made this monument for Eutychia, his own freedwoman. Farewell, good Eutychia.
EDCS-08501636.summary: This bilingual (Latin and Greek) funerary inscription is a dedication by Krates of Tralles for his freedwoman Eutychia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501639.translation: Hellas, the pantomime, rests here, aged 14. Sotericus to his most devoted son.
EDCS-08501639.summary: This is a tombstone for Hellas, a 14-year-old pantomime, dedicated by Sotericus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501640.translation: To the Divine Shades, Tiberius Iulius Diadochus, the generous dendrophorus.
EDCS-08501640.summary: This epitaph is a dedication to Tiberius Iulius Diadochus, a dendrophorus (tree-bearer), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501641.translation: To Iulia Felicissima, a minor official, Hilara, who lived 7 years and 6 months. Publius Iulius Felix, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, and Iulia Novella, made this for their sweetest daughter while alive, and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501641.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by parents to their daughter, Iulia Felicissima, who died at a young age.

EDCS-08501645.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Mercator, son of Senilis, a citizen of the Bellovaci. Callistus and Pompeia for their patron.
EDCS-08501645.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Mercator by Callistus and Pompeia, likely his freedmen or clients.

EDCS-08501646.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Nicia, the lyre-player. Iulia [---]
EDCS-08501646.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nicia, a musician, with a partial dedication by Iulia.

EDCS-08501648.translation: For the public treasury of Vienna.
EDCS-08501648.summary: This inscription likely records a payment or dedication to the public funds of the city of Vienna.

EDCS-08501649.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Rufinus, steward of our Emperor's XXth detachment of the inheritance tax office at Vienna. Taminia Severina for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-08501649.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Taminia Severina to her husband, Rufinus, who was a steward in the imperial tax administration.

EDCS-08501655.translation: Aetherius said as he died: 'Bury my body here; may Mother Earth, giver of all things, who gave it, herself cover it.'
EDCS-08501655.summary: This is a funerary epitaph expressing the dying wish of Aetherius to be buried in the earth.

EDCS-08501656.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Africanius. Quintus Connius Zosimus, the heir.
EDCS-08501656.summary: This funerary inscription records the heir, Quintus Connius Zosimus, responsible for the burial of Africanius.

EDCS-08501657.translation: Quintus Antonius Gallicanus arranged for this.
EDCS-08501657.summary: This is a brief statement indicating that Quintus Antonius Gallicanus was responsible for the arrangement of a monument or burial.

EDCS-08501658.translation: To Apollinaris, dearest son, aged 3 years.
EDCS-08501658.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apollinaris, a young child.

EDCS-08501659.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Augustalis Sacer and Benigna.
EDCS-08501659.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Augustalis Sacer and Benigna.

EDCS-08501660.translation: Aurelia Creste dedicated this to the eternal memory of her most deserving husband Aurelius Sapricus.
EDCS-08501660.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501663.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Bittius Puster, from his son and his wife to their most excellent husband.
EDCS-08501663.summary: A funerary dedication by a son and wife to Bittius Puster, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501664.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to the eternal memory of Socchia Enneanis, who had the sweetest and most noble genius beyond her years, and lived for 8 months and 24 days. Lucius Boconius Photinus, her father, and Alpia Castina, her mother, bereft by the sudden loss of their most beloved and only remarkable son, taken from them too soon for his age, erected this for him and for themselves and their descendants, and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501664.summary: A detailed funerary monument dedicated by grieving parents to their young daughter Socchia Enneanis, with her lifespan provided, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501666.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Cappia Graecina, from Lucius Cappius Decuminus, to his most beloved wife.
EDCS-08501666.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, Cappia Graecina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501667.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Publius Cassius Iulianus, deceased at 2 years and 36 days. Publius Fa nius Iulianus and Cassia, his parents, for their most sweet son.
EDCS-08501667.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their young son, Publius Cassius Iulianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501668.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for the eternal peace of Titus Cassius Lucinulus. Mercatorius Sessor and Cassia Veratia made this for their most sweet son and for themselves and their descendants while alive, and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-08501668.summary: A funerary monument made by parents for their son, Titus Cassius Lucinulus, also intended for themselves and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501669.translation: To Clodia Gratina, daughter of Sextus. Lucius Caecilius Aequalis, for his wife and himself.
EDCS-08501669.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, Clodia Gratina, and himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501672.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Connia Gemina, an incomparable woman who lived 24 years. Connius Aquilius and Theseus, for their most devoted and sweet sister, and Connius Chrysopaes, freedman, dedicated this under the axe, and Verrius Attianus, for his most chaste wife.
EDCS-08501672.summary: A complex funerary monument dedicated by siblings, a freedman, and another individual for Connia Gemina and Verrius Attianus' wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501673.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Cornelia Mapilla.
EDCS-08501673.summary: A funerary dedication to Cornelia Mapilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501677.summary: A self-dedicated funerary monument by Decidus Gratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501678.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Sextus Donnius Eutychius made [this] for his dearest son, Sextus Donnius Eutyches, aged 5 years.
EDCS-08501678.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 5-year-old boy named Sextus Donnius Eutyches, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-08501679.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Marcella, daughter of Marcellus, deceased at 22 years. Quintus Veratius Decidianus [made this] for his most holy wife, and Marcus Eccius Marcellus for his dearest sister.
EDCS-08501679.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcella, dedicated by her husband Quintus Veratius Decidianus and her brother Marcus Eccius Marcellus.

EDCS-08501680.translation: To the spirits of the dead. To Eyrice, most dutiful mother-in-law and daughter-in-law. They took care to set this up and dedicated it under the tile. You lie here unmerited, they placed [this] according to your age.
EDCS-08501680.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a daughter-in-law and mother-in-law to Eyrice, with a lament about her untimely death.

EDCS-08501681.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Eutyches, the sweetest infant, who lived.
EDCS-08501681.summary: A funerary epitaph for an infant named Eutyches.

EDCS-08501683.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Gainia Valeriana, deceased at 21 years. Caius Giamillius Merops and Camulia Marcella [made this] for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-08501683.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gainia Valeriana, aged 21, dedicated by her parents Caius Giamillius Merops and Camulia Marcella.

EDCS-08501684.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Decimus Gallius Sacer made [this] for his father, Decimus Gallus Lascivus, and for himself, while alive.
EDCS-08501684.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decimus Gallus Lascivus, commissioned by his son Decimus Gallius Sacer for both his father and himself.

EDCS-08501686.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Quintus Grattius Proclio, the best husband. Caprilia Mnemosyne, and also for Grattiae Proclae, [their] daughter.
EDCS-08501686.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Caprilia Mnemosyne to her husband Quintus Grattius Proclio and their daughter Grattiae Proclae.

EDCS-08501687.translation: To the spirits of the dead and eternal memory. Marcus Iulius Euplus and Iulia Accepta made [this] for their only dearest son, Lucius Iulius Acceptus, who lived 15 years, 10 months, and 2 days, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-08501687.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Acceptus, aged 15 years, 10 months, and 2 days, commissioned by his parents for him and themselves.

EDCS-08501689.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Lucius Iulius Victor, [her] husband. Iulia Thallusa.
EDCS-08501689.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Victor, dedicated by his wife Iulia Thallusa.

EDCS-08501690.translation: To the spirits of the dead and eternal memory. His sons placed [this] for Iulius Victor Vindex and dedicated it under the tile.
EDCS-08501690.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by the sons to Iulius Victor Vindex, with evidence of it being placed under the tile.

EDCS-08501691.translation: For the perpetual security of Iulia Brundisina, Iulia Ingenua [erected this] for her most dutiful mother and Lucius Iulius Glaphyrus for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-08501691.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a daughter and her husband to a deceased woman, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501692.translation: To the Divine Shades: Aulus Eprius Pardus set this up for Iulia Nicopolis.
EDCS-08501692.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Eprius Pardus to his deceased wife, Iulia Nicopolis, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08501694.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal security: Caius Modestius Ismaragdus [erected this] for his most beloved, dutiful, and sweetest wife, Iulia Iuliana.
EDCS-08501694.summary: A funerary monument erected by Caius Modestius Ismaragdus for his wife Iulia Iuliana, emphasizing her virtues and his affection.

EDCS-08501696.translation: To the Divine Shades: Publius Labenius Trophimus [erected this] for Labenia Nemesis, his excellent and most dutiful freedwoman and wife, dearest to him for her merits.
EDCS-08501696.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Labenius Trophimus to his freedwoman and wife, Labenia Nemesis, highlighting her qualities and his love.

EDCS-08501697.translation: For his daughter Nae, for Marcus Lattius Optatus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-08501697.summary: A fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, commemorating a daughter named Nae and possibly her father Marcus Lattius Optatus.

EDCS-08501701.translation: To the Divine Shades: Lucius Lucretius Alcimus [for] Lucretia Crates, his sister.
EDCS-08501701.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Lucretius Alcimus for his sister Lucretia Crates.

EDCS-08501702.translation: To the Divine Shades: Iulia Tetricilla, mother [erected this] for Lucretia Saturnina, and Lucius Titius Titianus for his wife.
EDCS-08501702.summary: A funerary dedication with two dedicants: a mother for her daughter Lucretia Saturnina, and a husband for his wife, also named Lucretia Saturnina.

EDCS-08501703.translation: To Lupa, his most sweet wife, while he lived.
EDCS-08501703.summary: A funerary dedication made by a husband to his wife Lupa, indicating it was erected during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501704.translation: To the Divine Shades: Look as you pass this monument, so that you may know how great he was, born from humble origins, a glory to his country, his eloquence honored by so many inscriptions; he no longer feels the burden. Quintus Luttius Valerius arranged for this to be set up while he lived.
EDCS-08501704.summary: A self-commemorating funerary epitaph by Quintus Luttius Valerius, who erected it during his lifetime to celebrate his achievements in eloquence and his contributions to his country.

EDCS-08501706.translation: Quintus Mallonius Bathyllus, while he lived, for himself.
EDCS-08501706.summary: A self-dedicated funerary monument erected by Quintus Mallonius Bathyllus during his lifetime.

EDCS-08501708.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Messius Italus and Verus, [son] of Messius Veratianus.
EDCS-08501708.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deified dead by Messius Italus and Verus, likely setting up a tomb for themselves and their family.

EDCS-08501709.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Marcus Modestus Modestinus. Optatia Siora [erected this] for her most excellent husband and for the spirits of the underworld, for Marcus Modestus Optatus, her dearest son.
EDCS-08501709.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Optatia Siora for her husband, Marcus Modestus Modestinus, and her son, Marcus Modestus Optatus, who was dearest to her.

EDCS-08501710.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Nigidia Aureliana.
EDCS-08501710.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Nigidia Aureliana.

EDCS-08501711.summary: This is a simple epitaph identifying Octavius as the son of Andonis.

EDCS-08501712.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Paetenius Tertius, deceased, aged 17 years. Paetenius Tertius, his father, [erected this] for his most dutiful, sweetest, and best son and for himself.
EDCS-08501712.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Paetenius Tertius at age 17 and was set up by his father, also Paetenius Tertius, for his son and himself.

EDCS-08501715.translation: Publius Pantius Priscus, [erected this] for his wife, Pompeia, daughter of Cnaeus, Potita, and for himself while alive.
EDCS-08501715.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Pantius Priscus for his wife, Pompeia Potita, and also for himself, indicating he intended to be buried there.

EDCS-08501716.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Publius Pompullius Carpus.
EDCS-08501716.summary: A funerary dedication to Publius Pompullius Carpus.

EDCS-08501717.translation: Albana, mother, [for] Primula, daughter of Niger.
EDCS-08501717.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Albana, the mother, for her daughter, Primula.

EDCS-08501718.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintilla, the wife of Marcus.

EDCS-08501723.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Lucius Sennius Germanus erected [this] for his most dutiful son, Lucius Aterius Augustalis.
EDCS-08501723.summary: This funerary inscription records that Lucius Sennius Germanus set up a monument for his very dutiful son, Lucius Aterius Augustalis.

EDCS-08501724.translation: Grattia Pithane and Claudius Mummius set this up for their most holy friend Sergiae Zosime while she was alive.
EDCS-08501724.summary: A funerary monument erected by Grattia Pithane and Claudius Mummius for their friend Sergiae Zosime.

EDCS-08501725.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Titus Servilius Bellinus and his wife Dognia Iullina for their Iuninus and Iulianus.
EDCS-08501725.summary: A funerary inscription by Titus Servilius Bellinus and Dognia Iullina for deceased members of their family.

EDCS-08501726.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Cassia, a wretched mother, set up this altar and dedicated it under the axe for her son Servi Severus, who was twenty-four years old, her incomparable son.
EDCS-08501726.summary: A funerary altar dedicated by Cassia for her twenty-four-year-old son Servi Severus.

EDCS-08501731.translation: Taia Saturnina made this for her own people while alive.
EDCS-08501731.summary: A funerary marker made by Taia Saturnina for her family during her lifetime.

EDCS-08501732.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Titus Terentius Abascantus for Terentia Hedone, his freedwoman and dearest wife.
EDCS-08501732.summary: A funerary inscription by Titus Terentius Abascantus for his freedwoman and wife, Terentia Hedone.

EDCS-08501733.translation: Sextus Attius Flavius for Terentia Martia, his incomparable friend and soul, and for himself, while alive.
EDCS-08501733.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Sextus Attius Flavius for Terentia Martia and himself.

EDCS-08501734.translation: Iucunda, his daughter, saw to it that this be made according to the will of Marcus Titius Macrinus, son of Titus.
EDCS-08501734.summary: A funerary commission executed by Iucunda as per the will of Marcus Titius Macrinus.

EDCS-08501736.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and eternal rest: Publius Seius Asclepiodotus for his incomparable daughter Titia Seia, who died aged twenty-two years, five months, and twenty-five days.
EDCS-08501736.summary: A funerary monument by Publius Seius Asclepiodotus for his daughter Titia Seia.

EDCS-08501737.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Trebonia Zotice for her most dutiful son, Decimus Trebonius Abascantus.
EDCS-08501737.summary: A funerary inscription by Trebonia Zotice for her son Decimus Trebonius Abascantus.

EDCS-08501738.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Amabilis Aventinus, freedman, for his most excellent patron, Caius Valerius Montanus.
EDCS-08501738.summary: A funerary inscription by the freedman Amabilis Aventinus for his patron Caius Valerius Montanus.

EDCS-08501739.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Valerius Geminus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501740.translation: Quintus Valerius Severus to his father, Quintus Valerius Gratus.
EDCS-08501740.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Quintus Valerius Severus to his father, Quintus Valerius Gratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501742.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Caius Valerius Symphorus and his wife Threptena, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-08501742.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Caius Valerius Symphorus, his wife Threptena, and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501744.translation: Lucius Tertinius Sextus dedicated this under the axe to Valeria Attica, also known as Amantia, his wife.
EDCS-08501744.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Tertinius Sextus to his wife Valeria Attica (Amantia), indicating it was dedicated 'under the axe,' a practice associated with burials, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501745.translation: Titus Marius Tiro and Aulus Iulius Paris to Valeria Cupita, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-08501745.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Titus Marius Tiro and Aulus Iulius Paris to Valeria Cupita, daughter of Titus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501746.translation: Rufinus and Servandus erected this to their mother, Valeria Ecate, and to Rufina, and to their sister, at their own expense.
EDCS-08501746.summary: This is a funerary dedication erected by Rufinus and Servandus to their mother, Valeria Ecate, and to their sister Rufina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501747.translation: To Valeria Iulia, who died aged 18 years, 8 months, and 25 days, for her most beloved daughter and incomparable, most chaste wife, by Caius Alfidius Hermogenes and Atticus.
EDCS-08501747.summary: This epitaph commemorates Valeria Iulia, who died at the age of 18 years, 8 months, and 25 days, and was dedicated by Caius Alfidius Hermogenes (as her husband) and Atticus (as her child), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501751.translation: Divicius Lascivus to his most devoted wife, Vettia Modestina, to the Divine Spirits.
EDCS-08501751.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Divicius Lascivus to his devoted wife Vettia Modestina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501752.translation: To Gaius Vibius Littulus, son of Lucius, of the Voltina tribe, to his brother.
EDCS-08501752.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Vibius Littulus, son of Lucius, from the Voltina tribe, made by his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501753.translation: Lucius Vibrius Daphnus for himself and for his descendants.
EDCS-08501753.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Vibrius Daphnus for himself and his descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501756.translation: I, father Vitalinus, and mother Martina, have written not for great glory but out of love for our three sons. Within 27 days, we laid here our son Sapaudus, who lived for 7 years and 26 days, our daughter Rustica, who lived for 4 years and 20 days, and our daughter Rusticula, who lived for 3 years and 33 days.
EDCS-08501756.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of three young children and commemorates their parents, Vitalinus and Martina.

EDCS-08501760.translation: A common wall for Marcus and Sextina.
EDCS-08501760.summary: This inscription likely marks a shared boundary or property line between two individuals, Marcus and Sextina.

EDCS-08501763.translation: Nagelius, pious son of Fasurius and Maina the sweetest, lived his life full of wisdom and books; he died in Leusona, having lived 8 years.
EDCS-08501763.summary: This epitaph records the death of a young boy named Nagelius, highlighting his piety, wisdom, and love for books, and mentions his parents Fasurius and Maina.

EDCS-08501790.translation: Here rests a man of distinction, of good memory, a judge, who lived and passed away in October during the consulship of the most illustrious Faustus.
EDCS-08501790.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a distinguished judge who died in October during the consulship of Faustus.

EDCS-08501791.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Calumniosa, who lived 40 years and died on the 15th of the Kalends of June during the consulship of the most illustrious Symmachus.
EDCS-08501791.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calumniosa, who died at the age of 40 during the consulship of Symmachus.

EDCS-08501792.translation: In this tomb is laid Severianus, of good memory, who embraced religion with a devoted mind; his soul returning to God the creator, he left his earthly body to the earth, having completed 32 years of life. He died the day before the Ides of August. He will rise again in Christ our Lord, after the second consulship of Longinus and the consulship of Faustus.
EDCS-08501792.summary: This Christian funerary inscription records the death of Severianus at age 32, his devotion to religion, and his belief in resurrection, dating it to after the consulships of Longinus and Faustus.

EDCS-08501795.translation: In this tomb rests, of good memory, Petrunia, who lived 48 years and died on the 15th of the Kalends of November, after the consulship of the consul Mavortius.
EDCS-08501795.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Petrunia at the age of 48, dating it after the consulship of Mavortius.

EDCS-08501796.translation: Here rests, of good memory, Romanus, a religious man, who lived eighty years and passed away in peace on the 14th of the Kalends of October, after the consulship of the most illustrious Venantius.
EDCS-08501796.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Romanus, a religious man, at the age of 80, dating it after the consulship of Venantius.

EDCS-08501797.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Simplicius, who lived more or less 90 years and died in peace on the 5th of the Kalends of November, during the consulship of the most illustrious Felix.
EDCS-08501797.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Simplicius, who lived approximately 90 years, dating it to the consulship of Felix.

EDCS-08501801.translation: He died in Christ on the eighth of March, during the consulship of the most illustrious Florentius and Anthemus.
EDCS-08501801.summary: This epitaph records a death in Christ, mentioning the consuls Florentius and Anthemus.

EDCS-08501803.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Dalmatius the young man, who lived in the world for four years and 7 days. He died on the 8th day before the Kalends of December, when Opilio was consul, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-08501803.summary: This is a tomb inscription commemorating Dalmatius, a young man, with a precise date of death given by consul.

EDCS-08501804.translation: In this tomb rests, of good memory, Scurpillosa the devout woman, who lived more or less 48 years. She died in peace on the day before the Kalends of September, when Opilio was consul, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-08501804.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Scurpillosa, a devout woman, with her approximate age and date of death noted by consul.

EDCS-08501805.translation: Here rests in peace, of good memory, Gernus, who lived more or less 31 years. He died on the 9th day before the Kalends of September, when Opilio was consul, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-08501805.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gernus, providing his approximate age and date of death by consul.

EDCS-08501807.translation: Here rests in peace, a servant of God, Uranius, who lived 43 years. He died on the 14th day before the Kalends of January, when Olybrius was consul, a most distinguished man. He will rise again in Christ.
EDCS-08501807.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Uranius, a servant of God, noting his age and date of death by consul, with a Christian resurrectional statement.

EDCS-08501808.translation: Here rests Spiavera, 7 years and [some number of] days, in the discipline of HA [---], heaven [---].
EDCS-08501808.summary: This fragmented inscription commemorates Spiavera, noting her age, possibly related to religious discipline.

EDCS-08501815.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, the venerable Maria, a devout woman and one who fears the Lord, who lived more or less 85 years. She died in Christ on the third Ides of June, again after the consulship of Johannes, a most distinguished man, the consul.
EDCS-08501815.summary: This tomb inscription honors the venerable Maria, a devout woman, noting her age, date of death, and the consulship of Johannes.

EDCS-08501819.translation: Resting in peace, of good memory, a penitent, friend of the church, and friend to all, who lived more or less 85 years. He died in Christ on February 17th, after the consulship of Basilius, a most distinguished man, the consul, in the sixth indiction. You who come with devoted heart, humbly, carefully, seeking help from here, your wondrous usefulness and praiseworthy will indeed are celebrated, for the temples of two saints are adorned with inscriptions, and through them, what great gifts are bestowed upon the servants of Christ. VIVITACVS: virtue always lifts from the right.
EDCS-08501819.summary: This lengthy inscription commemorates a penitent and church benefactor, noting his age, date of death, consulship of Basilius, and includes a theological reflection on charity and divine favor.

EDCS-08501820.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Flurinus, who lived more or less 40 years. He died in peace on the day before the 3rd Kalends of August, seventeen times after the consulship of Basilius, a most distinguished man, the consul, in the VI indiction.
EDCS-08501820.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Flurinus, noting his approximate age and the specific dating by multiple consulships of Basilius and the indiction.

EDCS-08501821.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Dulcisius the deacon, who lived more or less 80 years. He died in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of June, eighteen times after the consulship of Basilius, a most distinguished man, the consul, in the VIII indiction.
EDCS-08501821.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Dulcisius, a deacon, noting his approximate age and the specific dating by multiple consulships of Basilius and the indiction.

EDCS-08501822.translation: In this tomb rests in peace, of good memory, Liveria the innocent, who lived more or less 15 years. She died in peace on the 14th day before the Kalends of November, [after] twenty times after the consulship of Basilius, a most distinguished man, the consul, in the VIIII indiction.
EDCS-08501822.summary: This tomb inscription records the death of Liveria, noted as innocent, giving her approximate age and a precise date based on the consulship of Basilius and the indiction.

EDCS-08501823.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Epiphanius of good memory, excellent in character, gentle in his birth, most devoted in faith, dear to citizens, pious to the poor; he always prayed, which he deserved to obtain. He lived more or less 95 years, died in Christ on the Kalends of July, 22 years after the consulship of the most illustrious man Bassus, in the eleventh indiction.
EDCS-08501823.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Epiphanius, recording his virtues, lifespan, death date, and consulship (likely 5th/6th century AD).

EDCS-08501824.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Dulcitia, handmaid of God, of good memory, most excellent in character, with a generous spirit and most bountiful charity; she lived more or less 35 years, died in peace on the new Kalends of May, 10 years after the consulship of the most illustrious man Bassus, in the fourteenth indiction.
EDCS-08501824.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dulcitia, a nun, recording her virtues, lifespan, death date, and consulship (likely 5th/6th century AD).

EDCS-08501826.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Pompedius, servant of God, who lived more or less 12 years, died in peace on the twelfth Kalends of January, 30 years after the consulship of the most illustrious man Bassus, in the fourth indiction.
EDCS-08501826.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompedius, a young boy, recording his lifespan, death date, and consulship (likely 5th/6th century AD).

EDCS-08501829.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Vitildes, an infant of good memory, who lived two years and three months, died in peace in the 37th year after the consulship of the most illustrious man Bassus, in the fourteenth indiction.
EDCS-08501829.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Vitildes, recording her lifespan, death date, and consulship (likely 5th/6th century AD).

EDCS-08501830.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Bertefrida, innocent, honest, beautiful, charming, useful, cautious; she lived more or less 8 years, died on the Kalends of September in the 66th year after the consulship of the most illustrious man Bassus, in the ninth indiction.
EDCS-08501830.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bertefrida, a young girl, recording her virtues, lifespan, death date, and consulship (likely 5th/6th century AD).

EDCS-08501837.translation: Agrecius Domesticus rests here in peace; he lived 33 years and 3 months.
EDCS-08501837.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Agrecius Domesticus, recording his lifespan and death.

EDCS-08501838.translation: Armentaria rests here in peace; she lived 4 years and 6 months, to rise again when the day of the Lord has come.
EDCS-08501838.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Armentaria, a young child, recording her lifespan and her resurrection in Christian belief.

EDCS-08501840.translation: Auxilius lived 37 years, 6 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-08501840.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Auxilius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-08501842.translation: Castina rested in peace; she lived 25 years.
EDCS-08501842.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Castina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08501843.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Esmeria of good memory, handmaid of God, who lived
EDCS-08501843.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Esmeria, a handmaid of God, recording her name and beginning her lifespan.

EDCS-08501845.translation: Eufrasius the blessed rests here in peace, who lived 70 years, 2 months, and 7 days, to rise again on the day the Creator comes to heaven.
EDCS-08501845.summary: This epitaph records the death and resurrection hopes of Eufrasius, who lived 70 years, 2 months, and 7 days.

EDCS-08501846.summary: This incomplete inscription mentions Eufrasia, indicating she lived.

EDCS-08501847.translation: Eunomiola rests here in peace, who lived twenty-nine years together.
EDCS-08501847.summary: This epitaph states that Eunomiola rests in peace after living twenty-nine years.

EDCS-08501848.translation: To the Divine Manes. When Eustacia had lived seventy years, this chosen one is placed in a tomb, leaving her mortal limbs to the earth in old age, but recalling her soul to heaven according to her merits.
EDCS-08501848.summary: This sepulchral inscription honors Eustacia, who lived seventy years, and describes her body being interred while her soul is believed to ascend to heaven.

EDCS-08501849.translation: Foedula, who by the Lord's mercy left the world, lies here in the tomb which devoted faith granted, under the right hand of Martinus, once a leader, reborn in the font of God she laid down her sins, and now the martyrs granting a fitting resting place, she reveres the emeritus leader Cerbasus and Protasus, having obtained a faithful rest, she confessed and lies united with the saints.
EDCS-08501849.summary: This epitaph commemorates Foedula, who died and was buried through the faith of Martinus, and is now united with saints and martyrs.

EDCS-08501850.translation: Geronsia lies here, in the heights of peace, venerable for her years and merits, a servant of God, who, revered among all the emeritus servants of Christ, has now departed.
EDCS-08501850.summary: This epitaph records the death of Geronsia, described as a venerable servant of God who has departed to join other emeritus servants of Christ.

EDCS-08501851.translation: Forb, son of Pradia and Habacuc, one of the born
EDCS-08501851.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions Forb, son of Pradia and Habacuc, and possibly his birth.

EDCS-08501852.translation: Iniuriosus rests here in peace, who lived 4 years, 9 months, and 1 day, to rise again in Christ. His mother Euladia made this.
EDCS-08501852.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iniuriosus, who lived 4 years, 9 months, and 1 day, and states his mother Euladia commissioned the monument.

EDCS-08501853.translation: Leonia rests here in peace, who lived 4 years, 6 months.
EDCS-08501853.summary: This epitaph states that Leonia rests in peace after living 4 years and 6 months.

EDCS-08501854.translation: Lopa rests here, who lived 50 years and 4 months, she will rise again in Christ.
EDCS-08501854.summary: This epitaph records that Lopa lived 50 years and 4 months and expresses the hope for her resurrection in Christ.

EDCS-08501855.translation: Lupicinus, who lived for about 35 years and died on the third day before the Nones of October, rests here in peace, expecting to rise again with the saints.
EDCS-08501855.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Lupicinus, who lived for approximately 35 years.

EDCS-08501856.translation: Maganus rests here in peace, who lived for three years, eight months, and 20 days; may he rest in Christ.
EDCS-08501856.summary: This epitaph commemorates Maganus, who died after living three years, eight months, and 20 days.

EDCS-08501857.translation: Maiori of good memory rests here in peace.
EDCS-08501857.summary: This is a simple epitaph stating that Maiori rests in peace.

EDCS-08501858.translation: He who always distributed mystical rites to the people, adorned with piety and honor, lies in this sacred tomb; whom the whole Elysian field calls Marinus.
EDCS-08501858.summary: This epitaph describes Marinus, who was known for distributing religious rites and is honored in the Elysian Fields.

EDCS-08501859.translation: Marina, formerly by name, rests here in peace with her three granddaughters.
EDCS-08501859.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marina and her three granddaughters.

EDCS-08501860.translation: Martinus lies here, who lived for two years, 2 months, and 3 days, in peace.
EDCS-08501860.summary: This epitaph marks the burial of Martinus, who died at the age of two years, two months, and three days.

EDCS-08501861.translation: Martina rests here eternally in peace, who lived a flourishing life.
EDCS-08501861.summary: This epitaph states that Martina rests eternally in peace after living a flourishing life.

EDCS-08501862.translation: Mauricius, an innocent infant, who lived for 3 years, 6 months, and 8 days, rests here in Christ and in peace; his parents, Flavius Gentianus and Palesta, the fathers of both, placed this inscription out of love for their son.
EDCS-08501862.summary: This epitaph commemorates the infant Mauricius, who died at the age of 3 years, 6 months, and 8 days, and was memorialized by his parents.

EDCS-08501864.translation: Mercasto, who lived a pleasant life for 60 years, rests here in peace.
EDCS-08501864.summary: This epitaph records that Mercasto lived for 60 pleasant years and rests in peace.

EDCS-08501865.translation: Nigrinianus, the subdeacon, who lived for about eighty years, rests here in peace, having departed on the 18th day before the Kalends of May, expecting to rise again in Christ.
EDCS-08501865.summary: This epitaph commemorates Nigrinianus, a subdeacon, who died at approximately eighty years of age on April 14th and is expected to rise again in Christ.

EDCS-08501866.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Pascasius the deacon of good memory, who lived 59 years more or less, died in Christ seven days before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-08501866.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Pascasius, a deacon, with his age and date of death, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501867.translation: In this tomb rests Patricius of good memory, who lived 65 years, died on the Ides of March in peace.
EDCS-08501867.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Patricius, with his age and date of death, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501868.translation: Here rests in peace Peregrinus, who lived 6 years and months.
EDCS-08501868.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Peregrinus, stating his age in years and months, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501869.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription recording the name Pre and his lifespan, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501870.translation: Lucia Procasia, daughter, lived 8 years and 5 days.
EDCS-08501870.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the lifespan of Lucia Procasia, a young girl, with her age in years and days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08501875.translation: Severinus departed from this world, he had lived 3 years, 5 months, and 6 days. Decentius departed from this world, he had lived 2 years, 8 months, and 7 days. They were the sons of Helarius and Dalmatia.
EDCS-08501875.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the deaths of two young brothers, Severinus and Decentius, with their ages and parentage, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501876.translation: Here rest in peace the Silviae, who lived 4 years and months.
EDCS-08501876.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the deaths of multiple individuals named Silviae, stating their age in years and months, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501877.translation: Chastity, faith, charity, piety, obedience, and whatever else God commanded women to possess, Sofroniola, adorned with these good qualities, rests in peace. Martinianus, her husband, will dedicate the inscription according to custom. Died the eighth day before the Ides of June.
EDCS-08501877.summary: This is a tomb inscription honoring Sofroniola, detailing her virtues and mentioning her husband Martinianus, with her date of death, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501878.translation: In the name of Christ, in this tomb rests in peace Terencius of good memory, who lived.
EDCS-08501878.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription, starting with a Christian invocation and recording the name Terencius and his lifespan, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501879.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription recording a partial name and age, likely from the Late Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-08501880.translation: Here rests in peace Valeria, who lived for 3 years, months, and 11 days, to be resurrected in Christ.
EDCS-08501880.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria, a young child, indicating her burial and hope for resurrection.

EDCS-08501881.translation: Under this title, Valiaricus and his wife Licinia chose for themselves while alive in pious times.
EDCS-08501881.summary: This inscription records a self-dedication by Valiaricus and his wife Licinia during their lifetime.

EDCS-08501882.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating the burial of Venantius.

EDCS-08501883.translation: Here rests in peace Veneriosa, who lived for 28 years.
EDCS-08501883.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Veneriosa.

EDCS-08501884.translation: In this tomb rests Viliaric, father of the poor, of good memory, who lived for 85 years and died in peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of February in the eighth indiction.
EDCS-08501884.summary: This inscription commemorates Viliaric, a notable figure described as the 'father of the poor', detailing his age at death and the date of his passing.

EDCS-08501885.translation: In this tomb rests in peace Matlia, a religious woman of good memory, who lived for more or less 70 years and died in peace in Augustas.
EDCS-08501885.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and approximate age of Matlia, a religious woman.

EDCS-08501887.translation: The remains of Olibi, a presbyter, rest in this tomb, who first lived with a pure conscience, faithful, prudent, kind, and beloved, for more or less [unknown number] years.
EDCS-08501887.summary: This inscription commemorates Olibi, a presbyter, highlighting his virtuous qualities and life lived in faith.

EDCS-08501892.translation: Flo, wife and disciple, devoted her sacred head to this tomb, lacking the end of life, and [offered] tender [gifts] to the [gods?].
EDCS-08501892.summary: This inscription seems to commemorate Flo, a wife and disciple, possibly relating to a dedication or memorial.

EDCS-08501893.translation: Full of piety and merits, Mercatus has earned honors, living, and will always see [them] after death, deposited on the fifteenth day before the Kalends.
EDCS-08501893.summary: This inscription celebrates the merits and piety of Mercatus, suggesting posthumous recognition.
