EDCS-08501894.translation: He did not deceive hours, seeking the true gifts of God; he cultivated various vanities with his senses, accumulated riches, but conquered avarice which used to conquer all things, and at the same time, by conquering himself, abstained from vices; charity settled in his holy heart, and his pure simplicity felt no stain; his age was fifty-one years; O Christ, he obtained your rewards in the centuries; he died in Christ on the 3rd day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-08501894.summary: This lengthy epitaph praises a deceased individual who lived 51 years, highlighting his spiritual journey of overcoming avarice and vices, and his devotion to Christ, with his death date provided.

EDCS-08501905.translation: Seterom lived with Augustus for three years and four months.
EDCS-08501905.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Seterom, likely a freedman or child, with a reference to Augustus, possibly indicating the period of their life or relationship, from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08600717.translation: Titus Crispius Cesa, AV M Secundus, and C. Iustus erected this at their own expense for Titus Crispius, a soldier of the Ninth Legion.
EDCS-08600717.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by colleagues or friends for Titus Crispius, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600721.translation: Julius Tenci willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Trittia.
EDCS-08600721.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Tenci dedicated to Trittia, possibly a deity or a deceased person, expressing fulfillment of a vow, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600724.translation: Here rests in Christ Baldo, a man of noble birth, who died on the 5th day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-08600724.summary: This is a Christian tombstone inscription for Baldo, a nobleman, who died shortly before November 1st, from the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-08600727.translation: Here rests in peace Crescentia, of good memory, who lived for more or less 10 years and died the day before the Kalends of November, in the 6th indiction.
EDCS-08600727.summary: This is a Christian tombstone inscription for Crescentia, who died the day before November 1st in the 6th indiction, likely from the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-08600731.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Firmo, son of Magullonis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600733.translation: Lucius Cornelius Valentinus and Rogatus, son of Valerianus, willingly and deservedly dedicated this to Hercules out of their own free will.
EDCS-08600733.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Cornelius Valentinus and Rogatus to the god Hercules, expressing fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600735.translation: Lucius Cornelius Valentinus and Cornelia Lucina willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Hercules.
EDCS-08600735.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Cornelius Valentinus and Cornelia Lucina to the god Hercules, expressing fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600737.translation: To LLA THALI, Caius Julius, son of HAI.
EDCS-08600737.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to LLA THALI, son of Caius Julius and HAI, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600740.translation: Here rests in peace Paulinus, of good memory, who lived for more or less 35 years and died on the 12th day before the Kalends of April, in the fifth indiction. Here rests in peace Tecla, of good memory, who lived for more or less 5 years and died on the 4th day before the Kalends of November, in the sixth indiction.
EDCS-08600740.summary: This is a Christian tombstone inscription recording the deaths of Paulinus and Tecla, with specific dates and indiction years, likely from the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-08600753.translation: Saturninus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Parcae (Fates).
EDCS-08600753.summary: This is a votive inscription from Saturninus to the Parcae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900473.translation: For the memory of Julia Anniola, deceased, aged seven years. Marcus Iulius Callistus, for his dearest foster-child.
EDCS-08900473.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Anniola, erected by Marcus Iulius Callistus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900474.translation: Gemellianus made this at the waters of the Helvetii.
EDCS-08900474.summary: This inscription records Gemellianus dedicating something at a spa or medicinal spring in the territory of the Helvetii, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901045.translation: I will dig up the adversary.
EDCS-08901045.summary: This inscription is a short, potentially hostile statement, its context and date are unclear.

EDCS-09001488.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Lucius Attenius Rusticus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001494.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Sempronius Polyclitus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001498.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Lucius Valerius Vit[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001499.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Marcus Annius R[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001502.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Pompeius Eros, possibly as a dedicator or the subject of a memorial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001503.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Pompeius Urbanus, possibly as a dedicator or the subject of a memorial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001504.summary: This is a personal name, likely the owner or subject of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09001505.summary: This is a personal name, likely the owner or subject of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09001506.summary: This is a personal name, likely the owner or subject of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09001507.summary: This is a personal name, likely the owner or subject of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09001508.summary: This is a personal name, likely the owner or subject of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09001511.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Tiberius Julius Eudoxus, daughter of Terna Suadulla.
EDCS-09001511.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Eudoxus, daughter of Suadulla.

EDCS-09001512.translation: 95 and a half. Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 195 and a half. G. Primus received for Charitianus from Aelia Aellana 57 and a half, 195 and a half. Anicetus.
EDCS-09001512.summary: This inscription appears to record financial transactions or distributions, possibly related to inheritance or bequests, involving several individuals and quantities measured in units and half-units.

EDCS-09001513.translation: 90. Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 207 and a half. G. Primus received for Charitianus from Aelia Aellana 57 and a half, 207 and a half. Anicetus.
EDCS-09001513.summary: This inscription appears to record financial transactions or distributions, possibly related to inheritance or bequests, involving several individuals and quantities measured in units and half-units.

EDCS-09001514.translation: 96. Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 194 and a half. G. Primus received for Charitianus from Aelia Aellana 57 and a half, 194 and a half. Anicetus.
EDCS-09001514.summary: This inscription appears to record financial transactions or distributions, possibly related to inheritance or bequests, involving several individuals and quantities measured in units and half-units.

EDCS-09001515.translation: 91. Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 215 and a half. G. Primus received for Charisianus from Aelia Aellana 57 and a half, 215 and a half. Anicetus.
EDCS-09001515.summary: This inscription appears to record financial transactions or distributions, possibly related to inheritance or bequests, involving several individuals and quantities measured in units and half-units.

EDCS-09001516.translation: Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 295.5 (units) of charisianum (wine or oil) for Aelia Aeliana, 57.5 units, and 295.5 units again, from G. Primus.
EDCS-09001516.summary: This inscription records a transaction of 295.5 units of charisianum, likely a commodity, involving Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus and Aelia Aeliana, dated by the format of the numbers.

EDCS-09001518.translation: Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 195.5 (units) of veturianum (wine or oil) for Pontius Astius, 10 units, from Martialis. G. received 195.5 units.
EDCS-09001518.summary: This inscription documents a transaction of 195.5 units of veturianum, likely a commodity, involving Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus and Pontius Astius, with contributions from Martialis and G.

EDCS-09001519.translation: Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus received 213.5 (units) of veturianum (wine or oil) for Pontius Astius, 10 units, from Martialis. G. received 213.5 units.
EDCS-09001519.summary: This inscription records a transaction of 213.5 units of veturianum, likely a commodity, involving Lucius Antonius Epaphroditus and Pontius Astius, with contributions from Martialis and G.

EDCS-09001520.translation: Melissus and Peregrinus Antonius received 1293.5 (units) of allecr for Marciana Aelia, 56 units, from C. Onius. Anicetus received 1293.5 units.
EDCS-09001520.summary: This inscription details a transaction involving Melissus and Peregrinus Antonius, concerning allecr for Marciana Aelia, with Anicetus as a recipient.

EDCS-09001521.translation: Quitius Vinisius Serenus received 207.5 units, twice, from V. busti ti Astig.
EDCS-09001521.summary: This inscription records the reception of 207.5 units of something, twice, by Quitius Vinisius Serenus, indicating a transaction or payment.

EDCS-09001523.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription or name stone for Quintus Tossius Priscus.

EDCS-09001524.translation: Gaius Octavius Dasius made this in Forum Iulii.
EDCS-09001524.summary: This is a dedication or construction inscription indicating that Gaius Octavius Dasius made this monument or object in Forum Iulii.

EDCS-09200338.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Maritumus, son of Secundus.

EDCS-09200339.translation: To the Divine Shades of Acceptus Escingomarus, son (of someone), and to the Divine Shades of Vernio, son of Acceptus.
EDCS-09200339.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the deceased, honoring Acceptus Escingomarus and Vernio, son of Acceptus.

EDCS-09200345.translation: To the Divine Shades, from Vettia Valeria to her dearest husband Quintus Cornelius Lepidus, son of Quintus, aged 30, and to his most dutiful son Quintus Cornelius Attianus.
EDCS-09200345.summary: This is a bilingual funerary inscription dedicated by a wife and son to their deceased husband and father, Quintus Cornelius Lepidus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-09200346.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Calvius Exoratus to Maritumus and Valeria Ricconis, his wife, daughter of Ricconis.
EDCS-09200346.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Calvius Exoratus to his wife Valeria Ricconis and Maritumus.

EDCS-09200347.translation: Lucius Valerius Cometius, veteran of the Eighth Augustan Legion, served in the vanguard, his heir by will.
EDCS-09200347.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Valerius Cometius, a veteran of the Eighth Augustan Legion, who served in the vanguard and appointed his heir by will.

EDCS-09200350.translation: Here lies in peace Vernaclus, who lived for 10 years and months.
EDCS-09200350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vernaclus, who lived for 10 years and some months.

EDCS-09200588.translation: Here lies in peace of good memory Teodemodos, who lived approximately 41 years; I noted it on the ninth day before the Kalends of January, in the tenth indiction.
EDCS-09200588.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Teodemodos, who lived approximately 41 years and died on December 24th in the 10th indiction.

EDCS-09200589.translation: Sext(i) Solli Demosthenianus to his most devoted father and Thaeodotia Basilissa to her incomparable husband and Sollius Olympiodoros to his dearest freedman.
EDCS-09200589.summary: This funerary inscription is a multi-part dedication from Sextus Sollius Demosthenianus, Thaeodotia Basilissa, and Sollius Olympiodoros to their respective family members.

EDCS-09200593.translation: Here lies in peace of good memory Gundiisclus, in hope of the resurrection of Christ's mercy, who lived in the world 68 years, died in Christ on the 10th day before the Kalends of February, after the consulship of Basilius, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-09200593.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gundiisclus, who lived 68 years and died on January 23rd, after the consulship of Basilius, in the 11th indiction, with a hope of resurrection.

EDCS-09200594.translation: To Gaius Ap Vercius Gallicus, son of Vercius, from the temple treasury.
EDCS-09200594.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Ap Vercius Gallicus from the temple treasury (stipe).

EDCS-09200595.translation: Here lies in peace of good memory Adica, who lived six years and five months, died in Christ on the nineteenth day before the Kalends of September, twenty-three times after the consulship of the venerable and most illustrious consul Basilius, in the thirteenth indiction.
EDCS-09200595.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Adica, who lived 6 years and 5 months and died on August 14th, after the consulship of Basilius, in the 13th indiction.

EDCS-09200598.translation: Here lies in peace Dulcitius, who lived 7 years and 23 days, to be resurrected in Christ.
EDCS-09200598.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Dulcitius, who lived 7 years and 23 days and is described as expecting resurrection in Christ.

EDCS-09200600.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Catia Catiola to her dearest husband Gaius Cacusius Lucanus, duovir of the aerarium.
EDCS-09200600.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Catia Catiola to her husband Gaius Cacusius Lucanus, who held the office of duovir of the aerarium.

EDCS-09200601.translation: Always devoted to charitable works, she rejoiced in the fruit of her repentance, and by Christ's grace, lived for sixty-seven years, departing this life on the eighth day before the Ides of December, when Mavortius was consul, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-09200601.summary: This inscription records the death of a woman who lived a penitent life, dated by the consul Mavortius, likely in the 4th or 5th century CE.

EDCS-09200602.translation: Sacred to Maia Augusta. Titus Eppius Iullinus, son of Decimus, by vow.
EDCS-09200602.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Maia Augusta by Titus Eppius Iullinus.

EDCS-09200603.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Titus Eppius Iullinus, son of Decimus, by vow.
EDCS-09200603.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mercury Augustus by Titus Eppius Iullinus.

EDCS-09200604.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Titus Eppius Iullinus, son of Decimus, by vow.
EDCS-09200604.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mercury Augustus by Titus Eppius Iullinus.

EDCS-09200612.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mars Augustus for Rudianus.

EDCS-09200618.translation: Caius Bicatus Potitus, a centurion of the first rank, and Viria Terentia, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-09200618.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Bicatus Potitus and Viria Terentia, who prepared it for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-09200619.translation: To Lucius Maecius Maelon, son of Tertius, who died at Ostia on the Tiber, aged 50. His sons made this.
EDCS-09200619.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius Maecius Maelon, set up by his sons.

EDCS-09200620.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Titus Sammins Tertiolo, a scribe of the treasury, who died at the age of 26. Connia Conniola to her most excellent husband and Sammius Connius to his father.
EDCS-09200620.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Sammins Tertiolo, set up by his wife and son.

EDCS-09200621.translation: To Mercury Augustus. Dannia Martina, by vow.
EDCS-09200621.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mercury Augustus by Dannia Martina.

EDCS-09200622.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Texatius Stephanus placed this for Paulina and S[---], his incomparable wife and dearest son, and dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-09200622.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Quintus Texatius Stephanus for his wife and son, likely marking a burial under an 'ascia' (axe) symbol.

EDCS-09200623.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius. Caecus, attendant of Isis, set this up.
EDCS-09200623.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Aesculapius by Caecus, an attendant of the goddess Isis.

EDCS-09200624.translation: Lucius Surius Iucundus willingly and deservedly paid his vows to the Fortunes.
EDCS-09200624.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Fortunes made by Lucius Surius Iucundus.

EDCS-09200625.translation: Sextus Claudius Valerianus gave this altar with its ornaments to the Mother Isis, as he had vowed.
EDCS-09200625.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the Mother Isis by Sextus Claudius Valerianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09200626.translation: Decimus Decmanius Caper ordered this to be set up by his will.
EDCS-09200626.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument ordered by Decimus Decmanius Caper in his will, dedicated to the god Mars.

EDCS-09200627.translation: Cassi Severinus Censorinus to Mars Augustus.
EDCS-09200627.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus by Cassi Severinus Censorinus.

EDCS-09200628.translation: Titus Cassius Eros, sacred to the August Mother.
EDCS-09200628.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the August Mother by Titus Cassius Eros, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-09200629.translation: Lucretia, freedwoman of Quintus, [dedicated this] to the Nemetial Mother.
EDCS-09200629.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Nemetial Mother by Lucretia, a freedwoman, possibly from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09200631.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mercury by Lucius Manilius Silanus.

EDCS-09200633.translation: Decimus Decmanius Caper ordered this to be set up by his will.
EDCS-09200633.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument ordered by Decimus Decmanius Caper in his will, dedicated to the god Saturn.

EDCS-09200635.translation: To the divine spirits. Aurelius Primitivus, scribe of the station of Cularo, and Quartinia Mitana, his wife, give this gift for themselves and their heirs.
EDCS-09200635.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by Aurelius Primitivus and his wife Quartinia Mitana to the divine spirits (DM) for themselves and their heirs.

EDCS-09200640.translation: To Pompeius Pollio, military tribune of the First Legion.
EDCS-09200640.summary: This inscription honors Pompeius Pollio, identified as a military tribune of the First Legion.

EDCS-09200646.translation: To T. C. A. Secundus, quinquennial and a member of the college of seven men.
EDCS-09200646.summary: This inscription honors T. C. A. Secundus, who held the office of quinquennial and was a member of a college of seven men.

EDCS-09200658.translation: Marcus Rufius Marcianus made this vow.
EDCS-09200658.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Marcus Rufius Marcianus.

EDCS-09200659.summary: This inscription records an action performed by Lucius Scribonius Martinus.

EDCS-09200661.translation: To the Divine Manes of Titus Aelius Fortunatus and Papiria Quieta, parents, and of Titus Aelius Quietus, brother, from the most dutiful Titus Aelius Fortunatus.
EDCS-09200661.summary: This funerary inscription honors deceased family members: parents Titus Aelius Fortunatus and Papiria Quieta, and brother Titus Aelius Quietus, dedicated by Titus Aelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-09200662.translation: To the Divine Manes of Titus Aelius Taurus, freedman of the Augusti, from Sabinus Marcellinus and Taurus for his most dutiful father and incomparable wife, Italica.
EDCS-09200662.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Sabinus Marcellinus and Taurus to his father Titus Aelius Taurus, a freedman of the emperors, and to his wife Italica.

EDCS-09200664.translation: To Quintus Andius Quintanus, son of Quintinus, aged 20.
EDCS-09200664.summary: This inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Quintus Andius Quintanus, who died at twenty.

EDCS-09200666.translation: To the Divine Manes of Marcus Antonius Eudaemon and his wife Vireia Gratina, from their daughter Antonia Gratinula and Grata, for their most dutiful parents who perished and were buried together.
EDCS-09200666.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Antonius Eudaemon and his wife Vireia Gratina, who died and were buried together, dedicated by their daughters Antonia Gratinula and Grata.

EDCS-09200667.translation: To the Divine Manes of Apronia Casata, daughter of Sabinus, from Publius Helvius Masso, for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-09200667.summary: This funerary inscription honors Apronia Casata, daughter of Sabinus, dedicated by her husband Publius Helvius Masso.

EDCS-09200669.summary: This inscription records a dedication or mention of Atticilla by Iulianus, with missing details.

EDCS-09200670.translation: To Lucius Attius Julianus, son of Caius, and to Bellica, daughter of Titus, wife of Ingenuia, Julianus and Atticus, sons of Attius, [erected this] to [their] parents.
EDCS-09200670.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Julianus and Atticus to their parents, Lucius Attius Julianus and Bellica.

EDCS-09200672.translation: To the Divine Shades of Cassia Paulinula, aged 17 years. Publius Cassius Hermetio [erected this] to his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-09200672.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Publius Cassius Hermetio to his daughter Cassia Paulinula, who died at age 17.

EDCS-09200673.translation: To Publius Catilius Verterus, the heir in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09200673.summary: This inscription records that Publius Catilius Verterus is the heir of the deceased, as stated in their will.

EDCS-09200674.translation: To Quintus Ciltius Mansuetus of the Voltinian tribe, the heir in accordance with the will.
EDCS-09200674.summary: This inscription records that Quintus Ciltius Mansuetus is the heir of the deceased, as stated in their will.

EDCS-09200675.translation: To the Divine Shades and to the most devoted Crisus, son of Secundus, [erected this] to [his] most beloved father.
EDCS-09200675.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Crisus to his beloved father.

EDCS-09200676.translation: To the Divine Shades of Decimia Albina. Quintus Vettius Epictetus [erected this] to his most chaste wife.
EDCS-09200676.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Vettius Epictetus to his wife Decimia Albina.

EDCS-09200678.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal memory of Devillia Atilia. Lucius Lepidius Basilaeus had this set up and dedicated it under the tile to his most beloved wife.
EDCS-09200678.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Lepidius Basilaeus to his wife Devillia Atilia, who died at an unknown date.

EDCS-09200679.translation: To the Divine Shades of Devillia Catullina. Baebius Gratinus and Baebia Gratina [erected this] to their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-09200679.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Baebius Gratinus and Baebia Gratina to their mother Devillia Catullina.

EDCS-09200680.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal memory of Gnatia Verinula. Militius Titullinus dedicated this to his beloved wife under the tile.
EDCS-09200680.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Militius Titullinus to his wife Gnatia Verinula, who died at an unknown date.

EDCS-09200681.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal memory of Innocentius, son of Sico, aged six years, nine months, and sixteen days, a deserving son.
EDCS-09200681.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Innocentius, son of Sico, who died at the age of six years, nine months, and sixteen days.

EDCS-09200686.translation: To Labeo of the Elian tribe.
EDCS-09200686.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Labeo, likely a member of the Elian tribe, possibly dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200695.summary: This is a dedication to Rutilius Verinius, the purpose and date of which are unclear due to its fragmentary nature.

EDCS-09200702.translation: To Marcus Valerius's most dutiful mother.
EDCS-09200702.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Valerius to his highly devoted mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200704.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions two individuals, I. I. and Valerius, its precise context and date are unknown.

EDCS-09200711.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication mentioning R. as the son of Gaius, its context and date are uncertain.

EDCS-09200721.translation: Here rests John of good memory and Julia Devalentia, who lived 14 years and died in peace, in the consulship of the most illustrious Symmachus and Boethius.
EDCS-09200721.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription recording the death of John and Julia Devalentia in 487 AD.

EDCS-09200723.translation: To the spirits of the departed; here rests Pro[...]
EDCS-09200723.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld deities (Dis Manibus) and mentioning an individual named Pro[...], likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200724.translation: In this tomb rests NPA of good memory in the hope of resurrection.
EDCS-09200724.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription recording the burial of NPA, who died in the hope of resurrection, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-09200725.translation: Here rests in peace S [...] of good memory, who lived [...]
EDCS-09200725.summary: This is a fragmentary Christian funerary inscription for an individual named S, who died in peace and lived for an unspecified duration, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-09200728.translation: Marcus Sollius Marcellus ordered this to be set up by his will; Gaius Sollius Verus had it set up as heir.
EDCS-09200728.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcus Sollius Marcellus arranged for his tomb to be erected in his will, and Gaius Sollius Verus saw to its construction as his heir, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200729.translation: Maximilla ordered this to be set up in her will for Julia Vera, daughter of Quintus, a priestess of Augusta.
EDCS-09200729.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Maximilla to Julia Vera, a priestess of Augusta, with the inscription indicating it was ordered by will.

EDCS-09200730.translation: To Apollo, in fulfillment of a vow, from Iustus, slave of Mercurius and Ceres.
EDCS-09200730.summary: This is a votive offering to the god Apollo, made by a slave named Iustus.

EDCS-09200731.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Nigrius Crispinianus set this up for his most devoted wife, Iulia Decumilla, daughter of Decumus. Iulia I Musticus carved it.
EDCS-09200731.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Marcus Nigrius Crispinianus to his wife Iulia Decumilla, with the carver also identified.

EDCS-09200732.translation: Maximilla ordered this to be set up in her will for Publius Lucretius Parvolus, son of Publius, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-09200732.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Maximilla to Publius Lucretius Parvolus, indicating it was ordered by will.

EDCS-09200733.translation: Quintus Maccius [set this up] for Quintus Maccius Virilis.
EDCS-09200733.summary: This brief inscription likely records a dedication or commemoration by Quintus Maccius for Quintus Maccius Virilis.

EDCS-09200738.translation: Lupecinus rests in this tomb, Lupecinus of good memory, who lived for 58 years and 6 months. He died on the 7th day before the Kalends of March, after the consulship of Mavortius.
EDCS-09200738.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the name, age, date of death, and burial of Lupecinus, with a specific post-consular dating.

EDCS-09200739.translation: Pompeia Sextina, daughter of Titus, dedicated this to Titus Pompeius Albinus, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, tribune of the soldiers of the VI Victrix legion, sub-procurator of the province of Lusitania, and twice chief magistrate with judicial power of the colony Iulia Augusta Florentia Viennensium.
EDCS-09200739.summary: This is a dedication by Pompeia Sextina to her husband Titus Pompeius Albinus, detailing his military and civic career.

EDCS-09200741.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Valerius Marcellinus, his father.
EDCS-09200741.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Valerius Marcellinus dedicating the monument to his deceased son, Marcus Valerius Marcianus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09200744.translation: Caius Licinius Calvinus [dedicated] the basilica to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-09200744.summary: This inscription records Caius Licinius Calvinus dedicating a basilica to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-09200750.translation: [This monument was set up] for Lucius Iulius Albinus, to his father.
EDCS-09200750.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by an unnamed son to his father, Lucius Iulius Albinus.

EDCS-09200751.translation: To Mercury, Gaius Betutius Bassinus and Quintus Betutius Gratinus [fulfilled] their vow.
EDCS-09200751.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury, fulfilling a vow, made by Gaius Betutius Bassinus and Quintus Betutius Gratinus.

EDCS-09200752.translation: Titus Domitius Montanus, son of Titus, priest of Mercury, gave this from his own property.
EDCS-09200752.summary: This records a dedication to Mercury made by Titus Domitius Montanus, the priest of Mercury, from his own funds.

EDCS-09200754.translation: To Apollo Augustus, Sextus Atilius Tertius [fulfilled] his vow.
EDCS-09200754.summary: This is a votive dedication to Apollo Augustus by Sextus Atilius Tertius.

EDCS-09200758.translation: To Sextus Julius Valentinus, son of Sextus, prefect of the canton Vale, twice chief magistrate, Sextus Masvinnius Verinus [erected this] as the most excellent and deserving guardian who supported me like a parent for 14 years and brought me to a certain prosperity.
EDCS-09200758.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Sextus Masvinnius Verinus to his guardian, Sextus Julius Valentinus, a local official, who had provided for him for 14 years.

EDCS-09200759.translation: To Lucius Cassius Valentinus, son of Bassus, and to Duronia Gae, his wife.
EDCS-09200759.summary: This records the dedication of a tombstone to Lucius Cassius Valentinus and his wife Duronia Gae.

EDCS-09200760.translation: The steward of the Mithras association, XL, [assigned] plot 13 by the tower, 6 plots through the field.
EDCS-09200760.summary: This inscription records the allocation of land plots by the steward of the Mithras association.

EDCS-09200766.translation: To the departed spirits, for Marcus Ulpius Paulinus, my sweetest soul, who lived 32 years and 5 months.
EDCS-09200766.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Ulpius Paulinus, who died at the age of 32 years and 5 months.

EDCS-09200767.translation: To the departed spirits, [from] Gaius Didi Secundus, soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, from the century of Marius.
EDCS-09200767.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Didi Secundus, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion.

EDCS-09200768.translation: Dubnacus, son of Cobrovillus, erected this while alive for himself and for Bituna Troucetis, his wife, daughter of Namae Veruci.
EDCS-09200768.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Dubnacus, son of Cobrovillus, for himself and his wife Bituna.

EDCS-09200769.translation: To Pompeia Iunior, daughter of Iunicius, her most devoted father [erected this].
EDCS-09200769.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father to his daughter, Pompeia Iunior.

EDCS-09200770.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sextus Blaesius Pansinus to his most dutiful father, Sextus Blaesius Faustinus, and to his most dutiful wife, Labenia Martina.
EDCS-09200770.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sextus Blaesius Pansinus for his father and wife.

EDCS-09200771.translation: of Rufus, son of Lucius Rufus, son of Cato, of Sentinius Rufus.
EDCS-09200771.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a longer genealogy or dedication.

EDCS-09200772.translation: He ordered it to be set up to his freedman [---] IMO M by will.
EDCS-09200772.summary: This inscription records a testamentary disposition for a freedman, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09200773.translation: Here shines his great merit; here his first youth. Arcadius, once called by his lord, a pure servant to his lord, kind and gentle, and destined...
EDCS-09200773.summary: This is a fragmented epitaph praising the deceased, Arcadius, highlighting his virtuous character and service.

EDCS-09200776.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pompeius Octavianus to his dearest wife Parthenope, who lived 28 years, 5 months, and 5 days, a woman of strong and most holy spirit. We are mortal beings.
EDCS-09200776.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Pompeius Octavianus to his wife Parthenope, including details of her life and a philosophical reflection on mortality.

EDCS-09200779.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iustius Iustinus dedicated this, below the blade (i.e., with a funerary dedication), to his daughter Iustiola and to his incomparable wife Statoria Magniana.
EDCS-09200779.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Iustius Iustinus to his daughter and wife, indicating a burial beneath the inscription.

EDCS-09200783.translation: Sacred to Augustus, to the god Mercury Victor, the god Macniacus Vellaunus. Caius Capitoius Macrinus restored this.
EDCS-09200783.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a sacred monument dedicated to Mercury, Augustus, and Celtic deities by Caius Capitoius Macrinus.

EDCS-09200788.translation: To Mercury Augustus. Sennius Martius and Melius Martinianus, augurs, from the offerings.
EDCS-09200788.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury Augustus made by two augurs, using funds from offerings.

EDCS-09200790.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and eternal memory. Vitellius Silvinus, father, ordered this memorial to be made for his most dutiful son, Vitellius Dextrianus.
EDCS-09200790.summary: This funerary epitaph records a father's provision for a memorial for his son, Vitellius Dextrianus.

EDCS-09200792.translation: In this tomb rests Arigunde of good memory, who lived 8 years and died in peace eight times after the consulship of the most illustrious John, on the tenth day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-09200792.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Arigunde, detailing her age, date of death, and referencing a specific consul, suggesting a late antique date.

EDCS-09200793.translation: To Jupiter Baginatis, Corinthus, son of Nigidius Aelianus, according to his vow.
EDCS-09200793.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Baginatis by Corinthus.

EDCS-09200794.translation: Here rests in peace Aisberga, a girl pleasing to God, who preserved her virginal life with all honor, lived 24 years, died on the fourth day before the Kalends of December in the fifteenth indiction, when Olybrius the Younger was consul.
EDCS-09200794.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aisberga, dating to the consular year of Olybrius the Younger.

EDCS-09200795.translation: Sentinia Decumina made this for Titus Sentinius, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, her husband, and for herself.
EDCS-09200795.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Sentinia Decumina for her husband Titus Sentinius and herself.

EDCS-09200796.translation: To Asclepius Augustus, Marcus Pennius Apollonius.
EDCS-09200796.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to the god Asclepius Augustus by Marcus Pennius Apollonius.

EDCS-09200797.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to the goddess Juno by Severianus.

EDCS-09200802.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Sextus Vireius Sextus, decurion, dedicated the roof of a portico with its columns, two cloaks (?), and plasterwork from his own funds, when Pollio II and Aper II were consuls.
EDCS-09200802.summary: This is a dedication of public works, specifically the roof of a portico, for the welfare of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, funded by the decurion Sextus Vireius Sextus during the consulship of Pollio II and Aper II.

EDCS-09200803.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Sextus Vireius Sextus, decurion, dedicated the roof of a portico with its columns, two cloaks (?), and plasterwork from his own funds, when Pollio II and Aper II were consuls.
EDCS-09200803.summary: This is a dedication of public works, specifically the roof of a portico, for the welfare of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, funded by the decurion Sextus Vireius Sextus during the consulship of Pollio II and Aper II.

EDCS-09200804.translation: From the Augustan villagers, for Lucius Julius Frontonus, prefect of the cavalry, twice quinquennial magistrate.
EDCS-09200804.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the villagers of Augustana to Lucius Julius Frontonus, who held significant administrative and military positions.

EDCS-09200805.translation: Successus, son of Silvanus, cavalryman of the I Augusta Ala, first turma of Julius Octavus, with 14 years of service, lived 40 years.
EDCS-09200805.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Successus, a cavalryman who served for 14 years and lived to be 40.

EDCS-09200806.translation: Gaius Valerius Praefectus of the Octavian pagus, in his own name and that of his sons, to the Augustan villagers.
EDCS-09200806.summary: This inscription records an action or dedication by Gaius Valerius, prefect of the Octavian pagus, to the Augustan villagers.

EDCS-09200807.translation: To Caius Atisius Primus, public servant of the twentieth tax district of the province of Gallia Narbonensis, for his dearest wife Saturnina.
EDCS-09200807.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a public official in Roman Gaul for his wife, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200808.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to Ulpia Anthusa of Agrippinensis; Marcus Aurelius Adiutor, freedman of the Emperors, for his dearest wife.
EDCS-09200808.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Ulpia Anthusa by her husband, a freedman of the emperors, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200809.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to the eternal rest of Rhodia, deceased at 34 years of age; Nicephorus for his sweetest wife, who lived with me for 20 years, 10 months, and 12 days, and for Rhodius and Niceta, her sons, to their dearest mother.
EDCS-09200809.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rhodia, recording her age and the duration of her marriage, set up by her husband and sons, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200811.translation: Severinus and his grandsons took care to erect this and dedicated it under the axe symbol.
EDCS-09200811.summary: This records the funerary monument erected by Severinus and his grandsons, likely from the later Roman or early medieval period.

EDCS-09200812.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for a tombstone or a personal marker, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-09200814.translation: To his wife, 9 years old, acephalous, onni.
EDCS-09200814.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a dedication to a wife, possibly very young, with an unclear final word, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-09200815.translation: Here rests in good memory in peace Aliberga, who lived 30 years, died in Christ on the 3rd day before the Nones of February, after the consulship of Symmachus and Boethus, most illustrious men.
EDCS-09200815.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Aliberga, recording her age and date of death with consular dating, likely from the late 5th or early 6th century.

EDCS-09200816.translation: Here rests in good memory in peace Ingildus, who lived 4 years and 8 months, died in Christ on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November, in the third year after the consulship of Paulinus the Younger, most illustrious man and consul.
EDCS-09200816.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Ingildus, recording his age and date of death with consular dating, likely from the 6th century.

EDCS-09200817.translation: Here rests in good memory in peace the youth of untainted flesh, named Leudomarus, who lived 4 years and 8 days, died in Christ on the 15th day before the Kalends of March, in the sixth year after the consulship of Basilius, most illustrious man and consul.
EDCS-09200817.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for a young boy, Leudomarus, recording his age and date of death with consular dating, likely from the 6th century.

EDCS-09200818.translation: Here rests in good memory in peace Teoptecunda, who lived 10 years, died in Christ on the 6th day before the Kalends of December, in the ninth year after the consulship of John, most illustrious man and consul.
EDCS-09200818.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Teoptecunda, recording her age and date of death with consular dating, likely from the 6th century.

EDCS-09200819.translation: Here rests in peace of blessed memory Eusebia, a sacred girl devoted to God, whose praiseworthy life earned her the right to have Christ as a bridegroom, like the wise virgins, with whom she will rise again.
EDCS-09200819.summary: An epitaph for Eusebia, a Christian girl, likely from the late Roman period, highlighting her virtuous life and Christian hope of resurrection.

EDCS-09200820.translation: Magnus lived here for 35 years and died in peace on the Kalends of February.
EDCS-09200820.summary: An epitaph for Magnus, recording his age and date of death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200821.translation: Quintus Valerius Senecio gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Augustus.
EDCS-09200821.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Valerius Senecio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200822.translation: To Gaius Aquillius Priscus, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-09200822.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Aquillius Priscus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200823.translation: To Claudia Albina, daughter of Tiberius Claudius Germanicus, naval officer of the fleet, from her husband Iulius Pompeius Priscus, to his most excellent wife.
EDCS-09200823.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Albina, daughter of a naval officer, dedicated by her husband Iulius Pompeius Priscus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200825.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, from Sextus Cornelius Pollio in his own name and in the name of his brother Lucius Cornelius Corbulon.
EDCS-09200825.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Cornelius Pollio on behalf of himself and his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200826.translation: Caius Betutius Hermes, a member of the college of six priests, decreed in his will that this be set up to Mars Augustus.
EDCS-09200826.summary: A funerary or dedication inscription for Caius Betutius Hermes, set up by his will to Mars Augustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200827.translation: To Titus Crispius Antiquus and his daughter Ambarria, daughter of Caius, and to Remulla, Quintus Virius Crispus for his brother and mother.
EDCS-09200827.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Quintus Virius Crispus to his brother Titus Crispius Antiquus and his mother Remulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200829.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Lucius Maternius Eutyches placed this for his most excellent wife Faustia Verula and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-09200829.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Faustia Verula by her husband Lucius Maternius Eutyches, including a dedication under the axe, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200833.translation: Here rests in peace Claudia, servant of God, who lived 34 years, 4 months, and 14 days, and died on the 19th day before the Kalends of February, after the consulship of Anthemius and Florentius, most distinguished consuls.
EDCS-09200833.summary: A Christian epitaph for Claudia, a servant of God, recording her lifespan and date of death within a specific consular period, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-09200834.translation: In this place rests Ervalda, a penitent woman, fearing God and a lady, in the hope of eternal resurrection, who lived forty-two years and six months and died on the fifth day before the Kalends of November in the 14th indiction.
EDCS-09200834.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ervalda, detailing her life and death, likely from late antiquity given the mention of 'indiction'.

EDCS-09200837.translation: Titus Silvinius Catullus, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-09200837.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating something to a deity, offered by Titus Silvinius Catullus.

EDCS-09200840.translation: Gratus, son of Cassius, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-09200840.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating something to a deity, offered by Gratus, son of Cassius.

EDCS-09200841.translation: Quintus Iulius Macrinus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the Genius of the household.
EDCS-09200841.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating something to the Genius of the household by Quintus Iulius Macrinus.

EDCS-09200844.translation: To Domitia, daughter of Decimus, of the Voltinian tribe, and to Gaius Vibrius Catianus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe.
EDCS-09200844.summary: This inscription records a dedication to two individuals, Domitia and Gaius Vibrius Catianus, likely a tombstone or a dedication of honor.

EDCS-09200847.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Titus Terentius Catullus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09200847.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury Augustus by Titus Terentius Catullus.

EDCS-09200848.translation: To Mercury and Minerva. Albanus Valerius.
EDCS-09200848.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the gods Mercury and Minerva by Albanus Valerius.

EDCS-09200849.translation: To Mercury Augustus. Sextus Apicius Successus, in accordance with his vow, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09200849.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury Augustus by Sextus Apicius Successus.

EDCS-09200851.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Caius, son of Voltinius Sabinus.
EDCS-09200851.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Emperor Augustus by Caius, son of Voltinius Sabinus.

EDCS-09200852.translation: To the god Mercury, the Propitious. By order of Uspirniolus.
EDCS-09200852.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury by Uspirniolus, possibly in fulfillment of a command or request.

EDCS-09200853.translation: Severianus placed [this] for the Unconquered God Nabarz.
EDCS-09200853.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Severianus to the god Nabarz, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-09200854.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Quintus Carminius [erected this] for Quintus Carminius Bellicus.
EDCS-09200854.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Carminius for Quintus Carminius Bellicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200855.translation: Marcus Licinius Ruso gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Bormo.
EDCS-09200855.summary: This inscription records Marcus Licinius Ruso fulfilling a vow to the deity Bormo, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200856.translation: Quintus Vettius Guticus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Borvo.
EDCS-09200856.summary: This inscription records Quintus Vettius Guticus fulfilling a vow to the deity Borvo, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200858.translation: Marcus Carminius Magnus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Lords for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-09200858.summary: This inscription records Marcus Carminius Magnus fulfilling a vow to household deities for his well-being and that of his relatives.

EDCS-09200860.translation: Lucius Daverius M gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the August Mother.
EDCS-09200860.summary: This inscription records Lucius Daverius M fulfilling a vow to the "August Mother", likely a local or imperial cult deity.

EDCS-09200862.translation: Quintus Clodius Potitus, freedman of Quintus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09200862.summary: This inscription records Quintus Clodius Potitus, a freedman, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200864.translation: [dedicated to]nio, son of Marcius, Vestinus of the Voltinian tribe, from Mallon, honored with all dignities in Vienna, and admitted to the most distinguished senatorial order.
EDCS-09200864.summary: This inscription honors Vestinus, son of Marcius, a member of the Voltinian tribe and from Mallon, for his public service and senatorial appointment in Vienna.

EDCS-09200886.translation: Caius Vibrius Punicus [erected this] for his father, Lucius Vibrius Punicus, a cavalry prefect, a first-rank centurion, and prefect of Corsica.
EDCS-09200886.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Caius Vibrius Punicus for his father, Lucius Vibrius Punicus, who held several distinguished military and administrative posts.

EDCS-09200887.translation: Titus Marcius Taurinus, prefect of the artificers, prefect of the Third Gallic Cohort, military tribune of the Sixth Legion Victrix, erected [this] for himself while alive.
EDCS-09200887.summary: This inscription records Titus Marcius Taurinus, who held several military and administrative positions, setting up a monument for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-09200888.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titia Crispinia. Decimus Titius Apolaustianus, Augustalis priest (member of the six-man board).
EDCS-09200888.summary: This funerary inscription records Decimus Titius Apolaustianus dedicating a monument to Titia Crispinia, serving as an Augustalis priest.

EDCS-09200890.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Decimus Titius Domitinus. The landowners of Aquae and Decimus Titius Domitius, for his father.
EDCS-09200890.summary: This funerary inscription records the landowners of Aquae and Decimus Titius Domitius dedicating a monument to Decimus Titius Domitinus, likely his father.

EDCS-09200891.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titia Chelidon. The landowners of the district, through the care of her husband Gaius Iulius Marcellinus.
EDCS-09200891.summary: This funerary inscription records the district landowners dedicating a monument to Titia Chelidon, with her husband Gaius Iulius Marcellinus overseeing the dedication.

EDCS-09200892.translation: The decemvirs (ten officials) of the district of Aquae and its patrons, from their own funds, for the gift of the pottery workshops which they granted to the possessors and inhabitants of the vicus of Aquae for a feast and a lake, along with its produce; the patrons Rufius Iulianus Smerius Mansuetus, the decemvirs Canius Decimus Titius Hyginus Tincius Derco Iulius Spinter Smerius Licinianus Romanius Valerius Cacusius Decimianus Aelius Decumanus Sollius Seius R.
EDCS-09200892.summary: This inscription records the decemvirs and patrons of the district of Aquae dedicating a gift of pottery workshops for a feast and lake, funded by their own contributions.

EDCS-09200898.translation: For Titus Atilius Priscianus Rufinus, to his most dutiful mother and only example.
EDCS-09200898.summary: This dedication is made by Titus Atilius Priscianus Rufinus to his mother, praising her piety and exemplary character.

EDCS-09200900.translation: To the spirits. The son of Didius lies here.
EDCS-09200900.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription identifying the deceased as the son of Didius.

EDCS-09200901.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Eleutherio, most sweet infant, who lived 6 years and 42 days. Decimus Titius Rhenus and Titia Eleutheris, his devoted parents, for their most devoted son.
EDCS-09200901.summary: This funerary inscription records Decimus Titius Rhenus and Titia Eleutheris dedicating a monument to their infant son, Eleutherio.

EDCS-09200902.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for a tombstone or marker, identifying Gnaeus Eppius Gallus.

EDCS-09200905.translation: For Lucius Iulius Capito and Iulia Tertia. Gnaeus Iulius Lucinus, for his parents.
EDCS-09200905.summary: This inscription records Gnaeus Iulius Lucinus dedicating a monument to his parents, Lucius Iulius Capito and Iulia Tertia.

EDCS-09200906.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Marius Iaracion. Titia Severina, to her most holy husband, and Decimus Titius Severinus, for his father.
EDCS-09200906.summary: This funerary inscription records Titia Severina dedicating a monument to her husband, Marcus Marius Iaracion, and Decimus Titius Severinus dedicating one to his father.

EDCS-09200923.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Lucius Pompeius Iustus, freedman of Marcus, made this for his mother Pompeia Marina and his departed [parents].
EDCS-09200923.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Pompeius Iustus to his mother and other deceased family members.

EDCS-09200924.translation: Pompeius Carpophorus to his dearest wife, Pompeia Pia.
EDCS-09200924.summary: A funerary dedication from Pompeius Carpophorus to his wife Pompeia Pia.

EDCS-09200925.translation: To their sweetest daughter, Rutilia Ursa, lia Uccu and Rutilius Ruittacus, parents.
EDCS-09200925.summary: A funerary dedication from parents to their daughter Rutilia Ursa.

EDCS-09200926.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Titia Apate to her most dutiful husband, Decimus Titius Hilarius, and his sons, and his brother Decimus Titius Hermes.
EDCS-09200926.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Titia Apate to her husband, sons, and brother.

EDCS-09200927.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Titia Euelchia and Lucius Titius Martinus set this up for their son, Decimus Titius Secundus.
EDCS-09200927.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Titia Euelchia and Lucius Titius Martinus to their son Decimus Titius Secundus.

EDCS-09200929.translation: Gaius Valerius Senorigis, son of Camulatus, while alive, [made this] for himself and for Valeria Sunua, his daughter Niceta, his wife, and for Gaius Valerius, son of Camulatus, Cosso, a soldier of the 15th legion, and Lucius Scabrius Simco.
EDCS-09200929.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Valerius Camulatus for himself and his family, including his wife, daughter, and son who was a soldier.

EDCS-09200934.translation: Here rests Aunemundus of good memory, who lived in peace for 60 years and six months. He departed this life on the 14th day before the Kalends of June, after the consulship of Symmachus.
EDCS-09200934.summary: A tombstone recording the death and lifespan of Aunemundus, with a date after the consulship of Symmachus (late 4th/early 5th century CE).

EDCS-09200935.translation: Eufrasius the priest made this by his vow in honor of Saint Peter the Apostle.
EDCS-09200935.summary: A votive offering by a priest named Eufrasius dedicated to Saint Peter the Apostle.

EDCS-09200937.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcianus Sorolianus [made this] by vow.
EDCS-09200937.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcianus Sorolianus.

EDCS-09200941.translation: [Son] of Certus and for the ornaments of the villagers of Trajan's Parthica, Vantesicae.
EDCS-09200941.summary: An inscription possibly relating to local contributions or dedications for the village of Vantesicae, possibly connected to Trajan's Parthica.

EDCS-09200944.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record the dedication of a bathhouse by Gaius Sennius, possibly dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09200947.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Divilia Aurelia, deceased, aged fifteen years, six months, and twenty-three days. Rutilius Aurelius and Divilia Luciana, her most unhappy parents, erected this for Rutilius Aurelius while still alive, and dedicated it under the pickaxe.
EDCS-09200947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, Divilia Aurelia, erected by her parents, who also set up a tomb for themselves.

EDCS-09200949.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Licinius Sextinus. Licinius Secundinus and Licinius Capito, sons, [dedicated this to their father] Licinius Sextus.
EDCS-09200949.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Licinius Sextinus by his sons, Licinius Secundinus and Licinius Capito.

EDCS-09200950.translation: Marcus Taius Modestus, while alive, for himself and his family, and for Lucius Taius Modestus's son, Voltinia Martinus.
EDCS-09200950.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Marcus Taius Modestus for himself, his family, and Voltinia Martinus.

EDCS-09200951.translation: For Taia, daughter of Marcus, Secundina.
EDCS-09200951.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Taia, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-09200952.translation: To the Divine Manes and eternal rest. For Titia, dearest soul, who lived 25 years, 3 months, and for Sergia Saturnina. [Also dedicated by...]
EDCS-09200952.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Titia, who died at a young age, and Sergia Saturnina.

EDCS-09200958.translation: To the Divine Manes and eternal memory. For Romania Veratia, dearest wife, who lived 40 years, 3 months, and 5 days. Aurelius Domitianus, her husband, saw to the erection for his incomparable soul and dedicated it under the pickaxe.
EDCS-09200958.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Aurelius Domitianus to his wife, Romania Veratia, and includes details of her lifespan and his devotion.

EDCS-09200959.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to record a person's name and filiation.

EDCS-09200962.translation: To the Numinia of the August Emperors and to the god Apollo. Beccus [dedicated this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09200962.summary: This is a votive inscription to the divine powers of the emperors and Apollo, dedicated by Beccus.

EDCS-09200964.translation: ...by Iullina, priestess of the province of Narbonensis.
EDCS-09200964.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to Iullina, a priestess holding a provincial office in Narbonensis.

EDCS-09200965.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Sextius, father of three sons, to be set up by T. Iulius.
EDCS-09200965.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Sextius, likely set up by a relative or friend, intended for the underworld deities.

EDCS-09200970.translation: Caius Blaesius Gratus, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, gave for this sundial, with its entire building and all its fittings and locks, and for the administration of this sundial, a slave, from his own funds, ten thousand sesterces and more, and furthermore four thousand sesterces.
EDCS-09200970.summary: This inscription records a generous endowment by Caius Blaesius Gratus for the construction and upkeep of a public sundial, including the provision of a slave.

EDCS-09200971.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Rutilius, son of Celto, of the Voltinia tribe, Rutilion, deceased, aged forty years, father of five children. Titus Rutilius Celto, his dearest and most devoted son.
EDCS-09200971.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rutilius Rutilion, commemorating his family and age, erected by his devoted son.

EDCS-09200973.translation: To Apollo Virotutus. Titus Rutilius Buricus.
EDCS-09200973.summary: A dedication to the deity Apollo Virotutus by Titus Rutilius Buricus.

EDCS-09200974.translation: Gaius Ateius Peculiaris, prefect of the territory of Apollo, had this made from double the usual contribution.
EDCS-09200974.summary: This inscription details a votive offering or construction project commissioned by Gaius Ateius Peculiaris, prefect of the territory of Apollo.

EDCS-09200976.translation: Vinicius Severus, in his own name and in the name of his father Lucius Vinicius Latinus, dedicates this altar to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09200976.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Vinicius Severus, honoring both himself and his father.

EDCS-09200977.summary: A simple dedication to the god Mercury Augustus by Gaius Spurius.

EDCS-09200984.translation: Sextus Iulius Optatus, priest of Mars at Vienna, dedicated from his own funds.
EDCS-09200984.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Sextus Iulius Optatus, who held the priesthood of Mars in Vienna, using his personal funds.

EDCS-09200987.translation: Sennius Marcianus and Sextus Caprilius Att[icus].
EDCS-09200987.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment naming two individuals, possibly related to a joint undertaking or dedication.

EDCS-09200990.translation: To Quintus, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, and Ca[...].
EDCS-09200990.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that seems to be a dedication or a memorial, mentioning an individual named Quintus and a tribal affiliation.

EDCS-09200991.translation: Gaius Claudius, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, and Ecle and Lucius Iulius Optatus, soldiers of the cohort.
EDCS-09200991.summary: This inscription appears to list names, possibly related to military service or a collegium, with tribal affiliation and military rank indicated.

EDCS-09200993.translation: Private wall of Titus Iulius B.
EDCS-09200993.summary: This is a very short inscription indicating ownership of a private wall belonging to Titus Iulius B.

EDCS-09200994.translation: Titus Iulius Voltinia Ustus Pollio took care to restore it for Titus Iulius Usto.
EDCS-09200994.summary: This inscription records Titus Iulius Voltinia Ustus Pollio restoring something for Titus Iulius Usto, likely a building or monument.

EDCS-09200995.translation: Sextus Iulius Optatus gave from his own money.
EDCS-09200995.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Sextus Iulius Optatus from his personal funds, likely for a public or religious purpose.

EDCS-09200996.translation: Private wall of Lucius Licinius Titurianus.
EDCS-09200996.summary: This is a very short inscription indicating ownership of a private wall belonging to Lucius Licinius Titurianus.

EDCS-09200997.translation: To the departed spirits: Marcus Drippius Ianuarius to his dearest wife, Liguria Martina, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-09200997.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Drippius Ianuarius to his wife, Liguria Martina, indicating a personal memorial.

EDCS-09200998.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a name on a monument or property, identifying Quintus Pompeius Adiutor.

EDCS-09200999.summary: This inscription likely identifies a sculpture and its owner or subject, Publius.

EDCS-09201005.translation: Lucius Tincius Paculus made it passable.
EDCS-09201005.summary: This inscription records Lucius Tincius Paculus making a path or road accessible, suggesting a public works contribution.

EDCS-09201007.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to the god Mercury and possibly also to Titus Coelius, or by Titus Coelius.

EDCS-09201008.translation: Sacred to Vintius Augustus. Titus Valerius Crispinus, priest and prefect of the canton of the Diarenses, dedicated the temple.
EDCS-09201008.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to the god Vintius Augustus by Titus Valerius Crispinus, who held the offices of priest and prefect of his canton.

EDCS-09201009.translation: His heirs, by his will, for Gaius Iuventius Secundinus of the Voltinian tribe.
EDCS-09201009.summary: This inscription records a posthumous dedication for Gaius Iuventius Secundinus, set up by his heirs according to his will.

EDCS-09201010.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. His most devoted and incomparable sons erected [this monument] for their father, Lucius Romanus Euprepes.
EDCS-09201010.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by the sons for their deceased father, Lucius Romanus Euprepes.

EDCS-09201012.translation: To the god Vintius Pollux. Gnaeus Terentius Terentianus, son of Billo, [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-09201012.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gnaeus Terentius Terentianus to the god Vintius Pollux.

EDCS-09201016.translation: Celatus, freedman of Iullus, [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-09201016.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Celatus, a freedman.

EDCS-09201018.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gnaeus Iulius Terentianus Maximus.
EDCS-09201018.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Gnaeus Iulius Terentianus Maximus.

EDCS-09201019.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Sextus Pompeius Macrinus.

EDCS-09201020.translation: To Apollo. Gaius Verrius Aurelius [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-09201020.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Verrius Aurelius to the god Apollo.

EDCS-09201022.translation: Servilia Terentia willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Augusta Athubodua.
EDCS-09201022.summary: This inscription records Servilia Terentia fulfilling a vow to the goddess Augusta Athubodua.

EDCS-09201024.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a vow fulfilled by Iullus.

EDCS-09201025.translation: Firmus, son of Hilarius, to Mars, on account of merits, during the consulship of Gaius Ateius Capito and Gaius Vivius Postumus.
EDCS-09201025.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars by Firmus, son of Hilarius, likely made during the consulship of Ateius Capito and Vivius Postumus.

EDCS-09201027.translation: To the departed spirits and eternal memory of Tiberia Maxima, Aurelius Romanus, protector (of rank) ducenarius, for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-09201027.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aurelius Romanus to his wife, Tiberia Maxima.

EDCS-09201030.translation: Julius Saturninus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars.
EDCS-09201030.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Saturninus to Mars.

EDCS-09201031.summary: This brief inscription records that Sabinianus was responsible for the construction of a monument or work.

EDCS-09201032.translation: Decimus Julius Capito, according to his vow, to Jupiter and Mars.
EDCS-09201032.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Decimus Julius Capito to Jupiter and Mars.

EDCS-09201035.translation: Heirs in accordance with the will, for Titus Riccius Frontonus, son of Titus, a IIvir of the treasury, and for Titus Riccius Frontonus, his father, and for Quintus Riccius Fidus, his brother.
EDCS-09201035.summary: This inscription records the beneficiaries of a will, with heirs making provisions for Titus Riccius Frontonus, his father, and his brother.

EDCS-09201036.translation: In this tomb rests Ebrovaccus of good memory, who lived for 13 years and 4 months and passed away on the 10th day before the Kalends of September, during the consulship of the most illustrious man Mavortius; under this consul, the redemption of Brandobrich from King Gudomar was received.
EDCS-09201036.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ebrovaccus and also mentions a significant political event involving the redemption of Brandobrich during the consulship of Mavortius.

EDCS-09201037.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Apollo by Marcus Aufustius Catuso.

EDCS-09201038.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Apollo by Marcus Veratius Mercator.

EDCS-09201040.translation: To Jupiter, Marci filius N Assignus.
EDCS-09201040.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Assignus, son of Marci.

EDCS-09201041.translation: Marcus Cassius Mercator erected this monument for Jupiter, Mars, and Mercury, on his own behalf and in the name of his brother Lucius Cassius Aspre.
EDCS-09201041.summary: This dedication to Jupiter, Mars, and Mercury was set up by Marcus Cassius Mercator for himself and his brother.

EDCS-09201042.translation: Titus Vipius Verecundus, freedman of Titus Vipius Celsus, dedicated this arch with its adornments to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-09201042.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch with its ornaments to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Vipius Verecundus, a freedman.

EDCS-09201043.translation: Two yokes for Jupiter Optimus Maximus of Stabulum and Aulus.
EDCS-09201043.summary: This brief inscription records two yokes dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Stabulo and Aulus.

EDCS-09201044.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Gaius Sennius Sabinus.
EDCS-09201044.summary: This is a dedication to Mars Augustus by Gaius Sennius Sabinus.

EDCS-09201046.translation: Marcus Attius Birr paid his vow to Mercury Augustus.
EDCS-09201046.summary: Marcus Attius Birr fulfilled a vow to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-09201047.translation: Marathonius gladly paid his vow to Mercury of our Augustus, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201047.summary: Marathonius willingly fulfilled a vow to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-09201048.translation: Victor, freedman of Augustus, erected this monument at his own expense to the Divine Numen of the Emperors and the imperial household.
EDCS-09201048.summary: Victor, a freedman of Augustus, dedicated this monument from his own funds to the Divine Numen of the Emperors and the imperial family.

EDCS-09201049.translation: Lucius Sanctius Marcus, a citizen of the Helvetii, gladly paid his vow to the god Silvanus for the safety of the upper treasury officials and his friends, and gave it from his own property.
EDCS-09201049.summary: This dedication to Silvanus for the health of officials and friends was made by Lucius Sanctius Marcus, a Helvetian citizen, who also fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-09201051.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Rufia Aquilina, daughter of Caius At...
EDCS-09201051.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Rufia Aquilina, daughter of Caius At[...].

EDCS-09201052.translation: Aemilius Tutor, son of Lucius, erected this for Lucius Aemilius Tutor, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe, who held the office of a judge, prefect of engineers, and flamen of Mars and the Augustan cult in Rome.
EDCS-09201052.summary: This monument was erected by Aemilius Tutor for his father, Lucius Aemilius Tutor, honoring his extensive public and religious career.

EDCS-09201053.translation: To Blandius Latinus, son of Caius, of the Voltinian tribe, centurion of the I Italica, centurion of the II Augusta, centurion of the IX Hispana, centurion of the XX, decorated with gifts.
EDCS-09201053.summary: This inscription honors Blandius Latinus, a centurion who served in several legions and received military decorations.

EDCS-09201055.translation: To Valerius Celer, son of Gnaeus, of the Aruntian tribe, prefect of the I Cohort of Lusitanians.
EDCS-09201055.summary: This inscription honors Valerius Celer, a prefect of a cohort of Lusitanians.

EDCS-09201056.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Tig, military tribune of the XXII Legion, assistant to the prefect, dedicated this at his own expense for himself and his family, to Titus Flavius Exom, decurion of the Suebi Nicretes, his father, Titus Flavius Aspro, his brother, and Romula, his mother and wife.
EDCS-09201056.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Tig for his deceased family members, including his father, brother, and wife.

EDCS-09201058.translation: Lucius Julius Brocchus, son of Publius, of the Voltinian tribe, also known as Valerius Bassus, prefect of the engineers, twice military tribune of the VIII Augusta Legion, duovir for dispensing justice, tresvir for pursuing public affairs, augur, pontifex, duovir and flamen in the colony of Equus, has granted a lake to the villagers of Geneva.
EDCS-09201058.summary: This inscription records Lucius Julius Brocchus, a prominent local official and priest, granting a lake to the villagers of Geneva in the colony of Equus.

EDCS-09201059.translation: Lucius Julius Brocchus, son of Publius, of the Voltinian tribe, also known as Valerius Bassus, prefect of the engineers, twice military tribune of the VIII Augusta Legion, duovir for dispensing justice, tresvir for pursuing public affairs, augur, pontifex, duovir and flamen in the colony of Equus, has granted a lake to the villagers of Geneva.
EDCS-09201059.summary: This inscription records Lucius Julius Brocchus, a prominent local official and priest, granting a lake to the villagers of Geneva in the colony of Equus.

EDCS-09201060.translation: To Titus Julius Valerianus, son of Titus, of the Cornelia tribe, patron of the colony, duovir of the treasury, tresvir for pursuing public affairs, military tribune of the VI Victrix Legion, prefect of the engineers, flamen of August, pontifex. His daughter Iulia Vera dedicated this to her excellent father.
EDCS-09201060.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicated by Iulia Vera to her father, Titus Julius Valerianus, who held numerous important civic and religious offices.

EDCS-09201062.translation: A plot of land, purchased by decree of the decurions, made private, as it is now enclosed. Florus writes.
EDCS-09201062.summary: This inscription records the private purchase of a plot of land previously decreed by the decurions, with a note from Florus.

EDCS-09201063.translation: To Gaius Arsinus Marcianus, a most excellent and pious youth, who fulfilled his duty among his fellow villagers. The aedile. A hostile hand tore this away from me. My dear wife and most unhappy parents' only-born son, after the others. Atis to his most loving and deserving wife.
EDCS-09201063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Arsinus Marcianus, a youth who died prematurely, lamented by his wife Atis and parents.

EDCS-09201064.translation: To Sextus Attius Carpophorus, a quinquennial duovir of the Augustales.
EDCS-09201064.summary: This inscription honors Sextus Attius Carpophorus, who served as a quinquennial duovir of the Augustales.

EDCS-09201065.translation: To Decimus Julius Capito, son of Decimus, of the Voltinian tribe, augur, duovir of the treasury, flamen of Mars, military tribune.
EDCS-09201065.summary: This inscription honors Decimus Julius Capito, who held several significant religious and military positions.

EDCS-09201066.translation: Gaius Plinius Faustus, for his son Lucius Plinius Faustus Sabinus, 12 years of age, son of Faustus, Caius Plinius Marci, son of Marcius, aedile twice, duovir of the colony of Iulia Equestris, flamen of Augustus, had this erected in his lifetime.
EDCS-09201066.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a father for his young son, who held several important local offices in the colony of Iulia Equestris.

EDCS-09201067.translation: Titus Riccius Frontonus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, duovir of the treasury, erected by his heir according to his will.
EDCS-09201067.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Riccius Frontonus, who was a duovir aerarii (official in charge of the treasury), erected by his heir.

EDCS-09201068.translation: To Sabina Materna, daughter of Sextus, priestess.
EDCS-09201068.summary: This is a dedication to Sabina Materna, a priestess and daughter of Sextus.

EDCS-09201069.translation: Quintus Stardius Macer, Gaius Stardius Pacatus, Gaius Albucius Philogenes, Gaius Statius Anchialus, Gaius Novellius Amphio, Publius Cornelius Amphio, members of the quattuorviri.
EDCS-09201069.summary: This inscription lists six individuals who were members of the quattuorviri (a college of four magistrates), likely functioning as an official body.

EDCS-09201072.translation: Verria Verula dedicated this monument, erected under the axe, to her most devoted daughter Calpurnia Verna.
EDCS-09201072.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Verria Verula for her devoted daughter Calpurnia Verna, with the monument being 'erected under the axe', a common funerary practice.

EDCS-09201073.translation: To the divine shades, Marcus Iulius Marcianus, to his brother Marcus Connius Secundus.
EDCS-09201073.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Marcus Iulius Marcianus to his brother, Marcus Connius Secundus.

EDCS-09201074.translation: To Vitalio, 25 years old, son of Gaius Craxxius Hilarus, of the Voltinia tribe, his dearest father.
EDCS-09201074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from a father to his son Vitalio, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-09201075.translation: Publius Decius Troucetei, son of Vepius, of the Voltinia tribe, Esunertus. During the consulship of Gaius Asinius Gallus and Gaius Marcius Censorinus.
EDCS-09201075.summary: This inscription records Publius Decius Troucetei and mentions the consuls Gaius Asinius Gallus and Gaius Marcius Censorinus, dating it to their consulship.

EDCS-09201084.translation: Marcus Iulius Marcianus made this for himself and his descendants during his lifetime.
EDCS-09201084.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Iulius Marcianus for himself and his descendants, indicating it was prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-09201085.translation: To the divine shades, for Decimus Iulius Festus, son of Decimus Iulius, of the Voltinia tribe, Modestinus, their most devoted patron. His freedmen took care of it.
EDCS-09201085.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by the freedmen of Decimus Iulius Festus to their devoted patron.

EDCS-09201086.translation: To the memory of Quintus Julius Sergius, his freedmen, Fullonius Iustinus, Terentia Belatulla, and Julius Macrinus, his heirs, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09201086.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the freedmen and heirs of Quintus Julius Sergius.

EDCS-09201087.translation: To the Divine Manes and eternal rest for Mansuetiniana Iustina, his dearest freedwoman and incomparable wife, most holy woman; her patron, Caius Mansuetinus Paternus.
EDCS-09201087.summary: This funerary dedication is for Mansuetiniana Iustina, freedwoman and wife, by her patron Caius Mansuetinus Paternus.

EDCS-09201088.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Nammus Numida.

EDCS-09201089.translation: To the Divine Manes for Palladius Politicus, her most dutiful son-in-law, his mother-in-law arranged for this to be placed.
EDCS-09201089.summary: This funerary monument was erected for Palladius Politicus by his mother-in-law.

EDCS-09201090.translation: To the Divine Manes and eternal rest for Procus.
EDCS-09201090.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Procus.

EDCS-09201091.translation: To Gaius Segellius Terrenus, heir by testament.
EDCS-09201091.summary: This inscription records Gaius Segellius Terrenus as an heir by testament.

EDCS-09201092.translation: To the Divine Manes for Valentinus Asprias; Verecunda arranged for this to be placed for her most dutiful husband.
