EDCS-09201092.summary: This funerary monument was erected for Valentinus Asprias by his wife Verecunda.

EDCS-09201093.translation: To Valerius, son of Kanius; Voltinius, son of Kano.
EDCS-09201093.summary: This inscription records a relationship between Valerius and Voltinius, possibly indicating a familial connection or legal status.

EDCS-09201094.translation: To Sextus Uccius Secundus, his brother Albus dedicated from his own means.
EDCS-09201094.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Albus for his brother Sextus Uccius Secundus.

EDCS-09201095.translation: To the eternal memory of Verria Verula, her incomparable husband Coius Astutus arranged for this to be made and dedicated it beneath the axe.
EDCS-09201095.summary: This funerary monument was erected and dedicated by Coius Astutus for his wife Verria Verula.

EDCS-09201104.translation: Of good memory, Ursolus, who lived for 1 year.
EDCS-09201104.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Ursolus, dated by its style to the Late Roman period.

EDCS-09201111.translation: In this tomb rests the handmaid of God Bricciofrida, who lived for [unknown number of] years.
EDCS-09201111.summary: A tomb inscription for Bricciofrida, a Christian woman, noting her lifespan. The inscription is fragmentary.

EDCS-09201112.translation: To the divine Mars Augustus, Gaius Titius Decumanus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201112.summary: A dedication to Mars Augustus by Gaius Titius Decumanus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09201113.translation: In this tomb rests of good memory Bertegiselus, who lived in peace for more or less 45 years and passed away on the Ides of the Kalends of November in the fourth year of the reign of our lord Theodoric the king, in the twelfth indiction.
EDCS-09201113.summary: A tomb inscription for Bertegiselus, a Christian, providing details of his death date and reign of King Theodoric, placing it in the early 6th century.

EDCS-09201114.translation: Peregrinus the consul redeemed [this] always in peace.
EDCS-09201114.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly recording a civic action or a dedication by a consul named Peregrinus.

EDCS-09201115.translation: To the goddess Soionis Augusta, Luccius Marcianus and Sennius Marianus set this up at their own expense in the private property of the Upeii pupils.
EDCS-09201115.summary: A dedication to the goddess Soionis Augusta by Luccius Marcianus and Sennius Marianus on land belonging to the Upeii children.

EDCS-09201116.translation: To Albinus, Catia Decumina dedicated [this] to her dearest son under the axe.
EDCS-09201116.summary: A tomb inscription dedicated by Catia Decumina to her son Albinus, indicating a specific burial rite ('sub ascia').

EDCS-09201121.translation: Here rests in peace of good memory Amatus, who lived in peace for more or less 5 years and passed away on the day and at the time written above.
EDCS-09201121.summary: A tomb inscription for Amatus, who died young, noting his lifespan and referencing a previously stated date of death.

EDCS-09201123.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal peace, Mariana set this up for her most dutiful son Constantius.
EDCS-09201123.summary: A tomb inscription from a mother, Mariana, to her son Constantius, dedicated to the underworld deities and eternal peace.

EDCS-09201124.translation: To the Divine Shades and eternal memory, Petronius Mascellio set this up for his dearest wife Petronia Maria and for their mother Mar.
EDCS-09201124.summary: A tomb inscription by Petronius Mascellio for his wife Petronia Maria and his mother Mar, dedicated to the underworld deities.

EDCS-09201125.translation: To my brother and to myself, Cintugnata daughter of Boudi, to her son Pollio.
EDCS-09201125.summary: This is a dedication on a funerary monument from a wife to her deceased husband and for herself.

EDCS-09201127.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Maspetius Severianus to his dearest brother Aulus Maspetius Severus.
EDCS-09201127.summary: This is a dedication on a funerary monument from a brother to his deceased brother.

EDCS-09201128.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Maspetius Verus to his dearest daughter Maspetia Vera.
EDCS-09201128.summary: This is a dedication on a funerary monument from a father to his deceased daughter.

EDCS-09201129.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For the Maximi, from Cupari Vocronnesses.
EDCS-09201129.summary: This is a dedication on a funerary monument, likely from a freedman to his patrons.

EDCS-09201130.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to the eternal memory of Gettius Ursulus, who lived 22 years. Gettia Rufina and Gettius Ursi [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201130.summary: This is a dedication on a funerary monument from a wife and son to their deceased husband and father.

EDCS-09201132.translation: To the August Mothers of Obelense. Iulius Secundus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201132.summary: This is a dedication to a local deity or deities by a Roman citizen.

EDCS-09201134.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Petronius Diadumenus, a priest of the Augustales and member of the quattuorvirs, arranged for the construction of two altars for Prisciana, his dearest wife. He paid 200 sesterces for the work.
EDCS-09201134.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a wife, made by her husband who held prominent local priesthoods and magistracies.

EDCS-09201135.translation: Marcus Primius Maximus to his father Marcus Primius Mansuetus, who served as flamen and as a member of the quattuorvirs in his homeland. Marcus Primius Maximus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201135.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a son to his deceased father, who held local religious and political offices.

EDCS-09201136.translation: To Lucius Valerius Optatus, son of Publius, of the Voltinian tribe, who served as a member of the quattuorvirs, as Augustal flamen, as tribune of soldiers in the Third Gallic Legion, and as prefect of the craftsmen.
EDCS-09201136.summary: This is an epitaph recording the distinguished career of a local official and military officer.

EDCS-09201137.translation: To Lucius Pinarius Optatus, a worshipper of the Lares, [dedicated by] Sextus Antonius Mansuetus and Lucius Valerius Rufinus.
EDCS-09201137.summary: This is a dedication to an individual, likely a priest or devotee, by his companions.

EDCS-09201138.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Secundilla, mother, to her most dutiful son Marcus Campanius Secundus.
EDCS-09201138.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Campanius Secundus, set up by his mother, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201139.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Daverius Eutyches and Terentia Eleusina, parents, set this up for their dearest son Censorinus Dionysius.
EDCS-09201139.summary: A tombstone for Censorinus Dionysius, erected by his parents, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201140.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of the son of Helvinus, Albinus Helvinus, to his incomparable brother.
EDCS-09201140.summary: A tombstone for the brother of Albinus Helvinus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201142.summary: A tombstone for Macrina Amneraix, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201144.translation: Silvinius Eutychianus set up this memorial to Pardula, most deserving.
EDCS-09201144.summary: A tombstone for Pardula, set up by Silvinius Eutychianus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201145.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Secundus and Secundinus, to their most dutiful father Titus Petronicus Secundillus, and Mna his wife.
EDCS-09201145.summary: A tombstone for Titus Petronicus Secundillus and his wife Mna, set up by their sons, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201147.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius and Marina set this up for their most dutiful daughter, out of devotion.
EDCS-09201147.summary: A tombstone for an unnamed daughter, set up by her parents Gaius and Marina, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marra, daughter, set this up for Tessia Marcellina.
EDCS-09201148.summary: A tombstone for Tessia Marcellina, set up by her daughter Marra, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201149.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelia and Luci Cascellia set this up for Tincia Tyche, an excellent woman.
EDCS-09201149.summary: A tombstone for Tincia Tyche, set up by Coelia and Luci Cascellia, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201150.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Gaius Valerius Daedalus and his daughter Valeria Ingenua.
EDCS-09201150.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Valerius Daedalus and his daughter Valeria Ingenua, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201151.translation: He placed [this] for his most deserving father.
EDCS-09201151.summary: This inscription is a dedication set up by an unnamed son for his deserving father.

EDCS-09201155.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Domitius Licinianus [set this up] for his dearest mother, Castricia Secunda.
EDCS-09201155.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Domitius Licinianus for his mother, Castricia Secunda.

EDCS-09201156.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Gaius Maximius Atticianus made the monument.

EDCS-09201157.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Severus made [this] for his dearest wife, Sollia Ursa, and for himself while alive.
EDCS-09201157.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulius Severus for his wife Sollia Ursa and himself, indicating he made it during his lifetime.

EDCS-09201158.translation: [This monument is for]RIO, son of Lucius, [from] Voltinia Vaamiamansueta.
EDCS-09201158.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication for a person named Rio, son of Lucius, set up by Voltinia Vaamiamansueta.

EDCS-09201159.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Cai Verius Primulus, son of Antonius.
EDCS-09201159.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Cai Verius Primulus, son of Antonius.

EDCS-09201160.translation: Here rests in peace the deacon Domnolus, who lived 39 years and 3 days. He died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of May in the 12th year of the reign of lord Alaric.
EDCS-09201160.summary: This inscription records the death of Domnolus, a deacon, specifying his age and the date of his death relative to the reign of Alaric, likely in the 5th century CE.

EDCS-09201161.translation: In this tomb rests Severus, an innocent lector of good memory, who lived thirteen years in peace. He died on the tenth day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-09201161.summary: This inscription commemorates Severus, a young lector, detailing his age at death and the date of his passing.

EDCS-09201167.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Fabius Zoilus made [this] for himself and for his dearest wife, Consuadullia Primilla, while alive, so that we might have [it].
EDCS-09201167.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Lucius Fabius Zoilus for himself and his wife, Consuadullia Primilla, indicating it was constructed during their lifetimes.

EDCS-09201168.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Tiberius Iulius Valerianus died at the age of 5 years, 7 months, and 6 days. Iulius Crantor and Terentia Valeria [set this up] for their sweetest son.
EDCS-09201168.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulius Crantor and Terentia Valeria for their son, Tiberius Iulius Valerianus, detailing his young age at death.

EDCS-09201169.translation: Lucius Vireius Valerianus made this for his dearest son, who lived 7 months. P S.
EDCS-09201169.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Vireius Valerianus to his son, with a measurement of the son's lifespan.

EDCS-09201172.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Petronius Severinus for his most dutiful son, Valerio Petronio.
EDCS-09201172.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Petronius Severinus to his son, Valerio Petronio.

EDCS-09201174.translation: to her son Mansuetus. Ilia, daughter of Suadurigius.
EDCS-09201174.summary: A funerary epitaph, possibly for a son named Mansuetus, dedicated by Ilia.

EDCS-09201175.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Gemella and Marcus Aurelius Fuscus made this for their dearest son, Marcus Aurelius Fuscus, who lived 2 years, 5 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-09201175.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Iulia Gemella and Marcus Aurelius Fuscus to their son, including his age at death.

EDCS-09201176.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sertorius Aelianus for his deserving wife, Iulia Rufina.
EDCS-09201176.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Marcus Sertorius Aelianus to his wife, Iulia Rufina.

EDCS-09201179.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Trophimus, freedman, for his deserving patron, Titus Manlius Filetus.
EDCS-09201179.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Trophimus, a freedman, to his patron, Titus Manlius Filetus.

EDCS-09201182.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The patron for his excellent freedman, Marcus Lepidius Hero.
EDCS-09201182.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a patron to his freedman, Marcus Lepidius Hero.

EDCS-09201183.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Terentia Marcellina for her mother, Quinti Rufi Marcellini.
EDCS-09201183.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Terentia Marcellina to her mother, Quinti Rufi Marcellini.

EDCS-09201184.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The parents for Iulius Camulus Verus.
EDCS-09201184.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by parents to their son, Iulius Camulus Verus.

EDCS-09201185.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcellus and Marculus, sons, and Carina, wife, for Marcellinus, son of Marcella.
EDCS-09201185.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by the sons and wife to Marcellinus, son of Marcella.

EDCS-09201187.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Karus to his most dutiful father, Kari Marci Celsi, his freedman.
EDCS-09201187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Karus for his father, Kari Marci Celsi, who was also a freedman.

EDCS-09201188.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus, son of Lucius Hortensius Sy.
EDCS-09201188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus, son of Lucius Hortensius Sy.

EDCS-09201189.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Sammius Vernus.
EDCS-09201189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sammius Vernus.

EDCS-09201190.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aegrilia Florentina, his most dutiful daughter, who lived 32 years, 7 months, and 10 days. Aulus Aegrilius Trophimus made this.
EDCS-09201190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aulus Aegrilius Trophimus for his daughter, Aegrilia Florentina, providing her age at death.

EDCS-09201191.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Virilla, aged 25.
EDCS-09201191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Virilla, stating her age at death.

EDCS-09201192.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Vinicius, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, Cominianus.
EDCS-09201192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vinicius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-09201194.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Vinicius, son of Julianus, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-09201194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vinicius, son of Julianus.

EDCS-09201195.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lituccia Secundina to her most dutiful brother. To the spirits of the departed. For Servata, daughter of Icarus, deceased at the age of 33. Icarus, her most dutiful father, set this up.
EDCS-09201195.summary: This epitaph records the death of Servata, daughter of Icarus, aged 33, and also mentions Lituccia Secundina, her brother, suggesting family relations.

EDCS-09201198.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintilla, daughter of Quintus. Quintus Iulius Quintinus, her most dutiful father, placed this while alive.
EDCS-09201198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Quintus Iulius Quintinus for his daughter, Quintilla, indicating he erected it during his own lifetime.

EDCS-09201200.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Lucretius Primulus, his son.
EDCS-09201200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Lucretius Primulus, son of the dedicator.

EDCS-09201201.translation: Pompeia Ianuaria to her most devoted son, Materni, aged 18.
EDCS-09201201.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a boy named Materni at the age of 18, commemorated by his mother Pompeia Ianuaria.

EDCS-09201202.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Primus placed this tombstone for his best friend, Primo, by his will.
EDCS-09201202.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Primus dedicating a tomb to his friend Primo, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-09201203.translation: To the departed spirits, for Gaius Romanio Pedon.
EDCS-09201203.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Romanio Pedon.

EDCS-09201204.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Virillius Marcellinus to his most devoted son, Lucius Virillius Severinus.
EDCS-09201204.summary: This tombstone inscription records Lucius Virillius Marcellinus commemorating his son, Lucius Virillius Severinus.

EDCS-09201205.translation: To their most devoted father, Gaius Vassillius Quartulus, his sons Lucius Vassillius Quartinus and Marcus Vassillius Quartus.
EDCS-09201205.summary: This dedication records two sons, Lucius Vassillius Quartinus and Marcus Vassillius Quartus, honoring their father Gaius Vassillius Quartulus.

EDCS-09201209.translation: By the will of Titus Carisius Maternus.
EDCS-09201209.summary: This inscription indicates a disposition made by will by Titus Carisius Maternus.

EDCS-09201210.translation: To the departed spirits, for Maria Senilis, his daughter Severa.
EDCS-09201210.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Maria Senilis by her daughter Severa.

EDCS-09201211.translation: Titus V. Marcellus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-09201211.summary: This inscription records Titus V. Marcellus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-09201212.translation: Glycon dedicated this to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09201212.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Glycon.

EDCS-09201213.translation: To the departed spirits. The guild of carpet-makers to their master, their best colleague, from the funeral fund.
EDCS-09201213.summary: This funerary inscription records the guild of carpet-makers honoring their master and colleague, Titus Craxxius Severinus, using their funeral fund.

EDCS-09201214.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Cantiius Servatus to his most devoted father, Caius Cantius Paternus.
EDCS-09201214.summary: This funerary inscription records a son dedicating a monument to his deceased father.

EDCS-09201216.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Samoniccia Severina, mother, and Oppius Severianus, son, to Catalia Servata; Severianus to his sister.
EDCS-09201216.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother and son dedicating a monument to Catalia Servata, and Severianus also dedicating it to his sister.

EDCS-09201217.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Troccius Marcellinus to his wife, Asia Severilla.
EDCS-09201217.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband dedicating a monument to his deceased wife.

EDCS-09201218.translation: Flavius, pontifex, to his most devoted sister.
EDCS-09201218.summary: This funerary inscription records Flavius, a priest, dedicating a monument to his sister.

EDCS-09201219.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Frontinius Servatus and Secundina Paterni, parents, to their most devoted daughter Frontinia Servata; and Titus Varenius Severus to his most rare wife, who lived 35 years, 7 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-09201219.summary: This funerary inscription records parents dedicating a monument to their deceased daughter, and Titus Varenius Severus dedicating it to his wife, with her lifespan specified.

EDCS-09201220.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Secundini Primula, daughter of Publius, to her most devoted son, Secundus.
EDCS-09201220.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother dedicating a monument to her deceased son.

EDCS-09201221.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Placida, while alive, set this up for herself; Placida to her most excellent companion.
EDCS-09201221.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman dedicating a monument to herself and also to her companion.

EDCS-09201222.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Secundius Paternus and Secundia Iulia to their most excellent father, Gaius Secundius Iulianus.
EDCS-09201222.summary: This funerary inscription records two children dedicating a monument to their deceased father.

EDCS-09201223.translation: To Secundia Satulla, daughter; to Julia, mother.
EDCS-09201223.summary: This inscription records dedication to a daughter and a mother, likely funerary.

EDCS-09201224.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Frontinius Servatus to his wife Secundina Paterni; and Secundinus and Secundus to their mother, the most excellent.
EDCS-09201224.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband dedicating a monument to his wife, and their sons dedicating it to her.

EDCS-09201225.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus S(ecundus?) Serv(ilianus?) Severinus, son.
EDCS-09201225.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Servinus by his son.

EDCS-09201226.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Frontonius Quintinus to his most excellent mother, Smeria Quintilla.
EDCS-09201226.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Smeria Quintilla by her son, Publius Frontonius Quintinus.

EDCS-09201228.summary: This inscription records a place where lightning struck and was buried, likely a ritual act.

EDCS-09201229.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Dattovir and Lucius Cirratus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09201229.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Dattovir and Lucius Cirratus to Jupiter.

EDCS-09201230.translation: Lucius Pompeius Puer, freedman of Lucius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-09201230.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury by Lucius Pompeius Puer.

EDCS-09201234.translation: To Gaius Viredius Severus, Six-Time Augustalis.
EDCS-09201234.summary: This is an inscription honoring Gaius Viredius Severus, who held the prestigious office of Augustalis six times.

EDCS-09201235.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Attius Attianus to his father, Sextus Attius Secundus.
EDCS-09201235.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Sextus Attius Secundus by his son, Sextus Attius Attianus.

EDCS-09201236.translation: To Aulus Cornelius Kano, son of Marcus, his wife Tertia [erected this].
EDCS-09201236.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Cornelius Kano, erected by his wife Tertia.

EDCS-09201237.translation: To Titus Cornelius Nasson, son of Titus, his wife Tertia [erected this].
EDCS-09201237.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Cornelius Nasson, erected by his wife Tertia.

EDCS-09201238.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Felicio erected [this] to his mother, Felicula, and to Acilia.
EDCS-09201238.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Valerius Felicio to his mother Felicula and Acilia.

EDCS-09201239.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Faenius, son of Lucius, to his father.
EDCS-09201239.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Faenius to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201241.summary: This inscription appears to be a name and perhaps a cognomen, possibly an epitaph or a dedication, of Gaius Sennius Iullus.

EDCS-09201242.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Severa, daughter of Secundus.
EDCS-09201242.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Severa, daughter of Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201243.translation: Pompeius Blastus to his wife, Statia Secundina, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09201243.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Pompeius Blastus to his wife, Statia Secundina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201244.translation: Titulla, daughter of Senecio, while alive, for herself.
EDCS-09201244.summary: This inscription records Titulla, daughter of Senecio, making a provision for herself during her lifetime, possibly a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-09201245.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Valerius Severus to his most dutiful daughter, Valeria Severilla, aged 21.
EDCS-09201245.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Valerius Severus to his daughter Valeria Severilla, who died at the age of 21, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201246.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. The son of Lucius Faenius to his father.
EDCS-09201246.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the son of Lucius Faenius to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201249.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Gaius Aelius Secundinus. To his son. M. Secundilla.
EDCS-09201249.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Aelius Secundinus, with mention of his son and possibly M. Secundilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201251.translation: Valerius Felicionis fulfilled his vow to Apollo, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201251.summary: This is a votive offering by Valerius Felicionis to the god Apollo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201252.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Frontonius Primulus and Frontonius Severus and Frontonia Quintilla to their most excellent father, Quintus Frontonius, son of Quintinus.
EDCS-09201252.summary: This is a funerary dedication by three children to their father, Quintus Frontonius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201253.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Marcus Tullius Ammianus to his most dutiful father, Marcus Tullius Avitus, a commissioner of the treasury of the pontiffs.
EDCS-09201253.summary: This funerary inscription records a son's dedication to his father, who held a financial position within the pontifical administration, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09201254.translation: Vitulus, son of Ateponis, for himself and his family, while alive.
EDCS-09201254.summary: This is a self-dedicatory funerary inscription, indicating the owner purchased or set up the tomb for himself and his family during his lifetime, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201255.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Caius F Secundinus and Caecilia Varenia set this up for their most dutiful son, Caius F Caecilianus, who lived 22 years and 25 days.
EDCS-09201255.summary: This funerary monument was erected by parents for their young son, providing his name, lifespan, and noting his filial piety, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201257.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Iulius TI [dedicated this] to Iulia Dionysiadas.
EDCS-09201257.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Iulia Dionysiadas by Iulius TI, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201258.translation: Lalus, while alive, set this up for himself.
EDCS-09201258.summary: This is a self-dedicatory funerary inscription, indicating the owner secured his burial place during his lifetime, characteristic of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201259.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Primulus set this up for his brother, Secundinus, son of Secundus.
EDCS-09201259.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by a brother to his deceased sibling, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201260.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: his children [set this up] for their most excellent brother, Quintus F Severus.
EDCS-09201260.summary: This funerary inscription records children dedicating a monument to their highly esteemed brother, Quintus F Severus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201261.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Crasius Severinus, Lucius Crasius Primulus, and Crasia Luci filia Secundilla to their most excellent mother, Smeria, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-09201261.summary: This funerary inscription records multiple children dedicating a monument to their highly valued mother, Smeria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201262.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Tacitius Ianuaris, Lucius Tacitius Severinus, and Lucius Tacitius Severus to their most excellent father, Lucius Tacitus Severus.
EDCS-09201262.summary: This funerary inscription records sons dedicating a monument to their esteemed father, Lucius Tacitus Severus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201263.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Sextus Valerius Perpetuus, by his will, ordered this to be made for his father, Sextus Valerius Auctus, and his mother, Accepta.
EDCS-09201263.summary: This funerary inscription records a son who, according to his will, commissioned a monument for his deceased parents, Sextus Valerius Auctus and Accepta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201264.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Lucius Aemilius Philumenus, to his wife Livia Chrysopolis.
EDCS-09201264.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Aemilius Philumenus to his wife Livia Chrysopolis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201265.translation: To the Divine Spirits: To Vallia Beronice, deserving, and her sons Generosus and [others].
EDCS-09201265.summary: A funerary dedication by sons to their mother Vallia Beronice, indicating familial affection and respect.

EDCS-09201266.translation: Lucius Combarillius Fuscinus willingly made this monument from his own money to the Lares Augusti.
EDCS-09201266.summary: This is a dedication of a monument to the imperial household gods (Lares Augusti) by Lucius Combarillius Fuscinus, who funded it voluntarily.

EDCS-09201267.translation: Titus Iuventius Voltinia Secundus, as aedile, made this for himself and for his wife, Cornelia Sammi daughter.
EDCS-09201267.summary: A funerary epitaph by Titus Iuventius Voltinia Secundus, an aedile, for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201268.translation: To the Divine Spirits: To Lucius Cornelius Superstitis and his wife Pompeia Fusca, Lucius Cornelius Romanio and Lucius Cornelius Ianuaris, to their patron.
EDCS-09201268.summary: A funerary dedication by freedmen Lucius Cornelius Romanio and Lucius Cornelius Ianuaris to their deceased patron Lucius Cornelius Superstitis and his wife Pompeia Fusca.

EDCS-09201269.translation: To Eunia Tertia, daughter of Sextus, and Salcellae, daughter of Terentius Titus, Quintus Pullius Auricula, son of Quintus, to his mother and sister.
EDCS-09201269.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Pullius Auricula to his mother Eunia Tertia and his sister Salcellae, reflecting family mourning.

EDCS-09201270.translation: To Iulia Parucia, aged 8 years. Her mother Baebia Blanda.
EDCS-09201270.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Parucia, an eight-year-old girl, set up by her mother Baebia Blanda.

EDCS-09201271.translation: To Titus Iuventius Martialis, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, and to Gaius Iuventius Saturninus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-09201271.summary: A funerary dedication likely for two brothers, Titus Iuventius Martialis and Gaius Iuventius Saturninus, both from the Voltinia tribe.

EDCS-09201272.translation: Mamidia, daughter of Lutevus, made this for herself, her husband, and her son, while alive.
EDCS-09201272.summary: A funerary monument set up by Mamidia, daughter of Lutevus, for herself, her husband, and son during her lifetime.

EDCS-09201274.translation: To Titus Octavius Niger, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe. His mother Mamidia.
EDCS-09201274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Octavius Niger, son of Titus, set up by his mother Mamidia.

EDCS-09201275.translation: To the shades of Quintus Rutilius Euelpistus, from Atimetus, his freedman.
EDCS-09201275.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman to his deceased patron.

EDCS-09201276.translation: To Toutodivicus, son of Antillus, and to Iulia Porronis, daughter of Porro. [---]
EDCS-09201276.summary: A dedication to two individuals, likely a couple, with a lacuna indicating missing information.

EDCS-09201277.translation: Quintus Senius Furius, a priest of Augustus and member of the *quattuorviri*, made this in his lifetime for himself and for his wife, Claudia Tyche.
EDCS-09201277.summary: A funerary monument set up by a priest of Augustus for himself and his wife during their lifetimes.

EDCS-09201278.translation: To the shades of Aemilia Fortunata, on account of her dutifulness, from her daughter Aemilia Mo[---]
EDCS-09201278.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter to her deceased mother, honoring her piety.

EDCS-09201281.translation: Anicia Notata willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the nearest (gods/ancestors).
EDCS-09201281.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely to protective deities or ancestors.

EDCS-09201282.translation: To the shades of Valeria Sextina, daughter of Quintus, priestess of the Augusta.
EDCS-09201282.summary: A funerary inscription for a priestess of the imperial cult, identified by her father's name and status.

EDCS-09201283.translation: To the shades of Moccia, daughter of Caius, of Silvinae, the centonarii of Ugernenses, on account of her merits.
EDCS-09201283.summary: A funerary dedication to a member of the *centonarii* guild, honoring her merits.

EDCS-09201284.translation: Iulia Honorata (set this up) for her husband, Aulus Attius Festus.
EDCS-09201284.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her deceased husband.

EDCS-09201285.translation: To the shades of Aturia Calliste, from her children Trophimus and Chiostatus.
EDCS-09201285.summary: A funerary dedication by children to their deceased mother.

EDCS-09201286.translation: Secundina Cominia, from her own means, (dedicated this) to Fidelis, freedwoman of Primula, her companion in life.
EDCS-09201286.summary: A funerary dedication by a female companion to her deceased partner, likely a fellow slave or freedwoman.

EDCS-09201287.translation: To Iulia Nicene, freedwoman of Titus, for her most devoted daughters, aged 16.
EDCS-09201287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman named Iulia Nicene, dedicated by her daughters.

EDCS-09201288.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Iulius Phoebus to his dearest wife, Iulia Pyrallis, freedwoman of Titus.
EDCS-09201288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Pyrallis, dedicated by her husband, Titus Iulius Phoebus.

EDCS-09201289.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Licinius Irenaeus to his wife Licinia Decumina, and Licinius Titullus to his mother.
EDCS-09201289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Sextus Licinius Irenaeus to his wife Licinia Decumina and by Licinius Titullus to his mother.

EDCS-09201291.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Octavius Diadumenus, his most deserving friend, by Valeria Calliopis.
EDCS-09201291.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Octavius Diadumenus, dedicated by his friend Valeria Calliopis.

EDCS-09201292.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Iulius Alexander erected this for his mother Octavia Nice and his son Priscianus, and for himself while alive.
EDCS-09201292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Iulius Alexander for his mother, son, and himself.

EDCS-09201293.translation: To Postumia Lepida, daughter of Quintus, by her mother.
EDCS-09201293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Postumia Lepida, dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-09201294.translation: To Lucius Rubrius Rufus and his wife Iulia.
EDCS-09201294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Lucius Rubrius Rufus and his wife Iulia.

EDCS-09201295.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sempronia Panthianes, whom her husband Iulius Hermia buried in this sarcophagus, which he had placed for her.
EDCS-09201295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Panthianes, buried by her husband in a sarcophagus he provided.

EDCS-09201296.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Sennius Primus and his wife Messia Dubitata. Erected by their sons Lucius Sennius Saturninus, Lucius Caratius Secundus, and Lucius Sennius Servatus.
EDCS-09201296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sennius Primus and his wife Messia Dubitata, dedicated by their sons.

EDCS-09201297.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Veratia Severa, her most devoted mother Atilia Valerilla.
EDCS-09201297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veratia Severa, dedicated by her mother Atilia Valerilla.

EDCS-09201298.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Arethusa, Titus Julius Helis [erected this] for his dearest companion.
EDCS-09201298.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Julius Helis to his companion Arethusa.

EDCS-09201299.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Domitia Celsina, daughter of Decimus; Titus Julius Julianus, her husband [erected this].
EDCS-09201299.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Julius Julianus to his wife Domitia Celsina.

EDCS-09201301.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Caius Gellius Maternus, deserving.
EDCS-09201301.summary: A funerary dedication to Caius Gellius Maternus.

EDCS-09201303.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Ovarnia.

EDCS-09201305.translation: To the Nymphs, Lucius Julius Ascanius willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201305.summary: A votive offering to the Nymphs by Lucius Julius Ascanius.

EDCS-09201306.translation: To the Nymphs, Lucia, daughter of Gaius Aquilinus, willingly fulfilled her vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201306.summary: A votive offering to the Nymphs by Lucia, daughter of Gaius Aquilinus.

EDCS-09201307.translation: To the Nymphs, Lucius Lucretius Euprepes willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201307.summary: A votive offering to the Nymphs by Lucius Lucretius Euprepes.

EDCS-09201308.translation: To the Nymphs, Quintina, daughter of Maximus, willingly fulfilled her vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201308.summary: A votive offering to the Nymphs by Quintina, daughter of Maximus.

EDCS-09201310.translation: Quintus Minicius Apicla willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201310.summary: A votive offering by Quintus Minicius Apicla.

EDCS-09201311.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Titus Cornelius Cornelianus.
EDCS-09201311.summary: A funerary dedication to Titus Cornelius Cornelianus.

EDCS-09201312.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Vindia, daughter of Quintus, Quartina.
EDCS-09201312.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Vindia, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-09201313.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gratilla Successa, aged 19, daughter of Successa. Her mother Karantia Successa set this up.
EDCS-09201313.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gratilla Successa, aged 19, and records her mother Karantia Successa as the dedicator.

EDCS-09201314.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Titia Niceforidi. Her daughter Privata Melausa set this up.
EDCS-09201314.summary: This funerary inscription records that Titia Niceforidi's daughter, Privata Melausa, set up the monument.

EDCS-09201315.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Sextus A. Titianus. His father Sextus A. Terentius set this up.
EDCS-09201315.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Sextus A. Terentius set up the monument for his son, Sextus A. Titianus.

EDCS-09201316.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gaius Vassillius Terentius. Primulus and Severus set this up for their most dutiful father. To the spirits of the departed, for Vassillia Secundilla. Primulus and Severus set this up for their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-09201316.summary: This inscription is a double funerary dedication by Primulus and Severus for their father, Gaius Vassillius Terentius, and their mother, Vassillia Secundilla.

EDCS-09201317.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Quintus Marius Dubitatus. Maria Quintia, daughter of Quintus, set this up for her most excellent freedman.
EDCS-09201317.summary: This funerary inscription records that Maria Quintia dedicated the monument to her freedman, Quintus Marius Dubitatus.

EDCS-09201318.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for.”.
EDCS-09201318.summary: This is a funerary dedication to.”.

EDCS-09201319.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Quintus Carantius Valentinus.
EDCS-09201319.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Carantius Valentinus.

EDCS-09201322.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Helvia Marcianis. Titus M. Sextilius set this up for his most excellent mother.
EDCS-09201322.summary: This funerary inscription records that Titus M. Sextilius set up the monument for his mother, Helvia Marcianis.

EDCS-09201323.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Lucius Quintulus, aged 14 years and 30 days. Fortunata, his mother, grieving, set this up for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201323.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Quintulus, aged 14 years and 30 days, and notes that his grieving mother, Fortunata, also set it up for herself while alive.

EDCS-09201324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Massonia Materna.
EDCS-09201324.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Massonia Materna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201325.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Matugeno, son of Montanus.
EDCS-09201325.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Matugeno, son of Montanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201326.translation: ia, daughter of Solibitis, for herself and for her son Paternus and for her daughter Mansueta.
EDCS-09201326.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman, daughter of Solibitis, dedicating a monument for herself and her children, Paternus and Mansueta.

EDCS-09201327.translation: Titus Attius, son of Titus, Voltinia tribe, Marcellus.
EDCS-09201327.summary: This inscription likely records the name and tribal affiliation of Titus Attius Marcellus, possibly as a tombstone or dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201328.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Titus Attius Paternus.
EDCS-09201328.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus Attius Paternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201329.translation: Sextus Pompeius Blaesus made this for his brother Gaius Pompeius, son of Sextus, Voltinia tribe, Blaesus.
EDCS-09201329.summary: This inscription records Sextus Pompeius Blaesus dedicating a monument to his brother, Gaius Pompeius Blaesus, identifying their father and tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201330.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Valeria, daughter of Caius, Verula and her heirs.
EDCS-09201330.summary: This funerary dedication is made by the heirs of Valeria, daughter of Caius Verula, for her spirit, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201331.translation: rio, son of Lucius, B tia. IM Secunda. Lucius, son of Arrius, [erected this?].
EDCS-09201331.summary: This fragmented inscription seems to record the names Lucius, son of Arrius, and possibly B tia IM Secunda, with Lucius possibly being the dedicator.

EDCS-09201332.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Iulia Sestula. SIE IVS A RI E RI.
EDCS-09201332.summary: This funerary dedication is to Iulia Sestula, with the remaining text being illegible or formulaic, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201333.translation: Iulia Secundilla, daughter of Titus, set this up for herself while alive, and to the spirits of the underworld, for her most excellent husband, Iullinus, son of Maria.
EDCS-09201333.summary: This inscription records Iulia Secundilla setting up a monument for herself and her husband Iullinus, son of Maria, during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201334.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Servilius to his most devoted wife, Iulia Titulla.
EDCS-09201334.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Julius Servilius to his wife, Iulia Titulla.

EDCS-09201335.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lukania Materna to her excellent mother, Servilia Primula.
EDCS-09201335.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Lukania Materna to her mother, Servilia Primula.

EDCS-09201336.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Andorourus, son of Tertius.

EDCS-09201337.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Namantius Severinus.
EDCS-09201337.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Namantius Severinus.

EDCS-09201338.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Primus and Secundilla set this up for their brother.
EDCS-09201338.summary: This funerary epitaph records Primus and Secundilla setting up a monument for their brother.

EDCS-09201339.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Primus, son of Tertius, for his brother, out of duty.
EDCS-09201339.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Primus, son of Tertius, by his brother out of piety.

EDCS-09201340.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Matugenus, son of Primus.
EDCS-09201340.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Matugenus, son of Primus.

EDCS-09201341.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Primia Severa to her husband, Secundus Severus.
EDCS-09201341.summary: This funerary epitaph records Primia Severa dedicating a monument to her husband, Secundus Severus.

EDCS-09201342.translation: Titus Tedicnius Secundus for himself and for his wife, Iulia.
EDCS-09201342.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Tedicnius Secundus dedicating a monument for himself and his wife, Iulia.

EDCS-09201343.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Camillus and Camilla.
EDCS-09201343.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Camillus and Camilla.

EDCS-09201344.translation: Marcus Ligarius Tertius and his wife, Mansueta Tertulli, (set this up) for their most devoted brother, Marcus Ligarius Tertiolus, and for themselves.
EDCS-09201344.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by parents for their son and themselves, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201345.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Sextus Pompeius Blaesus, his son.
EDCS-09201345.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits of Sextus Pompeius Blaesus, set up by his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201346.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Quintina, daughter of Vitalis.
EDCS-09201346.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits of Quintina, daughter of Vitalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201347.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titia, daughter of Sextus, and Materna. Caius Silvanius Maternus (set this up) for his most excellent mother.
EDCS-09201347.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a son, Caius Silvanius Maternus, for his mother Titia Materna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201349.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Severius Maternus. Titia Materna took care to set this up while alive for herself and her son.
EDCS-09201349.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Titia Materna for herself and her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201350.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Domitia, daughter of Lucius, Rustica. Quintus Domitius Onesimus (set this up) for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-09201350.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Quintus Domitius Onesimus for his wife Domitia Rustica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201351.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication for Mansuetus, son of Andorour, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201352.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Severinus, son of Caius. Caius Firminus (set this up) for his most devoted son.
EDCS-09201352.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Caius Firminus for his son Severinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201353.translation: Tertiola, a most unhappy mother, placed this for her most devoted son; she placed this for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201353.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a mother, Tertiola, for her son and herself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201354.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titia, daughter of Titullus. Catia Maternus, son of Marcellus, placed this for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-09201354.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Catia Maternus for his wife Titia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201355.translation: Caius Sulpicius Illiomarus paid his vow to the gods for services rendered.
EDCS-09201355.summary: This is a votive dedication by Caius Sulpicius Illiomarus to the gods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201356.translation: Sacred to Ceres. Sextus Oleatius Vegetius gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201356.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Ceres by Sextus Oleatius Vegetius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201357.translation: To the Divine Manes. Virga tus Inus, son of Carassounius, [erected this] for his most devoted father.
EDCS-09201357.summary: This is a funerary dedication for the son of Carassounius to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201358.translation: Severa, daughter of Asprius, [erected this] for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201358.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severa, daughter of Asprius, made by herself during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201359.translation: Quintus Vitalius Victor [erected this] for his grandmother.
EDCS-09201359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed grandmother, erected by Quintus Vitalius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201360.translation: Tito TV I I Titiola and Secundilla, his daughter, and Servata, his wife, erected this while alive for their most devoted father.
EDCS-09201360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a father, erected by his wife and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201361.translation: To the Divine Manes. [Erected for] Cai Blaesius Optatus.
EDCS-09201361.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Cai Blaesius Optatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201363.translation: Blandus, son of Privatus, erected this for himself and for Secundilla, his wife, daughter of Bocuri, while alive.
EDCS-09201363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Blandus, son of Privatus, and his wife Secundilla, erected by Blandus himself during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201364.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Firmia, Severa, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09201364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Firmia Severa, daughter of Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201365.translation: Pompeia erected this for Lucius Pompeius Atepatus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-09201365.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Pompeius Atepatus, son of Lucius, erected by Pompeia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201366.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quartus Aelius Secundinus.

EDCS-09201367.translation: Gratula, mother, to her daughter Secundilla, daughter of Secundinus.
EDCS-09201367.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gratula to her daughter Secundilla.

EDCS-09201368.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Servatus, the boy, and Secundilla to their mother, Servata, daughter of Servatus.
EDCS-09201368.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Servatus and Secundilla to their mother Servata.

EDCS-09201369.translation: Behold Scipio, son of Veaxsio. Scipio, son of...
EDCS-09201369.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph for Scipio, son of Veaxsio.

EDCS-09201370.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintia, daughter of Quintus, made this while alive.
EDCS-09201370.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Quintia during her lifetime.

EDCS-09201372.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Castius Julianus and Iulia Se[---] to Lucius Castius Theseus.
EDCS-09201372.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Castius Julianus and Iulia Se[---] to Lucius Castius Theseus.

EDCS-09201373.translation: Gaius Aurelius willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Brigantes deities.
EDCS-09201373.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Aurelius to the Brigantes deities.

EDCS-09201374.translation: To the Genius of our Lucius, Gaius Valerius Germanus, his friend.
EDCS-09201374.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Valerius Germanus to the Genius of Lucius.

EDCS-09201375.translation: Casuna willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-09201375.summary: This is a votive inscription by Casuna to the Matres (Mothers deities).

EDCS-09201376.translation: Hail Doris, farewell Doris. Doris, freedwoman of Sextilia Severa, to her husband Liber.
EDCS-09201376.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Doris, freedwoman of Sextilia Severa, to her husband Liber.

EDCS-09201377.translation: To the Divine Shades of Sextus Licinius Rogatus.
EDCS-09201377.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sextus Licinius Rogatus.

EDCS-09201378.translation: To Lucius Calosus, son of Quartus.
EDCS-09201378.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Lucius Calosus, son of Quartus.

EDCS-09201379.translation: Marcus Messius Maternus, son of [---], set this up for Rufinia Paternus, To the Divine Shades.
EDCS-09201379.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Messius Maternus for Rufinia Paternus.

EDCS-09201380.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Servilia Atureni.

EDCS-09201381.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Solimarus, son of Leiturro.

EDCS-09201383.translation: To the Divine Shades of Titus Vesmerius Tertius.
EDCS-09201383.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Vesmerius Tertius.

EDCS-09201384.translation: To Vittia Marcella, dearest daughter of Lucius Gellius and granddaughter of Vegetius, and to Vittia F[---]. To the Divine Shades.
EDCS-09201384.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vittia Marcella and Vittia F[---].

EDCS-09201385.translation: Gallus Iulius Honoratus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09201385.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gallus Iulius Honoratus to Jupiter.

EDCS-09201386.translation: Sextus Pompeius, known by the surname Pandus, whose property this small shrine is by inheritance from his ancestors, set this up for the Nymphs, because in old age he has often benefited from this spring as much as he did in his youth.
EDCS-09201386.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Sextus Pompeius to the Nymphs for a spring.

EDCS-09201387.translation: Lucius Gellius Sentronis, son of [---], gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the temple of Ritona.
EDCS-09201387.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Gellius Sentronis for a temple dedicated to Ritona.

EDCS-09201388.translation: To Gaius Domitius Paterninus, soldier of the 15th Legion, Gaius Domitius Paterninus Laurin(us) Celti, his son, Laurin(us) Celti, his son, was asked by his will to set this up.
EDCS-09201388.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the 15th Legion, set up by his son as requested in his will.

EDCS-09201389.translation: To Lucius Porcius Karus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, soldier of the II Augusta Legion, optio, standard-bearer.
EDCS-09201389.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Porcius Karus, a soldier and officer in the II Augusta Legion.

EDCS-09201390.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Blaesus, Augustalis priest, and to Marcus Cornelius Niger and Gaius Cornelius Silvinus, for the city of Iulia Baeteris.
EDCS-09201390.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Cornelius Blaesus, an Augustalis priest, for the city of Iulia Baeteris, possibly involving Marcus Cornelius Niger and Gaius Cornelius Silvinus.

EDCS-09201391.translation: [E]ight... [---] ... Aelius [---] Mesminus, holder of the position of I Vir, [---]ariam.
EDCS-09201391.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record an official, possibly named Aelius Mesminus, and mentions a priestly or magistracy role.

EDCS-09201392.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Quintius Maternus set this up for his mother, Attia Marcella, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-09201392.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Quintius Maternus for his mother, Attia Marcella.

EDCS-09201393.translation: To the Divine Shades. Domitia Materna set this up for herself and for her most excellent husband, Sextus Aurelius Onesimus, while alive.
EDCS-09201393.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Domitia Materna for herself and her husband, Sextus Aurelius Onesimus, during their lifetime.

EDCS-09201394.translation: To Lucius Baebius Cassianus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe. Domitia Bulla, daughter of Domitius, his wife.
EDCS-09201394.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Baebius Cassianus, set up by his wife, Domitia Bulla.

EDCS-09201395.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sextus Betutius Euprepes set this up for his excellent freedwoman Olympina, daughter of Betutius, and for himself.
EDCS-09201395.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sextus Betutius Euprepes for his freedwoman Olympina and for himself.

EDCS-09201396.translation: To Caesianus Cattonis, son of Maxsimus. Lattonis, daughter of Marcus. Caesianus, son of Marcus, for himself and his parents.
EDCS-09201396.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Caesianus for himself and his parents, and also commemorates Caesianus Cattonis and Lattonis.

EDCS-09201397.translation: To the Shades. Pompeia, his mother, [set this up] for Severilla.
EDCS-09201397.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Pompeia for her daughter, Severilla.

EDCS-09201398.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Castricius Primulus to his most dutiful brother, Quintus Castricius Severus.
EDCS-09201398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Quintus Castricius Primulus dedicating a tomb to his brother, Quintus Castricius Severus.

EDCS-09201399.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Valerius Nivalis to his excellent wife, Comagia, daughter of Comagus, called Severa.
EDCS-09201399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Lucius Valerius Nivalis dedicating a tomb to his wife, Comagia Severa.

EDCS-09201400.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Fulvius Carpus to his most dutiful wife, Iulia, daughter of Iulus.
EDCS-09201400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Gaius Fulvius Carpus dedicating a tomb to his wife, Iulia.

EDCS-09201401.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. The parents to Titus Macrinius Severinus.
EDCS-09201401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from parents dedicating a tomb to their son, Titus Macrinius Severinus.

EDCS-09201402.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Ortelius Marcianus to his excellent father, Marcianus.
EDCS-09201402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Titus Ortelius Marcianus dedicating a tomb to his father, Marcianus.

EDCS-09201403.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Recepta to her most dutiful brother, Parassius.
EDCS-09201403.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Antonia Recepta dedicating a tomb to her brother, Parassius.

EDCS-09201404.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Petronia Roda to her most dutiful son, Lucius Petronius Lucilianus, for his filial piety.
EDCS-09201404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Petronia Roda dedicating a tomb to her son, Lucius Petronius Lucilianus, acknowledging his piety.

EDCS-09201405.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pompeius Eros to his excellent wife, Pompeia Terentia.
EDCS-09201405.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Pompeius Eros dedicating a tomb to his wife, Pompeia Terentia.

EDCS-09201407.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Severina to her excellent father, Titus Privatius Titullus.
EDCS-09201407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Severina dedicating a tomb to her father, Titus Privatius Titullus.

EDCS-09201408.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quartus T. S. [made this] for his son, Quarto T. Severinus, while alive.
EDCS-09201408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Quartus T. S. dedicating a tomb to his son, Quarto T. Severinus, during the son's lifetime.

EDCS-09201409.translation: To Quintius Primulus for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-09201409.summary: This is a tombstone inscription likely set up by Quintius Primulus for his deceased wife, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201410.translation: To the Divine Shades. Terentia Severa set this up for her husband Gaius Samonicius Sabinus and her daughter Vibiae Afroditae, out of dutiful love; Terentius Secundus set this up for Terentia Severa out of dutiful love.
EDCS-09201410.summary: This inscription honors deceased family members, a husband and daughter, with a secondary dedication to Terentia Severa, possibly the wife and mother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09201411.translation: To the Divine Shades. Coelius Aemilianus, heir.
EDCS-09201411.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Coelius Aemilianus is the heir responsible for the burial or memorial of Gaius Secundus Musclosus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201412.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cornelia Verina set this up for her most dutiful mother Segeia Decumina.
EDCS-09201412.summary: This epitaph records Cornelia Verina setting up a memorial for her mother, Segeia Decumina, demonstrating filial piety, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09201413.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pomponia Tertulla set this up out of dutiful love for Severinus, son of Severus.
EDCS-09201413.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Pomponia Tertulla for Severinus, son of Severus, emphasizing dutiful love, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201414.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Valentinus for his most rare wife Sextia Severa, who died at the age of 22.
EDCS-09201414.summary: This tombstone inscription records Quintus Iulius Valentinus commemorating his wife Sextia Severa, who died young, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09201415.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Spurius Vitalis for his patron Titus Spurius Gratinus of the Voltinian tribe, of most rare character.
EDCS-09201415.summary: This inscription honors Titus Spurius Gratinus, a patron, by his client Titus Spurius Vitalis, highlighting the patron's exceptional qualities, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201417.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titia Philematio made this for herself and her family while alive.
EDCS-09201417.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Titia Philematio made arrangements for her own burial and that of her family during her lifetime, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201418.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Marci filia Marcella set this up for her patron Sextus Valerius Eresistratus.
EDCS-09201418.summary: This epitaph records Valeria Marci filia Marcella dedicating a memorial to her patron Sextus Valerius Eresistratus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09201419.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Valerius Paternus for his father Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, and for his mother Laelia Quartilla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09201419.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Lucius Valerius Paternus's parents, Lucius Valerius and Laelia Quartilla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201420.translation: To Marcus Valerius Optatus; Cornelia Optata, his mother, [erected this] for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-09201420.summary: A funerary monument set up by a mother for her son, identified as Marcus Valerius Optatus.

EDCS-09201421.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Gaius Vireius Virilis, son of Hortensius, [erected this] for Vireia Severilla.
EDCS-09201421.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Vireia Severilla by her relative, Gaius Vireius Virilis.

EDCS-09201424.summary: A brief inscription recording a testament or a bequest, likely associated with Sirico.

EDCS-09201425.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Iulius Servatus and Iulia Severilla, parents, erected [this] for their most dutiful son, Lucius Iulius Saeverianus.
EDCS-09201425.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by parents to their son, Lucius Iulius Saeverianus.

EDCS-09201426.translation: To Maria Secundilla; Helvia Gemella, her mother, and Maecia Severina, her grandmother.
EDCS-09201426.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Maria Secundilla, set up by her mother and grandmother.

EDCS-09201427.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Titius Tramuleius Tertius.
EDCS-09201427.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Titius Tramuleius Tertius.

EDCS-09201428.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For the best of fathers, Publius Frontonius Fronto.
EDCS-09201428.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a son to his father, Publius Frontonius Fronto.

EDCS-09201429.summary: A very brief inscription possibly marking a site struck by lightning.

EDCS-09201430.translation: Licinia, daughter of Publius Acceptilla, made the portico of Aramon, in fulfillment of a vow, from her own money.
EDCS-09201430.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a portico by a woman named Licinia Acceptilla, funded by herself and in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09201431.translation: To Iuppiter for the Coriobedenses and the Budenicenses.
EDCS-09201431.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, likely on behalf of or by the tribes named Coriobedenses and Budenicenses.

EDCS-09201432.translation: To Mars Budenicvs, from Gratus, son of Severus.
EDCS-09201432.summary: An inscription dedicating an offering to the god Mars Budenicvs by Gratus, son of Severus.

EDCS-09201433.translation: Sulevia Ledennica, a vow to Minerva.
EDCS-09201433.summary: An inscription recording a vow made by Sulevia Ledennica to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-09201434.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Lucius Antonius Macrinus.
EDCS-09201434.summary: A tomb inscription honoring the deified spirit of Lucius Antonius Macrinus.

EDCS-09201435.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Quintus Cosconius Firminus, aged 22 years and 4 months. His mother, Iulia Firmina.
EDCS-09201435.summary: A tomb inscription for Quintus Cosconius Firminus, erected by his mother, who states his age at death.

EDCS-09201437.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Attius Secundus, son of Marcus Attius. His son, Marcus Attius Iulianus, [erected this] for his most devoted father, who lived 11 years, 11 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-09201437.summary: A tomb inscription for Marcus Attius Secundus, detailing his age at death and erected by his son.

EDCS-09201438.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, in memory of the most holy Seccarius Sabinus. Lucius Sabinius Severus [erected this] for his most beloved father.
EDCS-09201438.summary: A tomb inscription honoring Seccarius Sabinus, erected by his son Lucius Sabinius Severus.

EDCS-09201445.translation: Sacred to the sanctity of Jupiter and Augustus. Lucius Lucilius Cestius.
EDCS-09201445.summary: An inscription dedicating a sacred space or object to Jupiter and Augustus by Lucius Lucilius Cestius.

EDCS-09201446.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Quintus Karius Virilis. His sons, Karius Macrinus and Karius Campanus [erected this] for their father.
EDCS-09201446.summary: A tomb inscription for Quintus Karius Virilis, erected by his sons.

EDCS-09201447.translation: Lucius Silvius Paternus [erected this] for his wife, of the rarest character, with whom he lived 32 years without any quarrel, and [erected this] for himself while alive.
EDCS-09201447.summary: A tomb inscription for a wife, erected by her husband who also made provision for himself, highlighting their harmonious marriage of 32 years.

EDCS-09201448.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Titus Suadius Titullus. His heirs [erected this] for their best father.
EDCS-09201448.summary: A tomb inscription for Titus Suadius Titullus, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-09201449.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Titia Danotala erected this for her mother Vetullia Terentulla.
EDCS-09201449.summary: A funerary dedication by Titia Danotala for her mother Vetullia Terentulla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201450.translation: Valerius fulfilled his vow to his own Mars willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201450.summary: A votive dedication to the god Mars by Valerius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201451.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Caius Siricius [erected this] for Severus, son of Caius Siricius.
EDCS-09201451.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Siricius for his son Severus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201452.translation: Esciggorix, son of Ammon, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-09201452.summary: A votive dedication to the god Apollo by Esciggorix, son of Ammon, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201453.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Marcus Luccconius Paternus [erected this] for his most dutiful wife Ciria Quintina.
EDCS-09201453.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Luccconius Paternus for his wife Ciria Quintina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201454.translation: The people of Nemausus to the good helper Letinnon by imperial order.
EDCS-09201454.summary: A dedication by the citizens of Nemausus (modern Nîmes) to a deity or personified force named Letinnon, likely set up by imperial command.

EDCS-09201455.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Marcus Attius Epitynchanus [erected this] for his comrade.
EDCS-09201455.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Attius Epitynchanus for his contubernalis (a close companion, possibly a fellow soldier or partner in a slave household), likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201456.summary: A dedication by Gaius Domitius to the god Mars, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201457.translation: Philippus, the great architect, is buried here.
EDCS-09201457.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Philippus, identified as a 'great architect', likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201459.translation: Philemon, freedman of Quintus Julius Scydus, [served as] Sextumvir (member of the board of six priests).
EDCS-09201459.summary: An inscription identifying Philemon, a freedman of Quintus Julius Scydus, as holding the office of Sextumvir, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201460.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Ecimaria Gratilla, and to Ecimaria Maxima, her daughter, and Ecimaria Melitina, her sister, and Caius Octavius Maritimus, her husband.
EDCS-09201460.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ecimaria Gratilla by her daughter, sister, and husband.

EDCS-09201461.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lucius Iunius Avitus and Valeria Ianuaria, to their most devoted parents Karus and Avita.
EDCS-09201461.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Iunius Avitus and Valeria Ianuaria by their parents, Karus and Avita.

EDCS-09201462.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titus Valerius, and to his excellent wife PA [---] and his parents; Cnaeus [---] set this up.
EDCS-09201462.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Cnaeus for Titus Valerius, his wife, and his parents.

EDCS-09201463.translation: Quintina, daughter of Austeris, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Castor.
EDCS-09201463.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintina, daughter of Austeris, to Castor.

EDCS-09201464.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Sextus Fabius Mansuetus, from Ventidia Tertulla at her own expense.
EDCS-09201464.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Fabius Mansuetus, paid for by Ventidia Tertulla.

EDCS-09201465.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Rutilia Marcella, daughter of Lucius, who lived 17 years. Eutyches, the freedman [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201465.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rutilia Marcella, who died at 17, dedicated by her freedman Eutyches.

EDCS-09201466.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Fabricius [---] Montanus, prefect of the vigiles and tribune of soldiers of the III Cyrenaic Legion; he ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-09201466.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabricius Montanus, who was a prefect of the vigiles and a military tribune, ordered to be built by his will.

EDCS-09201467.translation: Caecilius Cassius, son of Bitulla, from Tagassi.
EDCS-09201467.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Cassius, son of Bitulla.

EDCS-09201468.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Diogenes. Auxesis, his mother, set this up for her son.
EDCS-09201468.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Auxesis for her son Diogenes.

EDCS-09201470.translation: To Quinto Fabio Fruticis, his freedman Atticus, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-09201470.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quinto Fabio Fruticis, paid for by his freedman Atticus as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-09201471.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Fabius Donatus.

EDCS-09201472.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Lucius Fabius Hyginus. Fabius Euhodus and Fabius Onesimus, his freedmen, erected this. To the Spirits of the Departed of Lucius Fabius Hyginus.
EDCS-09201472.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by freedmen to their patron, Lucius Fabius Hyginus.

EDCS-09201473.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Lucius Iulius Anthus. Iulia Glaphyra, his wife, erected this for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201473.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by Iulia Glaphyra for herself and her husband, Lucius Iulius Anthus.

EDCS-09201474.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Lucius Pompeius Doryphorus.
EDCS-09201474.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Pompeius Doryphorus.

EDCS-09201475.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Fronto, son of Gnaeus Pompeius.
EDCS-09201475.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Gnaeus Pompeius Fronto, son of Gnaeus Pompeius.

EDCS-09201476.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Caius Sertorius Philonis. Albinus, his son, [erected this].
EDCS-09201476.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by Albinus to his father, Caius Sertorius Philonis.

EDCS-09201477.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Silvinia, daughter of Silvanus.
EDCS-09201477.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Silvinia, daughter of Silvanus.

EDCS-09201478.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Titus Soillius Valerianus.
EDCS-09201478.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus Soillius Valerianus.

EDCS-09201479.translation: Titus Helvius Vitalis, six times Augustalis, erected this for himself and his wife Saturnina Optata while alive.
EDCS-09201479.summary: This inscription records Titus Helvius Vitalis, a six-times Augustalis, dedicating a monument for himself and his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-09201480.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Caius Fabius Martinus, his most excellent husband, a six-times Augustalis, and of his very rare son. Caius Fabius [Martinus], who died at the age of 18 years. Aelia Homullina, his wife, [erected this] for herself and [him].
EDCS-09201480.summary: This funerary inscription records Aelia Homullina dedicating a monument to her husband, Caius Fabius Martinus (a six-times Augustalis), and their son who died at age 18.

EDCS-09201481.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Aemilia Helidi, Sempronius Aca [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201481.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aemilia Helidi by Sempronius Aca.

EDCS-09201482.translation: Paternus, their son, set this up for his father Nundinus and his mother Materna.
EDCS-09201482.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Paternus for his parents, Nundinus and Materna.

EDCS-09201483.summary: This inscription simply records the name and parentage of Seranus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-09201484.translation: Her parents, while alive, set this up to the spirits of the underworld for Marcia, daughter of Marcus, a travelling merchant.
EDCS-09201484.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the parents, while alive, for their daughter Marcia, a merchant.

EDCS-09201485.translation: Varenius set this up for his most devoted daughter Domina, of Pannonian origin.
EDCS-09201485.summary: This inscription records Varenius setting up a monument for his daughter Domina, who was from Pannonia.

EDCS-09201487.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Severina, daughter of Bassus, Lucius Censorius Secundus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201487.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Lucius Censorius Secundus for Severina.

EDCS-09201488.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Casunia Servata, daughter of Casuna, she ordered this to be made in her will.
EDCS-09201488.summary: This funerary inscription states that Casunia Servata, daughter of Casuna, left instructions in her will for this monument to be erected.

EDCS-09201492.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Carinia Marcellina, Quintus Carinius Celsus set this up for his most deserving wife.
EDCS-09201492.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Quintus Carinius Celsus for his wife Carinia Marcellina.

EDCS-09201493.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Lucius Paetinius Paternus, his son Lucilius [dedicated this] for his excellent father.
EDCS-09201493.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Lucilius for his father, Lucius Paetinius Paternus.

EDCS-09201494.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Titus Senocondius Serapio, his wife Quintia Servatilla, his grandson Senocondius Secundus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201494.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Senocondius Secundus for his wife Quintia Servatilla and his grandson Titus Senocondius Serapio.

EDCS-09201496.translation: To Quadratus, grandfather, to Coblanvon, grandmother, to Lucia daughter of Quadratus, aunt, to Vegeta daughter of Quadratus, aunt.
EDCS-09201496.summary: This inscription is a dedication to deceased family members, including grandparents and aunts, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201497.translation: Tertulla, daughter of Lucius Catupris, and Lucia, daughter of Lucius Vassedonis.
