EDCS-09201497.summary: This inscription records two women, Tertulla and Lucia, identified by their patronymic and filiation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201498.translation: Domitia, daughter of Licinus, made (this) from her own resources. Lucia, granddaughter, and Tertula, daughter of Nurius.
EDCS-09201498.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Domitia, with a mention of her granddaughter Lucia and Tertula, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201499.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, of Primus, son of Primitivus.
EDCS-09201499.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (spirits of the underworld) for Primus, son of Primitivus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201500.summary: This inscription records Varro, identified by his filiation to Sextus Canius, likely a funerary or honorary inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201501.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Sextus Matucenus Frontinus, veteran of the XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis legion, by the order of Matucenia Placida, to her father, and to Syronia Quieta, her husband.
EDCS-09201501.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran, Sextus Matucenus Frontinus, with dedications made by his daughter Matucenia Placida and his wife Syronia Quieta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201502.translation: To Gnaeus Octavius Caiaucus, son of Gnaeus, according to the will of Caius Octavius, son of Gnaeus, of the Voltinia tribe, and Certus.
EDCS-09201502.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gnaeus Octavius Caiaucus, made as specified in the will of Caius Octavius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201503.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Firmia Blaesina, daughter of Blaesus. Titus Firmius Victor (dedicated this) to his mother.
EDCS-09201503.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Firmius Victor to his mother, Firmia Blaesina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201506.translation: To Titus Terentius Virillus, from his freedman.
EDCS-09201506.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a freedman to his patron, Titus Terentius Virillus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201508.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Adgubillus and Adgubiouna, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Aesculapius.
EDCS-09201508.summary: This is a votive inscription where Lucius Cornelius dedicates a payment to the god Aesculapius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201509.translation: Aulus Julius Leonas [dedicated] the gift which he had promised to Domestica, a freedwoman of Anubis, from his own money.
EDCS-09201509.summary: This is a dedication by Aulus Julius Leonas of a promised gift to a freedwoman.

EDCS-09201510.translation: Lepo, son of Gnaeus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-09201510.summary: This is a votive dedication to Apollo by Lepo, son of Gnaeus.

EDCS-09201511.translation: He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Fates.
EDCS-09201511.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Fates, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09201512.translation: Titus Pomponius, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-09201512.summary: This is a dedication by Titus Pomponius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09201514.translation: A lightning strike of the gods, buried.
EDCS-09201514.summary: This inscription records the burial of a lightning strike, considered a divine event.

EDCS-09201515.translation: A lightning strike of the gods, buried.
EDCS-09201515.summary: This inscription records the burial of a lightning strike, considered a divine event.

EDCS-09201516.translation: Telesphorus and Zosimus, freedmen, [dedicated this] to the genius of our Avitus.
EDCS-09201516.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Avitus by his freedmen Telesphorus and Zosimus.

EDCS-09201517.translation: Chelido, a freedwoman, [dedicated this] to the genius of our Publius Cornelius.
EDCS-09201517.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Publius Cornelius by his freedwoman Chelido.

EDCS-09201518.translation: Cerialis, a client, [dedicated this] to the genius of our Severus.
EDCS-09201518.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Severus by his client Cerialis.

EDCS-09201519.translation: Ascanius, a slave, [dedicated this] to the genius of our Caius.
EDCS-09201519.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Caius by his slave Ascanius.

EDCS-09201520.translation: To the genius of our Decimus, Titus Pompeius Peregrinus, his friend.
EDCS-09201520.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Decimus by his friend Titus Pompeius Peregrinus.

EDCS-09201521.translation: To the genius of our Quintus, Icarus, his freedman.
EDCS-09201521.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Quintus by his freedman Icarus.

EDCS-09201522.translation: To the genius of our Quintus, Trophimus, his slave.
EDCS-09201522.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Quintus by his slave Trophimus.

EDCS-09201523.translation: To the genius of our Titus, Euporus, his freedman.
EDCS-09201523.summary: This is a dedication to the genius of Titus by his freedman Euporus.

EDCS-09201524.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deities Ialus and Forto.

EDCS-09201526.translation: Cinnamus gladly paid his vow to Isis, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201526.summary: This is a votive inscription from Cinnamus to the goddess Isis.

EDCS-09201527.translation: Quintus Senius Eupius, freedman of Primitivus, gladly paid his vow to Augusta Isis, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201527.summary: This is a votive inscription from Quintus Senius Eupius to the goddess Isis Augusta.

EDCS-09201528.translation: Titia Savinis, weaver of the sanctuary, gladly paid her vow to Isis, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201528.summary: This is a votive inscription from Titia Savinis, identified as a sanctuary weaver, to the goddess Isis.

EDCS-09201529.translation: Gnaeus Pomponius Fronto, attendant, paid his vow to Juno, as he had vowed, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201529.summary: This is a votive inscription from Gnaeus Pomponius Fronto, an attendant, to the goddess Juno.

EDCS-09201530.translation: Agathopus, Cupitus, and Mansueta, freedmen, to our Juno Ibullae.
EDCS-09201530.summary: This inscription records a dedication by freedmen Agathopus, Cupitus, and Mansueta to the goddess Juno Ibullae.

EDCS-09201531.translation: To our Juno Mettia, from Bacchius, her freedman.
EDCS-09201531.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Mettia by her freedman, Bacchius.

EDCS-09201532.translation: To our Juno Quieta, from Vitalis, her freedman.
EDCS-09201532.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Quieta by her freedman, Vitalis.

EDCS-09201533.translation: To our Juno Severilla, from Onesimus Corinthus, her freedman.
EDCS-09201533.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Severilla by her freedman, Onesimus Corinthus.

EDCS-09201534.translation: To the Mountain Junes, Cinnamis paid his vow.
EDCS-09201534.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to the Mountain Junes by Cinnamis.

EDCS-09201535.translation: To Juno Regina, Quintus Valerius Donatus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201535.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Juno Regina by Quintus Valerius Donatus.

EDCS-09201537.translation: To Jupiter, Aurelius Albanus paid his vow.
EDCS-09201537.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Jupiter by Aurelius Albanus.

EDCS-09201538.translation: To Jupiter and Nemausus, Titus Flavius Hermes, supervisor of the work on the basilica, a marble worker and stonecutter, paid his vow.
EDCS-09201538.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Jupiter and Nemausus by Titus Flavius Hermes, a supervisor of basilica construction and a craftsman.

EDCS-09201540.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus and Nemausus, Caius Iulius Tiberi filius Tiberinus, from Berytus, first-rank centurion, paid his vow.
EDCS-09201540.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus and Nemausus by Caius Iulius Tiberi filius Tiberinus, a centurion from Berytus.

EDCS-09201541.translation: To I.N.P., Verus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201541.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to I.N.P. by Verus.

EDCS-09201543.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Lares, from Natalis Luttacus.
EDCS-09201543.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Augustan Lares by Natalis Luttacus.

EDCS-09201545.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Lares and Minerva of Nemausus. Avicantus Titus Cassius Felicio, freedman of Titus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09201545.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Avicantus Titus Cassius Felicio to the Augustan Lares and Minerva of Nemausus.

EDCS-09201546.translation: Severus, son of Secundinus, dedicated this to the god Liber Pater.
EDCS-09201546.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Liber Pater by Severus, son of Secundinus.

EDCS-09201547.translation: Paetus, son of Ireneus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09201547.summary: This inscription records Paetus, son of Ireneus, willingly fulfilling his vow.

EDCS-09201548.translation: Rufina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Lucubus.
EDCS-09201548.summary: This inscription records Rufina willingly fulfilling her vow to Lucubus.

EDCS-09201549.translation: Marcus and Lucius, sons of Hermolaius, dedicated this to Mars and our Genius.
EDCS-09201549.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and the Genius of Flavius by Marcus and Lucius, sons of Hermolaius.

EDCS-09201550.translation: Salvius, son of Secundinus, made this votive offering to Augustus and Mars Britovius.
EDCS-09201550.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Salvius, son of Secundinus, to Augustus and Mars Britovius.

EDCS-09201551.translation: Valerius Martialis, son of Aulus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars Britovius.
EDCS-09201551.summary: This inscription records Valerius Martialis, son of Aulus, willingly fulfilling his vow to Mars Britovius.

EDCS-09201552.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus and Lacavus, from bronze collected by subscription.
EDCS-09201552.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering to Mars Augustus and Lacavus, funded by collective contributions.

EDCS-09201553.translation: Lucius Classius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Mother.
EDCS-09201553.summary: This inscription records Lucius Classius willingly fulfilling his vow to the Mother goddess.

EDCS-09201554.translation: Marcus Pullius Celsus and Lucius Domitius Sedulus made this votive offering to Mercury Augustus.
EDCS-09201554.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Pullius Celsus and Lucius Domitius Sedulus to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-09201555.translation: To Mercury Augustus, Titus Valerius Gratus, in fulfillment of a vow to his patroness Valeria.
EDCS-09201555.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury Augustus made by Titus Valerius Gratus on behalf of his patroness Valeria.

EDCS-09201557.translation: A veteran has willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-09201557.summary: This inscription records a veteran fulfilling a vow to Mercury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201560.translation: He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Minerva.
EDCS-09201560.summary: This inscription records an unknown individual fulfilling a vow to Minerva.

EDCS-09201561.translation: Caius Andolatius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Nemausus.
EDCS-09201561.summary: This is a dedication to the local deity Nemausus by Caius Andolatius.

EDCS-09201562.translation: Quintus Crassius Secundinus, quaestor of the colony, to Nemausus.
EDCS-09201562.summary: This inscription records Quintus Crassius Secundinus, the quaestor of the colony, dedicating something to Nemausus.

EDCS-09201563.translation: Quintus Iulius Bucca, by vow, fulfilled his vow to Nemausus.
EDCS-09201563.summary: Quintus Iulius Bucca made a votive offering to Nemausus.

EDCS-09201564.translation: Sextus Iulius Severus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Nemausus.
EDCS-09201564.summary: This inscription records Sextus Iulius Severus making a votive offering to Nemausus.

EDCS-09201565.translation: Marius Paternus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Nemausus for his father.
EDCS-09201565.summary: Marius Paternus dedicated a vow to the god Nemausus on behalf of his father.

EDCS-09201566.translation: Valeria Procilla to the god Nemausus.
EDCS-09201566.summary: This is a dedication to the god Nemausus by Valeria Procilla.

EDCS-09201567.translation: Virrius fulfilled his vow to Nemausus.
EDCS-09201567.summary: This inscription records Virrius making a votive offering to Nemausus.

EDCS-09201568.translation: To the god Nemausus, Sextus Utullius Perseus by his will set up a water clock and two silver lamps.
EDCS-09201568.summary: This inscription records a testamentary dedication of a water clock and lamps to the god Nemausus by Sextus Utullius Perseus.

EDCS-09201570.translation: To Nemausus Augustus, the censor Iugarius, if his surviving daughter had died, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09201570.summary: This inscription records a conditional vow made to Nemausus Augustus by the censor Iugarius.

EDCS-09201576.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs, C. I. Severina willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201576.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by C. I. Severina to the August Nymphs.

EDCS-09201577.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs, Lucius Decumius Decumanus, son of Tertius Baebius, and Lucius Annius Allobrox, son of Lucius Pompitienus Martialis, at their own expense.
EDCS-09201577.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Decumius Decumanus and Lucius Annius Allobrox to the August Nymphs.

EDCS-09201579.translation: To the Parcae, Lucius Valerius Hybrida willingly fulfilled his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201579.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Lucius Valerius Hybrida to the Parcae.

EDCS-09201581.translation: To the Proximi, Bituka willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201581.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Bituka to the Proximi.

EDCS-09201582.translation: To her Proximi, Calvina willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201582.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Calvina to her Proximi.

EDCS-09201583.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cornelia Cupita to her Proximi.

EDCS-09201584.translation: To his Proximi, Gratus Celeris, son of Celer, willingly fulfilled his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201584.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gratus Celeris to his Proximi.

EDCS-09201585.translation: To her Proximi, Hosela Soliarionis, daughter of Soliaris, willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-09201585.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Hosela Soliarionis to her Proximi.

EDCS-09201586.translation: Lalia Primula paid her vow to her Proxumi.
EDCS-09201586.summary: This is a dedication by Lalia Primula to her Proxumi, likely indicating a funerary or memorial context.

EDCS-09201587.translation: Paterna Cari's daughter paid her vow to her Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201587.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Paterna Cari's daughter to her Proxumi, suggesting a personal devotion or fulfillment of an obligation.

EDCS-09201588.translation: Phronimus paid his vow to his Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201588.summary: This is a dedication by Phronimus to his Proxumi, likely a funerary or memorial inscription.

EDCS-09201589.translation: Pollentus paid his vow to his Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201589.summary: This inscription records Pollentus paying his vow to his Proxumi, possibly in a funerary context.

EDCS-09201590.translation: Pompeia Pompulla paid her vow to her Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201590.summary: This is a dedication by Pompeia Pompulla to her Proxumi, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-09201591.translation: Marcus Porcius Iuvenalis paid his vow to his Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201591.summary: This inscription records Marcus Porcius Iuvenalis fulfilling a vow to his Proxumi, probably related to a funerary monument.

EDCS-09201592.translation: To his Proxumi, Quintina and Ve[...]
EDCS-09201592.summary: This is an incomplete inscription mentioning Quintina and Ve[--] in relation to Proxumi, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-09201593.summary: This brief inscription mentions Tertia in relation to Proxumi, probably a funerary dedication.

EDCS-09201594.translation: Urassia paid her vow to her Proxumi willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09201594.summary: This inscription records Urassia paying her vow to her Proxumi, suggesting a funerary or memorial context.

EDCS-09201595.translation: Romania paid her vow to her Proxumi.
EDCS-09201595.summary: This is a dedication by Romania to her Proxumi, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-09201596.translation: Quartana will gladly and deservedly pay her vow, Byrria Severilla.
EDCS-09201596.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Byrria Severilla on behalf of Quartana, likely a female slave or freedwoman.

EDCS-09201601.translation: Marcus Valerius Severus, pontifex, from the offerings, sails and an altar for the Augustan Victory.
EDCS-09201601.summary: This inscription records a dedication of sails and an altar to the Augustan Victory by Marcus Valerius Severus, a pontifex, using funds from offerings.

EDCS-09201602.translation: Severa, daughter of Nigrus, gladly and deservedly paid her vow to Vulcan and the Winds.
EDCS-09201602.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Severa, daughter of Nigrus, to the gods Vulcan and the Winds.

EDCS-09201603.translation: Marcus Adrastius Secundus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201603.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Marcus Adrastius Secundus.

EDCS-09201604.translation: Quintus Aemilius Titullus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201604.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Quintus Aemilius Titullus.

EDCS-09201605.translation: Caius Annius, son of Caius, Cornelia, as interrex, vowed and set this up.
EDCS-09201605.summary: This inscription records a vow and erection of a monument by Caius Annius, son of Caius, in his capacity as interrex.

EDCS-09201606.translation: Lucius Cirratius Martialis gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201606.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Lucius Cirratius Martialis.

EDCS-09201608.translation: Condolu gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-09201608.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Condolu.

EDCS-09201610.translation: Lucius Trebonius Secundus, Publius Servilius Fronto, Sextus Trogius Severus, Baebius Virilis [paid?].
EDCS-09201610.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly dedicants or officials, with an incomplete record of their action.

EDCS-09201612.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by an individual whose name is only partially preserved as GOH.

EDCS-09201613.translation: Sextus paid his vow willingly, as was deserved.
EDCS-09201613.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Sextus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09201621.translation: Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, built this in his third consulship.
EDCS-09201621.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Agrippa, indicating a building project he commissioned during his third consulship.

EDCS-09201631.translation: To Gaius Aemilius Berenicianus Maximus, consul for the seventh time, member of the college of epulones, proconsul of the most distinguished province of Narbonensis, legate with praetorian authority of the province of Asia, praetor, supreme official, chosen among the tribunes by the divine Antoninus Magnus, urban quaestor, tribune with a broad stripe of the IV Scythica legion, and also of the VII Gemina, in his second tribuneship, decemvir for judging lawsuits.
EDCS-09201631.summary: This is a lengthy senatorial career inscription detailing the offices held by Gaius Aemilius Berenicianus Maximus, dating from the Antonine period.

EDCS-09201632.translation: Lucius Aemilius Honoratus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, held these offices: III vir capitalis, quaestor, proconsul of the province of Pontus and Bithynia, legate of the same province, plebeian aedile, praetor, prefect of the grain supply by decree of the Senate, fetial priest, proconsul of the province of Crete and Cyrene; he held these offices, by the favor of the best princeps, earlier than is usually permitted by years.
EDCS-09201632.summary: This is an official career inscription for Lucius Aemilius Honoratus, detailing his numerous public offices and noting he achieved them earlier than usual due to senatorial favor.

EDCS-09201633.translation: He held the offices of III vir capitalis, quaestor, and legate of the province of Pontus and Bithynia, plebeian aedile, and praetor; he held these offices, by the favor of the best princeps, earlier than is usually permitted by years. A place was granted by decree of the decurions to Honoratus Quintus Solon Fadius Severinus, an outstanding man, because of his past merits and present generosity, so that the baths might be made available sooner for the use of the people; the woodworkers of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201633.summary: This inscription honors Honoratus Quintus Solon Fadius Severinus by granting him a place for baths, acknowledging his generosity and earlier-than-usual holding of public offices, with the work undertaken by the Nemausian woodworkers.

EDCS-09201634.translation: To Gaius Fulvius Lupus Servilianus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, who was appointed among the praetorians by Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasian, prefect of the Longinian cavalry wing, quaesitor of the treasury, pontifex, prefect of the watch; dedicated by Iulia Concessa, daughter of Decimus, his wife.
EDCS-09201634.summary: This is a dedication by a wife, Iulia Concessa, to her husband Gaius Fulvius Lupus Servilianus, listing his significant imperial appointments under Vespasian.

EDCS-09201636.translation: To the Divine Shades of Umidius Avitus, soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, a beneficiary under Iunius Homullus, under the supervision of Titus Vitrasius Pollio, legate of the emperor.
EDCS-09201636.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Umidius Avitus, a soldier and beneficiary, mentioning his legion and the supervising officers.

EDCS-09201638.translation: To Lucius Ranio Optatus, consul, proconsul of the province of Narbonensis, a most distinguished man, legate of the emperor, legal officer for Asturia and Callaecia, curator of the Salarian Way, curator of the public affairs of Urbs Salvia, legate of the diocese, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Sicily, decemvir for judging lawsuits, a most upright governor; the people of Nemausus publicly.
EDCS-09201638.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Nemausus to Lucius Ranio Optatus, honoring his distinguished career including consulship and various provincial governorships and administrative roles.

EDCS-09201643.translation: To the Divine Shades of Licinia Flavia, daughter of Lucius, flaminica of the Augusta, wife of Sextus Adgennius Macrinus, tribune of the VI Victrix legion, IV vir iure dicundo, pontifex, prefect of artisans.
EDCS-09201643.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Flavia, honoring her religious and civic roles as well as her husband's military and official positions.

EDCS-09201644.translation: To Gaius Aemilius Postumus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, who held all the offices in his colony, tribune of the soldiers of the VI Victrix legion; by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09201644.summary: This is a dedication by decree of the decurions of a colony honoring Gaius Aemilius Postumus for his service and full tenure of local offices, including a military tribuneship.

EDCS-09201645.translation: To Lucius Attius Lucanus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, standard-bearer, centurion, tribune of the I Cohort of Roman Citizens, freeborn.
EDCS-09201645.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Attius Lucanus with a list of his military offices.

EDCS-09201646.translation: Lucius Attius Lucanus, freedman of Lucius Attius Lucanus, formerly primipilaris, dedicated this himself and to his freedman Lucius while alive.
EDCS-09201646.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman for himself and his fellow freedman, referencing his patron's status.

EDCS-09201647.translation: Titus Iulius Festus, discharged soldier of Emperor Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, served 25 years in the XVI Legion; by decree of the decurions, he received 50 measures of grain, a bath, and his own dwelling free of charge in perpetuity, and a plot of land between the two towers, assigned by Publius Pusionius Peregrinus, a member of the board of four and the board of eleven.
EDCS-09201647.summary: This inscription records the benefits granted to a discharged soldier, Titus Iulius Festus, for his service in the XVI Legion, with details of the officials involved.

EDCS-09201649.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Priscus Firmius Gallicanus, soldier of the VIII Praetorian Cohort, son of Titus Julius Priscus.
EDCS-09201649.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Priscus Firmius Gallicanus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-09201650.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Julius Successus, soldier of the Germanician Army, aged 30.
EDCS-09201650.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Julius Successus, a soldier in the Germanician Army.

EDCS-09201651.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Valerianus, soldier of the XX Britannica Legion, a beneficiary of the procurator Augusti, served 10 years, 7 months, and 25 days; lived 31 years, 5 months, and 26 days. Julia, daughter of Iulius, for her most pious son and for herself, dedicated this while alive.
EDCS-09201651.summary: This funerary inscription records the military service and lifespan of Lucius Valerianus, a soldier in the XX Britannica Legion, and is a dedication by his mother.

EDCS-09201652.translation: To Lucius Sammius Aemilianus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, who held a public horse, admitted to the V decuriae, Lupercus, flamen of the province of Narbonensis, prefect of the II Cohort of Spanish Vascones, Roman citizens; Lucius Sammius Eutycus, archiereus of the synod, his foster-father.
EDCS-09201652.summary: This inscription lists the civic and religious offices held by Lucius Sammius Aemilianus and his foster-father.

EDCS-09201653.translation: and to Philomus, to Quintus Solonius Severinus, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, from the V decuriae, with a public horse, Lupercus, a member of the board of four in charge of the treasury, flamen of the province of Narbonensis, military tribune of the VIII Augusta Legion, patron of the citizenship of Forum Iulii.
EDCS-09201653.summary: This inscription continues the list of honors for Quintus Solonius Severinus, detailing his civic, religious, and military roles, and his status as patron.

EDCS-09201657.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sextus Adgennius Hermetis, a member of the board of six, Augustalis. Valeria Marcelia, daughter of Marcus, his wife.
EDCS-09201657.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Sextus Adgennius Hermetis, an Augustalis, by his wife.

EDCS-09201658.translation: To Sextus Aelius Straton, a member of the board of six, Augustalis. Abescantus, his freedman.
EDCS-09201658.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a freedman, Abescantus, for Sextus Aelius Straton, an Augustalis.

EDCS-09201660.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Anicetus, six-man official of Augustus and honorary councilor, and to Iulia Theophila, his wife; they dedicated this to themselves while alive.
EDCS-09201660.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a couple, Lucius Aemilius Anicetus and Iulia Theophila, who held municipal offices.

EDCS-09201661.translation: To the Divine Shades of Lucius Aemilius Asyncritus, six-man official of Augustus. Lucius Aemilius Gamus, Aemilia Silvina, and Lucius Aemilius Gamicus dedicated this to their most devoted patron.
EDCS-09201661.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by freedmen to their patron, Lucius Aemilius Asyncritus, who was a six-man official of Augustus.

EDCS-09201662.translation: To the Divine Shades of Quintus Aemilius Firmus, aedile of the colony, and of his wife Cloustria Suavis. Their son Aemilianus dedicated this.
EDCS-09201662.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a son to his parents, Quintus Aemilius Firmus, an aedile, and his wife.

EDCS-09201663.translation: To the Divine Shades of Aemilia, daughter of Lucius, Titia, priestess of Augustus. She ordered this to be set up by her will.
EDCS-09201663.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a will for Aemilia Titia, who served as a priestess of Augustus.

EDCS-09201664.translation: To the Divine Shades of Titus Attius Quartio, aedile of the colony of the Nemausenses. Titus Attius Carpophorus and Titus Attius Ianuaris dedicated this to their patron.
EDCS-09201664.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Attius Carpophorus and Titus Attius Ianuaris to their patron, Titus Attius Quartio, an aedile of the colony.

EDCS-09201665.translation: Sextus Allius Repentinus, aedile of the colony, made this for himself and for his father Sextus Allius, his mother Annia, his brother Sextus Allius Nundinus, also an aedile of the colony, his brother Allius Vegetus, and his excellent wife Memmia, while alive.
EDCS-09201665.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Sextus Allius Repentinus, an aedile, for himself and his family, including his father, mother, brothers, and wife.

EDCS-09201666.translation: To the Divine Shades of Antonius Eutychetes, a corporately appointed six-man official of Augustus.
EDCS-09201666.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonius Eutychetes, who held the municipal office of six-man official of Augustus.

EDCS-09201667.translation: To the Divine Shades of Arionius Vindulo, a freedman and six-man official of Augustus. Vegetus, a freedman, dedicated this.
EDCS-09201667.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by a freedman, Vegetus, to Arionius Vindulo, who was also a freedman and a six-man official of Augustus.

EDCS-09201669.translation: To the memory of Marcus Attius Voltinius, son of Marcus, Paterno, honored with the public equestrian rank, also a councilor of the colony of Apollinare Reiorum, and a councilor of honorary rank of the colony Augusta Nemausi, aged 25, serving in office. Coelia Paterna, daughter of Sextus, dedicated this to her most devoted son.
EDCS-09201669.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Coelia Paterna to her son, Marcus Attius Paterno, who held equestrian rank and served as a councilor in two colonies before his death at age 25.

EDCS-09201670.translation: To the Divine Shades of Quintus Aurelius Euhelpistus, corporately appointed six-man official of Augustus. Quintus Curius Aurelianus and his freedman Aurelius Eutyches dedicated this.
EDCS-09201670.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Quintus Curius Aurelianus and his freedman Aurelius Eutyches to Quintus Aurelius Euhelpistus, a six-man official of Augustus.

EDCS-09201671.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Politicus Phoebus Restitutus, a freedman, and Syntyche, a freedwoman, for their former master Quintus Aurelius Hermes, a full citizen-priest and cloth merchant.
EDCS-09201671.summary: A funerary dedication by freedmen for their deceased patron, Quintus Aurelius Hermes, who held the position of Augustalis and was a cloth merchant.

EDCS-09201672.translation: Gaius Aurelius Parthenius, honored with the decurional offices of the colony Augusta Nemausi, a full citizen-priest of the colony Copia Claudia Augusta Lugudunense, and also of Narbo Martius and Firma Iulia Secundanorum at Arausio and Forum Julii, where he also held gratuitous offices.
EDCS-09201672.summary: An inscription honoring Gaius Aurelius Parthenius for his distinguished civic career and various public offices held in several Roman colonies, including Nemausus, Lugdunum, and Narbo.

EDCS-09201673.translation: To the full citizen-priest, from the will.
EDCS-09201673.summary: A brief inscription indicating a funerary dedication or endowment made according to the will of the deceased, who was an Augustalis.

EDCS-09201674.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Boduacius Karus made this for himself and his parents, for Titus Boduacius Karus and Gaia Messoris, daughter of Karus.
EDCS-09201674.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Boduacius Karus for himself and his parents, Titus Boduacius Karus and Gaia Messoris.

EDCS-09201675.translation: For Titus Guttur, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe, quaestor of the colony. Anteros Hyllus, his freedman.
EDCS-09201675.summary: A funerary dedication by the freedman Anteros Hyllus for his patron Titus Guttur, who served as quaestor in a Roman colony.

EDCS-09201677.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Titus Carantius Daphnus, a full citizen-priest. Carantia Lais and Carantius Fortunatus for their excellent patron.
EDCS-09201677.summary: A funerary inscription by Carantia Lais and Carantius Fortunatus honoring their deceased patron, Titus Carantius Daphnus, who was an Augustalis.

EDCS-09201678.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Carantia Tyches. Titus Carantius Daphnus, a full citizen-priest, erected this for his most excellent freedwoman, a woman of most rare character, while he was alive.
EDCS-09201678.summary: A funerary monument erected by Titus Carantius Daphnus, an Augustalis, for his freedwoman Carantia Tyches, praised for her exceptional character.

EDCS-09201679.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Cascellius, a full citizen-priest, formerly prefect of the engineers, prefect of the watch, and of arms, and for his wife Antonia Titulla.
EDCS-09201679.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Cascellius, who held multiple high-ranking military and civic positions, and to his wife Antonia Titulla.

EDCS-09201680.translation: For Casuria IVIII, Augustal priestess. Domitius Alypus for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-09201680.summary: A funerary inscription by Domitius Alypus for his wife Casuria, who held the distinguished religious office of Augustal priestess.

EDCS-09201683.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Cornelius Maximus, son of Marcus, a full citizen-priest and pontiff from the treasury, erected by [himself?].
EDCS-09201683.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Cornelius Maximus, who served as an Augustalis, pontiff, and held financial responsibilities within the treasury, possibly erected by himself.

EDCS-09201684.translation: To Lucius Domitius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, Axiounus, procurator, twice duovir.
EDCS-09201684.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Domitius, son of Lucius, a freedman and official, likely commemorating his civic service.

EDCS-09201685.translation: To the Divine Shades. Domitia Graecina, Augustal flaminica, set this up for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201685.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Domitia Graecina, an Augustal flaminica, for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-09201686.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Helvius Ecimarius, of the Voltinia tribe, aedile of the colony, and his wife Togicia Erucilla.
EDCS-09201686.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Helvius Ecimarius, aedile of the colony, and his wife Togicia Erucilla.

EDCS-09201687.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Publius, freedman of [Publius?], Eros, Voltinius, six-time Augustalis, censor, and for Censorina.
EDCS-09201687.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Publius Eros, a freedman and prominent local official, and Censorina.

EDCS-09201688.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Firminus Lucanus, freedman, six-time Augustalis, honored with decurional ornaments at Nemausus.
EDCS-09201688.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Firminus Lucanus, a freedman and local dignitary honored for his service at Nemausus.

EDCS-09201690.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Gaius Fulvius Niger, six-time Augustalis, corporatus, and decurion, and for his most rare and most holy father, Fulvia Cassiana, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-09201690.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Gaius Fulvius Niger, a local official, by his daughter Fulvia Cassiana, honoring her father.

EDCS-09201691.translation: To Lucius Gappius Secundus, four-time official of the treasury. For his most devoted husband, Pompia Servatilla.
EDCS-09201691.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Pompia Servatilla to her devoted husband, Lucius Gappius Secundus, a treasury official.

EDCS-09201692.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Titus Geminius Titanus, son of Titus, prefect of the vigiles and armorum. Acutia Epiteuxis set this up for her best husband and for herself while alive.
EDCS-09201692.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Acutia Epiteuxis for her husband Titus Geminius Titanus, a prefect, and for herself.

EDCS-09201693.translation: Marcus Gessius Augur and his wife Tettia Crescens, priestess of Isis, erected this monument for themselves on their own land, so that it would not pass to heirs.
EDCS-09201693.summary: This inscription records Marcus Gessius Augur and his wife Tettia Crescens, a priestess, setting up their own tomb to avoid inheritance claims.

EDCS-09201694.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Hortensia Vitalis, daughter of Marcus, Augustal flaminica. For her most excellent patroness, Hortensia Philete.
EDCS-09201694.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Hortensia Vitalis, an Augustal flaminica, by her patroness Hortensia Philete.

EDCS-09201695.translation: To the divine shades of Marcus Iccius Soterichus, a member of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales), his wife Obelia Philete made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-09201695.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iccius Soterichus, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-09201696.translation: To the divine shades of Verus Indamus, of the Voltinian tribe, formerly *aedilis* (magistrate) of the colony of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201696.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Verus Indamus, a former magistrate of Nemausus.

EDCS-09201697.translation: Domitia Eorte made this, while alive, for herself and for her husband who deserved well of her, formerly *aedilis* (magistrate) of the colony of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201697.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Domitia Eorte for herself and her husband, a former magistrate of Nemausus.

EDCS-09201699.translation: To the divine shades of Lucius Julius Agilis, a member of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales), by his freedman Natalis.
EDCS-09201699.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Julius Agilis, a member of the college of the six, erected by his freedman.

EDCS-09201700.translation: For Lucius Julius Capras, a member of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales), his wife Romania and his stepson Lucius Privignus Peculiaris erected this at their own expense.
EDCS-09201700.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the wife and stepson of Lucius Julius Capras, a member of the college of the six.

EDCS-09201702.translation: By decree of the decurions, land was granted to Julius Festus through Pusonius Peregrinus, by decree of the decurions and of Pusonius Peregrinus, a *IIII vir* (magistrate), to Titus Julius Festus.
EDCS-09201702.summary: This inscription records the granting of land by a decree of the local council to Titus Julius Festus.

EDCS-09201703.translation: To the divine shades of Lucius Julius Hesychus, a member of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales), and of Julia Thalusae, erected by their freedwoman Quieta.
EDCS-09201703.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Julius Hesychus and Julia Thalusae, dedicated by their freedwoman.

EDCS-09201704.translation: To Lucius Julius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Voltinian tribe, a member of the *aerarium* (treasury officials) and a corporately enrolled member of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales) of Nemausus, a plot of land was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09201704.summary: This inscription records the granting of land by the decurions to Lucius Julius Niger, a local official.

EDCS-09201705.translation: To Lucius Julius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Voltinian tribe, Aurelius Servatus, who had held all offices in his colony, a corporately enrolled member of the college of the six (IIII viri corporati Nemausenses), was granted a plot of land as patron, by decree of the decurions, at the request of the people.
EDCS-09201705.summary: This inscription records the granting of land by the decurions to Lucius Julius Niger, a prominent local figure and patron, at the people's request.

EDCS-09201706.translation: To the divine shades of Quintus Julius Pyrrhus and Quintus Julius Marsyas, members of the college of the six (IIIIII viri Augustales), and to theirs.
EDCS-09201706.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Julius Pyrrhus and Quintus Julius Marsyas, members of the college of the six.

EDCS-09201707.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Julia Ianuaria to her excellent father, Titus Julius Titullinus, decurion of Vienna.
EDCS-09201707.summary: A funerary dedication from Julia Ianuaria to her father, Titus Julius Titullinus, a decurion from Vienna.

EDCS-09201708.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: to Tertius Julius Varius, aedile of the Augustan colony of Nemausus, and to Tertia, his wife.
EDCS-09201708.summary: A funerary dedication to Tertius Julius Varius, an aedile of Nemausus, and his wife.

EDCS-09201709.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Julius Lupus to his excellent father, Lucius Julius Vegetus, a priest of the Augustales.
EDCS-09201709.summary: A funerary dedication from Lucius Julius Lupus to his father, Lucius Julius Vegetus, a priest of the Augustales.

EDCS-09201710.translation: Lucius Julius Hesychus to his companion, Iulia Thallusae, priestess of the Augustales.
EDCS-09201710.summary: A funerary dedication from Lucius Julius Hesychus to his companion, Iulia Thallusae, a priestess of the Augustales.

EDCS-09201711.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Lucretius Honoratus to his excellent wife, Iulia Titullina, priestess of the Augustales of Cabellio, and Quintus Lucretius Honoratus to his most devoted mother.
EDCS-09201711.summary: A joint funerary dedication from Lucius Lucretius Honoratus and Quintus Lucretius Honoratus to their wife/mother, Iulia Titullina, a priestess.

EDCS-09201712.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Serana, wife of Titus Carus Soterichus, priest of the Augustales.
EDCS-09201712.summary: A funerary dedication from Serana to her husband, Titus Carus Soterichus, a priest of the Augustales.

EDCS-09201714.translation: Lucius Letius Marullus, priest of the Augustales and decurion of the ornamentarii, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-09201714.summary: A self-funded funerary monument made by Lucius Letius Marullus, a priest of the Augustales and a decurion.

EDCS-09201715.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Licinia Pia to her most devoted parents, the priest of the Augustales, Gaius Licinius Martialis, and Fabricius Chresimus.
EDCS-09201715.summary: A funerary dedication from Licinia Pia to her parents, Gaius Licinius Martialis (a priest of the Augustales) and Fabricia Chresimi.

EDCS-09201716.translation: Quintus Lucretius Honoratus made this for his excellent father, Honoratus, priest of the Augustales, iuris dicundi, and prefect of the vigiles and armorum, out of devotion.
EDCS-09201716.summary: A funerary dedication from Quintus Lucretius Honoratus to his father, Honoratus, who held multiple high civic and military offices.

EDCS-09201717.translation: Lucius Lucretius Lascivus, priest of the Augustales, made this for himself and his wife, Licinia Saturnina, while alive.
EDCS-09201717.summary: A self-funded funerary monument made by Lucius Lucretius Lascivus, a priest of the Augustales, and his wife.

EDCS-09201718.translation: Quintus Magius Epithynchanus, a six-man Augustalis and ornamental decurion, made this while alive for himself.
EDCS-09201718.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Quintus Magius Epithynchanus to himself.

EDCS-09201719.translation: To the departed spirits. Epitychanus, freedman and heir of Quintus Magius Zosimus, a six-man Augustalis, and of Pompeia Acerronia, his wife, and of Fabia Helpidis.
EDCS-09201719.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Magius Zosimus, a six-man Augustalis, his wife Pompeia Acerronia, and Fabia Helpidis, and was set up by Epitychanus, his freedman and heir.

EDCS-09201720.translation: Gaius Marcius Philologus, a six-man Augustalis, placed this while alive for himself.
EDCS-09201720.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Gaius Marcius Philologus to himself.

EDCS-09201721.translation: Pompeia Toutodivicis, daughter of Toutodivix, for Gaius Marcius Voltinia Celsus, son of Marcius, a six-man, and for her husband Pompeia Toutodivicis, daughter of Toutodivix.
EDCS-09201721.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Pompeia Toutodivicis for her husband Gaius Marcius Voltinia Celsus and herself.

EDCS-09201722.translation: He lived 20 years. Gaius Marius Cupitus, son of Gaius Marius Iuven Julianus, who was honored with decurional ornaments.
EDCS-09201722.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Gaius Marius Iuven Julianus, who had received ornamental honors, and was set up by his son Gaius Marius Cupitus.

EDCS-09201723.translation: To the departed spirits. His most dutiful daughter placed this for her father Gaius Marius Onesimus, a six-man Augustalis, public haruspex.
EDCS-09201723.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gaius Marius Onesimus, a six-man Augustalis and public haruspex, and was set up by his daughter.

EDCS-09201724.translation: M. Merops, freedman and heir of a six-man Augustalis.
EDCS-09201724.summary: This funerary inscription honors a six-man Augustalis and was set up by his freedman and heir, M. Merops.

EDCS-09201725.translation: For Lucius Messius Euhodus, a six-man Augustalis, and most dutiful father.
EDCS-09201725.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated to Lucius Messius Euhodus, a six-man Augustalis and father, by an unknown person.

EDCS-09201726.translation: To Lucius Messius Silvinus, aedile of the colony of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201726.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Messius Silvinus, an aedile of the colony of Nemausus.

EDCS-09201727.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Albius Maternus placed this for his friend Marcus Nemonis Titus, a six-man Augustalis, a corporatus of the colony of Augusta Nemausensis, and for Antonia Servata, his wife.
EDCS-09201727.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Nemonis Titus, a six-man Augustalis, his wife Antonia Servata, and was set up by their friend Lucius Albius Maternus.

EDCS-09201728.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Numerius Messor, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, prefect of the vigiles and arms, from his devoted son; and to Iulia Valentina, from her excellent brother Numerius Nula and Numina.
EDCS-09201728.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Numerius Messor, a prefect of the vigiles, and his wife Iulia Valentina, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09201729.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Numeria Tertulla, a priestess, from her daughter Numeria ria.
EDCS-09201729.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numeria Tertulla, a priestess, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201730.translation: To Gaius Pinarius Albus, son of Lucius, aedile of the colony, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-09201730.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Gaius Pinarius Albus, an aedile of the colony, erected after his death as per his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201731.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The IIII viri Augustales ordered this to be made in accordance with the will of Gaius Pomponius Diomedes.
EDCS-09201731.summary: This funerary inscription states that the IIII viri Augustales carried out the wishes expressed in the will of Gaius Pomponius Diomedes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201732.translation: To Decimus Pompeius Homunculus, my father, from Q.
EDCS-09201732.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Decimus Pompeius Homunculus by his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201733.translation: To Pompeius Turunus, a IIII vir.
EDCS-09201733.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Pompeius Turunus, a member of the IIII viri, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201734.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gnaeus Reuconius Sextinus, quaestor of the Augustan colony of Nemausus, from his freedmen, his excellent patron.
EDCS-09201734.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gnaeus Reuconius Sextinus, a quaestor of Nemausus, dedicated by his freedmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201736.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This was ordered to be made] in accordance with the will of Tertius Sammius Karius, quaestor of the Voltinia tribe in Nemausus, and Sammia Attica, his wife, and Lucius Hortentius Karius, their son.
EDCS-09201736.summary: This funerary inscription records that the monument was erected according to the will of Tertius Sammius Karius, a local official, and includes his wife and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201737.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Sammia Secundina, daughter of Lucius, a priestess of Augustus, from her heirs.
EDCS-09201737.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sammia Secundina, a priestess of Augustus, dedicated by her heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201738.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Sammia Severina, daughter of Quintus, priestess of Augustus in Nemausus, from Gaius Terentius Anicetus, to his most excellent friend and to himself, erected during his lifetime.
EDCS-09201738.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sammia Severina, a priestess of Augustus in Nemausus, dedicated by her friend Gaius Terentius Anicetus, who also provided for himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09201739.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Scarius Rufinus, a member of the board of six Augustan priests, set this up for himself and his family in his lifetime.
EDCS-09201739.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Scarius Rufinus for himself and his family.

EDCS-09201740.translation: For Secundus, freedman of Postumus, a member of the board of six Augustan priests.
EDCS-09201740.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Secundus, a freedman and priest.

EDCS-09201741.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Senucius Servatus, quaestor of the Augustan colony of Nemausus, and his wife Iulia Helpis, the best of wives, set this up for themselves while alive.
EDCS-09201741.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcus Senucius Servatus and his wife for themselves.

EDCS-09201742.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Severius Severinus, aedile of the Augustan colony of Nemausus, ordered this to be set up by his will.
EDCS-09201742.summary: This is a funerary dedication ordered by Lucius Severius Severinus in his will.

EDCS-09201743.translation: To Quintus Soillius Valerianus, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe, a member of the board of four Augustan priests, quaestor from the treasury, pontiff, prefect of the watch, who held the public horse, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09201743.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Soillius Valerianus with public recognition, including the public horse, as decreed by local officials.

EDCS-09201744.translation: To Quintus Soillius Valerianus, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe, who held the public horse, having fulfilled all priesthoods at home and in the province, as patron of the inhabitants of Cabelles, Avennenses, Forojulienses, and Aptenses.
EDCS-09201744.summary: This inscription details the extensive public and religious career of Quintus Soillius Valerianus, recognizing his patronage.

EDCS-09201745.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Eucharistus and Germanus, freedmen of Sextus Spurius Silvinus, a member of the board of six Augustan priests, set this up.
EDCS-09201745.summary: This funerary dedication was set up by the freedmen of a priest.

EDCS-09201746.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Tasgius Fortunatus, freedman, set this up for his most excellent patron, Quintus Tasgius Hermes, a member of the board of six Augustan priests.
EDCS-09201746.summary: This is a dedication by a freedman to his deceased patron, who was a priest.

EDCS-09201747.translation: To Gaius Terentius Philetus, member of the board of six Augustan priests.
EDCS-09201747.summary: This inscription records the membership of Gaius Terentius Philetus in a prestigious priestly college.

EDCS-09201748.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Tertia, daughter of Titus, a priestess of the Augustan cult, by her will.
EDCS-09201748.summary: This funerary monument was erected according to the will of Tertia, a priestess.

EDCS-09201749.translation: To Trophimus, patron, one of the six Augustan priests, a corporatus.
EDCS-09201749.summary: This inscription honors Trophimus, a patron and corporatus, who held one of the six Augustan priesthoods.

EDCS-09201750.translation: To Titus Turpilius Capito, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe, aedile of the colony.
EDCS-09201750.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Titus Turpilius Capito, aedile of the colony, who was a member of the Voltinian tribe.

EDCS-09201751.translation: To Gaius Valerius Lussor, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, quaestor of the colony.
EDCS-09201751.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Gaius Valerius Lussor, quaestor of the colony, who was a member of the Voltinian tribe.

EDCS-09201752.translation: To Lucius Valerius Philumenus Cartarius, one of the six Augustan priests.
EDCS-09201752.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Valerius Philumenus Cartarius, a member of one of the six Augustan priesthoods.

EDCS-09201753.translation: To the Divine Manes, from Titulla, wife of Gaius Valerius Saturninus, quaestor of the colony of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201753.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titulla, honoring her husband Gaius Valerius Saturninus, a former quaestor of the colony of Nemausus.

EDCS-09201754.translation: From Calidia Munatia, daughter of Servatus, who held all the offices.
EDCS-09201754.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Calidia Munatia in honor of her father Servatus, who had held all the local offices.

EDCS-09201755.translation: To Publius Varius Trophimus, one of the six Augustan priests.
EDCS-09201755.summary: This inscription honors Publius Varius Trophimus, a member of one of the six Augustan priesthoods.

EDCS-09201756.translation: To the Divine Manes, from Sempronia Statuta, in memory of her most devoted father, Lucius Velloudius Stratus, a decurion of the Sanitienses and an honorary decurion of the colony Augusta Nemausensis.
EDCS-09201756.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Sempronia Statuta for her father, Lucius Velloudius Stratus, who was a decurion and an honorary decurion of the colony Augusta Nemausensis.

EDCS-09201757.translation: Eternal sleep to Aulus Veratius Severus, who held all the offices in his colony.
EDCS-09201757.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Veratius Severus, who had served in all the offices of his colony.

EDCS-09201758.translation: To the Divine Manes, from the Augustan goddess of the Vocontii, the curator of the games, and the corporatus priest, Gaius Veratius Trophimus.
EDCS-09201758.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Gaius Veratius Trophimus, who held multiple civic and religious roles including Augustan priest, curator of the games, and corporatus.

EDCS-09201759.translation: Marcus Verius Victor, council member of the colony of Iulia Augusta Apollinaris Reiorum, and also of the colony of Augusta Nemausensis, an ornamentarius, made this tomb for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-09201759.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Verius Victor, an ornamentarius from two colonies, who dedicated the monument to himself and his family.

EDCS-09201760.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titia, wife of Virillius, dedicated this to Marcus Vernonius Virillion, her most excellent husband, who was an aedile of the colony of Virillia; and Vernonius Titus, her son, to his most devoted father.
EDCS-09201760.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Vernonius Virillion, an aedile of the colony of Virillia, dedicated by his wife and son.

EDCS-09201761.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Vettius Helis, a member of the college of Augustales, and Vettia Servanda, his wife, set this up for themselves while alive.
EDCS-09201761.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vettius Helis and his wife Vettia Servanda, both members of the college of Augustales, who dedicated it to themselves while alive.

EDCS-09201762.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Fuscina dedicated this to her husband, Gaius Vettius Ermetis, a member of the college of Augustales.
EDCS-09201762.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vettius Ermetis, a member of the college of Augustales, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-09201763.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Maria Cresime dedicated this to her most excellent husband, Gaius Vireius Voltinia Virilis, son of Gaius, a member of the college of four men with judicial power.
EDCS-09201763.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vireius Voltinia Virilis, a member of the college of four men with judicial power, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-09201764.translation: To Sextus Virillius Severinus, son of Sextus, of the Voltinian tribe, member of the college of four men with judicial power, pontiff, and prefect of the vigiles and armorum, by Primitivos, his freedman.
EDCS-09201764.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Virillius Severinus, a prominent official and priest, dedicated by his freedman.

EDCS-09201765.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Apicia Asclepias, freedwoman of Lucius Apicius Candidus, a member of the college of six men, dedicated this to her most devoted patron.
EDCS-09201765.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Apicius Candidus, a member of the college of six men, dedicated by his freedwoman.

EDCS-09201766.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Romanius Epictetus dedicated this to his most devoted Zosimus, a member of the college of six men.
EDCS-09201766.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Zosimus, a member of the college of six men, dedicated by Romanius Epictetus.

EDCS-09201774.translation: Promactus Oele D gave the dedication and his shield.
EDCS-09201774.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a shield by Promactus Oele D.

EDCS-09201778.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelia Cosmia dedicated this to her most devoted brother, Sextus Aurelius Zosimus, a stater of Nemausus.
EDCS-09201778.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Aurelius Zosimus, a stater of Nemausus, dedicated by his sister.

EDCS-09201779.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iuventia Fortunata to her companion Secundio, slave of the colony.
EDCS-09201779.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Secundio, a slave of the colony, by his companion Iuventia Fortunata.

EDCS-09201784.summary: This is a dedication or commission by Titus Crispius Reburrus, likely for a monument or artifact.

EDCS-09201795.summary: This is a dedication or commission by Titus Crispius Reburrus, likely for a monument or artifact.

EDCS-09201797.translation: To Beryllus, the essedarius (chariot fighter), a freedman, Greek by nation, 25 years old. His wife Nomas to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-09201797.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Beryllus, a 25-year-old Greek essedarius, dedicated by his wife Nomas.

EDCS-09201798.translation: Faustus, an essedarius, freedman, Arab by nation, to his companion, with his own money, for 37 crowns. From the munificence of Caius Pomponius Martialis.
EDCS-09201798.summary: This funerary epitaph records Faustus, an Arab essedarius, who earned 37 crowns and was commemorated by his companion using funds from Caius Pomponius Martialis.

EDCS-09201799.translation: Columbus Serenianus, the Murmillo, 25 years old, Aeduan by nation, rests here. His wife Sperata.
EDCS-09201799.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Columbus Serenianus, a 25-year-old Aeduan Murmillo gladiator, by his wife Sperata.

EDCS-09201800.summary: This inscription simply records the name and profession of Iuvencus, a Murmillo gladiator, likely on his tomb or a related monument.

EDCS-09201801.translation: Lucius Pompeius, the Retiarius, with 9 crowns, Viannesian by nation, 25 years old. His wife Optata dedicated this from her own funds.
EDCS-09201801.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Optata for her husband Lucius Pompeius, a 25-year-old Retiarius gladiator who had won 9 crowns.

EDCS-09201802.translation: To the Retiarius Gaius Heius Phoebus, with 13 crowns. His wife gave this from her own funds.
EDCS-09201802.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Gaius Heius Phoebus, a Retiarius gladiator with 13 crowns, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-09201803.translation: Aptus the Thraex, Alexandrian by nation, 37 years old. His wife Optata dedicated this from her own funds.
EDCS-09201803.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aptus, a 37-year-old Thraex gladiator from Alexandria, dedicated by his wife Optata.

EDCS-09201805.translation: Thraex Heracla, free, aged 30, is buried here. Peregrinus set [this up] at his own expense.
EDCS-09201805.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Thraex Heracla, set up by Peregrinus.

EDCS-09201806.translation: Lucius Sestius Latinus, the trainer, dedicated this to the Thracian Quintus Vettius Gracchus, winner of three crowns, aged 30, a Spaniard by nation.
EDCS-09201806.summary: This is a dedication for Quintus Vettius Gracchus, a Thracian gladiator, by his trainer.

EDCS-09201807.translation: Sextus Spurius Piperolus, a banker, [set this up] for himself and his family and his wife Secunda, while alive.
EDCS-09201807.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Spurius Piperolus, a banker, set up for himself and his family.

EDCS-09201808.translation: Lucius Fabius Hermes, a dropacistes, set this up for himself and his family, while alive.
EDCS-09201808.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fabius Hermes, a dropacistes, set up for himself and his family.

EDCS-09201810.translation: To the Divine Shades: Vitalis, comrade of Primion, who worked in the iron mines.
EDCS-09201810.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Vitalis, comrade of Primion.

EDCS-09201811.translation: Gnaeus Pompius Maximus Fontanus, son of Gnaeus, [set this up] for himself and for Gnaeus Pompius Maximus, his grandfather, son of Gnaeus, and for Gaius Marius, his father, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-09201811.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Pompius Maximus Fontanus, set up for himself, his grandfather, and his father.

EDCS-09201812.translation: Censor Iugarius, while alive, [set this up] for himself and...
EDCS-09201812.summary: This is a partial funerary epitaph for Censor Iugarius, set up for himself.

EDCS-09201813.translation: To the Divine Shades: For Quintus Valerius Virrilio, a student of law, and for his sister Valeria Quita. Annia, mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-09201813.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Valerius Virrilio and his sister Valeria Quita, dedicated by their mother Annia.

EDCS-09201814.translation: To the Shades: Of Reburrus, son of Virilis, a linen merchant.
EDCS-09201814.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Reburrus, son of Virilis, a linen merchant.

EDCS-09201815.translation: To the Divine Shades: For Caius Antistius Antus, the doctor. Syneros, freedman of Antistia Irena.
EDCS-09201815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Antistius Antus, dedicated by his freedman Syneros.

EDCS-09201816.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, of Titus Caecilius Optatus, aedile of the colony.
EDCS-09201816.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus Caecilius Optatus, an aedile of a colony.

EDCS-09201817.translation: To Flavia Hedo, a doctor, by her will.
EDCS-09201817.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the doctor Flavia Hedo, according to her will.

EDCS-09201818.translation: To Lucius Avidius Secundus, a musician; his wife Festa.
EDCS-09201818.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the musician Lucius Avidius Secundus, set up by his wife Festa.

EDCS-09201819.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Lucius Trebonius Nicephorus Patillus, a tavern keeper; Maximius Epaphroditus, to his best friend.
EDCS-09201819.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Maximius Epaphroditus to his friend Lucius Trebonius Nicephorus Patillus, a tavern keeper.

EDCS-09201820.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Gaius Julius Albus, a food vendor; his wife Spuria Euplia.
EDCS-09201820.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Julius Albus, a food vendor, set up by his wife Spuria Euplia.

EDCS-09201821.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Afrodis Symmele of the troop of Galli from Memphis and Paris, pantomime performers, with Sextus as administrator.
EDCS-09201821.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Afrodis Symmele, a pantomime performer, associated with a troop from Memphis and Paris.

EDCS-09201822.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Marcus Hortensius Primus, a musician in the sacred rites.
EDCS-09201822.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcus Hortensius Primus, a musician associated with religious ceremonies.

EDCS-09201823.translation: Hail Naevius, be well, whoever you are, to Gnaeus Naevius Diadumenus, a slave dealer in Greek.
EDCS-09201823.summary: This is a greeting and wish for well-being directed towards Gnaeus Naevius Diadumenus, a dealer in Greek slaves.

EDCS-09201824.translation: Gaius Terentius Romulus, also known as Drimulus, wrestler or masseur, made this in his lifetime for himself and his family.
EDCS-09201824.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Terentius Romulus, who was involved in athletic activities, for himself and his family.

EDCS-09201825.translation: Lucius Valerius Secundinus, twice master of the guild of water-skin makers of Nîmes, placed this in his lifetime.
EDCS-09201825.summary: This is a funerary monument placed by Lucius Valerius Secundinus, who twice held the leadership of the guild of water-skin makers in Nîmes.

EDCS-09201826.translation: To Gaius Maius Saturninus, a horn-blower.
EDCS-09201826.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Maius Saturninus, who held the rank of cornicularius, possibly a military or administrative official.

EDCS-09201827.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Brittius Saturninus.

EDCS-09201828.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Faustus Terpnus Strobilus had this made at his own expense for Lucius Iulius Phtongus, his most devoted patron.
EDCS-09201828.summary: This funerary dedication, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, honors a patron named Lucius Iulius Phtongus, with Faustus Terpnus Strobilus as the dedicator.

EDCS-09201830.translation: The first of the freedmen magistrates, Gnaeus Aemilius Dionysius, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-09201830.summary: This inscription records Gnaeus Aemilius Dionysius, holding the office of first freedman magistrate, commissioning a work at his own expense.

EDCS-09201831.translation: To Albucius Postumus of the Voltinian tribe, from Aquae, and to Titus Albucius Tertius of the Voltinian tribe, from Aquae.
EDCS-09201831.summary: This inscription records a dedication to two individuals, Albucius Postumus and Titus Albucius Tertius, both from Aquae and belonging to the Voltinian tribe.

EDCS-09201832.translation: Titus Firmius Marinus, son of Firmanus, of the Voltinian tribe, a Vocontian, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-09201832.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Titus Firmius Marinus, indicating his tribal affiliation and origin.

EDCS-09201833.summary: This inscription simply identifies Sextus Iulius Balbus and notes his likely origin or association with Galatia.

EDCS-09201834.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Articilla placed this, while alive, for her most excellent husband, Tertius Pompeius Maternus, a citizen of Reia, and for herself.
EDCS-09201834.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Iulia Articilla for her husband Tertius Pompeius Maternus and herself, identifying him as a citizen of Reia.

EDCS-09201835.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Parents [set this up] for Lucius Taurinus Aurelius, citizen of Elusa, aged 23.
EDCS-09201835.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Taurinus Aurelius, a 23-year-old citizen of Elusa, commemorated by his parents.

EDCS-09201836.translation: Andusia Brugetia, Tedusia, Vatrute, Ugerni, Sextantius Briginnus, Statuma, Virinnus, Ucetia, Segusion.
EDCS-09201836.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly a dedication or record of individuals associated with a particular place or event, likely of Gallic origin.
